followed, there are no restrictions on the positional relationship between stones or the length of the string.Also, stones are small enough to be treated as points, and strings are thin enough to be treated as line segments.Furthermore, she never connects two or more strings to the same pair of stones, nor does she tie both ends of a string to the same stone.Marie initially drew magic circles by gluing stones to the flat walls of her house.However, she eventually realized that there was a magic circle that she could never create.After a while, she devised a method to determine whether a magic circle could be created on a flat wall, that is, on a two-dimensional plane.A magic circle cannot be created on a two-dimensional plane if and only if it contains the following parts.In order to draw such a magic circle, she devised a spell that fixed stones in the air.In three-dimensional space, these magic circles can also be drawn.On the contrary, she discovered that any magic circle can be drawn in three-dimensional space.Now, here's the problem.One day Marie decided to create a magic circle of footlights at the entrance to her house.However, since she had already set up various magic circles in the narrow entrance, she had to draw the magic circle for the footlight on the only space left, a one-dimensional straight line.Write a program that determines whether or not the magic circle of some footlights that she designed can be drawn on a straight line, and outputs a yes if it can be drawn, and a no if not.The magic circle is given by the number of stones n, the number of strings m, and the information of m strings.The string is represented by the numbers u and v of the stones at each end of it.Stones are numbered from 1 to n, where n is between 1 and 100,000, and m is between 0 and 1,000,000.A sequence of multiple datasets is given as input.The end of the input is indicated by a line of two zeros.Each dataset is as follows.1st line Number of stones n Number of strings m (integer integer; half-width space separated) 2nd line 1st string information u v (integer integer; half-width space separated) 3rd line 2nd string information: m+1 line m-th string information The number of datasets does not exceed 20.Prints yes or no on one line for each input dataset.

Kyugo decided to observe ant nests as part of his free research during his summer vacation.The transparent observation case that his grandfather prepared for his grandson is very unique and has the shape shown in Figure 1.This case consists of two congruent convex polygons s1 and s2 and several rectangles.Place one of the rectangular sides other than s1 and s2 so that it touches the floor.When Mr. Kyugo started observing, he noticed that even though the amount of soil contained was the same, the height of the soil varied depending on the selection of the bottom surface (Figure 2).Mr. Kyugo wanted to know how much more expensive it would be depending on how the case was placed.Write a program that takes as input the shape of convex polygon s1, the distance d between s1 and s2, and the volume of soil V, and outputs the maximum value of the soil height.The soil shall be level as soon as the bottom of the case is placed on the floor.The shape of s1 in the transparent case is given by n vertices on a two-dimensional coordinate plane.These vertices are entered in counterclockwise order.Let n be between 3 and 100, and d and V each be an integer between 1 and 10,000.In addition, the coordinates (x, y) of the vertices of the polygon, where x and y are integers between -1,000 and 1,000, respectively.V does not exceed the volume of the case.A sequence of multiple datasets is given as input.The end of the input is indicated by a line of zeros.Each dataset is as follows.1st line n d V (Integer Integer Integer; separated by half-width spaces) 2nd line Coordinates of the 1st vertex x y (Integer Integer; separated by half-width spaces) 3rd line Coordinates of the 2nd vertex: n+1 Line Coordinates of the nth vertex The number of datasets does not exceed 20.Outputs the maximum soil height for each input dataset.The answer must have an error of no more than 0.00001.

Aika's family runs a small coffee shop.The scones that Aika's mother baked were delicious and the shop was very busy.One of the waitress Aika's jobs is to deliver scones that are baked one after another to the customers' seats.The baked scones are placed on trays and lined up on the counter.Let K i be the number of scones on the i-th tray.Aika must deliver exactly m scones to each customer.Aika can hold as many trays as she wants at once, and she can hand out scones to one customer from multiple trays, or to multiple customers from one tray.There are so many customers coming to the coffee shop that even if you carry all the scones on the counter, you won't be able to reach them all.However, after delivering to as many customers as possible, there may be fewer than m scones left over.Aika will receive such scones as a reward for helping out.At this point, Aika suddenly thought.If you deliver scones to customers with only some of the trays instead of holding all the trays at once, the number of scones you have left should be different.By choosing the right tray, you may be able to have more scones left over.Aika decided to choose one continuous range of trays on the counter so that her mother wouldn't find out that she was choosing trays intentionally.Also, Aika only has one chance to receive scones because her father and mother carry out the remaining trays.Now, how many scones can Aika receive?Please write a program to calculate.The number of trays, n, is between 1 and 30,000, and m is between 1 and 100,000.Also, each element K i of the sequence is 0 or more and 2 32 -1.A sequence of multiple datasets is given as input.The end of the input is indicated by a line of two zeros.Each dataset is as follows.1st line n m (integer integer; separated by half-width spaces) 2nd line Information about scones on the tray K 1 K 2 ... K n (all integers; separated by half-width spaces) K i : Number of scones on the i-th tray The number of datasets does not exceed 50.Outputs the maximum number of scones you can receive for each dataset in one line.

Dear players, welcome to PC Koshien.Computer Koshien is celebrating its 10th anniversary this year, but the number of questions asked and the total score vary from year to year.Each question has a score determined according to its difficulty level.When there are 10 questions and each question is given a score, please create a program that outputs the total.Input is given in the following format.The input consists of 10 lines, and the integer s i (0≤ s i ≤ 100) representing the score of question i is given in the i-th line.Outputs the total score on one line.

To ride the Shinkansen, you need two tickets: a boarding ticket and a limited express ticket.These are separate tickets because there are some routes that do not use the Shinkansen, but for routes that only use the Shinkansen, a single ``ride/limited express ticket'' may be issued that serves as both a boarding ticket and a limited express ticket.Automatic ticket gates must read these tickets and open the gate only when the correct ticket is inserted.Please create a program that determines whether one ``passenger ticket'' and one ``limited express ticket,'' or both, or one ``boarding/limited express ticket'' has been inserted, and determines whether the automatic ticket gate door should open or close.Input is given in the following format.The input consists of one line containing three integers separated by a single space.b 1 indicates the inserted state of the "passenger ticket," b 2 indicates the inserted state of the "limited express ticket," and b 3 indicates the inserted state of the "ride/limited express ticket."The input state is represented by 0 or 1, where 0 means it is not turned on and 1 means it is turned on.However, the only possible combinations of input states are as follows.Outputs Open or Close, which indicates whether the automatic ticket gate is opened or closed, in one line.

I decided to plant vegetables in my home garden.I had n seeds, so I sowed them one seed a day over n days.All seeds have sprouted and are growing rapidly.I can't wait for harvest time.One day, as I was watering my seedlings as usual, I noticed something strange.There should be n vegetable seedlings, but there is one more.Weeds have started to grow.I want to pull them out immediately, but the problem is that all the seedlings look very similar and I can't tell the difference between vegetables and weeds.The clue is how fast the vegetables grow.This vegetable continues to grow for a fixed length each day ever since it is sown.However, I don't know how many centimeters this "determined length" is.I also forgot how many days ago I planted the first seeds.The seedlings are lined up in a row, but the only thing I remember is that when I sowed the seeds, I sowed one seed each day, starting from the right.Please create a program that inputs the length of n+1 seedlings and outputs the length of weeds.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of zeros.Input is given in the following format.n (4 ≤ n ≤ 100) in the first line is an integer representing the number of vegetable seedlings.The second line contains n+1 integers separated by one space, and h i (1 ≤ h i ≤ 10 9 ) indicates the length of the i-th seedling from the left.An input such that h 1 h 2 ... h n+1 is an arithmetic progression cannot be given.The number of datasets does not exceed 500.Output the weed length for each dataset.

Perform the following operations on a four-digit number N consisting of numbers 0 to 9.At this time, it is known that any four-digit number will eventually become 6174, unless all digits are the same number (0000, 1111, etc.).For example, if N = 2012, 1st time (N = 2012): L = 2210, S = 0122, L-S = 2088 2nd time (N = 2088): L = 8820, S = 0288, L-S = 8532 3rd time (N = 8532): L = 8532, S =2358, L-S = 6174, and it takes 3 operations to reach 6174.Given a four-digit number consisting of numbers 0 to 9, write a program that calculates how many operations it takes to reach 6174.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a single line with 0000.Each dataset is given in the following format.The dataset has one row, and N (1 ≤ N ≤ 9999) indicates a four-digit number.If N < 1000, the upper digits are filled with 0s.The number of datasets does not exceed 10000.Outputs how many operations it took to reach 6174 for each data set in one line.However, if a number in which all digits are the same is given as input, it will output NA.

I'm a pipe fitter.As long as you have a pipe and a joint to connect the pipes, you can connect any pipe.Every day, my master gives me a pipe and a joint, and I connect it and give it to him.However, when there are too many pipes, it is not possible to connect them all in one day.Even at times like this, my boss smiles and hands me my salary.By the way, one day I noticed something strange.I often get paid more when I can't connect all the pipes than when I can connect them all.It was so weird that one day when my boss came over, I secretly looked at the memo that explained how to calculate my salary.Then, surprisingly, the salary was calculated as the sum of the number of pipes x the length of the pipes.However, if something is connected with a joint, it is considered a single pipe.Now you can see why it is cheaper to connect them all.For example, as shown in the figure below, if you connect all three pipes of length 1 and two joints of length 2, you will get one pipe with length 1+2+1+2+1 = 7, so 1 × (7) = 7.However, if you use only one joint, the length 1+2+1 =If you connect two pipes, one with a length of 4 and the other with a length of 1, you will get more pay than if you connected them all.I don't know why my boss decides his salary this way, but now I know how to get more money!Now, write a program that calculates the maximum amount of salary you can get, given the number of pipes.The input consists of multiple data sets.Each data set is given in the following format: The number of pipes n (2 ≤ n ≤ 65000) is given.The second line is the length of the i-th pipe.Consisting of n-1 integers, j i (1 ≤ j i ≤ 1000) indicates the length of the i-th joint.The length of the connected pipes does not exceed 100.For each data set, output the maximum payable amount on a single line.For the data set given as input, the output value must be within the range of 32-bit unsigned integers.

Shinya watched a program on TV called ``The Great Mayan Prophecy! Will the World End in 2012?''In the end, I wasn't sure whether the world would end or not, but I became interested in the Mayan "Long Calendar," which was introduced in the program.The program explained the following:``What day will my 20th birthday be on the Mayan calendar?'' Shinya wanted to represent various days on the Mayan long-term calendar.Now, for Shinya, please create a program to convert between the Western calendar and the Mayan long-term calendar.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a single line.Each dataset is given in the following format.or The dataset consists of one line containing one string.b.ka.t.w.ki is the Maya Long Calendar date and y.m.d is the Western Calendar date.The units given are as follows.The maximum number of days in the Western calendar changes depending on whether it is a major month, a minor month, or a leap year (a leap year is a year that is a multiple of 4 and is either not divisible by 100 or divisible by 400).The Maya Long Calendar dates range from 0.0.0.0.0 to 12.19.19.17.19.However, 0.0.0.0.0 in the Mayan long calendar corresponds to 2012.12.21 in the Western calendar.Also, the date range in the Western calendar is from 2012.12.21 to 10000000.12.31.The number of datasets does not exceed 500.If the input is the Western calendar, the Mayan long-term calendar is output, and if the input is the Mayan long-term calendar, the Western calendar is output in the same format as the input.Even if the input Western calendar is converted into the next cycle of the Mayan long-term calendar, it can be output in b.ka.t.w.ki format.

Taro made a Sugoroku so that everyone could play with it at a children's party.To make the game more interesting, I wrote instructions such as ``Go forward 6 places'' and ``Go back 5 places'' on some of the Sugoroku squares other than ``Friday'' and ``Friday''.Spin the roulette wheel, move forward by the number that appears, and if instructions are written on the squares that stopped, move according to those instructions.However, it does not follow the instructions of the square you follow the instructions to.In roulette, you can win any number between 1 and a certain number with equal probability.Also, if you get a larger number than reaching the "top" or if you follow the instructions you will advance beyond the "top", you will move to the "top".If you follow the instructions and end up going back before the "Furibashi" point, you will want to return to the "Furibashi" point.However, depending on the instructions of the roulette wheel and the squares, there may be cases where you cannot reach the "finish".For example, let's say you made a sugoroku like the one shown in the picture.If you use a roulette wheel that only rolls 1 and 2, you can reach the top if 1 and 2 roll in that order, but if 2 rolls first, you will never reach the top no matter what happens after that.Without realizing it, Taro got carried away and wrote instructions in various squares.Therefore, on Taro's behalf, please create a program that determines whether or not there are cases in which it is impossible to reach the top depending on the instructions on the roulette wheel and the squares.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line gives the maximum number max (2 ≤ max ≤ 250) that the roulette will roll, and the second line gives the number n (2 ≤ n ≤ 250) of squares other than "start" and "end".In the following n lines, a number d i (-n ≤ d i ≤ n) representing the designation of each square is given.When d i is zero, it means that no instruction is written, when it is a positive number, |d i | means to go forward, and when it is negative, |d i | means to go back (here, |x| represents the absolute value of x).All values ​​entered are integers.The number of datasets does not exceed 100.The determination result is output in one line for each data set.Depending on the instructions on the roulette wheel and the squares, if there is a case where the player cannot reach the top, it outputs ``NG'', otherwise it outputs ``OK''.

Yuta is addicted to the popular game ``Beat Panel'' at the local game center.This game consists of a total of 16 panel-shaped buttons arranged in a 4x4 grid as shown in the figure.As shown in the diagram, the buttons are arranged from top left to bottom right in the order of Button 1, Button 2, ..., Button 16.In the game, a beat sound will be played at regular intervals and an end sound will be played at the end.Multiple buttons will light up at the same time as the beat sound plays.There may be cases where not a single one lights up.Players can use the fingers of both hands to press multiple buttons at the same time, just once, from the time a beat sound is heard until the next sound is heard.It is also possible to press nothing at all.The game ends as soon as the end sound sounds.Yuta has learned c ways to press the button, and every time a beat sound is heard, he chooses one of these ways to press the button.If the buttons you press are lit, they will go out and the number of buttons that go out will be added to the player's score.Also, once a button lights up, the light will not go out until the button is pressed.Create a program that takes as input the way the button lights up when the beat sounds n times and how to press the button in c ways that Yuta has learned, and outputs the maximum score that Yuta can earn.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of two zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line consists of two integers separated by a space.n (1 ≤ n ≤ 30) is the number of beat sounds, and c (1 ≤ c ≤ 30) is the number of button presses learned.The following n+c lines specify how the button lights up and how the button is pressed.The input items in a line are separated by a single space.a k,i represents the way button i lights up when the kth beat sound is heard, and b j,i represents whether button i is pressed in the jth way out of c ways.The value of a k,i is 0 means "no light" and 1 means "light".The value of b j,i is 0 means ``do not press'' and 1 means ``push.''The number of datasets does not exceed 20.For each data set, output the maximum score in one line.

The reciprocal of any real number other than 0 is a real number, but the reciprocal of an integer is not necessarily an integer.This is why in C language etc., 3.0/2.0*2.0 = 3.0 but 3/2*2 = 2.However, if we consider integers that have the same remainder when divided by a prime number to be the same, we can make all integers other than 0 have reciprocals.When the integers x and y have equal remainders when divided by p, we write x ≡ y (mod p).When p is a prime number, if we consider such x and y to be the same, then x ≡ n (mod p) for any integer x from 0 to p−1, so we can see that we only need to consider the world consisting of the set { 0, 1, 2, ..., p−1 }.Addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division in this world are as follows.In this way, the four arithmetic operations of addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division all fall within the range of 0 to p−1.In this case, the set { 0,1,...,p−1 } is called a finite field of p.Within this finite field, we can construct arithmetic expressions using addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, numbers from 0 to p−1, and parentheses.We know from the famous theorem a p−1 ≡ 1 (mod p), called Fermat's little theorem, that all elements other than 0 in the finite field of p have reciprocals (where p is a prime number and a and p are relatively prime).All elements x of the finite field of p are coprime to p, so from this theorem, x*x p-2 ≡ 1 (mod p), and x p-2 is the reciprocal of x.Now, given a prime number and an expression, write a calculator program that calculates the expression in a finite field of prime numbers.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of input is indicated by the line 0:, which is simply a 0 followed by a colon.Each dataset is given in the following format.The dataset has one row, p (2 ≤ p ≤ 46000) is a prime number, and exp is an arithmetic expression.exp consists of addition, subtraction, multiplication, division (+, -, *, /, respectively), parentheses, and a number from 0 to p−1.One or more spaces may appear before and after operators, numbers, parentheses, etc.The length of exp cannot exceed 100,000.The number of datasets does not exceed 1000.Output the calculation results for each dataset.The output format is as follows.val is the result of calculating the arithmetic expression exp in the finite field of p.However, if division by 0 is included, NG is output.Also, do not leave spaces before or after addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, parentheses, or numbers that appear in exp.However, leave one space before and after =.Also, leave a space between val and the opening parenthesis, and between mod and p.

Once upon a time, there was a large mansion surrounded by a high wall.The owner of the house loved cats very much, and always prepared delicious meals for the cats that occasionally visited.The hungry cats jumped over the high wall and ran straight to the food placed all over the mansion.One day, the owner found some cats lying inside the mansion.The cats ran around the mansion in search of food, causing them to bump into each other and fall.Considering the safety of the cats, my husband decided to find a better place to put the food.Seen from above, the mansion's walls form a polygon.To make it easier for the cats to find the food, the owner decided to place the food only at the vertices of the polygon within the premises.Also, cats are fickle, so you can't predict which point on the perimeter of the polygon they'll enter the mansion from.Therefore, the owner also decided to arrange the food so that no matter which point the cat enters from, it can go straight to any of the food from that point.This condition can be met by placing rice at all vertices.However, it is difficult to replenish the rice and go around, so the owner wanted to place the rice at as few vertices as possible.Now, at least how many places should the host place the rice?Create a program that takes as input a polygon representing the mansion's wall and finds the minimum number of vertices on which to place rice.However, the cat can only go straight inside the polygon (the sides must be included inside the polygon).The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The number of vertices of the polygon, n (3 ≤ n ≤ 16), is given in the first line.The next n lines give the coordinates of the vertices of the polygon.Each of the n lines consists of two integers separated by a space.x i (-1000 ≤ x i ≤ 1000) is the x-coordinate of the i-th vertex, and y i (-1000 ≤ y i ≤ 1000) is the y-coordinate of the i-th vertex.The vertices of a polygon are given in order such that adjacent vertices are visited counterclockwise.The number of datasets does not exceed 20.For each dataset, output the number of vertices where rice is placed in one line.

Aka Beko, trying to escape from 40 thieves, gets lost in the streets of City A.Aka Beko wants to go to City B, where his new hideout is located, but his map is stolen by thieves.Mr. Cobo, one of the thieves, sympathized with Aka Beko and felt sorry for him.So, he secretly sent a message to Aka Beko, saying, ``I would like to help you go to City B, but I have to make sure that my friends don't find out, so I would like to tell you the directions directly, but I cannot.However, I can answer your questions.''When Mr. Kobo receives a question from Aka Beko asking, ``How about the route ○○?'', he says yes if it is a route that will get you exactly from city A to city B, and no otherwise.Check the directions according to the map below.Each city is connected by one-way streets, each marked with a number 0 or 1.Aka Beko specifies directions using a sequence of numbers.For example, 0100 is a route from city A to city B via cities X, Z, and W.If you choose a route that is not on the map, you will be lost in the desert and will never reach City B.Aka Beko cannot know the name of the city it is in, so it must reach City B exactly when it finishes following the directions.Mr. Kobo secretly hired you to create a program to answer questions from Aka Beko.I would like you to create a program that, when inputting a question from Aka Beko, outputs Yes if the route is exactly the right way to get from City A to City B, otherwise it outputs No.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of input is indicated by a single line with a # (sharp).Each dataset is given in the following format.A sequence p of numbers 0 and 1 is given in one line, indicating the route.p is a string of no more than 100 characters.The number of datasets does not exceed 100.Outputs Yes or No on one line for each dataset.In the fourth dataset, the route passes through City B and reaches City Y, so No is output.In the fifth data set, the route passes through the same city several times, but the route takes you exactly to city B, so it outputs Yes.In the sixth data set, the user wanders into the desert from city X and cannot reach city B, so it outputs No.In the last data set, the route is to pass through City B, then go through City X and City Z to arrive at City B, so we output Yes.

Repeat the following sorting operation for the blocks stacked as shown in Figure a.For an integer k greater than or equal to 1, the number expressed as k×(k+1)/2 (e.g. 1, 3, 6, 10, ...) is called a triangular number.If the total number of blocks is a triangular number, repeating the above sorting is expected to result in a triangle whose height is 1 at the left end and increases one by one toward the right (Figure d shows a case where the total number is 15).Given the initial sequence of blocks, if a triangle of blocks like the one explained above can be created with a predetermined number of operations or less, write a program that outputs the minimum number of operations until the triangle is obtained.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.Each data set has two rows and represents the initial sequence of blocks.N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100) indicates the number of blocks in the bottom row.b i (1 ≤ bi ≤ 10000) indicates the number of blocks stacked at the i-th position from the left.b i and b i+1 are separated by a single space.The total number of blocks is 3 or more.The number of datasets does not exceed 20.For each data set, output the number of sorting operations performed until a triangle is created in one line.However, if a triangle cannot be created or if the number of operations exceeds 10,000, -1 is output.The first data set corresponds to the case shown in the figure.The fourth data set corresponds to the case where a triangle cannot be created because the total number of blocks is not a triangular number.The last dataset corresponds to cases where the total number of blocks is triangular but the number of operations exceeds 10000.

As shown below, consider a 32-bit real number type in which the 7 bits from the right are the decimal part, the following 24 bits are the integer part, and the leftmost 1 bit is the sign part (b 1 , ... , b 32 represent 0 or 1).To convert this format into a decimal representation that is easier for humans to understand, interpret it as follows.(-1) Sign part × (integer part + decimal part) In the above expression, the value of the integer part is b 8 + 2 1 ×b 9 + 2 2 ×b 10 + ... + 2 23 ×b 31.For example, if the integer part is as follows, the value of the integer part is calculated as follows.1 + 2 1 + 2 3 = 1 + 2 + 8 = 11 On the other hand, the value of the decimal part is (0.5) 1 ×b 7 + (0.5) 2 ×b 6 + ... + (0.5) 7 × b 1.For example, if the decimal part is as follows, the decimal part is calculated as follows.0.5 1 + 0.5 3 = 0.5 + 0.125 = 0.625 Furthermore, in the case of the following bit string that includes the sign part, integer part, and decimal part, the decimal number represented by the entire bit string is as follows (however, -1 to the 0th power is 1).(-1) 0 × (1 + 2 + 8 + 0.5 + 0.125) = 1 × (11.625) = 11.625 Write a program that, when Q 32-bit bit strings are input, outputs the decimal notation of the real number represented by those bit strings without any errors.The input consists of one data set.Input data is given in the following format.The number of bit strings Q (1 ≤ Q ≤ 10000) is given in the first row.The input bit string s i is given to the following Q rows.Assume that the input bit string is given in hexadecimal notation (for example, 0111 1111 1000 1000 0000 0000 0000 0000 is given as 7f880000).In other words, each Q row contains 8 hexadecimal numbers, each consisting of 4 bits of binary numbers, arranged without any spaces in between.The table below shows the correspondence between decimal, binary, and hexadecimal numbers.Hexadecimal letters a through f are given in lower case.Outputs the decimal representation of the real number represented by each bit string, one line at a time.However, if all digits below a certain digit in the decimal part are 0, the digits below that digit shall be omitted, but as an exception, if the decimal part is 0, one 0 will be output in the decimal part.

I decided to move and decided to leave this place.I don't have any regrets about this land itself, but there is one thing that bothers me.It's about the plum tree I planted in my garden.Every year, I looked forward to this plum blossom blooming.After I leave here, I will have one less thing to look forward to in spring.I wonder if the scent of my plum blossoms will travel on the wind to the house I'm moving to and make me enjoy spring.In Japan, there are three flowers that symbolize spring.There are three: plum, peach, and cherry blossom.In addition to my plums, the scent of these flowers will reach the place I move to.However, I want to live in a house where only the scent of plum blossoms reaches me the most days.As shown in the diagram, the scent of flowers spreads out in a fan-shape, and its area is determined by the direction and strength of the wind.The sector spreads symmetrically around the wind direction w, and has an area whose radius is the wind strength a.The angle d at which the scent spreads is determined by the type of flower, but the direction and strength of the wind vary depending on the day.However, on the same day, the wind direction and strength are the same everywhere.I have data on the locations of plums, peaches, and cherry blossoms other than mine, the angles at which the scent of each type of flower spreads, and potential houses to move to.Additionally, there is data on wind direction and strength for several days.The locations of plum, peach, and cherry trees other than my plum tree, as well as my house, are shown in coordinates with the origin of my plum tree.Using these data, let's write a program to find the house where only the scent of plum blossoms reaches me the most number of days.I'm a talented programmer!The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of two zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The numbers given on each line are separated by a single space.In the first line, the number of potential houses to move to H (1 ≤ H ≤ 100) and the number of wind records R (1 ≤ R ≤ 100) are given.The next line H gives the location of the house to which the user will move.hx i and hy i are integers between -1000 and 1000 that indicate the x and y coordinates of the i-th house.The next line gives the number U of plum trees other than mine, the number M and S of peach and cherry trees, and the angles du, dm, and ds at which the fragrance of plums, peaches, and cherry blossoms spreads.The range of U, M, and S is 0 to 10.The unit of angle is degrees, which is an integer between 1 and 180.The following row U gives the positions of plum trees other than my plums, the following M row gives the positions of the peach trees, and the following S row gives the positions of the cherry trees.uxi and uyi, mxi and myi, and sxi and syi are integers between -1000 and 1000 that indicate the x and y coordinates of the i-th plum, peach, and cherry tree, respectively.The following R line gives the wind record.w i (0 ≤ w i < 360) and a i (0 < a i ≤ 100) are integers representing the direction and strength of the wind on day i.The direction of the wind is expressed as an angle measured counterclockwise from the positive direction of the x-axis, and is expressed in degrees.The input can be considered to satisfy the following conditions.The number of datasets does not exceed 50.For each data set, output the numbers of all the houses in which only my plum scent arrives the most days on a single line in descending order.Separate house numbers with a space.Do not output blank characters at the end of a line.However, if there is no day when only the scent of my plum blossoms reaches any house, it will be output as NA.

I will give you N cards.Each card has only one natural number written on it.However, the same number is never written.From now on, I will ask you a random natural number.Please give me the largest remainder when you divide the number written on the card you have by the number I told you.For example, let's say you have three cards labeled 9, 3, and 8.If I say "4," find the remainder when you divide 9, 3, and 8 by 4.The remainders are 1, 3, and 0, but the largest remainder is 3, so 3 is the correct answer.Let's get started.picture?Is it a big deal if you have a lot of cards?It can't be helped.Now let's use a computer to find the largest remainder.Write a program to find the largest remainder when the number written on the card is divided by the number asked.Please note that you will be asked multiple questions, not just once, but you will not be asked the same number of questions more than once.The input consists of one data set.Input data is given in the following format.The number of cards N (2 ≤ N ≤ 300000) and the number of questions Q (2 ≤ Q ≤ 100000) are given in the first line separated by one space, and the number c i (1 ≤ c i ≤ 300000) written on the cards is given in the second line separated by one space.The number q i (1 ≤ q i ≤ 300000) given as a question is given in the following Q line.Outputs the maximum remainder on one line for each question.

You have obtained a Japanese dictionary of Izua, the official language of Izua, and the Izua alphabet (list of letters).There are N types of letters in the Izian alphabet.The order of the words that appear in the Izúa national dictionary is in the alphabetical order of the Izúa language.When we look at the dictionary, we find that every word in the dictionary has N letters and contains one of each of N types of letters.After further research, I discovered that the dictionary lists all possible arrangements of N different letters.From this discovery, you can now tell where a word appears in the dictionary.Let's use this knowledge to surprise the people of Izua.First, arrange N letters one by one in alphabetical order.Next, ask them to repeat the operation of changing the order of any two letters R times.You will have to guess where the word will appear in Iza's Japanese dictionary.To prepare for this, please create a program to find the location of a word in a Japanese dictionary.However, the first word in alphabetical order is the 0th word.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line gives the number of letters that make up the alphabet, N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100000), and the second line gives the number of times R (0 ≤ R ≤ 50) to change the letters.The following R lines give the set of character positions to be swapped.s i and t i (1 ≤ s i < t i ≤ N) represent swapping of the s i -th and t i -th characters counting from the beginning for the ith time.s i and t i are separated by a single space.The number of datasets does not exceed 100.For each data set, a number indicating the position in the Japanese dictionary at which the word obtained at the time the replacement is completed is output on one line.However, since the value to be output can be very large, the remainder after dividing by 1,000,000,007 is output instead.Describe the first data set in the input/output example.For the sake of explanation, let us assume that the alphabet consists of three letters, 'A', 'B', and 'C', in order from smallest to largest.Arranging these letters in alphabetical order yields the string "ABC".If we swap the first character 'A' and the second character 'B' in this string, we get the string "BAC".Next, swap the second character 'A' and the third character 'C' in the resulting string to obtain the word "BCA".The dictionary lists six types of words, starting from the beginning: "ABC", "ACB", "BAC", "BCA", "CAB", and "CBA"."BCA" appears third in this list (note that the first "ABC" is number 0), so the answer is 3, the remainder of 3 divided by 1,000,000,007.

There is a game called Packet Monster.It's a game where you catch monsters, raise them, fight them, and trade them, and it's all the rage in Japan.In my class, of course.That's why I'm also raising monsters in this game so that I can play with everyone, but sadly I don't have anyone to play against.I'm not the type of person who is good at actively talking to people.So today, I'm raising my monster level all by myself, waiting for my classmates to invite me.And finally, the moment I've been waiting for has arrived.One of my classmates asked me if I wanted to compete against him.She proudly showed us her monsters.The general rules for packet monster battles are for each player to prepare N monsters and have them fight each other one at a time.Each monster has a number called "level" that indicates the degree of growth, and the monster with the highest level always wins.If the level is the same, it's a draw.Once you have fought a monster, you cannot fight it any more.After N one-on-one battles, the player with a majority of wins is the winner.The moment I saw her monster, I had a bad feeling.All of her monsters look very strong.I don't care if you lose as long as you fight with me.But if you lose too badly, I think people will think, ``It's not fun to fight this guy,'' and they won't want to play anymore.I really don't like that.So, I lied, "Sorry, I don't have N monsters yet."Even if you don't have a chance of winning in a battle N times, you might be able to win with a special rule that allows you to compete with fewer monsters.I said earlier that I don't care if we lose, but I'd still be happy if we won.She accepted this special rule.In other words, each player chooses k monsters from their possessions and lets them fight each other in turn, and the one with the majority (that is, a number greater than k/2) wins.I just found out about her monster level.I don't know which monsters she will choose and in what order she will bring them out.However, if I manage to decide the number of monsters I want to fight against, I may be able to win no matter what choice she makes.My request to all of you.Write a program that, when inputting the number N of monsters and the levels of the monsters that both of us have, outputs the minimum number k of monsters so that I can win no matter what choice she makes.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The number of monsters N (1 ≤ N ≤ 40000) is given in the first line.On the second line, the level a i (1 ≤ a i ≤ 100000) of your monster is given separated by one space.On the third line, the level b i (1 ≤ b i ≤ 100000) of the classmate monster is given separated by one space.The number of datasets does not exceed 50.For each dataset, if k (1 ≤ k < N) exists, output the minimum value in one line.If k does not exist or k is equal to N, output NA.In the first data set, I propose a three-monster battle and choose monsters with levels [9, 9, 9].Since you can defeat any monster other than your opponent's level 10, you can win at least two games.In a match between two animals, there is a possibility of one win and one loss, which is not a majority victory, so 2 is not an answer.For the second data set, I only need to choose the strongest monster.In the third data set, no matter which k monsters I choose and in which order, there is a chance that she will play the exact same monsters in the same order, resulting in a tie.Therefore, it is impossible to say, ``I can win no matter what choice she makes.''

There was a big old mansion in a certain place, and a cat lived there.As shown in the diagram, the mansion has a convex polygonal shape when viewed from above, and is made up of several rooms surrounded by straight walls.A wall is supported by columns at each end.The mansion is so old that every wall has a hole big enough for a cat to fit through.Cats can go between rooms in the mansion or between rooms and outside through holes in the walls.The owner of the mansion whistles to call the cat out of the mansion to feed it.The cat is very smart, and when the owner whistles, it makes the ``best choice'' and sneaks out of the mansion.That is, it exits through the hole the least number of times.Cats are very fickle and you don't know which room they are in.Therefore, the time it took for the cat to come out of the mansion varied from day to day, and the owner was at a loss because he did not know how long he should wait.One day, the owner noticed that the cat was taking a very long time to pass through the hole.This means that the time it takes for the cat to get out is determined by the number of times it passes through the hole.The owner thought that if he knew the maximum number of times the cat would pass through the hole if it made the ``best choice'', he could figure out how long the cat should wait, no matter what room it was in.The owner knows the layout of the mansion, but it is so large that he cannot calculate it himself.Write a program that reads the floor plan of the mansion and, if the cat makes the ``best choice,'' outputs the maximum number of holes it must pass through before getting out.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of two zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The numbers given on each line are separated by a single space.The number of columns C (3 ≤ C ≤ 100) and the number of walls W (3 ≤ W ≤ 300) are given in the first line.The column coordinates are given in the following line C.x i (-1000 ≤ x i ≤ 1000) is an integer that indicates the x-coordinate of the i-th column, and y i (-1000 ≤ y i ≤ 1000) is an integer that indicates the y-coordinate of the i-th column.Each pillar is assigned a number from 1 to C.The wall information is given in the following W line.s i (1 ≤ s i ≤ C), t i (1 ≤ t i ≤ C) indicate the numbers of the columns supporting both ends of the wall.The input can be considered to satisfy the following conditions.The number of datasets does not exceed 50.For each data set, if the cat makes the ``best choice'', the maximum number of holes it will pass through before getting out is output in one line.The second dataset corresponds to the floor plan of the mansion shown in the first diagram.As shown in the figure, when a cat is in a lightly colored room, the number of holes it must pass through before exiting is the ``best choice'': three.If there are cats in other rooms, you can go outside with less than two cats.Therefore, when the cat makes the ``best choice'', the maximum number of holes it will pass through before getting out is 3.

Izua University Elementary School is famous as one of Japan's leading training schools for competitive programmers.The school's faculty have a wide range of algorithmic knowledge and use it every day.As a teacher, you will be in charge of an arts and crafts class this year.In this class, each child is expected to complete one task during the year.The outline of the class is as follows.Students complete tasks using several of the K types of parts.The outline of the task creation is as follows.Any parts used for the assignment other than those included in the bag must be purchased separately.The conditions for purchasing parts are as follows.Under these conditions, you would like to keep the cost of classes as low as possible.Therefore, I decided to create a program that calculates the minimum total cost of purchasing parts for all children.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of three zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The numbers given on each line are separated by a single space.D (1 ≤ D ≤ 8) indicates the number of classes, K (1 ≤ K ≤ 8) indicates the number of types of parts, and L (1 ≤ L ≤ 8) indicates the number of parts that can be purchased in a day.c d,k (1 ≤ c d,k ≤ 100) indicates the price of part k on day d.M (1 ≤ M ≤ 200) is the number of task types, N (1 ≤ N ≤ M) is the number of students, and P (1 ≤ P ≤ 200) is the number of bags.r m,k (0 ≤ r m,k ≤ 2) indicates the number of parts k required for task m, and t p,k (0 ≤ t p,k ≤ 2) indicates the number of parts k in bag p.Tasks that do not require any parts or empty bags shall not be given.The number of datasets does not exceed 20.For each data set, output the minimum value of the total parts purchase costs for all students in one line.If N types of works cannot be completed, -1 is output.

A country has decided to introduce automatic ticket gates to its railway network.One of the difficult implementation issues is determining whether a given ticket allows travel between specified stations.Each ticket includes the boarding station and the alighting station.This ticket not only allows you to board the train at the boarding station and get off at the station where you get off, but also allows you to board the train or get off the train midway.This railway network has S stations, of which R stations are adjacent to each other and allow train travel in both directions without passing through other stations.There is only one track connecting adjacent stations.The distance between adjacent stations is the distance measured along this track.There may be multiple routes from one station to another depending on the shape of the railway network, but the route with the shortest distance is called the shortest route.If there is more than one such route, any of them is accepted as the shortest route.You can travel from station c to station d using a ticket from boarding station a and getting off at station b if there exists a route p that satisfies all of the following conditions.You will be given route map and ticket information.Next, given several pairs of starting points and ending points, write a program that determines whether the ticket allows you to travel from the starting point to the ending point.The input consists of one data set.Input data is given in the following format.The numbers given on each line are separated by a single space.The first line consists of two integers.S (2 ≤ S ≤ 100000) is the number of stations appearing on the railway map, and R (1 ≤ R ≤ 200000) is the number of adjacent station pairs.The following R line provides information about the tracks that directly connect adjacent stations.u i and v i (1 ≤ u i , v i ≤ S) indicate the station numbers at both ends of the i-th track.w i (1 ≤ w i ≤ 1000) is an integer representing the distance between these stations.However, each station is assigned a number from 1 to S without duplication, so u i ≠ v i.The next line consists of three integers.The first two integers represent the section of the ticket, where a is the boarding station and b is the disembarking station (1 ≤ a, b ≤ S).The third integer Q (1 ≤ Q ≤ 40000) indicates the number of questions.A question is given on the following Q line.c i and d i (1 ≤ c i , d i ≤ S) indicate the boarding station and alighting station of the i-th question.However, a ≠ b and c i ≠ d i.For each question, if you can move with the given ticket, print Yes on one line, otherwise print No.

Dear players, welcome to PC Koshien.イベントに参加するには体調管理が大切です。気温が大きく変動する季節の変わり目には体に負担がかかり、風邪をひきやすいと言われています。一番気を付けなければいけない日は、最高気温と最低気温の差が最も大きい日です。１日の最高気温と最低気温が７日分与えられたとき、それぞれの日について最高気温から最低気温を引いた値を出力するプログラムを作成してください。Input data is given in the following format.入力は7行からなり、i行目には i 日目の最高気温 a i (-40 ≤ a i ≤ 40)と最低気温 b i (-40 ≤ b i ≤ 40)を表す整数が与えられる。すべての日において、最高気温 a i は必ず最低気温 b i 以上となっている。Outputs the temperature difference for 7 days in 7 lines.

今日は、アイヅ・エンターテインメント社イチオシのアイドルグループ「アカベコ＆コボウシ」のチケット発売日です。チケットには以下の４種類があります。Ｓ席 6000円 Ａ席 4000円 Ｂ席 3000円 Ｃ席 2000円 販売責任者のあなたは、ドキドキしながら発売開始を待っています。The sale is finally starting.Sales are going great!発売開始からしばらく経ったところで、それまでの注文をまとめた表を受け取りました。表の各行には、それまでに売れたチケットの種類と枚数が書いてあります。ただし、チケットの種類がＳ，Ａ，Ｂ，Ｃの順に現れるとは限りません。この表の行ごとの売上金額を求めるプログラムを作成してください。Input data is given in the following format.The input consists of 4 lines.i行目には、チケットの種類を表す整数 t i (1 ≤ t i ≤ 4)と枚数を表す整数 n i (0 ≤ n i ≤ 10000)が与えられる。チケットの種類を表す整数1, 2, 3, 4は、それぞれＳ席、Ａ席、Ｂ席、Ｃ席を表す。t 1 , t 2 , t 3 , t 4 の値として1から4までの数は必ず１度だけ現れるが、1, 2, 3, 4の順で与えられるとは限らない。Output the sales amount for each line.

アイヅ温泉には、浴場とプールがあり、浴場を利用するには入浴券を、プールを利用するにはプール券をそれぞれ買う必要があります。これらの券の値段は、日によって変わるかもしれません。また、アイヅ温泉には以下のようなルールがあります。温泉好きの貞吉さんとその友人たちは、アイヅ温泉に毎日のように通っています。彼らは皆気まぐれで、日によって使う枚数は異なります。アイヅ温泉には割引ルールがあるため、グループで協力して上手に買えば、合計料金を安く済ますことができるかもしれません。入浴券とプール券の料金、使用する入浴券の枚数とプール券の枚数が入力として与えられたとき、最も安い合計料金を出力するプログラムを作成してください。ただし、使用する枚数より多く券を買うことで合計料金が安くなるなら、買った券すべてを使わなくともよいものとします。Input is given in the following format.１行目の N (1 ≤ N ≤ 365)は料金を計算したい日数である。続く N 行に、 i 日目の入浴券の料金 x i (100 ≤ x i ≤ 1000)、プール券の料金 y i (100 ≤ y i ≤ 1000)、使用する入浴券の枚数 b i (0 ≤ b i ≤ 6)、使用するプール券の枚数 p i (0 ≤ p i ≤ 6)が与えられる。入浴券とプール券共に料金は50円刻みである。それぞれの日について最も安い合計料金を１行に出力する。

ジョウ君とヤエさんは仲の良いカップルです。ジョウ君はカプセル玩具自販機(ガチャポン)の景品を集めており、二人で出かけたときも、ガチャポンを見つけると何度かやってみるほどの熱の入りようです。ヤエさんは楽しそうなジョウ君をそばで見ているだけでしたが、ジョウ君の今度の誕生日プレゼントにガチャポンの景品をひとつあげることにしました。ヤエさんはガチャポン自体にはあまり興味がわきませんでしたが、できればジョウ君とおそろいの景品が欲しいと思っています。ヤエさんがやってみようと思うガチャポンは、１回のチャレンジで景品がひとつ出ます。品切れのものも含め景品が何種類あるのかと、それぞれの景品がいくつ残っているのかはわかります。しかし、1回のチャレンジでどの景品が出るかはわかりません。そこで、景品が出る順番にかかわらず、ヤエさんが同じ景品を必ず２つ得るために最低限必要なチャレンジの回数を出力するプログラムを作成してください。The input consists of multiple datasets.入力の終わりはゼロ1つの行で示される。Each dataset is given in the following format.各データセットは2行であり、1行目に景品が何種類あるかを表す整数 N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10000)が与えられる。続く1行に各景品がいくつ残っているのかを表す整数 k i (0 ≤ k i ≤ 10000)が与えられる。The number of datasets does not exceed 100.データセットごとに、同じ景品を必ず２つ得るために最低限必要なチャレンジの回数を出力する。ただし、不可能な場合はNAと出力する。１つ目のデータセットでは、運良く１種類目か２種類目の景品が連続で出て２回で済む可能性はあるが、同じ種類の景品を必ず２つ得るためには３回チャレンジする必要がある。２つ目のデータセットでは、２つ以上残っている景品がもともと無いので不可能である。３つ目のデータセットでは、景品は１種類だけなので２回のチャレンジでその景品が必ず２つ得られる。

百人一首の札を使った遊戯の１つに、「坊主めくり」というものがあります。絵札だけを使う簡単な遊戯なので広く楽しまれています。きまりには様々な派生型がありますが、ここで考える坊主めくりはN人の参加者で、以下のようなルールで行います。参加者数と札山に積まれた札の順番が与えられたとき、遊戯が終了した時点で各参加者が持っている札数を昇順で並べたものと、場に残っている札数を出力するプログラムを作成してください。The input consists of multiple datasets.入力の終わりはゼロ一つの行で示される。Each dataset is given in the following format.各データセットは2行であり、1行目に参加者数を表す整数 N (2 ≤ N ≤ 10)が与えられる。続く1行に札山に積まれた札の並びを表す文字列が与えられる。文字 c i は i 番目に引かれる札を表しており、Mが男、Sが坊主、Lが姫の札を表す。There are no spaces between each character.The number of datasets does not exceed 100.データセットごとに、遊戯が終了した時点での札数の並びを１行に出力する。札数の並びとして、各参加者が持っている札の枚数を昇順に並べだものの後に、場に残っている札の枚数を出力する。Separate each number with a space.Do not print spaces at the end of a line.

プログラマー養成校であるアカベ高校では、チーム戦の競技プログラマーの役割を以下の３タイプに分けています。この高校では以下のいずれかの陣形で３人一組のチームを構成しています。競技プログラミング部のコーチであるあなたは、これらの部員をうまく組み合わせ、できるだけ多くのチームを結成できるよう、毎年気を配っています。そこで、コーダーの人数、アルゴリズマーの人数、ナビゲーターの人数が入力として与えられたとき、最大何チーム作れるかを出力するプログラムを作成してください。入力は1つのデータセットからなる。Input data is given in the following format.1行目の Q (0 ≤ Q ≤ 100)はチーム数を求めたい年数である。続く Q 行に各年の役割別の人数が与えられる。各行にはコーダーの人数 c i (0 ≤ c i ≤ 1000)、アルゴリズマーの人数 a i (0 ≤ a i ≤ 1000)、ナビゲーターの人数 n i (0 ≤ n i ≤ 1000)が与えられる。年ごとに、作成できる最大のチーム数を1行に出力する。

白虎大学では毎年プログラミングコンテストが開催されています。チームの総数をNとしたとき、チームには1からNのチームIDがそれぞれ割り当てられています。コンテストは全てのチームの得点が0の状態から開始し、L秒間連続して行われます。今年のコンテストは、テレビで中継されることになりました。コンテストの間テレビに映るのは、その時点で最も得点の高いチームだけです。ただし、該当するチームが複数いるときは、その中でチームIDの一番小さいチームが映ります。映すべきチームが変わると、瞬時にカメラが切り替わります。あなたはコンテストのログを入手しました。ログには、あるチームの得点が変化した時のレコードがすべて含まれています。各レコードは「チーム d がコンテスト開始 t 秒後の時点で x 点獲得した」という形式で与えられています。なお、減点される場合もあるので、現在の得点が0より小さくなることもあります。コンテストのログを入力とし、コンテスト開始から終了までの間にテレビに映った時間が最も長いチームのIDを報告するプログラムを作成してください。入力は1つのデータセットからなる。Input is given in the following format.The first line consists of three integers.N (2 ≤ N ≤ 100000)はチーム数、 R (0 ≤ R ≤ 1000000)はログに含まれるレコードの数である。L (1 ≤ L ≤ 1000000)はコンテストの時間（長さ）を表す。続く R 行に各レコードの情報が与えられる。各レコードはチーム d i (1 ≤ d i ≤ N )がコンテスト開始 t i (0 < t i < L )秒後の時点で x i (-1000 ≤ x i ≤ 1000)点獲得あるいは減点されたことを示す。ただし、 t i , x i は整数であり、 t i-1 ≤ t i である。コンテスト開始から終了までの間にテレビに映った時間が最も長いチームのIDを１行に出力する。ただし、最も長いチームが複数あるときは、その中でチームIDの一番小さいチームを出力する。

プログラマー養成校のアカベ高校には、生徒自身で運営するユニークな勉強会があります。プログラマーは新しい技術を常に取り入れることが大切なので、この勉強会を通して自学自習の習慣を身につけることがこの活動のねらいです。生徒は全部で N 人おり、それぞれが入学時のプログラミングコンテストの結果で得られたスコアを持っています。勉強会では N 人の生徒のうち何人かがリーダーになり、各リーダーがそれぞれのグループを運営するとともに、自らの運営するグループに参加します。リーダー以外の生徒は、自分のスコアよりも低いスコアのリーダーが運営するグループには参加できません。また、0以上のある値 r を１つ決め、グループに参加する生徒とリーダーのスコアの差が r 以内となるようにしています。つまり、グループのリーダーのスコアが s のとき、自分のスコアが s を超えているか、あるいは s - r 未満ならば、そのグループには参加できません。あなたは勉強会の実行委員長であり、運営準備のためにシミュレーションを行うことにしました。シミュレーションでは、リーダーが誰もいない状態から始め、以下の操作を何回か繰り返します。このようなシミュレーションを行うプログラムを作成してください。入力は1つのデータセットからなる。Input is given in the following format.1行目に生徒の数 N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000000)、処理要求の数 Q (0 ≤ Q ≤ 1000)が与えられる。続く N 行に i 番目の生徒のスコアを示す整数 s i (0 ≤ s i ≤ 1,000,000,000)が与えられる。生徒は1,2,..., N で番号付けされているものとする。続く Q 行に処理要求 QUERY i が与えられる。Processing requests are given in chronological order.処理要求はADD, REMOVE, CHECKの3種類あり、各 QUERY i は以下のいずれかの形式で与えられる。または または ADD a は番号 a (1 ≤ a ≤ N )の生徒をリーダーに加えることを表す。REMOVE a は番号 a (1 ≤ a ≤ N )の生徒をリーダーから外すことを表す。CHECK x represents an output request.どのグループにも参加できない生徒の数の上限 x (0 ≤ x ≤ N )が与えられる。なお、入力は以下の条件を満たすものとする。時系列順に各出力要求の時点で、どのグループにも参加できない生徒がx人以下になるような最小の r を1行に出力する。ただし、どのような r を選んでも x 人以下にすることが不可能であればNAと出力する。

「ハッピーエンド問題」と呼ばれる数学の未解決問題に関連したプログラムを書いてみましょう。平面上に与えられた N 個の点から、ちょうど k 個の点を結んでできる凸多角形のうち、最も面積の小さいものを見つけるプログラムを作成してください。ただし、N個の点の座標を与えられた後、質問として凸多角形の角の個数 k がいくつか与えられます。（補足：ハッピーエンド問題について） 平面上にどの３点も同じ直線上に乗らないように N 個の点を置きます。そのとき、どのように点を置いても、k個の点をうまく選ぶとk個の角をもつ凸多角形が必ず作れると予想されています。今のところ、三角形ならば N =3、四角形ならば N =5、五角形ならば N =9、六角形ならば N =17であればよいことが、２００６年までに証明されています。また、三角形以上のすべてのk角形に対して、 N =1+2 k -2 という予想がありますが、いまだ証明されていません。これは１００年にわたり研究が進められている難問です。この問題には、「ハッピーエンド問題」という素敵な名前がつけられています。ある男女がこの問題を研究しているうちに仲良くなって、ついに結婚したことにちなんで、友人の数学者が名付けたそうです。It's romantic, isn't it?入力は1つのデータセットからなる。Input data is given in the following format.１行目に平面上の点の個数 N (3 ≤ N ≤ 40)が与えられる。続く N 行に各点の座標が与えられる。各点には1から N までの番号が、入力される順番に付けられている。x i と y i (-10000 ≤ x i , y i ≤ 10000)は i 番目の点のそれぞれ x 座標と y 座標を表す整数である。x 軸の正方向は右向き, y 軸の正方向は上向きに取るものとする。続く１行に質問の数 Q (1 ≤ Q ≤ N )が与えられる。A question is given on the following Q line.k i (3 ≤ k i ≤ N )は i 番目の質問である凸多角形の角の個数を表す。なお、入力は以下の条件を満たすものとする。質問ごとに、面積が最小の凸多角形の全頂点を1行に出力する。凸多角形の頂点で最も下にあるものの中で最も左にある頂点から順に、反時計周りで頂点の番号を出力する。頂点の番号の間は空白1つで区切る。Do not output blank characters at the end of a line.ただし、凸多角形が作れない場合はNAと出力する。

ジョウ君とヤエさんは昼休みにテニスをします。However, since time is limited during the lunch break, the following three rules will be followed regarding scoring to ensure that the time is short.以下の図は、ジョウ君とヤエさんの試合で起こり得るすべての状況を表しています。丸の中の左の数がジョウ君の得点、右がヤエさんの得点です。Ａと書いた矢印はジョウ君が１点とったことを、Ｂと書いた矢印はヤエさんが１点とったことを表します。When Joe and Yae's points are given, write a program that enumerates all the paths that can be taken in the above diagram from the start of the match to the point where the points are scored.The input consists of one data set.Input data is given in the following format.j (0 ≤ j ≤ 6) がジョウ君の得点、 y (0 ≤ y ≤ 6) がヤエさんの得点である。ただし、 j と y がともに 0 であることはない。また、 j が 6 のときは y は 4、 y が 6 のときは j は 4 である。上の図で、試合開始（0-0と書かれた丸）から与えられた得点が書かれた丸までのすべての経路を出力する。The route is represented by a row of letters (A, B) attached to the arrow in the diagram, and arranged in lexicographical order (the order in which words are listed in an English-Japanese dictionary).Output one route on one line.Do not output spaces before or after the route.

If you try to represent a large number, the number of characters will increase, and you will not be able to understand the scale, so it is quite troublesome.大きな数をわかりやすく表すために、人々は数の単位を使ってきました。江戸時代に書かれた「塵劫記」という本の中では、数の単位が次のように書かれています。For example, a very large number such as 2 to the 100th power can be expressed as 126 jo 7650 (jo) 6002 哓 2822 kyoku 9401 trillion 496 732 million 5376.Now, given positive integers m and n, write a program that expresses m to the n power using Jinkoki's units as shown above.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of two zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.m (2 ≤ m ≤ 20) が基数、 n (1 ≤ n < 240) が指数を表す。However, m n is less than 10 72.The number of datasets does not exceed 100.データセットごとに、 m n を塵劫記の単位で表した文字列を1行に出力する。ただし、各単位は以下の表記で出力する。m n を表す文字列は、1 から 9999 までの数と上の表に現れる単位を表す文字列からなる。文字列には、余分な 0 や単位を含めない。

You are trying to cut down a huge tree in front of you using N types of tools.Initially, the tree's durability is D and your experience points are 0, but if you hit the tree once with tool i, the tree's durability decreases by a i and you gain e i experience points.However, in order to use tool i, you must have experience points equal to or higher than r i.If the tree's durability becomes 0 or less, the tree will fall.Given information about the tree's durability and the tool, write a program that finds the minimum number of times a tree must be struck to cut it down.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of two zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.In the first line, an integer D (1 ≤ D ≤ 100) representing the durability of the tree and the number of types of tools N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100) are given.Information about tools 1 to N is given in the following N lines.a i (0 ≤ a i ≤ 100) and e i (0 ≤ e i ≤ 100) represent the tree's durability that decreases by using tool i once and the experience points that you can gain, respectively.r i (0 ≤ r i ≤ 100) represents the experience required to use the tool.a i , e i , r i are all integers.The number of datasets does not exceed 50.Outputs the minimum number of strikes required to cut down a tree in one line.However, if it is impossible to cut down the tree, NA is output.

Mugen Railway's Mugen Line has an infinite number of stations.The stations are numbered as …, -3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, …, and each station is adjacent to the other in the same order as the integers on the number line.You are currently boarding a train at a station with a certain number and are about to head to a station with a higher number.There are an infinite variety of high-speed trains running on the Mugen Line.They are called by number, such as 0 class rapid, 1 class rapid, 2 class rapid, 3 class rapid, etc.N-class rapid trains stop at stations with numbers that are multiples of 2 n.For example, 1st class rapid trains stop at stations …, -4, -2, 0, 2, 4, …, and 3rd class rapid trains stop at stations …, -24, -16, -8, 0, 8, 16, 24, ….0 class rapid trains stop at all stations, so Mugen Railway calls them ``rapid trains'' even though they actually stop at every station.All types of rapid trains take one unit of time to travel from one stop to the next.Also, assume that the time required to transfer between rapid trains can be ignored.Given a pick-up station s and a drop-off station d, write a program to find the minimum time required to travel from s to d.However, while moving from s to d, moving from a higher numbered station to a lower numbered station is not allowed.The input consists of one data set.Input data is given in the following format.The first line gives N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100), which represents the number of movements.In the following N lines, the boarding station number s i and the alighting station number d i (-1,000,000,000 ≤ s i < d i ≤ 1,000,000,000) are given.Outputs the minimum travel time required for each given boarding and alighting station in one line.

Dr. Iinuma discovered a mysterious microorganism in the fumarole of Mt. Bandai.When one male and one female microorganism unite, they release electrical energy.Studying this microorganism could save us from future energy crises.As we continued our observations, we discovered that microorganisms generate electrical energy only when they coalesce, and that microorganisms that have coalesced do not coalesce any further.Further observation revealed that the electrical energy released by the merger was determined by the amount of unknown particles (which he named B particles after Mt. Bandai) that the microorganisms had in their bodies.If the amounts of B particles in the bodies of the merging male and female are bm and bw, respectively, the electrical energy released by merging can be calculated using the formula | bm - bw | × (| bm - bw | - 30) 2.This discovery made it possible to calculate the maximum electrical energy that can be obtained from a population of microorganisms.Now, write a program that calculates the maximum electrical energy that can be obtained from a population of microorganisms, given the number of males and females in the population and the amount of B particles that each individual has.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of two zeros.Input data is given in the following format.M and W (1 ≤ M, W ≤ 12) in the first row are the numbers of male and female microorganisms, respectively.The second line gives the amount of B particles bm i (0 ≤ bm i ≤ 50) that the i-th male has in his body.The third line gives the amount of B particles bw i (0 ≤ bw i ≤ 50) that the i-th female has in her body.The number of datasets does not exceed 20.For each data set, output the maximum electrical energy obtained from a population of microorganisms in one line.

A super large Amidakuji was discovered in an ancient ruin.Scholars named this VLA (Very Long Amidakuji).The analysis revealed that the VLA consists of a combination of several parts with N vertical bars, often containing repeats.For example, the VLA shown in the figure consists of four parts using two types of parts A and B.Number the top of this VLA sequentially from the left as 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, trace the VLA from each number (see the supplement at the end of the question for how to trace the Amidakuji), and assign that number to the vertical bar where you reached the bottom.After tracing all the numbers, read the numbers at the bottom from left to right: 4, 2, 5, 1, 3.In this way, the sequence numbered 1,2,3,4,5 is converted to the sequence 4,2,5,1,3.They want to know how the sequence of numbers from 1 to N is transformed by VLA, but it seems difficult to trace it manually.Therefore, we asked talented programmers who were contestants at PC Koshien to perform a VLA simulation.VLAs are so long that they cannot convey information to programmers in their raw form.Therefore, scholars decided to represent the type of component with a single capital letter and to express the VLA as a formula.The parts are connected sequentially from the left using the + operator so that vertical bars with the same positional relationship match.For example, a sequence of parts connected in the order A, B, C, D from the top is expressed as A+B+C+D.m repetitions of the expression seq representing a sequence of parts can be written as m ( seq ).For example, two repetitions of A+B can be expressed as 2(A+B), which is the same as A+B+A+B.Also, if seq is one component a, the parentheses surrounding a can be omitted.The VLA that connects 4(A+B) and 3(X+Y) in this order can be expressed as 4(A+B)+3(X+Y).Also, parentheses can be nested like 2(4(A+B)+3(X+Y))+10C.Now, write a program that takes as input the number of vertical bars N, information about each part, and multiple VLA formulas, and outputs how a string of numbers from 1 to N is converted by the VLA represented by each formula.(Note: Regarding how to follow the Amidakuji) Start from the top of the vertical bar where the Amidakuji is located and proceed from top to bottom.However, at the point where there is a horizontal bar, it moves to another vertical bar connected by that horizontal bar.Repeat this until you reach the bottom of the vertical bar.The input consists of one data set.Input data is given in the following format.In the first line, an integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 12) representing the number of vertical bars and the number of part types K (1 ≤ K ≤ 26) are given.Information about K types of parts is given from the second line onwards.Each part is given in the following format.The number of equations E (1 ≤ E ≤ 100) is given in the next line.In the following E line, a string expression i (1 to 1000 characters) representing the VLA expression is given.The input satisfies the following conditions.For each expression, output the conversion results of columns 1 to N by VLA in one row.Separate each number with a space.

In 2XXX, humans built a wall and took refuge inside it to prevent the invasion of natural enemies that suddenly appeared.その結果、人類の活動領域はその壁で囲まれた範囲に限定されてしまった。This area is a W × H rectangle when viewed from above.Inside the area, there are several walls parallel to the x-axis or y-axis.An example of an activity area is shown in the figure below.人類は活動領域内を自由に移動することができるが、壁を越えるためには一定量の資源を消費しなければならない。ただし、壁を越えることはできるが（図中の (1)）、壁の交点を越えること (2)、壁や活動領域の境界の上を移動すること (3)、活動領域外に出ること (4) はできない。領域内部の壁の情報といくつかの始点と終点の組を入力し、始点から終点へ移動するために越えなければならない壁の数の最小値を計算するプログラムを作成しなさい。However, the wall is a line with no width.The input consists of one data set.Input data is given in the following format.1行目に領域の横の長さと縦の長さを表す整数 W , H (2 ≤ W,H ≤ 1,000,000,000) と壁の数 M (0 ≤ M ≤ 100) が与えられる。The following M lines give information about the line segment representing the wall.各行に与えられる４つの整数 px i , py i , qx i , qy i (0 ≤ px i , qx i ≤ W , 0 ≤ py i , qy i ≤ H ) はそれぞれ i 番目の線分の端点の x 座標、 y 座標、もうひとつの端点の x 座標、 y 座標を表す。The number of questions Q (1 ≤ Q ≤ 100) is given in the next line.Each question is given in the following Q lines.各質問に含まれる４つの整数 sx i , sy i , gx i , gy i (0 < sx i , gx i < W , 0 < sy i , gy i < H ) はそれぞれ始点の x 座標、 y 座標、終点の x 座標、 y 座標を表す。The input satisfies the following conditions.For each question, output the minimum number of times to cross the wall.

あなたはイズア地区におけるアルゴリズム検定試験を運営しており、運営費を最小にしたいと考えています。Operating costs are the sum of venue usage fees, bus usage fees, and transportation subsidies.This year, N candidates have registered, and we have secured M venues that can accommodate up to c i people each.You must assign each candidate to a venue.For venues allocated to more than one person, a venue fee of ¥100 is required regardless of the number of people.イズア地区は図に示すように東西方向と南北方向に 1 km 間隔で道が走っており、受験者の家と会場は交差点上にあると考えます。Each candidate can walk along the road from their home to the venue.また、この検定試験では、1 人以上受験者を受け入れる各会場につき 1 台のシャトルバスが運行されるので、受験者はバスを利用することもできます。However, you can only ride the bus that goes to the venue where you will take the test.各シャトルバスは、会場からの東西方向の距離と南北方向の距離の和が D 以内のすべての交差点に停車します（図は D = 3 の場合）。The bus fare is proportional to D, and if D increases by 1 km, it increases by B yen.つまり、シャトルバスを運行するには 1 会場あたり D × B 円の費用を支払う必要があります。Note that the same values ​​for D and B will be used at all venues.移動補助金とは、各受験者に必要な最低限の徒歩での移動に対して運営者が受験者に支払う費用で、1 km について 1 円を払う必要があります。あなたは受験者の家と会場の位置、会場の受け入れ可能人数と使用料、 D が 1 km 増えたときに加算される料金 B の情報を入力データとして持っており、各受験者への会場割り当てと D を決定することができます（ただし、 D は 0 以上の整数)。At this time, find the minimum operating cost.The input consists of multiple datasets.The end of the input is indicated by a line of three zeros.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line consists of three integers.N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100) is the number of examinees, and M (1 ≤ M ≤ 5) is the number of venues.B (0 ≤ B ≤ 1000) is the charge added when D increases by 1 km when operating a shuttle bus.The next N lines give the coordinates of each examinee's home.ax i , ay i (-1000 ≤ ax i , ay i ≤ 1000) indicate the x and y coordinates of examinee i's house, respectively.The following M row provides information about each venue.bx i , by i (-1000 ≤ bx i , by i ≤ 1000) indicate the x and y coordinates of venue i, respectively.c i (1 ≤ c i ≤ 100) indicates the number of people that venue i can accommodate, and f i (0 ≤ f i ≤ 100000) indicates the usage fee for venue i.However, the sum of c 1 to c M is greater than or equal to N.The input can be considered to satisfy the following conditions.The number of datasets does not exceed 10.Outputs the minimum test operating cost for each dataset on one line.

The phantom thief Aka Beko boldly stole the silver shachihoko from Tsurugajo.As a police inspector, you have received information that Aka Beko will give Shachihoko to his friend Mr. Kobou.However, Mr. Kobou won't go to Aka Beko's hideout, so it seems he will hand it over outside the hideout.You decide to ambush and capture Aka Beko.This town is an old castle town, so the roads are complicated and all roads are one-way.アカ・ベコがアジトから受け渡し場所までどの経路を通るのかはわからないが、人員不足のため、待ち伏せ場所は１つに設定しなければならない。You have selected several locations, including your hideout, researched the roads that connect them, and created a map.It seems that the delivery will take place somewhere on the map.Once the delivery location is known, select an ambush location as shown below.アジトと待ち伏せ場所の候補からなる地点をつなぐ道の情報のあとに、質問として受け渡し場所が複数入力されたとき、それぞれの受け渡し場所について待ち伏せ場所を出力するプログラムを作成してください。The input consists of one data set.Input data is given in the following format.The first line consists of two integers.N (3 ≤ N ≤ 100000) is the number of points, and M ( N -1 ≤ M ≤ 300000) is the number of all roads drawn on the map.The following M rows are given a path that directly connects adjacent points.s i and t i (1 ≤ s i ≠ t i ≤ N ) represent the numbers of the starting and ending points of the i-th road, respectively.However, the hideout number is 1, and all points can be reached from the hideout.The number of questions Q (1 ≤ Q < N) is given in the next line.A question is given on the following Q line.As the i-th question, the delivery location number r i (2 ≤ r i ≤ N ) is given.For each question, print the ambush location number on one line.

Dear players, welcome to PC Koshien.The final round of PC Koshien will be held at the University of Aizu, and one team will be assigned to one desk within the venue.In PC Koshien, each team has two people, so two chairs are required for each team.大学では、他にも様々なイベントの会場設営で机と椅子を準備する機会がありますが、必要な机と椅子の数も様々です。そこで、あるイベントに対して準備する机の数と、机１つあたりに必要な椅子の数が与えられたとき、必要な椅子の総数を計算するプログラムを作成してください。Input is given in the following format.入力は１行であり、必要な机の数 d (1 ≤ d ≤ 100) と机１つあたりに必要な椅子の数 c (1 ≤ c ≤ 10) を表す整数が与えられる。イベントにおいて必要な椅子の総数を１行に出力する。

Ever since the consumption tax increased to 8% in April, isn't your wallet full of coins?If you want to carry around the same amount of money, you'll want to use fewer coins.If the total amount of coins is 1,000 yen or more, you can exchange the coins for bills and eliminate the metabolic state of your wallet.お財布の中の硬貨の枚数が種類ごとに与えられたとき、硬貨をお札に両替できるかどうかを判定するプログラムを作成してください。Input is given in the following format.The input consists of one line.c1 、 c5 、 c10 、 c50 、 c100 、 c500 (0 ≤ c1, c5, c10, c50, c100, c500 ≤ 50) は、それぞれ、1円、5円、10円、50円、100円、500円硬貨の枚数を表す。If coins can be exchanged for bills, print 1, otherwise print 0 on one line.

There is a game in which P people take turns picking one stone from a set of K stones.If there are still stones left when the P person picks up a stone, the first person picks up one stone at a time.In this game, the person who takes the last stone wins.Given K and P, write a program to determine how many people will win.Input is given in the following format.The first line gives the number of times N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100) to play the game.In the following N lines, the number of stones K i (2 ≤ K i ≤ 1000) in the i-th game and the number of people participating in the game P i (2 ≤ P i ≤ 1000) are given.For each game, output how many people win on one line.

Kenjiro, a bus enthusiast, often uses buses in A city.One day, I suddenly had the idea to take pictures of all the buses that depart from the bus stop in front of Kenjiro's house.Two bus routes run through this bus stop: one bound for Mt. Iimori and one bound for Tsuruga Castle.I got timetables for each route, but it will be easier to take photos at bus stops if you compile them into one timetable.To help Kenjiro, please create a program that combines the timetables of two routes into one timetable in order of earliest departure time, starting at 0:00.Input is given in the following format.In the first line, an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100) representing the number of buses bound for Mt. Iimori is given, followed by the departure times of the buses bound for Mt. Iimori in descending order.The departure time consists of integers h i (0 ≤ h i ≤ 23) and m i (0 ≤ m i ≤ 59), and represents that the i-th bus departs at h i hour m i minute.On the second line, an integer M (1 ≤ M ≤ 100) representing the number of buses bound for Tsuruga Castle is given, followed by the departure times of the buses bound for Tsuruga Castle in order of earliest arrival.The departure time consists of integers k i (0 ≤ k i ≤ 23) and g i (0 ≤ g i ≤ 59), and represents that the i-th bus departs at k i hour g i minute.It is assumed that there are no buses departing on the same route at the same time.The timetables for two routes are compiled into one in order of earliest departure time based on 0:00, and are output in one line.However, if multiple buses depart at the same time, only one will be output.Separate the hour and minute of the departure time with :.If the minute is one digit, add one zero to the left and output as two digits.Separate the times with a space.

In the town where I live, there is a circular railway line consisting of N stations as shown in the diagram.Each station on this line is assigned a number from 0 to N -1 in order, and you can travel to the next station for 100 yen.Movement is possible in either direction.I would like to shop at several stations on this line.So I decided to create a program to calculate travel costs.Write a program that starts from a certain station and finds the minimum cost (in yen) of visiting all the stations where you want to shop.However, you can visit the same station as many times as you like, and you can visit the stations in any order.Input is given in the following format.The first line gives the number of stations N (2 ≤ N ≤ 100000), the number of shopping stations M (1 ≤ M ≤ 10000 and M < N ), and the departure station number p (0 ≤ p ≤ N -1).The next M row is given the station number d i (0 ≤ d i ≤ N -1) at which the customer will shop.However, all d i are different, and p is also different from any d i .Print the minimum cost on one line.

Let's solve the floppy cube by programming.A floppy cube is a three-dimensional puzzle made up of nine cubes with colored surfaces, as shown in the figure, and by rotating the rows of cubes, you can match the colors on each of the six sides.You can perform four types of operations on a floppy cube, as shown in the figure below, and with one operation, you can rotate three adjacent cubes at the edges 180 degrees.For ease of understanding, in the diagram, the initial state is marked with + (red) on the top, * (green) on the bottom, □ (yellow) on the right front, ● (blue) on the left front, ○ (light blue) on the right back, and ■purple on the left back.Given the initial state of the floppy cube, write a program to find the minimum number of operations required to solve the puzzle.Input is given in the following format.N (1 ≤ N ≤ 30) in the first line is the number of puzzles for which you want to calculate the number of operations.The initial state puzzle i of each floppy cube is given in the following N lines.Puzzle i is given in the following format.The information for each floppy cube consists of 30 integers p i (1 ≤ p i ≤ 6).p i represents the color of the face of the floppy cube when each face of the cube is numbered i as shown in the figure below.It may be assumed that the puzzle can be solved in at most eight operations.For each puzzle, output the minimum number of operations on one line.

Akabe High School holds a game every year in which all students participate.First, all N students line up in a circle in the schoolyard.As shown, each student wears a bib numbered from 0 to N -1.In the game, one baton is used, and initially the student with number 0 is holding the baton.From there, repeat the steps below M times.First, the student currently holding the baton declares a positive integer a.When a is an even number, the baton is passed clockwise, and when it is an odd number, the baton is passed to the next student counterclockwise, and the a-th student who receives the baton is eliminated.The student who drops out passes the baton clockwise to the student next to him and exits the circle.Students who remain in the circle after the game are exempt from after-school cleaning for one year.However, in recent years, as the number of students has increased, it has become difficult to attract all students.You, in the competitive programming department, have been asked to create a program that requires students to be exempted from cleaning in a simulation.Create a program that answers the question of whether a specified student is exempt from cleaning.Input is given in the following format.The input consists of three lines, and the first line gives the number of students N (10 ≤ N ≤ 200000), the number of repetitions M (5 ≤ M < N), and the number of questions Q (1 ≤ Q ≤ 1000) asking whether students are exempt from cleaning.The next line gives the integer a i (1 ≤ a i ≤ 100) declared by the student who first holds the baton in the i-th iteration.On the next line, a bib number q i (0 ≤ q < N) is given as a question.For each question, output whether the student with bib number q i is exempt from cleaning on the i-th line.Outputs 1 if cleaning is exempt, otherwise outputs 0.

On my way home one clear night, I looked up at the sky and saw countless stars.Stars that shine brightly, stars that are faintly visible, and stars of various brightness color the night sky.You suddenly thought.How wonderful it would be if my constellation existed in this starry sky.So you decide on a standard and decide to create your own constellation by randomly connecting the stars you can see from your room window.The standard is, ``Create a group of stars such that no matter which two stars you choose, the difference in brightness between them is less than or equal to a constant d, and choose the largest one as your constellation!''For example, consider the night sky seen through a window as shown in the figure (the outer rectangle is the window frame).In this night sky,
