there are nine stars with brightnesses of 1, 12, 2, 4, 1, 8, 3, 5, and 4, respectively.If d = 2, for example, the following three constellations A, B, and C will be created.We decided to determine the size of the constellations as follows.Choose the rectangle with the smallest area that contains all the stars of a certain constellation and whose sides are parallel to the window frame.Let the area of ​​this rectangle be the size of that constellation.For example, in the night sky above, the sizes of constellations A, B, and C are 12, 10, and 16, respectively, so constellation C is the largest constellation.Given the positions and brightness of N stars and an integer d, write a program to find the area of ​​the largest constellation.The position of the star is given in coordinates with the origin at the lower left corner of the window frame, and the axes are oriented as shown.Note that if a constellation has one star, or if the stars are in a straight line parallel to the axis, then the area of ​​the constellation is 0.Input is given in the following format.The number of stars N (1 ≤ N ≤ 200000) and the integer d (0 ≤ d ≤ 10 9 ) are given in the first line.In the following N lines, the integers x i (0 ≤ x i ≤ 2000000) and y i (0 ≤ y i ≤ 2000000) representing the coordinates of the i-th star, and the integer b i (0 ≤ b i ≤ 10 9 ) representing the brightness are given.The coordinates of the stars entered are all different.Outputs the area of ​​the largest constellation in one line.

There was a school for the strong, where the strong gathered.At the school's athletic meet, the strong people march in formation.While the strong always want to show off their power, most of them don't want to walk on their own.So, I thought that by placing some of them at the bottom and holding up a large number of people in a vertical line on top of them and walking, we could actually reduce the number of people walking.First, N strongmen are lined up in a row on the ground, and each one is numbered 1, 2, 3,..., N from the left side.The weight of the strong man with serial number i is w i and he can lift up to a maximum of c i .A strong man can lift the strong man standing next to him on either his left or right only if all of the following conditions are met:For example, consider the following formation of three strong men.As shown in the diagram below, when the weight that the 2nd strongman can lift is greater than w 3, the 2nd strongman can lift the 3rd strongman.Next, if the weight that the 1st strongman can lift is greater than w 2 + w 3, the 1st strongman can lift the 2nd strongman.Also, as shown in the diagram below, if the 3rd strongman lifts the 2nd strongman, the 1st strongman will be next to the 3rd strongman, who has the 2nd strongman, so the 1st strongman can lift the 3rd strongman.A 2 strong person cannot have both a 1 and a 3 strong person as shown in the diagram below.As the exclusive programmer of the Strong School, please find the minimum number of people who will be walking at the bottom.Input is given in the following format.The number of strong people N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000) is given in the first line.The following N lines give the maximum weight c i (1 ≤ c i ≤ 100000) and the weight w i (1 ≤ w i ≤ 100000) that the i-th strong man can lift.Output the minimum number of people on one line.

Today is Z University's open campus.Every year, on this day during lunch break, large numbers of high school students line up at the school cafeteria.Therefore, the University Z administration decided to predict the maximum length of the queue.As a result of the preliminary investigation, we have found the following:We also know that multiple people can line up at the same distance from the front, and that person number 1 will always be at the front of the line.Write a program to find the distance when matrices that satisfy all the given C constraints are arranged in such a way that the distance from the beginning to the end is the maximum.However, if it is possible to leave them as far apart as possible, output inf, and if it is impossible to arrange them to satisfy the constraints, output -1.Input is given in the following format.The number of high school students queuing in the queue N (2 ≤ N ≤ 100) and the number of constraints C (0 ≤ C ≤ 200) are given in the first row.In the following C line, each constraint i is given in the following format.Constraints do not include white space.The meanings of a i , o i , b i , s i , and d i are shown below.However, it is assumed that multiple constraints are not given to a given pair.Outputs the distance from the beginning to the end in one line.

Mr. Yasushi, a mathematician, enjoys listening to music, so he built an audio room in his home.I would like to invite friends and hold an unveiling party in the completed audio room.全員にできるだけ良い音で聴いてもらいたいので、部屋の中の何か所かで音を計測し、友人たちの位置での音の質を計算するつもりです。Select the measurement points from points 1 to 35, which are the intersections of the arc and line segment in the figure below.１つの線分上にある点は、オーディオシステムからの距離が 100ｃｍから 500ｃｍまで 100ｃｍずつ離れて並んでいます。１つの円弧状にある点は、もっとも右のもの（1 番から 5 番までの点）から反時計回りに、0°から 180° の範囲で 30°度刻みに並んでいます。このような円弧と線分に囲まれた領域内のどこかに友人が居ることになるので、友人の居る位置によっ て必要な計測点を選択します。The friend's position is given as an angle measured counterclockwise along an arc and distance from the audio system.The required measurement points are 1, 2, or 4 points as shown below.To help Mr. Benevolent, please write a program to find the number of the required measurement point.Input is given in the following format.The number of friends N (1 ≤ N ≤ 50) is given in the first line.The next N lines give the friend's position.友人の位置 r i (100 ≤ r i ≤ 500) はオーディオシステムからの距離を表す整数、 t i (0 ≤ t i ≤ 180) は円弧に沿って反時計回りに測った角度を表す整数である。For each friend's position, the measurement point numbers are output in ascending order on one line.

It is known that if you have one weight each of 1 gram, 3 gram, 9 gram, and 27 gram, you can use a balance to measure from 1 gram to 40 grams in 1 gram increments.For example, if you place the item you want to weigh and a 3 gram weight on one pan of the balance, and balance it by placing 27 gram and 1 gram weights on the other pan, you will find that the weight of the item you want to weigh is 27-3+1=25 grams.Furthermore, it is known that if you have one weight each of 1(=3 0 ) grams, 3 1 grams, ... , 3 n-1 grams, and 3 n grams, you can use a balance to weigh up to (3 n+1 -1)/2 grams.また、天びんが釣り合うようなおもりの置き方は一通りしかないことも知られています。量りたいものとおもりを天びんに置いて、釣り合うようなおもりの置き方を文字列で表すことができます。3 If you put an i-gram weight on the same plate as the item you want to measure, write "-", if you put it on the other plate, write "+", and if you put it on neither, write "0" at the i-th position from the right end of the string (the right end is counted as number 0).たとえば、先ほどの 25 グラムの例は +0-+ と表わせます。Now, given the weight of something you want to weigh, write a program that outputs a string that indicates how to place the weight so that the balance is balanced.ただし、３のべき乗グラムのおもりは、どのような重さのものでも必ず１つあるものとします。(Supplement: About symmetrical ternary numbers) When the weight of something you want to weigh is w, the string representing how to place the weight is a symmetrical ternary number of w.A symmetric ternary number is a number that is scaled by a power of 3 and is represented by writing a character representing the number 1, 0, or -1 in each digit.上の文字列では、文字「 + 」、「 0 」、「 - 」がそれぞれ数 1、0、-1 に対応します。For example, when weighing 25 grams, the number represented by a symmetric ternary number with a weight position of +0-+ is 1 × 3 3 + 0 × 3 2 - 1 × 3 1 + 1 × 3 0 = 25.Input is given in the following format.w (1 ≤ w ≤ 100000) は量りたいものの重さを表す整数である。おもりの置き方を表す文字列を出力する。ただし文字列の左端を 0 にしてはならない。

Aizu Municipal Nisshinkan is a school with a long history that promotes both the literary and military arts.The Nissinkan Marathon Club is famous for its passionate coaching, and conducts special training in which participants run a lap course for a set period of time.At this time, to prevent any club members from collapsing due to dehydration, we have water stations set up to provide drinks according to the ability of the club members.The distance (pace) that each member can run per unit time is determined for each member, and special training is conducted after setting up a water station so that everyone can drink water every unit time.Team members must pick up a filled container at the water station they arrive at after one unit of time and continue running.Furthermore, at the water station that you arrive at in the next unit of time, place the empty container, pick up the filled container, and continue running, repeating this process.Additionally, empty containers are refilled with beverages one unit of time after being placed, and can be served to anyone from that moment on.All members start from the same point without any containers.Practice ends when you run until the designated practice time (water supply is required at this time as well).Since multiple members may arrive at the same water station at the same time, multiple containers may be needed at one water station at the same time.Please create a program to find the minimum number of water containers needed to safely conduct Oni's special training.Input is given in the following format.The number of club members N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100), the length of the circuit course R (1 ≤ R ≤ 1000), and the determined practice time T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000) are given in the first line.In the following N rows, member i's pace p i (1 ≤ p i ≤ 1000) is given.Outputs the minimum number of water containers required in one line.

A "database" in a computer is a container for managing information, and a "database management system (DBMS)" is a mechanism for managing that information.Databases that serve multiple users require careful DBMS construction.For example, let's say a person retrieves a product from a warehouse and performs the following processing on the database.(1) Read the number of products N from the database.(2) Write the number of new products N -1 to the database.Suppose that user 1 finishes (1) and before starting (2), another user 2 takes out the product from the warehouse and performs (1).User 2 also reads the same number of items as user 1, so when they finish (2), the number of items will decrease by 2, but the database will only have 1 item, which is a strange result.To prevent such problems, DBMSs give users who are working on certain data the right to "lock" that data.Locking prevents other users from manipulating the value, so it won't return strange results.This ensures safe operation, but now introduces another problem.For example, what would happen if User 1 and User 2 tried to lock data A and B in the following order?(1) User 1 tries to lock data A → Success (Data A is locked) (2) User 2 tries to lock Data B → Success (Data B becomes locked) (3) User 1 tries to lock Data B → Data B is locked, so User 1 waits. (4) User 2 tries to lock Data A → Data A is locked, so User 2 waits.When executing (4), user 1 has locked A and user 2 has locked B, so (3) and (4) will never succeed.This is called a "deadlock."The DBMS must detect this.You can determine whether a deadlock is occurring at a certain point by writing down the dependencies of all users and data at that point and checking whether a cycle is occurring.Dependencies are represented by an arrow pointing from the data to the user if the user has already locked the data, or by drawing an arrow from the user to the data if the user is attempting to lock the data and is in a wait state.In the examples (1) to (4) above, the dependencies at the time of executing (4) will be as shown in the diagram on the upper right.At this time, if you proceed in the direction of the arrow, you can see that a deadlock has occurred because there is a cycle: User 1 → Data B → User 2 → Data A → User 1.To experience the pain of a DBMS, what I want you to do is write a program to detect such deadlocks.Input is given in the following format.The number N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000) of dependencies between users and data is given in the first line.The following N lines give the user and data dependencies rel i.There are two types of dependencies: lock and wait, and each rel i is given in one of the following formats.Or u lock d means that user u (1 ≤ u ≤ 100) has locked data d (1 ≤ d ≤ 100).u wait d indicates that user u (1 ≤ u ≤ 100) is attempting to lock data d (1 ≤ d ≤ 100) and is in a waiting state.However, it may be assumed that the input satisfies the following conditions.Outputs 1 on one line if a deadlock has occurred, and 0 if no deadlock has occurred.

Dr. Hideyo is conducting research every day and trying to develop new drugs.In order to develop a new drug, we must combine various substances to create a drug and conduct tests to find a good drug.As he experimented with various combinations, Dr. Hideyo discovered that combinations of substances could be represented in a tree diagram.The image on the right is an example of a dendrogram representing a drug formulation.In the diagram, the circles are called material nodes, and the triangles are called selection nodes.Material nodes represent substances.A selection node represents a selection of substances; it does not itself represent a substance.There are two types of selection nodes: or type (with ∨) and alt type (with ⇔).Also, a node with a ? indicates that it is optional.However, the children of the selected node (the nodes at the end of the downward branch) are never optional.It is assumed that different material nodes appearing in the dendrogram represent different materials.When compounding a medicine, start from the top node of the tree diagram and follow the nodes in order to select a node as shown below.Only when a node is selected, each branch downward from that node is followed.If you are not chosen, you will not follow them.Dr. Hideyo has given you a tree diagram showing the combinations of substances in a drug, and you have been instructed to find the total number of combinations there are.Given a dendrogram, write a program that outputs the total number of combinations.Input is given in the following format.The number of nodes N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000) is given in the first line.The following N lines give information about the i-th node, node i.Let the first node be the top node of the dendrogram.The following N -1 lines give an edge from the s i -th node to its child, the t i -th node (1 ≤ s i ≠ t i ≤ N ).From t 1 to t N -1, the numbers 2 to N appear only once.The node information is in the following format.The dendrogram obtained from the input satisfies the following conditions.Outputs the total number of all combinations obtained from the given dendrogram in one line.However, since the value to be output can be very large, the remainder after dividing by 1,000,000,007 is output instead.

Image recognition, which extracts useful information from images, is one of the most important research themes in computer science.It is widely applied to digital cameras, driving support systems, security systems, etc.Thanks to this type of research, we are able to perform a variety of processes using a wide variety of software and program collections for image analysis.On the other hand, by writing and analyzing programs on your own, you can learn about how they work and have fun spending time with them.Here, let's try a new kind of image recognition.An image is given as an input of N × N pixels, each pixel having an integer value, as shown below.Extract one rectangular frame with a line thickness of 1 pixel from this image.Write a program that extracts the frame that maximizes the sum of the values ​​of the pixels covered by the frame and reports the sum.However, as shown in the figure below, if the number of vertical and horizontal pixels is one or two, it is also considered a frame.Input is given in the following format.The number of vertical and horizontal pixels N (1 ≤ N ≤ 300) is given in the first line.In the following N rows, an integer p i,j (-1000 ≤ p i,j ≤ 1000) representing the value of the pixel in the i-th row and j-th column is given.Outputs the sum of pixel values ​​of the frame that maximizes the sum of pixel values ​​in one line.

Hayabusa2 will finally be launched at the end of this month.I'm sure many of you remember the excitement that filled Japan when Hayabusa returned four years ago.Seven years ago, Kaguya was launched and sent many clear images back to Earth as it orbited the moon.The diagram above shows the orbit of the moon, the positions of several moons, and the orbit of Kaguya around the moon in spatial coordinates with the earth as the origin (the z-axis is perpendicular to the page and pointing from bottom to top).The moon's orbit is a circle centered at the origin on a plane passing through the x and y axes.Kaguya's orbit around the moon is a circle on a plane parallel to the plane passing through the x and z axes, and its center coincides with the center of the moon.Assume that the moon is rotating in the direction of the arrow drawn along its orbit.In the diagram on the right, there are three positions of the moon, A, B, and C.Kaguya's orbit is a straight line that crosses the moon.Kaguya's orbit is circular because it revolves around the moon, but since we are looking from the positive direction of the z-axis, it appears as a straight line parallel to the x-axis in the diagram (note that even if the moon's position changes, it is always parallel to the x-axis).Assume that Kaguya is rotating in the direction of the arrow drawn on its orbit.If Kaguya hides behind the moon as seen from Earth, it will no longer be able to communicate directly with Earth.You, who are in charge of Kaguya's mission control, are trying to use a program to find out how long Kaguya will be hiding on the far side of the moon in a given amount of time.Given the position of the moon relative to the earth and the time t in minutes, write a program to find the time for Kaguya to hide behind the moon from that position until t minutes later.However, the Earth and Kaguya are considered points, and the moon is considered a sphere with a radius of 1800 km.The moon orbits with a radius of 380,000 km in 2,500,000 seconds, and Kaguya orbits the moon at an altitude of 100 km in 2 hours.The initial position of Kaguya is the position where Kaguya's orbit takes the maximum value in the z coordinate.Input is given in the following format.m (0 ≤ m < 360) is an integer representing the angle of the moon's position measured counterclockwise from the positive x-axis to the positive y-axis in the diagram above.t (1 ≤ t ≤ 10000) is an integer representing time measured in minutes.Outputs the time (in minutes) that Kaguya will be hidden behind the moon until t minutes have passed from its initial position as a real number.However, the error must not exceed plus or minus 1.0 minute.As long as this condition is met, any number of digits below the decimal point may be displayed.

You run an internet cafe.Today you are working to solve a problem that your customers keep pointing out.The problem is that the books on store shelves are not arranged in order of volume, making it troublesome to find the book you are looking for.The book with the most volumes in your store is "Detective Akabeko" (commonly known as "Akabeko").Because it is so long, we have prepared a special bookshelf for ``Akabeko.''The weight and thickness of each volume of a book varies, as does the width of each tier of a bookshelf and the maximum weight of books that can be placed on each tier.You decide to arrange the books on the bookshelf so that the following conditions are met.Write a program that finds the maximum number of volumes of ``Akabeko'' that can be arranged on this bookshelf when these conditions are met.Input is given in the following format.The first line gives the number of volumes of ``Akabeko'' M (1 ≤ M ≤ 200000) and the number of shelves of the bookshelf N (1 ≤ N ≤ 15).In the following M line, integers representing the weight w i (1 ≤ w i ≤ 100) and thickness t i (1 ≤ t i ≤ 100) of volume i of the ``Akabeko'' book are given.In the following N lines, integers representing the upper limit of the weight of the i-th shelf c i (1 ≤ c i ≤ 10 8 ) and the width b i (1 ≤ b i ≤ 10 8 ) are given.Outputs the maximum number of volumes of ``Akabeko'' that can be arranged on a bookshelf in one line.

The king of Akabeko has two princes.When the king abdicated, he decided to divide the country into two and give each prince one country to rule.The names of the new countries are Aka and Beko.In Akabeko country, there are N towns and M roads that connect the two towns.The king decided to distribute the towns and some roads of Akabeko country between the two countries using the following procedure.(1) Choose two towns and distribute them to Aka and Beko countries.(2) Select a town s that has already been allocated.Furthermore, select a town t that is connected to town s by one road and that has not yet been allocated.Then, the road between towns s and t and town t are distributed to the country to which town s is distributed.(3) Repeat (2) until you can no longer do it.Actually, the two princes are not very close, so the king wants to increase the distance between the two countries as much as possible.Here, the distance between two countries is the length of the shortest road connecting a town in Aka country and a town in Beko country.Given information about towns and roads in Akabeko country, write a program to find the maximum distance between Aka and Beko countries after distribution and how many distributions that result in such a distance.However, the two allocation results will be differentiated if different towns or roads are allocated to Aka and Beko countries.Input is given in the following format.The first line consists of two integers.N (2 ≤ N ≤ 100) is the number of towns, and M ( N -1 ≤ M ≤ N ( N -1)/2) is the number of roads.The next M line will be given a road that connects the two towns.s i and t i (1 ≤ s i ≠ t i ≤ N ) represent the numbers of the two towns connected by the i-th road.d i (1 ≤ d i ≤ 10 9 ) represents the length of the i-th path.The input satisfies the following conditions.The maximum distance between Aka and Beko countries after distribution and the number of combinations are output on one line separated by spaces.However, since the number of combinations after distribution can be very large, we output the remainder after dividing by 1,000,000,007 instead.

Dear players, welcome to PC Koshien.Computer Koshien is currently holding competitions in three categories: Programming, Mobile, and Best Picture CG.Write a program that calculates the total number of participants given the number of participants in each category.

Brothers Hiroshi and Kenjiro came to Lake Inawashiro to fish.The two decided to score points as shown below and compete based on the total score of the fish they caught.Create a program to determine whether Hiroshi and Kenjiro won or tied based on the number of fish they caught.

A frog is about to return to its burrow.The burrow is D centimeters in front of the frog, and the frog walks straight toward the burrow.There are only two actions that frogs can take.The frog aims to just land in the burrow without jumping over it.Write a program to find the minimum number of jumps a frog needs to make to return to its burrow.

A secret organization, AiZu AnalyticS, has launched a top-secret investigation.There are N people targeted, and they are assigned identification numbers from 1 to N.As an AZAS Information Strategy Researcher, you decide to determine the number of people among your targets who meet at least one of the following conditions:Write a program that calculates the number of people who meet the conditions when the identification numbers of people who belong to organization $A$, people who belong to organization $B$, and people who own product $C$ are given as input.However, be careful not to double count people who meet both conditions.(Supplement: Regarding the above conditions) Let $A$, $B$, $C$ be a set of selected elements from the set of natural numbers from 1 to $N$.The number of people satisfying the condition is the number of elements that satisfy $(\bar{A} \cap C) \cup (B \cap C)$ (the shaded area in the figure).However, let $\bar{A}$ be the complement of set $A$.

A programming contest will be held at Byakko University again this year.Several questions will be asked in the contest, and points will be assigned to each question based on its level of difficulty.The executive committee decided to calculate each team's score based on the following rules, taking into account both the number of problems solved and their score.``The team's score is the maximum A that satisfies the fact that out of the questions that a team answered correctly, there are at least A questions with a score of A or higher.'' Write a program that calculates a team's score from the number of questions that a team answered correctly and the scores for those questions.

The cloth coasters produced and sold by Aizu Takada City are known for their beautiful symmetrical designs.As part of quality control, Aizu Takada City has installed cameras on the production line to automatically verify that the images taken of each coaster are symmetrical.Each coaster is represented as a square black-and-white image of N × N pixels.Each pixel has a value of 0 or 1, corresponding to a white or black image.Along with updating the equipment on the production line, we have decided to update the software for the image analysis system.The new system has been devised to reduce the amount of communication data, and data is sent from the camera to the analysis system using the following method.For C coasters, write a program that inputs the pixel information of the first image and the difference information for the following C - 1 image, and reports the number of coasters that are vertically symmetrical and horizontally symmetrical.

Akira, the student council president of High School A, decides to investigate which club activities the students of High School A belong to.High school A has N students, numbered 1 to N, and M types of club activities, numbered 1 to M.There is no limit to the number of people in each club activity, and some club activities may have zero people.However, according to the school rules of High School A, students can only belong to one club activity.All students abide by this school rule.Akira asked student members to investigate and obtained records of K lines, each of which was one of the following:However, there may be discrepancies in this record that could cause someone to violate school rules.Akira started looking at the first line and decided to look for the first line where he could judge that there was a contradiction.Given a record of K lines, write a program to find the number of the first line that can be judged to be inconsistent.

Numerous stone tablets have been discovered in the ruins of the ancient nation of Iwashiro.Researchers have discovered that each stone tablet has one word engraved on it.However, due to weathering over the years, some stone tablets are difficult to decipher for the following reasons.There is only one place at most where moss grows on the stone slab.Also, although moss may grow on chipped stone tablets, both sides of the stone tablet are never chipped at the same time.Researchers have a dictionary containing words known from research prior to the discovery of the stone tablets.However, when trying to guess the original word from a stone slab with moss and chips due to weathering, it is not immediately clear how many of the words in the dictionary match.Write a program that counts the number of words in a given dictionary that seem to fit, given the information on the stone tablet.

The Aizu Archaeological Society has embarked on a survey of the ruins of the ancient nation of Iwashiro, which is submerged in the Hibara Sea.The ruins exist somewhere in the Hibara Sea.Therefore, we decided to use radar exploration from the coastline to get a rough idea of ​​the location of the ruins, and estimate the maximum distance from the coastline that we would have to investigate.An observation point will be set up on the coastline represented by a straight line as shown in the diagram above, and an exploration radar will be placed.Exploration radar can only tell that there are ruins within a semicircle of a certain size centered on the observation point.However, by combining multiple observation data, it is possible to narrow down the range to a narrower range.Given some observation data consisting of the location of an observation point and the radius indicated by a survey radar, write a program that calculates the maximum distance from the coastline that needs to be surveyed.

You will participate in a ski competition held at Mt. Bandai.In this competition, each athlete skis down the slope twice and competes for the shortest total time.There are many flags on the slopes, and lines are set between them for athletes to pass.Athletes slide downhill following a line from the starting point to the finishing point.The line is set up as follows.Players can choose the line extending from their current flag to determine their next flag.The choice of line is free, so athletes can take a different line to the goal each time they go downhill.The night before the competition, sports doctor Salt-kun predicts the conditions of the slope you will be skiing on.According to the report, the snow quality changes depending on the line you passed on your first downhill, so if you go down the same line your second time downhill, the time it takes may change.Salt-kun told us the time to pass each line the first time and the time to pass the second time.Using this information, you have to find a way to minimize the total time of two runs by morning.Given the conditions of the ski slope, write a program that calculates the shortest total time for two downhill runs.

Aizu Broadcasting Corporation's educational program (AHK Education) broadcasts a craft program for children called ``Play and Make.''Today we are going to make a box using construction paper, and I would like to see if we can make a rectangular parallelepiped using the rectangular construction paper we prepared.However, the drawing paper should not be cut or folded.Given six rectangles, write a program to determine whether a rectangular parallelepiped can be created using them.

On the same page as the item you are currently viewing, online shopping sites display several other items that have been purchased by other users in the past along with the item you are currently viewing.This is because it is believed that presenting products that are considered to be highly relevant can increase sales.Something similar can be seen in local supermarkets, where products that are often bought together are placed closer together (for example, bread and jam).Your job is to write a program that helps you improve product placement.This time, I would like to set a certain standard number of times and find a combination of two products that have been purchased together more than the standard number of times.Given information on products that were purchased together and a standard number of times, create a program that outputs the combination of two products that were purchased together more than the standard number of times.

Addition can be easily calculated by hand, but if some numbers are missing, is it easy to fill in the missing numbers?For example, in the following calculation by hand, if there is a condition that the numbers 1 to 9 appear only once, how many numbers will fit in the spaces C and E?In this case, the correct answer is 8 for C and 5 for E.Operations in which some numbers are missing are called worm-eaten calculations.If the condition that the numbers 1 to 9 appear only once remains the same, but many more numbers are missing as shown below, is there only one way to fill in the numbers that will be the correct answer?In fact, it is not always decided in the same way.Write a program that outputs the number of correct ways to fill in the squares, given the information for each square from A to I in a bug-eaten calculation shaped like the figure above.

PCK, which recycles Idunium, a precious metal produced in Aizu, has a network all over the country and collects Idnium with many collection vehicles.This company has established standards for the weight and number of lumps in order to improve processing efficiency.A unit called "bokko" is used to measure the weight of a lump.x Bocco's Aidunium weighs 2 x grams.In terms of gemstones, it's like a carat.Also, the unit called "marg" is used for the number of lumps.There are 2 y margs.It's like a dozen, which is the number of items in a box.However, x and y must be integers greater than or equal to 0.Collection vehicle i collects b i margs of aidunium weighing a i bocco.The Aidunium collected in this way is put into a furnace and melted to regenerate some Aidunium lumps, but the aim is to keep the number of Aidunium lumps as small as possible.At this time, the total weight of the collected Aidunium and the total weight of the regenerated Aidunium will not change.Given the weight in Bocco units and the number in Margs of the Aidunium lumps collected by the collection vehicle, write a program that finds the result that minimizes the number of Aidunium lumps after regeneration.

The country of Aizu, located in cyberspace, trades information with the country of Wakamatsu.The two countries are achieving economic development by exchanging useful data with each other.Both countries, whose national motto is fraternity, equality, and, above all, the old saying of the Aizu region, ``What cannot be done, must not be done,'' conduct regular surveys of the trade situation.In the survey, you will be given a table that calculates the amount of data in bytes minus the data outflow from Izu Country every nanosecond.From the table, find the longest interval whose values ​​sum to 0.The longer this interval is, the more equality is judged to be maintained.Given a table recording trade conditions, write a program to find the length of the longest interval where the sum of the values ​​is 0.

Have you ever tried running a program you worked so hard to create and it ended up in an infinite loop?It would be useful to be able to determine in advance whether or not a program will stop running, without having to actually run it.Unfortunately, it is impossible to make such a judgment for every program using the programming language you usually use.However, if you use a programming language with much lower computational power, you may be able to write a program that determines whether a program written in that language will stop.Consider a programming language called TinyPower.A program in this language is a sequence of lines.Write a line number at the beginning of each line of the program, followed by a statement.The types of sentences that can be written in this language are as follows.Line numbers are positive integers, and the same line number cannot appear more than once in a program.Variables are represented by a single lowercase letter, and constant and variable values ​​are integers.There is no need to declare the variable, and the initial value of the variable is 0.Program execution begins with the first statement, and the statements are executed in the order they are listed.However, as shown in the table above, if the value of the variable in the IF statement is not 0, the program jumps to the line specified by the line number written after the variable and continues execution from the statement written on that line.The program will stop when:Given a TinyPower program, write a program that determines whether it stops.

You are working on the development of a unique operating system, ``Unzugune 15,'' and are worried about the design of the scheduler, which determines performance.A scheduler is a program that expresses processes to be executed in units called tasks and determines in what order they should be executed.The scheduler manages tasks by assigning them numbers from 1 to N.All tasks have K attributes f 1 , f 2 ,..., f K , and each attribute has a unique value.However, for two tasks, all values ​​of corresponding attributes are never the same.A task may be given a task that must be completed before execution of the task can begin.The fact that task A must be completed before task B is expressed as "task A → task B."For example, if there is a relationship such as task 1 → task 2 and task 3 → task 2, processing of both tasks 1 and 3 must be completed before processing task 2.This kind of relationship is called a dependency relationship between tasks.However, you cannot reach a task by tracing dependencies from one task to another.The scheduler determines the order of execution according to dependencies.However, there are cases where the order cannot be determined simply based on dependencies alone.In such a case, the next task to be processed is selected based on the value of the attribute that each task has.Since the task of Unzugune 15 has a plurality of attributes, it is necessary to determine the execution order by considering the values ​​of all the attributes.To this end, an evaluation order is used that determines the order in which attributes are compared.Compare the attribute that comes first in evaluation order, and select the task with the largest value for that attribute.If there are multiple such tasks, the next attribute in evaluation order is compared, and the same procedure is repeated.For example, consider three tasks with the following three attributes:If the evaluation order is set to f 1 f 2 f 3 , f 2 f 1 f 3 , or f 2 f 3 f 1 , task X is selected.Furthermore, if the evaluation order is set to f 1 f 3 f 2 , f 3 f 1 f 2 , or f 3 f 2 f 1 , task Z is selected.A feature of the scheduler of Unzugune 15 is that the evaluation order of attributes can be changed any number of times during the process.The evaluation order can be changed once a certain number of tasks have completed execution.However, the initial evaluation order that the scheduler uses is predetermined.Write a program that reports the order in which tasks are executed, given the value of each task's attributes, task dependencies, and change information on the evaluation order.

Tadao-kun is playing a game using number sequences to exercise his brain.In this game, you are first given a random row of numbers from 1 to 9.You just erase that part from the number sequence.The rules are as follows.For example, in the case of the number sequence 1,2,3,3,2,2,1,2,2 as shown in the figure above, if you count from the left and delete the 3rd and 4th 3's, you will get 1,2,2,2,1,2,2 If you count from the left and delete the 2nd to 4th 2's, you will get 1,1,2,2 If you count from the left and delete the 1st and 2nd 1's, you will get 2,2Counting from the left, erase the first and second 2 to clear the game.However, there are some number sequences that cannot be cleared no matter how you erase the numbers.For example, the sequence 1,2,3,3,1,2 or 1,2,3,1,2,3.If it's a short number sequence, you can immediately see if you can clear it, and if you find out that you can't clear it, you can try a different number sequence, but it's not so easy when it comes to long number sequences.Write a program to determine whether a given number sequence can clear the above game.

University A will hold a programming contest again this year.As a member of the problem creation team, you are in charge of creating input data for a computational geometry problem.The input data you want to create is a collection of line segments that are parallel to the x or y axes and do not touch each other.You will develop a data generation program based on the following algorithm to generate input data.Write a program that sequentially inputs N line segments parallel to the x or y axis and determines whether each line segment is added to the plane.

PCK You are all having a game tournament.In this game tournament, the rankings will be changed at the end of the tournament using an Amidakuji lottery.There are N players in the tournament and there are N vertical bars in the Amida lottery.As shown in the diagram, the Amidakuji is made up of N - 1 parts, each of which is assigned a number from 1 to N -1.Each part is a part of the Amidakuji cut out horizontally.Each part has several horizontal bars, but all the horizontal bars within the part are at the same height.Horizontal bars do not connect to each other.At the end of the competition, vertical bars will be allocated from right to left, starting with the highest ranked player.PCK You are currently in last place, so you will start from the far left.For example, in the above diagram, PCK, who was in 6th place, can move up to 4th place (fourth bar from the right) by using this Amidakuji.In this game, the person in last place is given the right to assemble the Amida Lottery.PCK has successfully decided the order of the Amidakuji parts and is aiming for a come-from-behind victory.However, parts cannot be rotated.(Note: Regarding how to follow the Amidakuji) Start from the top of the vertical bar where the Amidakuji is located and proceed from top to bottom.However, at the point where there is a horizontal bar, it moves to another vertical bar connected by that horizontal bar.Repeat this until you reach the bottom of the vertical bar.Create a program that inputs the number of participants in the game and information on the parts of the Amida Lottery and determines whether PCK can win.If you can win, print out the sequence of parts of that Amidakuji.However, if there are multiple such arrangements, output the one with the smallest lexicographical number for the given parts.

The smallest unit of data handled by a computer is called a bit, and the amount of information expressed by multiple bits is called a word.Currently, many computers process one word as 32 bits.For a computer that represents one word in 32 bits, write a program that outputs the amount of data W given in words in bits.

The appearance of the sun is called ``sunrise,'' and the disappearance of the sun is called ``sunset.'' But what exactly is the position of the sun relative to the horizon?As shown in the diagram below, let's represent the sun as a circle and the horizon as a straight line.At this time, the sun's ``sunrise'' and ``sunset'' times are said to be the moment when the top of the circle representing the sun coincides with the straight line representing the horizon.After sunrise, the time when the top of the circle is above the straight line is daytime, and the time when the circle is completely hidden below the straight line is nighttime.Write a program that takes as input the height from the horizon to the center of the sun at a certain time and the radius of the sun, and outputs whether the time is "daytime," "sunrise or sunset," or "nighttime."

The ``Western calendar'' is a concept imported from the West, but in Japan, there is a concept called the Japanese calendar, which is a way to express calendar years by adding a year to the ``gengo.''For example, this year is 2016 according to the Western calendar, but it is 2016 according to the Japanese calendar.Both are commonly used ways to express the year, but have you ever experienced a situation where you know the year according to the Western calendar but don't know what year according to the Japanese calendar, or vice versa?Write a program that outputs the year in the Japanese calendar when the year is given in the Western calendar, and the year in the Western calendar when the year is given in the Japanese calendar.However, for simplicity, the correspondence between the Western calendar and the Japanese calendar is as follows.

Aizu Prefecture decided to build a new town in order to increase the population.For this purpose, we decided to cultivate a new rectangular piece of land and divide it into square sections, all of the same size.Preparation of this land requires costs proportional to the number of plots, but the prefecture wants to minimize this cost.Given the east-west and north-south lengths of newly cultivated land and the maintenance cost per plot, write a program to find the minimum maintenance cost when all plots are developed.

In computer graphics, polygon models are used to represent three-dimensional shapes.A polygon model is a model that creates surfaces by giving vertex coordinates and how those vertices are connected.A general polygon model can handle any polygon, but this time we will consider a polygon model consisting of triangles.Any polygon model can be represented as a collection of surface information representing triangles.One surface information is expressed by arranging three vertices.However, if they are made up of the same three points but arranged differently, they will represent the same surface information.For example, in the tetrahedron shown below, the surface formed by connecting vertices 1, 2, and 3 can also be expressed as vertices 2, 3, 1, vertices 3, 2, 1, etc.In this way, multiple pieces of the same surface information would be wasted, so it would be better to combine them into one.Given surface information, write a program that calculates the number of surface information that must be deleted to eliminate duplicate surfaces.

At the pancake shop where you work, pancake batter is baked in a horizontal row on a long iron plate.Pancakes can be completed by flipping them several times with a spatula.The number of times you need to flip the pancakes will vary depending on the pancake.Since the spatula is large, two adjacent pancakes will be flipped at the same time.At this time, the positions of these two cards will not be swapped.However, you can not only flip both ends of the pancake together with the pancake next to it, but you can also flip just one pancake.After flipping all the pancakes the required number of times, remove them all at once from the griddle and it's done.You don't want to flip the pancakes too many times than necessary or they will become tough.So you want to find a way to minimize the total number of times each pancake needs to be flipped before they're all done.Given the number of pancakes on a griddle and the number of times each pancake must be turned over before they are finished, write a program that calculates the minimum total number of times each pancake must be turned over (not the number of times the spatula must be used) until all the pancakes are turned over.

We researchers, who discovered the ancient nation of Iwashiro and proceeded with our investigation, finally discovered a temple in the center of Iwashiro.A stone tablet dedicated to the god of Iwashiro was kept in the temple.There were two strings written on the tablet: one sentence and one spell.In Iwashiro, the number of times a spell appears in a sentence has an important meaning.However, it is assumed that all the letters in the spell appear in sequence, even once in the sentence.For example, if the sentence is "abab" and the spell is "ab", "ab" appears three times in "abab" (ab ab, ab ab, a ba b), including those that are not consecutive.Given a sentence and a spell, write a program that prints out how many times the spell appears in the sentence.

Villages were dotted around the Wakamatsu Plain in Aizu Province.Some of the villages are connected by straight, traversable roads, and you can follow the roads between any of the villages on the plains.Each road requires maintenance fees depending on its length, but all the villages pooled their funds to maintain the roads.At one point, it was decided that all the villages would become one village, and a boundary line was drawn to surround the village.According to national regulations, a straight line connecting any two villages that make up a village must not pass outside the village (although it is allowed to cross the boundary line).Furthermore, in Aizu Province, there must be a road along the boundary line surrounding the village.Where there are no roads along the border, the government will build new roads.However, since the village pays for the maintenance of the road, the villagers want to keep the boundary line as short as possible.Furthermore, the villagers decided to minimize the total length of roads by eliminating roads that do not lie on the boundary lines, while still allowing the villagers to travel between all villages.Information about the location of the village and the original road is given.Write a program that calculates the minimum total length of roads when roads are placed on the boundary line and all villages can come and go.However, villages are points with no size, and roads are line segments with no width.

Byakko University holds a programming contest every year.The contest starts with a score of 0 for all teams, and points are added based on the answers.This contest will be ranked in order of highest score.Each team is assigned a number from 1 to N, where N is the total number of teams.If the scores are the same, the one with the lower number will be ranked higher.Byakko University has developed a ranking system for spectators to make the contest more exciting.As a member of the development team, you are tasked with creating a program that is part of this system.Create a program that updates scores and reports the numbers and scores of the teams in the specified rankings according to the given instructions.

You discover an old board game in the programming club room you belong to.It looked interesting so I decided to give it a try.This game consists of M events, and you must complete event i at time t i.However, your strength must be greater than or equal to s i at that time, and if you cannot make it greater than or equal to s i , the game will be over.Your strength at the start of the game (time 0) is 0, but you can increase your strength by purchasing items.Your money at the start of the game is 0, but it increases by 1 per unit of time.The board has N items arranged in order, each numbered from 1 to N. Item i has a price v i and purchasing it increases your strength by h i .If you have enough money, you can buy as many items as you want at any time, but you must choose from the remaining items starting with the lowest number.Each item disappears once purchased.Also, you can buy multiple items in succession at the same time, and in this case you can get the sum of the differences in h i of adjacent items as a bonus.For example, if you buy items 1, 2, and 3 at the same time at a certain time, your strength increases by h 1 + h 2 + h 3 plus | h 1 - h 2 | + | h 2 - h 3 |.You want to maximize your money after conquering all events.Create a program that takes item information and event information as input, and outputs the maximum amount of money after completing all events.

Aizu Broadcasting Corporation's educational program (AHK Education) broadcasts a craft program for children called ``Play and Make.''Today we are going to make a rectangle using sticks, and we would like to see if we can make a rectangle using the four sticks we prepared.However, the stick must not be cut or broken.Given the lengths of four rods, write a program that determines whether a rectangle with all of them as sides can be created.

Aizu Broadcasting Corporation's educational program (AHK Education) broadcasts a craft program for children called ``Play and Make.''This time it's time to make a box with sticks, and I'd like to see if you can make a rectangular parallelepiped using the 12 sticks you prepared.However, the stick must not be cut or broken.Given the lengths of 12 bars, write a program to determine whether a rectangular parallelepiped with all of them as sides can be created.

Given N different natural numbers.If we select four different ones and call them $A$, $B$, $C$, $D$, we would like to find the maximum value of the following formula.Given N different natural numbers, write a program that selects four different numbers and finds the maximum value of the above formula.

There is a game using playing cards called ``Sevens''.Here we will consider a game that makes it easier.Use 13 cards with numbers 1 to 13 written on them to form 7s.In a match, the game proceeds as follows between only two players.Given the card number of the first player, write a program that determines and outputs whether there is at least one winning procedure for the first player, no matter how the second player plays cards.

Yuki made Sugoroku so that everyone could play with it at children's parties.In this Sugoroku, squares are lined up in a ring, and each square has an integer greater than or equal to 1 written on it.Players choose a square as a starting point and place their pieces.Move the piece clockwise by the number written on that square.Move the piece clockwise again by the number written on the square where it stopped.Repeat this and when the piece stops on the square you selected as the starting point, it is "up".In reality, depending on how you select the trout, it may never turn out to be a good result.Yuki is trying to count the number of squares that will reach the ``Agari'' on this Sugoroku.Create a program that takes the Sugoroku information as input and reports the number of squares that can reach the "up".

Among real numbers, those with rotating decimal parts and those with finite digits can be expressed as fractions.Given a real number that can be expressed as a fraction, write a program that outputs an irreducible fraction (a fraction that cannot be further reduced) that is equal to the real number.

A relay race is held every year in Aizu Country.There are N towns scattered throughout Aizu Country, each numbered from 1 to N.Some towns are connected by roads that allow direct access to each other.You can also travel between each town by following several roads.The course for the tournament will be determined as follows:Tokuitsu, a monk from the land of Yamato, wants to open a new temple in the quietest possible town in the land of Aizu.Therefore, they want to know which towns have no possibility of being used as courses for relay races.Given information on roads connecting towns in Aizu, create a program to find towns that are unlikely to be used as courses for relay races.

Nobuo and Shizuo are playing a game of competing for territory on a rectangular island.As shown in Figure 1 below, the entire island is made up of square sections divided into a grid, and the profits and losses arising from each section are shown as integers.In this game, you move one piece to determine the boundaries of your territory.At the beginning of the game, the pieces are located at the northwest end of the island (Figure 1).When you move this piece to the southeastern tip of the island, the trajectory of the piece becomes the boundary line of your territory.The two players take turns moving their pieces.Pieces can only be moved to the grid point to the south or to the east (Figure 2).When a piece reaches the edge of the island, move it in the south or east direction, whichever direction it can move.The game ends when the pieces reach the southeast corner.After the game ends, the area northeast of the boundary line is the area of ​​the first player, and the area southwest is the area of ​​the second player (Figure 3).The sum of the gains and losses from the plots within each player's territory constitutes that player's score.Both players are quite familiar with the game and move their pieces accurately in such a way that the final result of subtracting the opponent's score from their own is the highest.Write a program that calculates the outcome at the end of the game, given the size of the island and the profit and loss resulting from each section.The result is the absolute value of the difference between the scores of Nobuo-kun and Shizuo-kun.

After entering high school, Takeko joined the programming club and gradually became absorbed in the fun of algorithms.Now, I'm thinking of competing in Programming Koshien when I'm in my second year.One day, Takeko learned about sorting algorithms and decided to design one herself.The sorting algorithm created by Takeko performs the following processing when given a sequence of one or more natural numbers with no duplicate elements as input.To estimate how much computational time this algorithm would require, Takeko decided to count the number of operations that move an element just after the end of the column.Write a program that takes column information as input and reports the number of operations that move an element just after the end of the column.

There are N ants on a large chessboard, each numbered from 1 to N.As shown in the figure, the chess board is a rectangle consisting of H x W squares, with white squares at the northwest corner, and white squares and black squares arranged alternately.Initially, every ant is in a square on the chessboard, facing either east or south.There can never be more than 2 ants in one square.The ants are now moving all at once.All ants move one square in the direction they are facing in one unit of time.However, if the destination is outside the chessboard, it will fall and disappear from the chessboard.When two ants land on the same square on a chessboard, the ants will do the following: Given the size of the chessboard and information about the ants, write a program that reports the numbers of the ants in the order in which they fall.However, if multiple ants fall at the same time, report the one with the lower number first.

You gave twins Ai and Zu a program for a game using strings.In this game, Ai-kun and Zu-kun each choose a substring from a string, compare them, and the person who chooses the smaller one gets points.The two competed and played many games.However, after playing the game over and over again with the same string of characters, I got tired of it.So you decide to modify the program so that the string changes.Given a string U of length N and Q statements, write a program that processes the following instructions.

AIZU At dusk, the country is crowded with tourists who stop and hold their smartphones facing the western sky.AIZU The country is a metropolis lined with countless buildings, and in the western sky, where there are long valleys between buildings, there is a spectacular view of the silhouettes of the buildings and the sunlight leaking through them.According to Mr. Wakamatsu of the AIZU Tourism Association, when only half of the circle representing the sun is obstructed, the view becomes especially spectacular.As shown in the figure, the western sky is represented by an x ​​- y plane with the horizon as the x axis and the trajectory of the sun's center as the y axis, the sun as a circle with radius R, and the silhouettes of each building as rectangles.The base of each building's silhouette is on the x-axis, and the sun sets perpendicular to the horizon from a sufficiently high position.The sun is blocked by the silhouette of a building or by the ground above the horizon, and then disappears below the horizon.Given the radius of the sun and the silhouette of each building, write a program to find the highest height (y-coordinate of the center) of the sun that blocks exactly half of the sun's area.

Alice and Brown are brothers in a family and each receives pocket money in celebration of the coming year. They are very close and share the total amount of the money fifty-fifty. The pocket money each receives is a multiple of 1,000 yen. Write a program to calculate each one’s share given the amount of money Alice and Brown received.

You are now in a bookshop with your friend Alice to buy a book, "The Winning Strategy for the Programming Koshien Contest,” just released today. As you definitely want to buy it, you are planning to borrow some money from Alice in case the amount you have falls short of the price. If the amount you receive from Alice still fails to meet the price, you have to abandon buying the book this time. Write a program to calculate the minimum amount of money you need to borrow from Alice given the following three items of data: the money you and Alice have now and the price of the book.
The 9th day of September 2017 is Saturday. Then, what day of the week is the X-th of September 2017? Given a day in September 2017, write a program to report what day of the week it is.

The supercomputer system L in the PCK Research Institute performs a variety of calculations upon request from external institutes, companies, universities and other entities. To use the L system, you have to reserve operation time by specifying the start and end time. No two reservation periods are allowed to overlap each other. Write a program to report if a new reservation overlaps with any of the existing reservations. Note that the coincidence of start and end times is not considered to constitute an overlap. All the temporal data is given as the elapsed time from the moment at which the L system starts operation.

I am a craftsman specialized in interior works. A customer asked me to perform wiring work on a wall whose entire rectangular surface is tightly pasted with pieces of panels. The panels are all of the same size (2 m in width, 1 m in height) and the wall is filled with an x (horizontal) by y (vertical) array of the panels. The customer asked me to stretch a wire from the left top corner of the wall to the right bottom corner whereby the wire is tied up at the crossing points with the panel boundaries (edges and vertexes) as shown in the figure. There are nine tied-up points in the illustrative figure shown below. Write a program to provide the number of points where the wire intersects with the panel boundaries. Assume that the wire and boundary lines have no thickness.

A plurality of trampolines are arranged in a line at 10 m intervals. Each trampoline has its own maximum horizontal distance within which the jumper can jump safely. Starting from the left-most trampoline, the jumper jumps to another trampoline within the allowed jumping range. The jumper wants to repeat jumping until he/she reaches the right-most trampoline, and then tries to return to the left-most trampoline only through jumping. Can the jumper complete the roundtrip without a single stepping-down from a trampoline? Write a program to report if the jumper can complete the journey using the list of maximum horizontal reaches of these trampolines. Assume that the trampolines are points without spatial extent.

Aizu Ocean Transport Company (AOTC) accepted a new shipping order. The pieces of the cargo included in this order all have the same square footprint (2m x 2m). The cargo room has a rectangular storage space of 4m width with various longitudinal extents. Each pieces of cargo must be placed in alignment with partition boundaries which form a 1m x 1m square grid. A straddling arrangement with a neighboring partition (for example, protrusion by 50 cm) is not allowed. Neither an angled layout nor stacked placement are allowed. Note also that there may be some partitions on which any cargo loading is prohibited. AOTC wishes to use the currently available ship for this consignment and needs to know how many pieces of cargo it can accommodate. Make a program to report the maximum cargo capacity of the cargo space given the following information: the depth (m) of the cargo space and the loading-inhibited partitions.

Bob is playing a popular game called "Dungeon". The game is played on a rectangular board consisting of W × H squares. Each square is identified with its column and row number, thus the square located in the x -th column and the y -th row is represented as ( x , y ). The left-most square in the top row is (0, 0) and the right-most square in the bottom row is ( W -1, H -1). Bob moves a character "BomBom" to clear the game. BomBom is initially located at (0, 0). The game is won if Bob successfully destroys all the enemy characters on the board by manipulating BomBom cleverly. The enemy characters are fixed on specific squares, and Bob can manipulate BomBom using the following two operations any number of times. BomBom consumes a Cost when it moves from one square to another. BomBom can use a bomb any number of times without consuming a Cost. Use of a bomb has no effect on BomBom’s behavior and it can move even to a square where an enemy character is located. Given the board size and enemy information, make a program to evaluate the minimum Cost BomBom consumes before it destroys all enemy characters.

You are given a string and a number k . You are suggested to generate new strings by swapping any adjacent pair of characters in the string up to k times. Write a program to report the lexicographically smallest string among them.

Aizu is a country famous for its rich tourism resources and has N cities, each of which is uniquely identified with a number (0 to N -1). It has a road network consisting of M one-way roads connecting a city to another. All the roads connecting the cities in Aizu have a row of cherry trees along their routes. For enhancing the cherry-viewing experience, a proposal was made to modify the road network so that a tourist can travel around all the roads. To achieve this target, it was decided to construct several one-way roads, each connecting two cities and abiding by the following rules. You, as a tourism promotion officer, are assigned with the task of writing a program for the road construction project. Write a program to determine the minimum number of roads to be constructed given the road network information in Aizu.

There is a network consisting of N machines (sequentially numbered from 0 to N -1) interconnected through N -1 bidirectional communication cables. Any two machines can perform bidirectional communication through one or more cables. From time to time, machines in the network are renewed. When a new machine is introduced, the cables directly connected to it are also replaced with thicker ones to cope with the increased volume of communication traffic. The communication fee arising from communication traffic between any two machines is calculated by summing the charges assigned to all the cables routing via the two. A unique charging scheme is employed in this system: if the size of a cable is a multiple of K , then the cable is not charged (free of charge). Other cables are charged according to their sizes. Based on the given information on the network topology and Q instructions, write a program to execute each instruction.

AHK Education, the educational program section of Aizu Broadcasting Cooperation, broadcasts a children’s workshop program called "Let's Play and Make." Today’s theme is "Make your own flag." A child writes his first initial in the center of their rectangular flag. Given the flag size and the initial letter to be placed in the center of it, write a program to draw the flag as shown in the figure below. The figure has "A" in the center of a flag with size 9 (horizontal) × 5 (vertical).

A project is underway to build a new viewing tower in Bange town called “Bange Hills Tower” whose selling point will be the gorgeous view of the entire main keep of Wakamatsu Castle from top to bottom. Therefore, the view line from the top of the tower must reach the bottom of the keep without being hindered by any of the buildings in the town. Write a program to calculate the minimum tower height required to view the keep in its entirety based on the following information: the planned location of the tower and the heights and locations of existing buildings. Assume all the buildings, including the keep, are vertical lines without horizontal stretch. “view of the entire keep” means that the view line from the tower top can cover the keep from the bottom to the top without intersecting (contacts at the top are exempted) any of the other vertical lines (i.e., buildings).

A trick of fate caused Hatsumi and Taku to come to know each other. To keep the encounter in memory, they decided to calculate the difference between their ages. But the difference in ages varies depending on the day it is calculated. While trying again and again, they came to notice that the difference of their ages will hit a maximum value even though the months move on forever. Given the birthdays for the two, make a program to report the maximum difference between their ages. The age increases by one at the moment the birthday begins. If the birthday coincides with the 29 th of February in a leap year, the age increases at the moment the 1 st of March arrives in non-leap years.

A boy PCK is playing with N electric metronomes. The i -th metronome is set to tick every t_i seconds. He started all of them simultaneously. He noticed that, even though each metronome has its own ticking interval, all of them tick simultaneously from time to time in certain intervals. To explore this interesting phenomenon more fully, he is now trying to shorten the interval of ticking in unison by adjusting some of the metronomes’ interval settings. Note, however, that the metronomes do not allow any shortening of the intervals. Given the number of metronomes and their preset intervals t_i (sec), write a program to make the tick-in-unison interval shortest by adding a non-negative integer d_i to the current interval setting of the i -th metronome, and report the minimum value of the sum of all d_i .

You are running a restaurant that serves dishes only three times a day. Each of your customer has his/her own separate time zones for eating breakfast, lunch and supper. Thus, your customer enjoys your dish only when he/she visits your restaurant to meet your serving time settings. Your wish is to modify your serving time arrangement so that as many as possible of your customers can enjoy your meals three times a day. Write a program to enable as many as possible of customers can enjoy your service three times a day, i.e. breakfast, lunch, and supper. A list of customers eating time zones is given, which you cannot change. Settings of your service time are your option. Coincidence between your service time and either edge of customer’s time zone (start or end time) does not hinder you to provide your service.

You have a cross-section paper with W x H squares, and each of them is painted either in white or black. You want to re-arrange the squares into a neat checkered pattern, in which black and white squares are arranged alternately both in horizontal and vertical directions (the figure shown below is a checkered patter with W = 5 and H = 5 ). To achieve this goal, you can perform the following two operations as many times you like in an arbitrary sequence: swapping of two arbitrarily chosen columns, and swapping of two arbitrarily chosen rows. Create a program to determine, starting from the given cross-section paper, if you can re-arrange them into a checkered pattern.

If you visit Aizu Akabeko shrine, you will find a unique paper fortune on which a number with more than one digit is written. Each digit ranges from 1 to 9 (zero is avoided because it is considered a bad omen in this shrine). Using this string of numeric values, you can predict how many years it will take before your dream comes true. Cut up the string into more than one segment and compare their values. The difference between the largest and smallest value will give you the number of years before your wish will be fulfilled. Therefore, the result varies depending on the way you cut up the string. For example, if you are given a string 11121314 and divide it into segments, say, as 1,11,21,3,14, then the difference between the largest and smallest is 21 - 1 = 20. Another division 11,12,13,14 produces 3 (i.e. 14 - 11) years. Any random division produces a game of luck. However, you can search the minimum number of years using a program. Given a string of numerical characters, write a program to search the minimum years before your wish will be fulfilled.

The Onogawa Expedition is planning to conduct a survey of the Aizu nature reserve. The expedition planner wants to take the shortest possible route from the start to end point of the survey, while the expedition has to go around the coast of the Lake of Onogawa en route. The expedition walks along the coast of the lake, but can not wade across the lake. Based on the base information including the start and end point of the survey and the area of Lake Onogawa as convex polygon data, make a program to find the shortest possible route for the expedition and calculate its distance. Note that the expedition can move along the polygonal lines passing through the nodes, but never enter within the area enclosed by the polygon.

A lottery is being held in a corner of the venue of the Aizu festival. Several types of balls are inside the lottery box and each type has its unique integer printed on the surfaces of the balls. An integer T is printed on the lottery box. In the lottery, you first declare two integers A and B , and draw up to M balls from the box. Let the sum of the integers printed on the balls be S . You can get a wonderful gift if the following two criteria are met: S divided by T gives a remainder greater than or equal to A , and S divided by T gives a quotient (fractional portion dropped) greater than or equal to B . Write a program to determine if you have any chance of getting the gift given the following information: the number of ball types, ball-type specific integers, the maximum number of balls to be drawn from the box, the integer printed on the lottery box, and two integers declared before drawing. Assume that each ball type has sufficient (≥M) population in the box. Note also that there may be a chance of getting the gift even without drawing any ball.

The students in a class in Akabe high-school define the relation “acquaintance” as: They define the relation “companion” as: A boy PCK joined the class recently and wishes to hold a party inviting his class fellows. He wishes to invite as many boys and girls as possible to the party and has written up an invitation list. In arranging the list, he placed the following conditions based on the acquaintanceship within the class before he joined. When T is in the list: PCK made the invitation list so that the maximum number of his classmates is included. Given the number of classmates N and the friendships among them, write a program to estimate the number of boys and girls in the list. All the classmates are identified by an index that ranges from 0 to N -1.

Hideyo has come by two aerial photos of the same scale and orientation. You can see various types of buildings, but some areas are hidden by clouds. Apparently, they are of the same area, and the area covered by the second photograph falls entirely within the first. However, because they were taken at different time points, different shapes and distribution of clouds obscure identification where the area in the second photograph is located in the first. There may even be more than one area within the first that the second fits equally well. A set of pixel information is given for each of the photographs. Write a program to extract candidate sub-areas within the first photograph that compare with the second equally well and output the number of the candidates.

A boy PCK had N straight iron bars, which were serially indexed. Unfortunately, the first M bars (0 ≤ M ≤ N ) among them were bent during transportation. They all suffered a perpendicular bend at one point. He is planning to make a cube using a set of bars selected using the following rules: X bars from bent ones, Y bars from straight ones, where 2X + Y = 12 . Any two bars can be jointed only at the apexes of the cube. He wants to count how many types of rectangular parallelepipeds (hereafter RP) he can make using these bars. Make a program to count out types (different shapes) of RPs that PCK can make using the following information: the number of bars and length of each one, position of the bend, and the number of bars to be used to construct an RP. Note that any two RPs similar in shape are considered identical: namely if the length of three defining sides of two RPs coincide if arranged in increasing/decreasing order (e.g., three sides of RP i and j are A_i, B_i, C_i , and A_j, B_j and C_j in increasing order, then the relations A_i = A_j, B_i = B_j , and C_i = C_j hold. Note also that the bars are sufficiently thin to allow you to consider them as idealized lines.

In Japan, temperature is usually expressed using the Celsius (℃) scale. In America, they used the Fahrenheit (℉) scale instead. $20$ degrees Celsius is roughly equal to $68$ degrees Fahrenheit. A phrase such as "Today’s temperature is $68$ degrees" is commonly encountered while you are in America. A value in Fahrenheit can be converted to Celsius by first subtracting $32$ and then multiplying by $\frac{5}{9}$. A simplified method may be used to produce a rough estimate: first subtract $30$ and then divide by $2$. Using the latter method, $68$ Fahrenheit is converted to $19$ Centigrade, i.e., $\frac{(68-30)}{2}$. Make a program to convert Fahrenheit to Celsius using the simplified method: $C = \frac{F - 30}{2}$.

It’s still hot every day, but September has already come. It’s autumn according to the calendar. Looking around, I see two red dragonflies at rest on the wall in front of me. It’s autumn indeed. When two red dragonflies’ positional information as measured from the end of the wall is given, make a program to calculate the distance between their heads.

I’m planning to have a party on my birthday. Many of my friends will come to the party. Some of them will come with one or more pieces of cakes, but it is not certain if the number of the cakes is a multiple of the number of people coming. I wish to enjoy the cakes equally among the partiers. So, I decided to apply the following rules. First, all the party attendants are given the same number of cakes. If some remainder occurs, a piece goes on a priority basis to the party host (that’s me!). How many pieces of cake can I enjoy? Given the number of my friends and cake information, make a program to calculate how many pieces of cake I can enjoy. Note that I am not counted in the number of my friends.

We have had record hot temperatures this summer. To avoid heat stroke, you decided to buy a quantity of drinking water at the nearby supermarket. Two types of bottled water, 1 and 0.5 liter, are on sale at respective prices there. You have a definite quantity in your mind, but are willing to buy a quantity larger than that if: no combination of these bottles meets the quantity, or, the total price becomes lower. Given the prices for each bottle of water and the total quantity needed, make a program to seek the lowest price to buy greater than or equal to the quantity required.

A Dudeney number is a positive integer for which the sum of its decimal digits is equal to the cube root of the number. For example, $512$ is a Dudeney number because it is the cube of $8$, which is the sum of its decimal digits ($5 + 1 + 2$). In this problem, we think of a type similar to Dudeney numbers and try to enumerate them. Given a non-negative integer $a$, an integer $n$ greater than or equal to 2 and an upper limit $m$, make a program to enumerate all $x$’s such that the sum of its decimal digits $y$ satisfies the relation $x = (y + a)^n$, and $x \leq m$.

Bozosort, as well as Bogosort, is a very inefficient sort algorithm.
