Archaeologists thought that among the square pillars found in the ruins, the one with the largest area must be the temple. Given the coordinates of the pillar's position, write a program that finds the square with the largest area among the four pillars and outputs that area. Note that the sides of the square are not necessarily parallel to the coordinate axes. In the example in the figure below,There are 10 pillars, four at coordinates (4, 2), (5, 2), (5, 3), (4, 3) and four at coordinates (1, 1), (4, 0), (5, 3), (2, 4) forming a square. The square with the largest area is the latter, and its area is 10. The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The input ends with a line containing one zero.The first line lists the number n of columns found in the ruins. In each of the n lines from the second line to the n + 1 line, the x and y coordinates of the columns are written separated by spaces. A single column does not appear more than once. n is an integer that satisfies 1 ≤ n ≤ 3000, and the x and y coordinates of the columns are 0.It is an integer between 5000 and above.30% of the scoring data satisfies 1 ≤ n ≤ 100, and 60% of the scoring satisfies 1 ≤ n ≤ 500.The number of datasets does not exceed 10.Output one integer for each dataset. If a square with four pillars exists, output the area of ​​the square with the largest area; if there is no such square, output 0.

You are a reporter for JOI newspaper and are in charge of sports articles.Until yesterday, a round-robin league match between n soccer teams was held in Croatia.The tournament executive committee appears to have ranked each team from 1st to nth based on match results and regulations.You were given the following information along with the outcome of some of the matches.Information 1 There were no tied games.Information 2 All teams have different rankings.Information 3 For all 1 ≤ a < b ≤ n, in a match between the team in place a and the team in place b, the team in place a always wins.In order to write an article, you decide to guess the rankings based on the wins and losses of some games and the information 1 3 that has been passed on.Create a program that, given the wins and losses of some games as input, outputs one ranking table that matches the information given.Also, determine whether there is a ranking table other than the output ranking table that matches the information conveyed.Here, the standings refer to the teams arranged in order from 1st to nth place.Suppose there are 4 teams, each team is numbered from 1 to 4, and their wins and losses are given in the following table.When row i, column j is ○, it means that the team with number i won in a match between the team with number i and the team with number j.Also, when it is ×, it means that the team with number j won.? means that no win or loss is given.At this time, there is only one ranking table that fits the information that has been conveyed: Assume that there are three teams, each team is numbered from 1 to 3, and wins and losses are given in the table below.At this time, there are two ranking tables that match the information conveyed:The input is given in the following format.The number of soccer teams, n, is written in the first line.Each team is assigned a number from 1 to n.The second line contains the number m of wins and losses in the given match.The 3rd line to m + 2nd line shows the outcome of the match.Each line contains two integers i and j separated by a space, representing that the team numbered i won against the team numbered j.n, m satisfy 1 ≤ n ≤ 5000, 1 ≤ m ≤ 100000.Of the test data used for scoring, 30% satisfies 1 ≤ n ≤ 7, 1 ≤ m ≤ 15.Also, 60% satisfies 1 ≤ n ≤ 100, 1 ≤ m ≤ 2000.The output consists of n + 1 lines.Output a ranking table that matches the information conveyed in the n lines from the 1st line to the nth line.Output the number of the i-placed team on the i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ n).On line n + 1, output an integer indicating whether there is a ranking table other than the output ranking table that matches the transmitted information.If it does not exist, output 0; if it does, output 1.The input and output corresponding to Example 1 are as follows.The input and output corresponding to example 2 are as follows.The following two types of output data can be considered for this input data.You can output either one.

Mobiles are widely popular as moving works of art.The IOI Japan Committee decided to create a mobile to publicize JOI.The JOI public relations mobile uses three types of elements: a stick, a string, and a weight, and is constructed as follows.However, all sticks must be balanced.The weight of the stick and string is negligible, so the weight of the stick and string is 0.In other words, for each stick, suppose that the stick is balanced when (total weight of weights hanging below the red end of the stick) × (length from the fulcrum of the stick to the red end) = (total weight of weights hanging below the blue end of the stick) × (length from the fulcrum to the blue end of the stick).We have already decided how the lengths of the rods will be tied together to form the mobile, but the weight of the weight has not yet been determined.The lighter the mobile, the easier it is to lift, so we want to make a mobile that is as light as possible.As mentioned above, find a way to attach weights that will minimize the total weight of the mobile while keeping all the rods balanced, and create a program that will output the total weight of the mobile at that time.The program is given the following information about the configuration of the mobile. The input consists of multiple data sets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The input ends with a line containing one zero.The number of sticks used in the mobile, n, is written on the first line.The following n lines (1 ≤ n ≤ 100) write the data for each stick.On the i + 1st line (1 ≤ i ≤ n), four integers p, q, r, b are written separated by spaces, and the number of sticks used in the mobile is written as follows:, the ratio of the length from the fulcrum to the red end and the length from the fulcrum to the blue end is p : q, the number of the rod suspended at the red end is r, and the number of the rod suspended at the blue end is b. However, the rod number 0 represents that a weight is suspended. Also, for any input, the minimum value of the weight of the mobile is wIf L is the maximum positive integer used to represent the ratio in the input, then wL < 2 31 is satisfied. The number of datasets does not exceed 15.Outputs the weight of the mobile in one line for each dataset.

Taro often shops at the JOI general store.JOI General Store has enough coins of 500 yen, 100 yen, 50 yen, 10 yen, 5 yen, and 1 yen, so I always pay the change in such a way as to get the least number of coins.When Taro shops at JOI General Store and takes out a 1,000 yen bill at the cash register, write a program that calculates the number of coins included in the change he receives.For example, in the case of input example 1, 4 must be output as shown in the figure below.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset consists of one line, and only one amount (an integer greater than or equal to 1 and less than 1000) that Taro will pay is written.The input data ends with a single zero line.The number of datasets does not exceed 5.Output the number of coins included in change for each dataset on one line.

Write a program that counts the number of places in a given string where three consecutive characters are arranged in the order JOI or IOI.The string consists only of uppercase letters of the alphabet.For example, the character string "JOIOIOI" in the figure below contains JOI in one place and IOI in two places.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is one line and consists of up to 10000 uppercase letters of the alphabet.Input ends at EOF. Number of datasets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output the number of JOIs found in the first line and the number of IOIs found in the second line.

There is a card game played by two people, such as the one below.Taro and Hanako will play against each other in this game.The game begins with Taro's turn.Both of them make it a point to always play the card with the smallest number written on it among the cards that can be played.Create a program that outputs the scores of Taro and Hanako when the cards dealt to Taro are input.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.There are n+1 lines of input.The first line contains an integer n.Each line from the 2nd line to the n+1th line contains an integer, representing the integer written on the card dealt to Taro.When n is 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output Taro's score on the first line and Hanako's score on the second line.

You are looking for constellations in a photograph of the starry sky.A photo always contains exactly one figure with the same shape, orientation, and size as the constellation you are looking for.However, there is a possibility that there are extra stars in the photo in addition to the stars that make up the constellation.For example, the constellation in Figure 1 is included in the photo in Figure 2 (circled).Translating the coordinates of a star in a given constellation by 2 in the x direction and −3 in the y direction results in its position in the photo.Given the shape of the constellation you want to find and the position of the star in the photo, write a program that calculates the amount of translation needed to convert the coordinates of the constellation to the coordinates in the photo.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line of the input contains the number m of stars that make up the constellation you want to search for.In the following m lines, integers indicating the x and y coordinates of the m stars that make up the constellation you want to search are written, separated by spaces.The number n of stars in the photo is written in the m + 2nd line.The next n lines are space-separated integers that indicate the x and y coordinates of the n stars in the photo.The positions of the m stars that make up the constellation are all different.Also, the positions of the n stars in the photo are all different.1 ≤ m ≤ 200, 1 ≤ n ≤ 1000.The x and y coordinates of all stars are between 0 and 1000000.When m is 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 5.The output for each dataset consists of one line, consisting of two integers separated by a space.These represent how much the coordinates of the constellation you want to search need to be translated in parallel to get the coordinates in the photo.The first integer is the amount of translation in the x direction, and the second integer is the amount of translation in the y direction.The first input/output example corresponds to the diagram above.

At IOI Confectionery, we have been baking rice crackers using the traditional method since our founding.This traditional manufacturing method involves grilling the front side over a charcoal fire for a certain period of time, then turning it over once the front side is cooked and grilling the other side over a charcoal fire for a certain period of time.While preserving this tradition, the rice crackers are baked by machine.This machine bakes rice crackers arranged in a rectangular shape with R (1 ≤ R ≤ 10) rows and C (1 ≤ C ≤ 10000) columns horizontally.Normally, it runs automatically, and once the front side is cooked, the rice crackers are all turned over and the other side is cooked.One day, while baking rice crackers, an earthquake occurred just before the rice crackers were to be turned over, and some of the rice crackers were turned over.Fortunately, the conditions of the charcoal fire remained appropriate, but if the front side were to be roasted any longer, the baking time stipulated by tradition since the company's founding would be exceeded, and the front side of the rice cracker would be too burnt to be shipped as a product.Therefore, I quickly changed the machine to manual operation and tried to flip only the rice crackers that had not yet been flipped.This machine can flip several horizontal rows at the same time or several vertical columns at the same time, but unfortunately it cannot flip each rice cracker one by one.If it takes a long time to turn the rice crackers, the front side of the rice cracker that was not turned over during the earthquake will become too burnt and cannot be shipped as a product, so we decided to turn several horizontal rows once at the same time, and then turn several vertical rows once at the same time to increase the number of rice crackers that can be baked on both sides without overcooking the front side, that is, ``shippable rice crackers'' as much as possible.Let's also consider cases where no horizontal rows are flipped, or no vertical columns are flipped.Write a program that outputs the maximum number of rice crackers that can be shipped.Suppose that immediately after an earthquake, rice crackers are in the state shown in the following figure.The black circle represents the state where the front side is burnt, and the white circle represents the state where the back side is burnt.If you flip the first row over, it will look like the following figure.Furthermore, if you flip the 1st and 5th columns over, it will look like the following figure.In this state, only nine rice crackers can be shipped.Note that R's upper limit of 10 is smaller than C's upper limit of 10000.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line of input contains two integers R and C (1 ≤ R ≤ 10, 1 ≤ C ≤ 10 000) separated by a space.The following row R represents the state of rice crackers immediately after the earthquake.In the (i+1)th row (1 ≤ i ≤ R), C integers a i,1 , a i,2 , ..., a i,C are written separated by spaces, and a i,j represents the state of the rice cracker in the i row and j column. When a i,j is 1, it means that the front side is baked, and when it is 0, it means that the back side is baked.When both C and R are 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output the maximum number of rice crackers that can be shipped in one line.

There are n islands in the JOI country, and each island is numbered from 1 to n.Currently, the JOI countries are progressing with the development of a sea route network that connects each island.You work at a ticket center that handles ship tickets.There are many people in JOI countries who want to travel between islands as cheaply as possible by boat, and they will fill out an order form with their starting point and destination and send it to you.Your job is, as soon as you receive an order form from a customer, to calculate the cheapest fare on the route between the departure point and the destination by connecting several ships, and then convey it to the customer.However, depending on the itinerary, it may not be possible to travel by ship.In that case, it is necessary to tell the customer that they cannot travel by ship.Also, in JOI countries, new ships connecting between islands are starting to operate one after another, and you will be informed of this information from time to time.When responding to customers, you must keep the latest information in mind.Create a program that requests a reply to the customer when the customer's order form and the operation information of a newly launched ship are given as input.The execution status for input example 1 and output example 1 is shown in Figure 1.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.Two integers n and k (1 ≤ n ≤ 100, 1 ≤ k ≤ 5000) are written in the first line of input.This means that the number of islands is n islands and the input consists of k + 1 rows.On the i + 1st line (1 ≤ i ≤ k), three or four integers are written separated by spaces.In the first stage, it is assumed that no ships are in operation.Of the input, the number of lines representing ship operation information is 1000 or less.Also, note that multiple ships may operate between islands.When both n and k are 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 5.Output each dataset in the following format.Let m be the number of lines representing order forms in the input.The output of each dataset consists of m lines, and in the i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ m), write an integer representing the reply to the i-th order form.In other words, if it is possible to travel between the departure point and destination of the i-th order form by connecting several ships, write the minimum value of the total fare.If it is impossible to travel, output -1.

A game is played by arranging white and black Go stones on the table. First, place a Go stone on the left edge of the table. Next, place a Go stone in the second place from the left. Repeat this n times to line up the n Go stones in a row horizontally. However, when placing a new i-th Go stone, replace the Go stones on the table according to the following rules. For example, when the first 7 Go stones are placed,Assume that ○○●●○○○ (○ represents a white Go stone, ● represents a black Go stone.) Given the order of Go stones placed as input, write a program that calculates the number of white Go stones placed on the table after arranging n Go stones. The input consists of multiple data sets.Each dataset is given in the following format.In the first line, a positive integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 100000) is written. In the i + 1st line (1 ≤ i ≤ n) from the second line onward, an integer c i representing the color of the i-th Go stone is written. If c i is 0, it means that the color of the i-th Go stone is white, and if c i is 1, it means iIndicates that the color of the Go stone placed in the second place is black. Of the scoring data, 50% of the allocated points satisfies n ≤ 10000. When n is 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of data sets does not exceed 10.For each data set, after arranging n Go stones, the number of white Go stones placed on the table is output in one line.

Given two strings, write a program that finds the longest string contained in both strings and returns its length. Here, the string s is included in the string t if s appears consecutively in t. An empty string, that is, a string with a length of 0, is included in any string. For example, the string ABRACADABRAcontains the following strings: ABRA, RAC, D, ACADABRA, ABRACADABRA, the empty string, etc. On the other hand, the string ABRACADABRA does not contain the following strings: ABRC, RAA,BA, K, etc. Example 1: Given the strings ABRACADABRA and ECADADABRBCRDARA, both contain strings CA, CADA, ADABRThe longest of these is ADABR, and its length is 5. The position of ADABR included in the two strings is shown below. ABRAC ADABR A ECAD ADABR BCRDARA Example 2: When UPWJCIRUCAXIIRGL and SBQNYBSBZDFNEV are given as strings,The only strings included in both are empty strings and their length is 0. The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The input consists of two lines, with the first string given on the first line and the second string given on the second line. The strings consist of uppercase English letters, and the length of each string is between 1 and 4000. For 30% of the scoring data, the length of each string is between 1 and 50. The end of input is indicated by EOF.The number of datasets does not exceed 10.Outputs the length of the longest string contained in both of the two strings given for each dataset on one line.

You are to play a darts game with the following rules. You can throw up to four arrows at a target. You don't necessarily have to throw all four arrows, or even one. The target is divided into N parts, and each part has a score P 1 ,..., P N written on it. The sum of the points S where the arrows stick is the basis of your score.SIf S is less than a predetermined score M, your score will be S. However, if S exceeds M, your score will be 0. Write a program that finds the maximum score you can get, given the score written in and the value of M. The input consists of multiple data sets.Each dataset is given in the following format.In the first line, the integers N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000) and M (1 ≤ M ≤ 200000000 = 2 × 10 8 ) are written in this order separated by spaces. In the 1 + i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ N ) from the second line onwards, P i (1 ≤ P i ≤ 100000000 = 10 8) is written. Of the scoring data, 20% of the allocated points satisfy N ≤ 100, and 50% of the allocated points satisfy N ≤ 300. When both N and M are 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 10.Outputs the maximum number of points you can get for each data set on one line. In the first example, if you get 3 arrows in the 15 point area and 1 arrow in the 3 point area, your score will be maximum, which is 48 points. In the second example, you will get 1 arrow in the 16 point area and 2 arrows in the 2 point area.If the book arrow hits you, your score will be maximum, which is 20 points.

A somewhat dangerous game is being played in a river where you jump from one bank to the other by jumping over stones one after another to get to the other bank. Now, consider that the stones are located on a grid, as shown in Figure 4-1. The number of rows is n. In Figure 4-1, n = 5. In this game, you start from one bank and make a normal jump or m.Cross over to the opposite bank by jumping in single rows of less than 3 times. A normal jump is jumping to any of the banks or stones in the row one row ahead of the row you are currently on, and a single jump is jumping to any of the banks or stones in the row two rows ahead of the row you are currently on.Assume that the next row ahead is the opposite shore. Now, in order to complete this game as safely as possible, I decided to consider the risk of jumping. Each stone has a fixed slipperiness. The risk of jumping when jumping from stone to stone, whether it is a normal jump or a single-line jump, is (slippery of the current stone + slippery of the stone to which you are jumping) x (horizontal distance traveled)However, the lateral movement distance is the difference in column numbers.Also, assume that the risk of jumping from one bank to another, or from one stone to another, is 0. Given n, m, and the position and slipperiness of each stone as input, write a program that finds the minimum sum of the jump risks when reaching the opposite bank.There are no more than 2. The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.In the first input line, two integers n and m are written separated by a blank space. These represent the number of lines and the number of allowed jumps to skip one line. n and m satisfy 2 ≤ n ≤ 150, 0 ≤ m ≤ (n+1)/2, respectively. The following n lines contain information about the stones in each line. Line i+1 (1 ≤ i≤ n) contains one integer k i (0 ≤ k i ≤ 10) followed by 2 × ki integers separated by spaces. These represent information about the stones in the i-th row counting from the starting bank. ki represents the number of stones in that row, and for the following 2 × ki integers, 2 × j - 1st (1 ≤ j ≤ k i )The integer x i,j represents the row number of the j-th stone in that row, and the 2 × j-th integer d i,j represents the slipperiness of the j-th stone in that row.and for another 20% of the points, m = 0. When n and m are both 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of data sets does not exceed 10.For each dataset, print one integer per line that represents the minimum total jump risk to reach the opposite bank. In Figure 4-2, let the numbers written on the stones represent the slipperiness of each stone. When crossing the stones in the order indicated by the arrows, the risk of each jump is 0, (2 + 2) × 1 = 4, (2 + 1) × 1 =3,(1 + 4) × 2 = 10,0, and the total is 17. In this case, the total jump risk is the minimum. This example corresponds to the first input/output example.

To promote the Information Olympics, I would like to create a signboard by painting a rectangular plywood board. Several rectangular pieces of masking tape have been pasted on the plywood board in the areas that I do not want to paint. Therefore, I decided to paint each area separated by masking tape using a different color. For example, in the case of Figure 5-1, I used five colors of paint.Write a program that calculates the number of paint colors to use, given the location of masking tape as input. However, the entire plywood board is not covered with masking tape, and all masking tape sides are parallel to one of the sides of the plywood board. The input consists of multiple data sets.Each dataset is given in the following format.On the first line, the width w (an integer that satisfies 1 ≤ w ≤ 1000000) and the height h (an integer that satisfies 1 ≤ h ≤ 1000000) of the plywood are written in this order, separated by spaces.The second line lists the number of masking tapes n (an integer that satisfies 1 ≤ n ≤ 1000).In the 2 + i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ n), the lower left coordinates (x 1 , y 1 ) and upper right coordinates (x 2 , y 2 ) of the i-th masking tape are x 1 , y 1 , x 2 , y 2 (0 ≤ x 1 < x 2 ≤ w, 0 ≤ y 1 < y 2 ≤ hHowever, the coordinates of the lower left corner of the plywood board are (0, 0) and the coordinates of the upper right corner are (w, h). Of the scoring data, 30% of the allocated points satisfy w ≤ 100, h ≤ 100, n ≤ 100. When h and w are both 0, it indicates the end of input.The number of datasets does not exceed 20.Outputs the number of paint colors used for each dataset in one line. The following example is for Figure 5-1.

JOI Shoji manages employees' working hours using time cards.When employees come to work, they use a special device to stamp their arrival time on their time cards.When you finish your shift and leave the office, stamp your leaving time on your time card.Time is handled using a 24-hour system.For security reasons, employees are required to come to work after 7:00.Additionally, all employees must leave the office before 11pm.Employees' leaving time is always later than their starting time.Given the arrival and leaving times of three JOI Shoji employees A, B, and C as input, write a program that calculates each employee's time at the office.The input consists of three lines.The first line shows Mr. A's arrival and leaving times, the second line shows Mr. B's arrival and leaving times, and the third line shows Mr. C's arrival and leaving times, separated by spaces.The time is written as three integers, each separated by a space.The three integers h, m, and s represent h hours, m minutes, and s seconds.7 ≤ h ≤ 22, 0 ≤ m ≤ 59, 0 ≤ s ≤ 59.Output Mr. A's time at the office on the first line, Mr. B's time at the office on the second line, and Mr. C's time at the office on the third line.If the office hours to be output are h hours, m minutes, and s seconds, output h, m, s in the order separated by spaces.

An online programming contest was held the other day.The computer clubs of W University and K University have been rivals for some time, and it was decided that this contest would be used to determine the superiority of the two.This time, 10 people each from these two universities participated in this contest.After a long discussion, it was decided that out of the 10 participants, the scores of the three with the highest scores were combined and used as the university's score.Data on the scores of participants from W University and K University are given.At this time, create a program to calculate the scores of each university.The input consists of 20 lines.Lines 1 to 10 contain integers representing the scores of each participant from University W, and lines 11 to 20 contain integers representing the scores of each participant from University K.All of these integers are between 0 and 100.Output the scores of University W and University K in this order, separated by spaces.

There are games like the following.N characters are lined up in a vertical column.The color of these characters is red, blue, or yellow, and in the initial state there are no more than four characters of the same color in a row in a row.The player can select a character at a certain position and change it to another color.If four or more characters of the same color line up in a row using this operation, those characters will disappear.If a character disappears and four or more characters of the same color line up in a row, those characters will also disappear, and this chain will continue until there are no longer any places where four or more characters of the same color are lined up in a row.The goal of this game is to minimize the number of characters remaining without disappearing.For example, in the leftmost state of the figure below, if you change the color of the sixth character from the top from yellow to blue, five consecutive blue characters will disappear, and in the end three characters will remain without disappearing.Given the color arrangement of N characters in the initial state, write a program that finds the minimum number M of characters remaining without disappearing when the color of the characters is changed in just one place.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line consists of only the number of characters N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10000).In the following N lines, one integer of 1, 2, or 3 is written, and the i + 1st line (1 ≤ i ≤ N) represents the color of the i-th character from the top in the initial state (1 represents red, 2 represents blue, and 3 represents yellow).When N is 0, it indicates the end of input.The number of datasets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output the minimum number M of characters remaining without disappearing on one line.

One cold winter day, JOI Taro decided to play by breaking the thin ice in the square.The plaza is rectangular and divided into m x n sections, m in the east-west direction and n in the north-south direction.Also, there are sections with thin ice and sections without.JOI Taro decided to move between sections while breaking the thin ice according to the following rules.Write a program to find the maximum number of sections that JOI Taro can move through while breaking thin ice.However, 1 ≤ m ≤ 90, 1 ≤ n ≤ 90.Given the input data, there are no more than 200,000 ways to move.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.There are n+2 lines of input.The first line contains an integer m.The second line contains an integer n.In each line from the 3rd line to the n+2th line, m numbers of 0 or 1 are written, separated by spaces, indicating whether or not there is thin ice in each section.If we describe the i-th section from the north and the j-th section from the west as (i,j) (1 ≤ i ≤ n, 1 ≤ j ≤ m), the j-th value in the i+2th row will be 1 if there is thin ice in section (i,j), and 0 if there is no thin ice in section (i,j).When m and n are both 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output the maximum number of sections that can be moved in one line.Example of a movement method that gives the maximum value for the first input example Example of a movement method that gives the maximum value for the second input example Example of a movement method that does not give the maximum value for the second input example

There are n cards with numbers from 1 to n written on them.First, make a pile of cards by stacking them in order such that the top card is number 1, the second card from the top is number 2, etc., and the bottom card is number n.Sort the cards by performing the following operation called "shuffle (x, y)" on the pile of cards (x, y are integers satisfying 1 ≤ x < y < n).Shuffle(x,y) Divide n cards into three piles: pile A consisting of the xth cards from the top, pile B consisting of the x+1st to yth cards, and pile C consisting of the y+1st to nth cards.Then, stack mountain B on top of mountain A, and then pile mountain C on top of that.For example, if you perform "shuffle (3,5)" on nine cards that are lined up in order, the numbers written on the nine cards will be 6, 7, 8, 9, 4, 5, 1, 2, 3 from the top.Write a program that calculates the number of cards with numbers r or less among the p-th to q-th cards counting from the top in the pile of cards after m-times of shuffling ``shuffle (x 1 , y 1 ),'' ``shuffle (x 2 , y 2 ),'' and ``shuffle (x m , y m )'' from the initial pile.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The input consists of m+3 lines.The number of cards, n, is written in the first line (3 ≤ n ≤ 1000000000 = 10 9 ).The second line contains an integer m that represents the number of shuffles (1 ≤ m ≤ 5000).The third line contains integers p, q, and r (1 ≤ p ≤ q ≤ n, 1 ≤ r ≤ n).On the i + 3rd line (1 ≤ i ≤ m), two integers x i , y i (1 ≤ x i < y i < n) are written separated by a space.When n is 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 5.For each data set, print the number of cards with numbers less than or equal to r among the p-th to q-th cards counting from the top in the pile of cards after m shuffling.If you perform "shuffle (3,5)" on the pile in the first input example, the cards will be 6, 7, 8, 9, 4, 5, 1, 2, 3 in order from the top.The 3rd to 7th cards counting from the top with numbers 4 or less are number 4 and number 1.Also, if you perform "Shuffle (3, 8)", "Shuffle (2, 5)", and "Shuffle (6, 10)" in order for the pile in the second input example, the cards will be 9, 10, 3, 11, 12, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 1, 2 from the top.There are 3 cards with numbers 5 or less among the 3rd to 8th cards counting from the top.

In some programming contests, there is a tradition of playing a game of bingo at a social gathering after the competition.However, the bingo cards used in this bingo game are somewhat special and are created according to the following conditions.Below is an example of a bingo card when N = 5, M = 50, and S = 685.I would like to make as many bingo cards as possible that meet the above conditions for a social gathering.However, do not make more than one identical card.Write a program that outputs the remainder when the maximum number of bingo cards that can be made is divided by 100000.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The input consists of one line, in which three positive integers are written separated by spaces, representing the size of the bingo card N (1≤N≤7), the upper limit of integers written in the grid M (1≤M≤2000), and the total number of integers written on the bingo card S (1≤S≤3000).However, given any input data, it is possible to create one or more bingo cards that satisfy the conditions.When N, M, and S are 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, divide the maximum number of bingo cards that can be made by 100000 and output the remainder on one line.For the third input example, the maximum number of bingo cards that can be made is 642499974501, so it outputs the remainder of 74501 when divided by 100000.

For an integer n (1 ≤ n), let P n be a string formed by alternating n + 1 I's and n O's starting from I. Here, I and O are the uppercase English letters I and O, respectively. Given an integer n and a string s consisting of only I and O, write a program that outputs the number of places P n is included in s. nWhen is 1 and s is OOIOIOIOIIOII, P 1 is IOI and is included in the four locations shown in Figure 1-2 below. Therefore, the output is 4. When n is 2 and s is OOIOIOIOIIOII, P 2 is IOIOI and is included in the four locations shown in Figure 1-3 below.It is included in 2 locations. Therefore, the output is 2. The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.In the first line, an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 1000000) is written. In the second line, an integer m (1 ≤ m ≤ 1000000) is written. m represents the number of characters in s. In the third line, a string s is written. s consists only of I and O. In all grading data, 2 n +1 ≤ m. Of the scoring data, 50% of the allocated points satisfies n ≤ 100, m ≤ 10000. When n is 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of data sets does not exceed 10.For each dataset, output one integer per line that indicates how many places the string P n is included in the string s. If s does not include P n, output 0 as an integer.

JOI Pizza sells pizza by home delivery along the ring line with a total length of d meters that passes through the center of the city. JOI Pizza has n stores S 1 , ... , S n on the ring line. The main store is S 1. Let d i meters be the distance traveled clockwise from S 1 to S i on the ring line. d 2 , ... ,d n is an integer between 1 and d - 1. d 2 , ... , d n are all different. When a pizza is ordered, the pizza is baked and delivered at the store with the shortest travel distance to the delivery destination so that the pizza does not get cold. The location of the delivery destination is expressed as an integer k between 0 and d - 1. This is the main store S 1This means that the distance traveled clockwise along the ring line from the point to the delivery destination is k meters. Pizza delivery takes place along the ring line, and no other route is allowed. However, you may move clockwise or counterclockwise on the ring line. For example, if the location of the store and the location of the delivery destination are as shown in the figure below (this example corresponds to Example 1 of "Input/Output Example"),The store closest to delivery destination 1 is S 2, so delivery is made from store S 2. In this case, the travel distance from the store is 1. Also, the store closest to delivery destination 2 is S 1 (main store), so delivery is made from store S 1 (main store). In this case, the travel distance from the store is 2. Total length of the loop line d , JOIGiven n the number of pizza stores, m the number of orders, n - 1 integers d 2 , ... , d n representing locations other than the main store, and integers k 1 , ... , k m representing the locations of delivery destinations, write a program that calculates the sum of the travel distance for each order (i.e., the distance from the nearest store to the delivery destination) over all orders.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line is a positive integer d (2 ≤ d ≤ 1000000000 = 10 9 ), the second line is a positive integer n (2 ≤ n ≤ 100000), and the third line is a positive integer m (1 ≤ m ≤ 10000), which is the number of orders. 4In the n - 1 lines after the nth line, integers d 2 , d 3 , ... , d n (1 ≤ d i ≤ d - 1) representing the locations of stores other than the main store are written in this order, and in the m lines after the n + 3 line, integers k 1 , k 2 , ... , k m (0 ≤ k i ≤ d - 1) representing the locations of the delivery destinations.are written in this order. For 40% of the scoring data, n ≤ 10000 is satisfied. Also, for 40% of the scoring, the total distance traveled and the value of d are both less than 1000000. Furthermore, for all the scoring data, the total distance traveled is 1000000000 = 10 9The following is true. When d is 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 10.For each dataset, one integer representing the total distance traveled during delivery is output on one line.

You are playing an Amida lottery with J. The Amida lottery consists of n vertical bars and m horizontal bars.The vertical bars are numbered from 1 to n from the left, and a positive integer s i is written at the bottom of the vertical bar i.The integer written at the bottom of the vertical bar i as you follow the path in order from the top is the vertical bar i.For example, in Figure 3-1, if you select vertical bar 1, you will get a score of 80 points, and if you choose vertical bar 2, you will get a score of 100 points.J decides to select k consecutive bars from bar 1 to bar k.J's score is the sum of the points you get when you select those k vertical bars.You can select one horizontal bar and delete it from the Amidakuji. (You do not have to delete it.) If you delete one horizontal bar, in the Amidakuji after the deletion, J's score will be the sum of the points when he selects k consecutive vertical bars from vertical bar 1 to vertical bar k. As input, the shape of the Amidakuji and the number of vertical bars that J chooses k.Write a program that finds the minimum score for Mr. J, given . The input consists of multiple data sets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first line contains four integers n , m , h , k separated by spaces. n (2 ≤ n ≤ 1000) is the number of vertical bars, m (1 ≤ m ≤ 100000) is the number of horizontal bars, h (2 ≤ h ≤ 1000) is the length of the vertical bars, and k (1 ≤ k ≤ n) is JIt represents the number of vertical bars you choose. The next n lines contain the points written at the bottom of the vertical bars. The i + 1st line (1 ≤ i ≤ n) contains a positive integer s i. Also, s 1 + s 2 + ... + s n ≤ 2000000000 = 2 × 10 9 is satisfied. Continued mThe positions of the horizontal bars are written in the rows.The horizontal bars are numbered from 1 to m.In the i + n + 1st line (1 ≤ i ≤ m), two integers a i , b i (1 ≤ a i ≤ n - 1, 1 ≤ b i ≤ h - 1) representing the position of the horizontal bar i are written, separated by a space, and the horizontal bar i is connected to the vertical bar a i and the vertical bar.Connect a i + 1 to represent that the distance from the top of horizontal bar i is b i.However, no two horizontal bars share an end point.Of the scoring data, if the horizontal bar is not deleted, 20% of the points will be the lowest.In addition, 30% of the points will be n ≤ 20, m ≤ 30, h ≤ 10.and 60% of the points satisfy m ≤ 1000. The end of the input is indicated by a line containing four zeros. The number of data sets does not exceed 10.For each dataset, the minimum score of J is output in one line. The first example corresponds to Figure 3-1, and when you delete horizontal bar 4 (the horizontal bar connecting vertical bar 1 and vertical bar 2 at a distance of 5 from the top), J's score is minimized. In example 2, J's score is minimized if you do not delete the horizontal bar. (Figure 3-3)(See.)

One day, Taro, who lives in JOI town, decided to make walking a part of his daily routine to improve his health. As shown in the diagram, Taro's house in JOI town has a grid of roads running east-west (H + 1) and roads running north-south (W + 1).Taro's house is at the northwestmost intersection, and he starts his walk from there.Hereafter, he starts from the north.The a-th and b-th intersections from the west are represented by ( a , b ). For example, the intersection where Taro's house is located is (1, 1). Taro thought it would be more interesting to have a different walking route every day, so he decided to write the characters ``East'' or ``South'' at H × W intersections from (1, 1) to ( H , W ) and take a walk every day according to the following rules.After thinking about this plan, Taro becomes curious about the route he will take on future walks.Write a program for Taro to predict the route of Taro's Nth walk.The input consists of multiple data sets.Each dataset is given in the following format.In the first line, three positive integers are written separated by spaces. These are the values of the three numbers H , W , and N in the problem statement. H , W , and N satisfy 1 ≤ H ≤ 1000, 1 ≤ W ≤ 1000, 1 ≤ N ≤ 10000000 = 10 7, respectively.From the second line, H+ In the first line, W integers are written separated by spaces. These represent information about the first character that Taro wrote at the intersection. If the j-th integer on the i + 1st line is 0, it means that the character written at the intersection ( i , j ) is "Minami", and if it is 1, it means that the character written at the intersection ( i , j ) is "East".For 30%, H ≤ 100, W ≤ 100, N ≤ 1000 are satisfied. When H , W , and N are all 0, it indicates the end of input. The number of data sets does not exceed 10.Output each data set in the following format. If the intersection where Taro ends his Nth walk is ( i , j ), output i and j in this order, separated by a space. In the above example, the route for the first to third walks will be as shown in the figure below.

Have you heard of Just Odd Inventions? The business of this company is to make ``just odd inventions.'' We'll call it JOI for short. JOI has two offices, each consisting of a grid of square rooms of the same size. Between all the rooms that meet on the sides, there is a door with an ID card authentication function. JOISince the company handles various levels of confidential information, a positive integer called the security level is set for each room, and unless the non-negative integer authentication level set for each office on the ID card is at least the security level, you cannot enter that room.Each office has only one entrance and exit through an elevator hall, and the room in the elevator hall has the lowest security level of 1.If the authentication level for that office is 0, you cannot even enter the elevator hall.JOIAt the company president's sudden proposal, the company has decided to conduct a tour for the general public to tour the company.You need to decide on the combination of authentication levels for the IDs to be given to the visitors.When the visitors find a door that can be opened, they must open it and enter (it is possible to visit the same room many times).Therefore, we do not want to make the authentication level for the visitors' IDs any higher than necessary.However, in order to make the tour more attractive, we will include the rooms in the elevator hall on the tour.If the authentication level of the ID card is set too low, this condition may not be met. JOI has two offices, and the kth office (k = 1, 2) has W k rooms in the east-west direction and H k rooms in the north-south direction, for a total of W k × H k rooms.Given the values of W k , H k , and R , the location of the elevator hall (How the company makes money by making ``just weird inventions'' is a top secret within the company, and no one except the company president knows about it.The input consists of multiple data sets.Each dataset is given in the following format.On the first line, a positive integer R (1 ≤ R ≤ 100000) is written. From the second line onwards, data for two offices are given in order. The office data is written as positive integers W k , H k , X k , Y k (1 ≤ X k ≤ W k ≤ 500, 1 ≤ Y k ≤ H k ≤ 500), followed by H kIn the i-th row of the j-th row, it is given as an integer L k, i,j (1 ≤ L k,i,j < 100000000 = 10 8 ) representing the security level of room ( i , j ) k. Also, R ≤ W 1 × H 1 + W 2 × H 2 is satisfied. Of the scoring data, for 30% of the points allocated, R , W k , H k≤ 100. R = 0 indicates the end of input. The number of datasets does not exceed 10.For each dataset, output the minimum value of the sum of the authentication levels of the ID cards to be requested in one line. In the first example, if the authentication level of the ID cards given to the visitor is set so that the authentication level of office 1 is 9 and the authentication level of office 2 is 6, the visitor will be able to access 5 rooms (rooms of office 1 (1, 1) 1 , (1, 2) 1 , (2, 1) 1and the rooms (1, 1) 2 , (2, 1) 2 ) of office 2. In this case, the sum of the authentication levels is 15. This is the minimum sum of authentication levels to be able to visit a total of 5 or more rooms. In the third example, the authentication level of the ID card given to the visitor is set to 9 for office 1 and 0 for office 2.If the settings are set so thatYou cannot visit any of the rooms in office 2. (You cannot even enter elevator hall (1, 1) 2.) In this case, the sum of the authentication levels is 9. This is the minimum value of the sum of the authentication levels to be able to visit a total of 6 or more rooms.

Taro bought 10 books.Later, I tried to find out the price based on the receipt, but the receipt was dirty and I couldn't read the price of a certain book.I decided to calculate the price of the book from the total price of the 10 books and the prices of the other 9 books.Write a program that outputs the price of a book whose price could not be read.Note that all book prices are positive integers.Also, there is no need to consider consumption tax.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.Each dataset consists of 10 rows, one positive integer per row.The integer on the first line represents the total price of the 10 books, and the integers on the second to tenth lines represent the read price.The total cost of the 10 books is less than 10,000 yen.When the total amount is 0, it indicates the end of input.The number of data sets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output the price of the book whose price could not be read in one line.

JOI is playing Goroku by himself.This Sugoroku has N squares in a straight line, each with movement instructions written on it.The starting point is the 1st square, and the goal is the Nth square.JOI repeats the following until he reaches the goal.Roll the dice, move forward from the current square by the number of numbers rolled, and follow the instructions on that square.Do not follow the instructions of the square you moved to.The goal is not only when the object stops exactly at the Nth square, but also when the destination exceeds the Nth square.Given a Sugoroku board and M dice rolls, write a program that outputs the number of times the dice must be rolled to reach the goal.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.Each dataset consists of 1+N+M rows.In the first line of the input, two integers N and M (2 ≤ N ≤ 1000, 1 ≤ M ≤ 1000) are written separated by a space.N represents the number of Sugoroku squares, and M represents the number of dice given.In the following N lines, one integer between -999 and 999 is written.The integer in the 1+ith row (1 ≤ i ≤ N) represents the indication of the i-th square of Sugoroku.Let the written integer be X.When X=0, it indicates "do nothing," when X>0, it indicates "go forward X squares," and when X<0, it indicates "go back |X| squares."However, |X| represents the absolute value of X.In the following M lines, one integer between 1 and 6 is written, and the number on the 1+N+j line (1 ≤ j ≤ M) represents the j-th roll of the dice.However, the numbers in the 2nd line and 1+Nth line are always 0.There are no squares with instructions to move them to a square before the first square.In addition, for any input data for scoring, the goal can be achieved by rolling the dice less than or equal to M times.When both N and M are 0, it indicates the end of input.The number of data sets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output an integer on one line that indicates how many times the dice must be rolled to reach the goal.The following figure shows the first input example.The following figure shows the second input example.

You decide to invite your friends at school and your friends' friends to your Christmas party.There are n students in the school you attend, and each student is assigned a number from 1 to n.Your number is 1.You have a list of who is friends with whom.Based on this list, write a program to find the number of students you will invite to your Christmas party.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.The first row of the dataset contains the number of students in the school n (2 ≤ n ≤ 500), and the second row contains the list length m (1 ≤ m ≤ 10000).The input consists of 2+m lines in total.In the 2+ith line (1 ≤ i ≤ m), two integers a i and b i (1 ≤ a i < b i ≤ n) are written separated by a space, indicating that the students with numbers a i and b i are friends.From the 3rd line to the 2+mth line, lines expressing the same friendship relationship do not appear twice.When both n and m are 0, it indicates the end of input.The number of data sets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, print the number of students you invite to the Christmas party on one line.In the first input example, your two friends are students numbered 2 and number 3.Also, students numbered 3 and 4 are friends, so student number 4 is your friend's friend.Students numbered 5 and 6 are neither your friends nor your friends' friends.Therefore, you invite three students numbered 2, 3, and 4 to your Christmas party.In the second input example, you have no friends.Therefore, the number of students you invite to the Christmas party is 0.

Hanako is playing with n cards (4 ≤ n ≤ 10).Each card has an integer between 1 and 99 written on it.Hanako chooses k cards (2 ≤ k ≤ 4) from these cards and arranges them in a horizontal line to form an integer.How many kinds of integers can Hanako create in total?For example, suppose you are given five cards 1, 2, 3, 13, and 21, and you want to choose three cards to make an integer.By arranging 2, 1, and 13 in this order, we can create the integer 2113.You can also create the same integer 2113 by arranging 21, 1, and 3 in this order.In this way, the same integer can be created from different combinations of cards.Given the integers written on n cards, write a program that finds the number of integers that can be made by selecting k cards from among them and arranging them in a horizontal line.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.Each dataset consists of 2+n rows.The first line indicates the number of cards n (4 ≤ n ≤ 10), and the second line indicates the number k (2 ≤ k ≤ 4) of cards to be selected.In the 2+i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ n), the integer between 1 and 99 written on the i-th card is written.When n and k are both 0, it indicates the end of input.The number of data sets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output the number of integers that Hanako can create in one line.In the first input example, by selecting two cards from among the four cards 1, 2, 12, and 1 and arranging them in a row horizontally, there are seven integers that can be created: 11, 12, 21, 112, 121, 122, and 212.

A Canadian city where JOI lives is divided into a grid pattern with w roads running straight north-south and h roads running straight east-west.The w roads in the north-south direction are numbered 1, 2, ..., w in order from the west.Also, the h roads in the east-west direction are numbered 1, 2, ..., h in order from the south.The intersection where the i-th north-south road from the west intersects with the j-th east-west road from the south is represented by (i, j).JOI lives near the intersection (1, 1) and commutes to work near the intersection (w, h) by car.Cars can only move along roads.Mr. JOI commutes only to the east or north in order to shorten his commuting time.The city also has the following traffic rules in place to reduce traffic accidents: After turning at an intersection, you are not allowed to proceed one block and then turn again.In this case, how many different commuting routes can JOI think of?Given w and h, write a program that outputs the remainder of dividing the number of JOI's commuting routes by 100000.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset consists of one line containing two integers w, h (2 ≤ w ≤ 100, 2 ≤ h ≤ 100) separated by a space.w represents the number of roads in the north-south direction, and h represents the number of roads in the east-west direction.When w and h are both 0, it indicates the end of input.The number of data sets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, divide the number of JOI's commuting routes by 100000 and output the remainder in one line.JOI's commuting route in the first input example (5 ways) In the first input example, there are 5 possible commuting routes for JOI as shown in the figure.Therefore, it outputs 5.In the second input example, there are 143688 possible commuting routes for JOI.Therefore, it outputs 43688, which is the remainder when 143688 is divided by 100000.

Santa Claus came to JOI town from the sky again this year.Every house in JOI town has a child, so this Santa Claus has to distribute presents to every house in JOI town.However, this year, the reindeer I'm with has a bit of a lack of sense of direction, and I can't get down anywhere other than on top of buildings, so I think I'll have to devise a slightly better route in order to distribute presents to all the homes.JOI town is organized into north, south, east, and west sections, and each section is either a house, a church, or a vacant lot.There is only one church in JOI town.According to the following rules, Santa Claus and his reindeer depart from the church and return to the church after distributing presents to every house once.Given the structure of a town as input, write a program that finds the number of routes Santa Claus and his reindeer can take to hand out presents.The input consists of multiple datasets.Each dataset is given in the following format.Each dataset has n+1 rows.The first line contains an integer m (1 ≤ m ≤ 10) and an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 10) separated by a space.In each line from the 2nd line to the n+1th line, m numbers of 0, 1, or 2 are written, separated by spaces, and each represents the status of each partition.If we describe the i-th parcel from the north and the j-th parcel from the west as (i,j) (1 ≤ i ≤ n, 1 ≤ j ≤ m), the value of j-th in the i+1st row will be 0 if parcel (i,j) is a vacant lot, 1 if it is a house, and 2 if it is a church.The number of churches is 1, and the number of houses is between 1 and 23.However, in the input data for scoring, the number of directions to be distributed will not exceed 2,000,000.When m and n are both 0, it indicates the end of input.The number of data sets does not exceed 5.For each dataset, output an integer on one line that represents the number of ways to distribute presents.Figure: All 6 directions for the second input example (the numbers represent the order of distribution)

You are a traveler traveling on the JOI Highway.The JOI Highway is a road that extends straight from east to west, and there are n post towns on the JOI Highway.The post towns are numbered from 1 to n in order from west to east.The westernmost post town on the JOI Highway is post town 1, and the easternmost post town is post town n.You are going on a journey for m days starting from post town 1. Your itinerary is determined as follows according to the sequence a1 , a2 , .This means to travel in a straight line up to 100000 = 10 5 Given the number of post towns n, the number of days of the journey m, information on the distance between post towns, and the number sequence a1 , a2 , .are written separated by spaces. n (2 ≤ n ≤ 100000 = 10 5 ) represents the number of post towns on the JOI road, and m (1 ≤ m ≤ 100000 = 10 5 ) represents the number of days of the journey. The following n − 1 line represents the distance between post towns on the JOI road. i + 1st line (1 ≤ i ≤ n− 1) contains a positive integer si (1 ≤ si ≤ 100) that represents the distance between post town i and post town i + 1. The following m rows contain a numerical sequence representing your travel method for m days. In the i + nth row (1 ≤ i ≤ m), a non-zero integer ai is written that represents your travel method on day i.In the scoring data, you will not move west of post town 1 or east of post town n. For 50% of the points allocated in the scoring data, n ≤ 100 and m ≤ 100 are satisfied. The output contains the remainder of dividing the total distance traveled by you over the m-day journey by 100000 = 10 5 1On the first day, you move from post town 1 to post town 3. On the second day, you move from post town 3 to post town 2. Then, on the third day, you move from post town 2 to post town 5, on the fourth day, you move from post town 5 to post town 7, and on the fifth day, you move from post town 7 to post town 4.The total distance you travel in a 5-day journey is 18.

There is a stick-shaped candy with a length of N millimeters (where N is an even number). Two JOI people decided to cut this candy into multiple pieces, each having a total length of N/2 millimeters.For reasons unknown, the ease of cutting this candy differs depending on the location.The two people examined the candy every 1 millimeter from the left and determined how many seconds it would take to cut at each location.2Write a program that finds the minimum number of seconds it takes for a person to cut the sweets to separate them. The first line of input contains the length N of the stick (2 ≤ N ≤ 10000, where N is an even number). The i + 1st line (1 ≤ i ≤ N − 1) of the input contains an integer ti (1) representing the number of seconds it takes to cut at the i-th millimeter from the left end.≤ ti ≤ 10000). Note that there are N − 1 places that can be cut. For 5% of the scoring data, the minimum value can be achieved by cutting at most two places, and for 10%, the minimum value can be achieved by cutting at most three places. For 20% of the points, N ≤ 20.The output consists of one line containing the minimum number of seconds it would take for two people to cut the candy apart. In this case, cutting at 1 mm and 4 mm from the left edge will take the minimum number of seconds. The number of seconds taken is 1 second and 6 seconds, for a total of 7 seconds.

JOI, who lives in Canada, has beautiful icicles growing under the eaves of his house. JOI, you decided to do some research on icicles. JOI: There are N (2 ≤ N ≤ 100000 = 10 5 ) icicles growing under the eaves of your house. These icicles are lined up in a straight line, starting from the left edge of the eaves at i cm(1 ≤ i ≤ N).The length of the i-th icicle is initially ai cm (ai is an integer greater than or equal to 1).These icicles grow according to the following rules: At the initial stage, the lengths of two adjacent icicles are all different.In this case, if enough time passes, all N icicles will break and the length will become 0 cm.JOIYou want to know how long it takes for the icicles to reach this state. Given the initial length of N icicles and the maximum icicle length L, write a program to find the time it takes for all the icicles to break. In the first line of input, an integer N representing the number of icicles and an integer L representing the maximum icicle length are written in this order, separated by spaces.Input i +The first line (1 ≤ i ≤ N) contains an integer ai (1 ≤ ai < L) that represents the initial length of the i-th icicle. Of the scoring data, 30% of the allocated points satisfy N ≤ 500 and L ≤ 1000. The output contains only one integer representing the time it takes for all icicles to break.It consists of one line. In example 1, the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th icicles break after 2, 8, 4, and 1 hours, respectively. Therefore, the time it takes for all icicles to break is 8 hours, so 8 is output.

A large-scale exposition will be held in JOI City. This expo has two themes, and each of the N exhibition facilities in JOI City is scheduled to exhibit exhibits based on one of the two themes. The location of a facility is represented by plane coordinates (x, y). To move from a facility at location (x, y) to a facility at (x′, y′),It takes the time |x − x′|You can assign themes however you like. Let M be the maximum travel time between two facilities holding exhibitions on the same theme. When the positions of N exhibition facilities are given, write a program that finds the minimum value of M. The first line of input indicates the number of facilities N (3 ≤ N ≤ 100000 = 10 5 ). The i + 1st line of input (1 ≤i ≤ N), two integers x i , y i (|x i | ≤ 100000 = 10 5 , |y i | ≤ 100000 = 10 5 ) representing the coordinates of each facility are written separated by a space. This means that the coordinates of the i-th facility are (x i , y i ). 2 at the same coordinateThere can never be more than one facility. For 40% of the points allocated in the scoring data, N ≤ 2000. The output consists of only one line. Output the minimum value of the maximum travel time M between two facilities exhibiting the same theme. In this case, for example, if one theme is assigned to a facility at coordinates (0, 0), (1, 0), (0, 1), (−1,−2), (−1, 1), the travel time between two facilities exhibiting the same theme will all be less than 3. Since it is not possible to make all travel times less than 2, we will output 3.

You want to get a treasure on the Nth basement floor of a dungeon. Initially, you are on the first basement floor, and your health is H (H is a positive integer). When you go down to the lower floor, your health is consumed. You know in advance how much health is consumed when you go down to the lower floor on each floor. On the other hand, there is one healing spring on each floor, and the spring is 1.The amount of health that can be recovered each time you use it is fixed. If your health falls below 0, you will die. Also, your health will never be higher than H. You can use the spring of recovery as many times as you want, but it takes time to recover, so you want to use the spring as few times as possible.Write a program that finds the minimum number of times the fountain must be used to reach the Nth underground floor without reducing the health to zero, given the amount of health that is recovered after each use. Also, once you go down to the lower floor, you cannot return to the upper floor until you get the treasure. The input consists of N lines. The first line of the input contains two integers N, H(2 ≤ N ≤ 100000 = 10 5, 1 ≤ H ≤ 10000000 = 10 7 ) are written with blanks as delimiters.N represents that there is treasure on the Nth underground floor, and H is the initial health (the physical strength at the stage of arriving at the first basement floor of the dungeon) and the maximum physical strength (the physical strength will not be greater than H due to recovery).The following N − 1 line contains 2In the 1 + ith row (1 ≤ i ≤ N − 1), integers di and hi are written, where di represents the physical strength consumed when descending from basement i floor to basement i + 1 floor, and hi represents the physical strength recovered when using the fountain once on basement i floor (0 ≤ di < H, 1 ≤ hi < H).There is a way to reach the floor. For 10% of the scoring data, N ≤ 1000 and H ≤ 1000 are satisfied. For 30% of the scoring, N ≤ 1000 is satisfied. For 50% of the scoring, the minimum number of times the spring can be used does not exceed 10 6.It consists of one line containing the minimum number of times the fountain is used to reach the floor. Note that in this problem, the range of integers to be handled may not fit within 32 bits.

Taro's daily routine is to visit three stores.After leaving home and visiting three stores in a fixed order, they return home.Occasionally, use a stopwatch to measure how many seconds it takes to travel each section and record the number of seconds.Given the measurement results for a certain day, write a program to find the total number of minutes and seconds of travel time for that day.The input consists of four lines, each line containing one positive integer.The integer in the first line is the number of seconds representing the travel time from the house to the first store.The integer on the second line is the number of seconds representing the travel time from the first store to the second store.The integer on the third line is the number of seconds representing the travel time from the second store to the third store.The integer on the fourth line is the number of seconds representing the travel time from the third store to the house.However, given the input data, the total travel time is guaranteed to be between 1 minute and 0 seconds and 59 minutes and 59 seconds.The output consists of two lines.When x minutes and y seconds (1 ≤ x ≤ 59 , 0 ≤ y ≤ 59 ), print x on the first line and y on the second line.

You have N rings.Each ring is engraved with a string of 10 uppercase letters of the alphabet.The ring is engraved with letters that connect the beginning and end of a string.There is no need to worry about reading the string engraved on the ring in reverse order.Given a string of characters you want to search for, write a program that finds the number of rings that contain that string.The input consists of 2+N lines.On the first line, the character string to be searched for is written, consisting of 1 to 10 uppercase letters of the alphabet.On the second line, the number of rings N (1 ≦ N ≦ 100) is written.The 2+ith line (1 ≤ i ≤ N) contains the 10-character string engraved on the i-th ring.Output an integer representing the number of rings that contain the string you want to search on one line.The ring in input example 2 contains one character string ``XYZ''.This is because the beginning and end of the ring string are connected.In Input Example 3, the first ring contains two strings of ``PQR,'' the second ring contains one string of ``PQR,'' and the third ring does not contain the string ``PQR.''Therefore, the number of rings that contain the string “PQR” is two.

At JOI High School, we created an N x N square mural using 1 x 1 square tiles and will exhibit it at the school festival.There are three colors of tiles: red, blue, and yellow.The design of the mural is as follows.First, paste red tiles on the outermost circle, and paste blue tiles on the inner circle.Furthermore, attach a yellow tile to the inner circumference.Repeat this until the N × N square is filled.The colors of the tiles used are red, blue, yellow, red, blue, yellow, etc. in order from the outermost circle.One day, as the school festival approached, it was discovered that K tiles from the mural were peeling off.So, I decided to purchase new tiles and paste the new tiles in the areas where they were peeled off.Write a program to find the color of the peeled tiles when the length of one side of the mural N, the number of peeled tiles K, and the positions of the K peeled tiles are given as input.For example, if N = 11, the design of an 11 × 11 mural is shown in the figure below.In addition, when N = 16, the design of a 16 × 16 mural is shown in the figure below.The input consists of 2+K lines in total.The first line shows the length of one side of the mural N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000000000 = 10 9 ), and the second line shows the number of peeled tiles K (1 ≤ K ≤ 1000).In the 2+ith row (1 ≤ i ≤ K), two integers ai and bi (1 ≤ ai ≤ N, 1 ≤ bi ≤ N) are written separated by a space, indicating that the i-th peeled tile is the tile in the ai-th column from the left and the bi-th row from the top.There are no duplicate rows representing the same tile from the 3rd row to the 2+K rows of the input.Also, with 40% of the input data given, N ≤ 1000 is satisfied.The output consists of K lines.Each row consists of one integer, and the integer in the i-th row (1 ≤ i ≤K) represents 1 if the i-th peeled tile is red, 2 if it is blue, and 3 if it is yellow.

JOI is in the first grade of elementary school.JOI really likes the addition and subtraction that he just learned, and when he sees numbers lined up, he puts an ``='' between the last two numbers, and always puts a ``+'' or ``-'' between the remaining numbers to create equations.For example, from "8 3 2 4 8 7 2 4 0 8 8", we can create the equation "8+3-2-4+8-7-2-4-0+8=8".JOI After creating an equation, you calculate and check whether it is a correct equation.However, JOI does not yet know negative numbers and cannot calculate numbers over 20.Therefore, among correct equations, only equations in which all numbers that appear in the middle of the calculation when calculating the left side from the left are between 0 and 20 are correct equations that JOI can confirm.For example, "8+3-2-4-8-7+2+4+0+8=8" is a correct equation, but since 8+3-2-4-8 that appears in the middle is a negative number, JOI cannot confirm whether this equation is correct.Given a sequence of numbers as input, write a program that finds the number of correct equations that JOI can create and check.The input file consists of two lines.The first line contains an integer N (3 ≤ N ≤ 100) that represents the number of numbers in a row.On the second line, N integers between 0 and 9 are written, separated by spaces.Given 60% of the input data, the number of correct equations that JOI can create and check does not exceed 2 31 -1.Also, given all the input data, the number of correct equations that JOI can create and check does not exceed 2 63 -1.In one line, output an integer representing the number of correct equations that JOI can create and check.In input example 1, JOI can create and check 10 correct equations, so he outputs 10.Note that in input example 2, the answer does not fit within the range of 32-bit signed integers.

The cheese factory in JOI Town has started producing cheese again this year, and a mouse has come out of its nest.JOI town is organized into north, south, east, and west sections, and each section is either a nest, a cheese factory, an obstacle, or a vacant lot.The mouse leaves its nest, visits all the cheese factories, and eats each piece of cheese.There are N cheese factories in this town, and each factory produces only one type of cheese.The hardness of cheese varies depending on the factory, and there is exactly one cheese factory that produces cheese with hardness from 1 to N.The mouse's initial health is 1, and each time it eats a piece of cheese, its health increases by 1.However, a mouse cannot eat cheese that is harder than its physical strength.A mouse can move to adjacent compartments in the north, south, east, and west in 1 minute, but cannot enter a compartment with an obstacle.You can even walk past a cheese factory without eating any cheese.Write a program to find the shortest time it takes to eat all the cheese.However, the time it takes for the mouse to eat the cheese can be ignored.There are H+1 lines of input.On the first line, three integers H, W, and N (1 ≤ H ≤ 1000, 1 ≤ W ≤ 1000, 1 ≤ N ≤ 9) are written in this order, separated by spaces.In each line from the 2nd line to the H+1th line, a string of W characters consisting of 'S', '1', '2', ..., '9', 'X', and '.' is written, each representing the status of each section.If we write the i-th plot from the north and the j-th plot from the west as (i,j) (1 ≤ i ≤ H, 1 ≤ j ≤ W), the j-th character in the i+1st row will be 'S' if the plot (i,j) is a nest, 'X' if it is an obstacle, '.' if it is a vacant lot, and the hardness is 1, 2, ..., 9.If the factory produces cheese, it will be '1', '2', ..., '9' respectively.The input has one nest and one factory that produces cheese of hardness 1, 2, ..., N.Other squares are guaranteed to be obstacles or open spaces.The mouse is guaranteed to eat all the cheese.Print in one line the integer that represents the minimum amount of time (in minutes) it will take to eat all the cheese.

The Japan Information Olympics Committee has decided to create a flag with the JOI logo as a motif to promote this year's Japan Information Olympics (JOI).The flag must be a ``good flag.''A ``good flag'' is one in which the letters J, O, or I are arranged in a rectangular shape with M rows and N columns, and the flag has at least one place where J, O, and I are lined up as shown in the diagram below (i.e., O is to the right of J, and I is below J).The figure below shows two examples of ``good flags.''The figure below shows two examples of what is not a "good flag".The values ​​of M, N, and the location of a part of the flag have been decided on which letter, J, O, or I, will be used, and this information is given as input.Write a program that calculates how many possible "good flags" there are, divides that number by 100000 (=10 5 ), and outputs the remainder.The input consists of 1 + M lines.The first line contains two integers M and N (2 ≤ M ≤ 20, 2 ≤ N ≤ 20), separated by a space, that represent the size of the flag.In the 1 + i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ M), a string consisting of N characters is written.Each character is one of J, O, I, or ?, and if the jth character (1 ≤ j ≤ N) is one of J, O, or I, it means that the character in the i-th row and j column has been decided to be J, O, or I, respectively, and if it is ?, it means that it has not been decided yet.Divide the number of possible "good flags" by 100000 (=10 5 ) and output the remainder in one line.In input example 1, there are four possible ``good flags'' as shown in the figure below.In input example 4, there are 2428218 possible ``good flags'', so the remainder of dividing it by 100000 (=10 5 ), 28218, is output.

After a long journey, the super-temporal immigrant ship carrying you has finally discovered a planet that appears to be habitable.As the name suggests, the planet named JOI Star is a harsh planet with three types of terrain: ``Jungle,'' ``Ocean,'' and ``Ice.''After a simple survey, a map of the area near the planned residence was created.The proposed residential area has a rectangular shape measuring M km from north to south and N km from east to west, and is divided into 1 km square sections.There are MN partitions in total, and the partition in the pth row from the north and the qth column from the west is represented by ( p , q ).The parcel in the northwest corner is (1, 1), and the parcel in the southeast corner is (M, N).The topography of each section is either ``jungle,'' ``sea,'' or ``ice,'' and is represented by a single letter: J for ``jungle,'' O for ``ocean,'' and I for ``ice.'' Now, in order to create a detailed migration plan, we decided to find out how many sections of ``jungle,'' ``ocean,'' and ``ice'' each are included within the rectangular area of ​​K locations.Given information about the planned residence and the area to be surveyed, write a program that calculates how many sections of ``jungle'', ``sea'', and ``ice'' are included in each area.1 ≤ M ≤ 1000 North-south length of the planned residence (km) 1 ≤ N ≤ 1000 East-west length of the planned residence (km) 1 ≤ K ≤ 100000 Number of areas to be surveyed Read the following input from the standard input.Print K lines representing the results of the investigation to standard output.On the jth line of the output, write three integers representing the number of "Jungle" (J) sections, the number of "Ocean" (O) sections, and the number of "Ice" (I) sections included in the jth survey area in this order, separated by spaces.Of the scoring data, 30% of the allocated points satisfy M ≤ 50 and K ≤ 100.For 50% of the points allocated, M ≤ 50 is satisfied.In this input example, the second region contains 3 sections of "Jungle", 5 sections of "Ocean", and 2 sections of "Ice" as shown in the above figure.

There is a long-established used bookstore in your town called JOI Used Bookstore, and you often use JOI Used Bookstore.Each book has a fixed standard price, and if you go to a JOI used book store, you can buy it at that price.At JOI used bookstore, we classify books into 10 genres such as novels, manga, and magazines.Genres are numbered from 1 to 10.JOI Used Bookstore has a service where you can buy books of the same genre at a high price if you buy them in bulk.Specifically, if you buy T books of the same genre at once, the purchase price for each book in that genre will be T -
