1 yen higher than the standard price.For example, if you sell books in the same genre with standard prices of 100 yen, 120 yen, and 150 yen to JOI used bookstore, the purchase prices will be 102 yen, 122 yen, and 152 yen, respectively.Now, you have decided to move suddenly due to personal circumstances.You have N books, but since it is difficult to take all the books with you to your new home, you decide to sell K out of N books to JOI used bookstore.Given the standard price and genre number of each of N books, write a program to find the maximum total purchase price.2 ≤ N ≤ 2000 Number of books you have 1 ≤ K < N JOI Number of books sold to used bookstores 1 ≤ C i ≤ 100000 = 10 5 Standard price of the i-th book 1 ≤ G i ≤ 10 Genre number of the i-th book Read the following input from the standard input.Output an integer representing the maximum value of the total purchase price on one line to the standard output.Of the scoring data, 20% of the points allocated satisfies N ≤ 20.For 20% of the points allotted, all book genres are 1 or 2.For 10% of the points allocated, both of these two conditions are met.For 30% of the points allocated, at least one of these two conditions is met.In this input example, when the second, fourth, sixth, and seventh books are sold, the purchase price of genre 2 books will increase by 2 yen per book, so the purchase price of these books will be as follows.Therefore, the total purchase price is 15 + 16 + 16 + 13 = 60 yen.At this time, the total purchase price is maximum.

There are N towns in the JOI country, and they are connected by M roads that can be passed in both directions.There are shopping malls in K towns, and citizens travel by road to one of these towns to shop.Depending on the location of your home, you may have to travel long distances to go shopping, which can be very inconvenient.In order to understand this situation, the king decided to find out how long the shortest distance to a town with a shopping mall could be depending on the location of the house.Since houses are sometimes built in the middle of roads (see the explanation of input example 1), this investigation is extremely difficult.Therefore, the king asked you, an excellent programmer, to create a program to conduct an investigation.Given road information and information about the town where the shopping mall is located, write a program to find the distance from the town where the shopping mall is located to the farthest point on the road (including the end point).You can ignore the distance it takes to travel within the town.2 ≤ N ≤ 3000 JOI Number of towns in the country 1 ≤ M ≤ 100000 = 10 5 JOI Number of roads in the country 1 ≤ K ≤ N Number of towns with shopping malls 1 ≤ l i ≤ 1000 Length of the i-th road Read the following input from the standard input.Output to standard output an integer that is the maximum value of the shortest distance to the town where the shopping mall is located, rounded to the nearest whole number.Of the scoring data, 40% of the allocated points satisfy K = 1.Input example 1 represents the following town.The length of all roads is 1.There is only one shopping mall in town 1.The farthest point to the town where the shopping mall is located is the point located on the road connecting Towns 2 and 3, at a distance of 0.5 from Town 2, and the distance to the town where the shopping mall is located is 1.5.Therefore, it outputs the value 2, which is the rounded value.

Late last year, Santa Claus forgot to give Christmas presents to the children of JOI Village.So, as an apology, I decided to deliver chocolate cake to the children.The delivery date is approaching tomorrow, so it's time to think about a travel plan.JOI Village is divided into a checkerboard shape with W roads running straight in the north-south direction and H roads running straight in the east-west direction.Let ( x , y ) represent the intersection where the th north-south road intersects with the y th east-west road from the south. JOI There are N houses in the village, and they are located at any intersection. Santa Claus can only move along the roads. The time it takes to travel between adjacent intersections is 1. JOI Since every house in the village has children, Santa ClausThey have to deliver one chocolate cake to every house in the village. Since it is a little dangerous to fly around with reindeer while carrying the precious chocolate cake, Santa Claus and his reindeer decide to land at one of the intersections in JOI Village, and Santa Claus will walk from there to deliver the chocolate cake. Santa Claus will not carry more than one chocolate cake at the same time.In other words, every time Santa Claus delivers a chocolate cake to one house, he returns to the intersection where he landed.Santa Claus decides to choose a travel plan that minimizes the time required from landing in JOI village to delivering chocolate cake to every house.Note that the time required from delivering the chocolate cake to the last house to returning to the intersection where you got off is not included in the required time.Also, do not consider anything other than the time it takes to travel.Given the information about the location of a house, write a program that calculates the minimum time required from landing in JOI village to delivering chocolate cake to all the houses, and the location of the intersection where Santa Claus should land to minimize the time, if Santa Claus chooses the intersection where he will land.1 ≤ W ≤ 1000000000 = 10 9 Number of roads extending in the north-south direction 1 ≤ H ≤ 1000000000 = 10 9 Number of roads extending in the east-west direction 1 ≤ N ≤ 100000 = 10 5 Number of houses 1 ≤ X i ≤ W Location of the i-th house,The number of the road extending in the north-south direction 1 ≤ Y i ≤ H The number of the road extending in the east-west direction at the location of the i-th house Read the following input from the standard input.Output the following data to standard output.Note that in this problem, the range of integers we are dealing with may not fit within 32 bits.Of the scoring data, 40% of the allocated points satisfy N ≤ 1000.Of the scoring data, 10% of the allocated points satisfy W ≤ 50, H ≤ 50, N ≤ 1000.For example, the following travel plan can minimize the time required.Note that you can get off at the intersection where the house is.

Have you heard of Just Odd Inventions? This company's business is to make ``just odd inventions.''We will call it JOI for short here.JOI is conducting research to confine many microorganisms alive in a single petri dish.There are N microorganisms to be investigated, numbered 1, 2, ..., N.When each microorganism is trapped in a petri dish, it instantly releases a harmful substance called foo (fatally odd object) into the dish.The amount of foo released by each microorganism is known.The foo released by all the microorganisms trapped in the petri dish is equally ingested by each microorganism in the petri dish.The tolerance amount of foo for each microorganism is known, and if the microorganism ingests more than this amount of foo, it will die.The amount of foo released by microorganism i is a i milligrams, and the allowable amount of foo is b i milligrams.In other words, when microorganisms i 1 , i 2 , ... , i k are confined in a petri dish, each microorganism in the dish ingests (a i 1 + a i 2 + ... + a i k )/k milligrams of foo, and microorganism i in the dish will die if this intake is greater than bi.Commissioned by JOI, you must trap as many microorganisms as possible alive in a petri dish.However, dead microorganisms have a negative effect on the environment inside the petri dish, so none of the microorganisms in the petri dish should be killed by ingesting foo.It should be noted that it is still a mystery how JOI makes a profit by making ``just a strange invention,'' and no one inside JOI except the company president knows about it.Given the number of microorganisms to be investigated, the amount of foo released and the allowable amount of foo for each microorganism, write a program to find the maximum number of microorganisms that can be confined in one petri dish.1 ≤ N ≤ 300000 = 3 × 10 5 Number of microorganisms to be investigated 1 ≤ a i ≤ 100000 = 10 5 Amount of foo emitted by microorganism i (milligrams) 1 ≤ b i ≤ 100000 = 10 5 Tolerable amount of foo of microorganism i (milligrams) Read the following input from the standard input.Output the maximum number of microorganisms that can be confined in one petri dish in one line to the standard output.Note that in this problem, the range of integers we are dealing with may not fit within 32 bits.Of the scoring data, 30% of the scores satisfy N ≤ 1000.In this example, if microorganisms 2, 4, and 5 are placed in a petri dish, the total amount of foo released will be 5 + 10 + 6 = 21 milligrams, and the amount of foo ingested by each microorganism will be 21/3 = 7 milligrams.The foo tolerance for microorganisms 2, 4, and 5 is 9, 12, and 7 milligrams, respectively, so none of the microorganisms in the petri dish will die.Also, it is not possible to put more than four microorganisms in a petri dish to avoid killing any of them.

At JOI Pasta Shop, the lunch set menu of recommended pasta and freshly squeezed juice is popular.When ordering this set menu, you choose one of each day's three types of pasta and two types of juice.The amount to be paid is the sum of the pasta and juice prices minus 50 yen.Given the prices of pasta and juice for a given day, write a program that finds the minimum price for that day's set menu.The input consists of 5 lines, each line containing one positive integer.The integer on the first line is the price of the first pasta.The integer on the second line is the price of the second pasta.The integer on the third line is the price of the third pasta.The integer on the fourth line is the price of the first juice.The integer on the fifth line is the price of the second juice.However, given the input data, the price of all pasta and juice is guaranteed to be between 100 yen and 2000 yen.Output the minimum price for that day's set menu in one line.In input/output example 1, when the second pasta is combined with the first juice, 700 + 198 - 50 = 848 is the minimum price for that day's set menu.In input/output example 2, when the third pasta and second juice are combined, 100 + 100 - 50 = 150 is the minimum price for that day's set menu.

Soccer is popular in JOI countries, and a league game called the JOI League is held every week.There are N teams in the JOI league, numbered from 1 to N.Matches of all combinations are played exactly once.In other words, N × (N - 1) / 2 matches are played.The winner or loser of each match is determined by the points scored by each team.The winning team has 3 points, and the losing team has 0 points.In case of a draw, both teams will earn 1 point.Rankings are determined by the total number of points earned by each team, and the difference in goals and losses is not considered.Teams with the same total number of points will be ranked higher.As an example, consider a league match with four teams.4 × (4 - 1) / 2 = 6 matches will be played.Suppose that the results are as shown in the table below.The left side of the hyphen is the score of the team next to it, and the side to the right is the score of the team vertically above it.At this time, team 2 with the most points wins first place.The teams with the next most points are Team 1 and Team 4, and these teams are both in second place.Team 3, which has the fewest points, is in 4th place.Write a program that finds the ranking of each team when the results of all games are given.The number of teams N (2 ≦ N ≦ 100) is written in the first line of the input file.The results of each match are written in the following N × (N - 1) / 2 lines.i + 1st line (1 ≦ i ≦ N × (N - 1) / 2) contains integers A i , B i , C i , D i (1 ≦ A i ≦ N, 1 ≦ B i ≦ N, 0 ≦ C i ≦ 100, 0 ≦ D i ≦ 100)are written with spaces between them, indicating that team A i and team B i played against each other, and team A i's score was C i points, and team B i's score was D i points.A i ≠ B i for all i, and no matches with the same combination are written.The output consists of N lines.Each row consists of one integer, and the integer in the i-th row (1 ≦ i ≦ N) represents the ranking of team i.Input/output example 1 corresponds to the example in the problem text.The results for input/output example 2 are as follows.

Chairman K is a regular customer of the JOI pizza shop located in the center of JOI city.Due to certain circumstances, he decided to start living frugally this month.So he wanted to order the pizza that had the most calories per dollar out of the pizzas available at JOI Pizza.Let's call this kind of pizza "the best pizza."The ``best pizza'' is not limited to just one type.At JOI Pizza, you can freely choose from N types of toppings and place them on top of the basic dough.It is not possible to have more than one topping of the same type.You can also order pizza without any toppings on the dough.The price of the dough is $A, and the price of each topping is $B.The price of a pizza is the sum of the price of the dough and the price of the toppings.In other words, the price of a pizza with k types of toppings (0 ≦ k ≦ N) is A + k × B dollars.The calories in the entire pizza are the sum of the calories in the dough and the calories in the toppings.Given the price of the dough, the price of the toppings, and the calorie value of the dough and each topping, write a program that calculates the number of calories per dollar for ``the best pizza.''The input consists of N + 3 lines.The first line contains an integer N (1 ≦ N ≦ 100) that represents the number of types of toppings.On the second line, two integers A and B (1 ≦ A ≦ 1000, 1 ≦ B ≦ 1000) are written separated by a space.A represents the price of the dough, and B represents the price of the toppings.The third line contains an integer C (1 ≦ C ≦ 10000) that represents the number of calories in the dough.In the 3 + i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ N), one integer D i (1 ≦ D i ≦ 10000) representing the number of calories in the i-th topping is written.Print the number of calories per dollar for ``best pizza'' in one line.However, truncate the decimal point and output as an integer value.In input/output example 1, adding the second and third toppings results in a pizza with 200 + 300 + 100 = 600 calories and a cost of 12 + 2 × 2 = $16.This pizza has 600 / 16 = 37.5 calories per dollar.Since this is the ``best pizza,'' output 37 by rounding down the decimal places of 37.5.

You love pasta, and you make it for dinner every day.You can make three types of pasta: tomato sauce, cream sauce, and basil sauce.I decided to think about the dinner schedule for N days.Choose one of three types of pasta each day.However, if you eat the same pasta continuously, you will get bored, so don't choose the same pasta for more than 3 days in a row.Also, pasta for K out of N days has already been decided.Given the value of N and information on pasta for K days as input, write a program that calculates the remainder when dividing the number of schedules that satisfy the conditions by 10000.The input consists of K + 1 lines.In the first line, two integers N and K (3 ≦ N ≦ 100, 1 ≦ K ≦ N) are written separated by a space.In the 1 + i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ K), two integers A i , B i (1 ≦ A i ≦ N, 1 ≦ B i ≦ 3) are written separated by a space.This means that the pasta for day A i has already been determined, and when B i = 1 it will be tomato sauce, when B i = 2 it will be cream sauce, and when B i = 3 it will be basil sauce.All A i (1 ≦ i ≦ K) are different.Given the input data, it is guaranteed that there is at least one schedule that satisfies the condition.Divide the number of plans that satisfy the condition by 10000 and output the remainder in one line.In input/output example 1, you consider a five-day schedule.The first and third days were tomato sauce, and the fourth day was cream sauce.Also, do not choose the same pasta for more than 3 days in a row.There are six plans that meet these conditions.In this table, 1 represents tomato sauce, 2 represents cream sauce, and 3 represents basil sauce.In input/output example 2, there are a total of 4112640 plans that satisfy the condition.Divide it by 10000 and output the remainder 2640.

JOI's building is shaped like a regular hexagon with sides of 1 meter connected together, as shown in the figure.As Christmas is approaching, JOI decided to decorate the walls of the building with illuminations.However, it would be wasteful to put illuminations on areas that cannot be seen from the outside, so we decided to only decorate the walls that can be accessed from the outside without passing through the building.The above figure is an example of the layout of JOI's buildings as seen from the sky.The numbers inside the regular hexagon represent the coordinates.Gray regular hexagons represent locations with buildings, and white regular hexagons represent locations with no buildings.In this example, the part indicated by the solid red line is the wall that will be decorated with illumination, and the total length of that wall is 64 meters.Given a map showing the layout of JOI's buildings, write a program that calculates the total length of the walls to be decorated.However, you should be able to move freely around the outside of the map, but not between adjacent buildings.In the first line of the input file, two integers W and H (1 ≦ W ≦ 100, 1 ≦ H ≦ 100) are written separated by a space.The following line H describes the location of JOI's buildings.In the i + 1st line (1 ≦ i ≦ H), W integers are written separated by spaces, and the jth integer (1 ≦ j ≦ W) is 1 if there is a building in the regular hexagon with coordinates (j, i), and 0 if there is not.In addition, there is always at least one building in the input data given.Maps are described using the following rules.Output the total length of the wall to be decorated with illumination in one line.Input/output example 1 corresponds to the example in the problem text, and the total length of the wall decorated with illumination is 64 meters.

When a positive integer is written in decimal notation (without leading zeros) and the digits increase and decrease in sequence, we call that number a ``zigzag number''.For example, 2947 is a zigzag number because the digits are in the order of increase → decrease → increase: 2 → 9 → 4 → 7.Also, 71946 is a zigzag number because the order is decrease → increase → decrease → increase.On the other hand, 123, 71446, 71442, and 88 are not zigzag numbers.Note that a 1-digit positive integer is considered to be a zigzag number.Write a program to find the remainder when dividing the number of zigzag numbers by 10000 among multiples of M between A and B and below.The input consists of three lines, each line containing one positive integer.The integer on the first line represents A, the integer on the second line represents B, and the integer on the third line represents M.These satisfy 1 ≦ A ≦ B ≦ 10 500 and 1 ≦ M ≦ 500.*Note that the values ​​of A and B may not fit into the data type that represents normal integers.Among the multiples of M between A and B, output the remainder when dividing the number of zigzag numbers by 10000 in one line.In input/output example 1, there are 13 zigzag numbers that are multiples of 5 between 100 and 200: 105, 120, 130, 140, 150, 160, 165, 170, 175, 180, 185, 190, 195.In input/output example 2, there are 50246 zigzag numbers that are multiples of 3 between 6 and 1234567, so we output the remainder of 246 when divided by 10000.

You were practicing programming for the JOI (Japan Information Olympics) finals when you noticed that the questions for this year's JOI qualifying rounds were all questions that dealt with numbers, and there were no questions that dealt with strings.So you decided to secretly become better at character string problems and gain an edge over your competitors.When I looked at past JOI questions, I realized that I needed to get used to character strings consisting of three types of letters: J, O, and I.So, let's consider such a string.You came up with the problem, ``Answer whether the given string has a substring called JOI,'' but you solved it quickly.If you want to solve a higher-level problem, you create a problem like the one below.A string t is a substring of the string s if it becomes s by adding some characters (even zero characters) to the beginning and end of t.For example, JJOOII is a substring of OJJOOIIOJOI.On the other hand, JOI is not a substring of JOOI.Also, for an integer k greater than or equal to 0, the JOI sequence at level k is a string of k characters J, k characters O, and k characters I arranged in this order.For example, JJOOII is a level 2 JOI sequence.I would like to find the JOI string with the maximum level among the JOI sequences that are substrings of a given string.Given a string S of length N consisting of three types of characters J, O, and I, write a program to find the maximum value of k such that the JOI sequence of level k is a substring of S.1 ≤ N ≤ 1000000 (= 10 6 ) Length of S Read the following data from standard input.Print on one line to the standard output an integer representing the largest value of k such that the JOI sequence at level k is a substring of S.Of the scoring data, 20% of the allocated points satisfy N ≤ 100.OJJOOIIOJOI contains JJOOII, which is a level 2 JOI string, as a substring, and does not contain JOI strings of level 3 or higher as a substring.

There are many cards with integers from 1 to 1000 written on them.Anna and Bruno use these cards to play the following game.Anna has a pile of A cards and Bruno has a pile of B cards.Anna creates a new pile by discarding any number of cards (even zero) from A.Bruno creates a new pile by discarding some cards from the top (even zero) and some cards from the bottom (even zero) from a pile of B, cards.However, when discarding, the remaining cards are not rearranged.If the two piles created in this way match, the number of cards included in one pile will be the score for both players.However, two piles are said to match if the number of cards in the piles, n, is the same, and the integers of the i-th card (1 ≤ i ≤ n) from the top are all the same.For example, suppose Anna has a pile of 5 cards and the integers written on them are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 from the top, and Bruno has a pile of 4 cards and the integers written on them are 3, 1, 4, 1 from the top.At this time, Anna discards cards numbered 2, 3, and 5, and Bruno discards the top 3 and bottom 1 cards, so their piles match.At this time, the number of cards in one of the remaining piles is 2, so both players receive a score of 2.I want to find the maximum value of the scores of two people.Given the information about the pile of cards held by Anna and Bruno, write a program that finds the maximum score between them.1 ≤ A ≤ 5000 1 ≤ B ≤ 5000 The integer written on the card must be between 1 and 1000.Read the following data from standard input.In the first line, integers A and B are written separated by spaces.On the second line, A integers are written separated by spaces, and the i-th integer (1 ≤ i ≤ A ) represents the integer written on the i-th card from the top of Anna's pile.On the third line, B integers are written separated by spaces, and the jth integer (1 ≤ j ≤ B ) represents the integer written on the jth card from the top of Bruno's pile.Output the integer representing the maximum score on one line to the standard output.Of the scoring data, 10% of the allocated points satisfies A ≤ 10, B ≤ 10.Of the scoring data, 50% of the allocated points satisfy A ≤ 100, B ≤ 100.This input/output example corresponds to the example in the question text.In this input/output example, there are two ways for two people to obtain a score of 3.When Anna discards cards 1, 2, and 3 and Bruno discards cards 2 and 3, their piles become 4, 5, and 4 from the top, and their score is 3 points.Also, when Anna discards cards 1, 5, and 4, and Bruno discards the top cards 4 and 5, their piles become 4, 2, 3, and 2.A person's score is 3 points.

Taro-kun decided to go to the summer festival held at JOI Shrine.There are N night stalls open along the road to the JOI shrine.Each night shop is numbered sequentially from 1 to N, and the enjoyment of playing and the time it takes to play are determined by integers.The enjoyment of playing at night shop i is A i and the time it takes to play at night shop i is B i.In addition, there is a fireworks display as a summer festival event, and the largest fireworks are set off at time S.Taro-kun wants to see the biggest fireworks.In order to enjoy both the night stalls and the fireworks, Taro decides to make a schedule from time 0, when he arrives at the summer festival, to time T, when the summer festival ends.Taro-kun chooses k (1 ≤ k ≤ N ) night establishments from among the night establishments and decides the time to visit each one using an integer.You cannot choose the same nightclub twice.Let the numbers of the selected night shops be y 1 , y 2 , ... y k in descending order, and let x y i be the time at which he visits night shop y i . Taro plays at night shop y i from time x y i to x y i + B y i.Taro-kun plays in the night stalls in descending order of number, and cannot play in two night stalls at the same time.Furthermore, the time required to travel between night shops can be ignored.After time T, the summer festival ends, so you can't hang out at the night stalls.Also, you cannot watch fireworks while hanging out at a night shop.However, if time S is the time when Taro starts playing or finishes playing at a certain night shop, Taro can watch the fireworks.In other words, the schedule must satisfy the following conditions.Let M be the sum of the enjoyment levels of the selected nightclubs A y 1 , A y 2 , ... A y k .Taro wants to make a plan so that M will be as large as possible.Write a program to find the maximum value of M when information on N night shops and times S and T are given.1 ≤ N ≤ 3000 Number of night stalls 1 ≤ T ≤ 3000 Time when the summer festival ends 0 ≤ S ≤ T Time when the biggest fireworks are launched 0 ≤ A i ≤ 100000 (= 10 5 ) Fun when playing at night stall i 1 ≤ B i ≤ 3000 Time taken to play at night stall iRead the following input from standard input.The first line of input contains integers N, T, and S separated by spaces, representing the number of night stalls N, T the time the summer festival ends, and S the time the largest fireworks are set off.The following N line contains information about the night shop.In the i + 1 (1 ≤ i ≤ N) line of the input, integers A i , B i are written separated by spaces, and represent that A i is the fun of playing at night shop i, and B i is the time it takes to play at night shop i.Also, for every input, it is guaranteed that at least one schedule can be set.Print the integer representing the maximum value of M on one line to standard output.Of the scoring data, 10% of the points allocated satisfies N ≤ 20.For 20% of the points allocated, S = 0 is satisfied.For 30% of the points allocated, at least one of these two conditions is met.Furthermore, there is no scoring data that satisfies both of these two conditions.In this example, M can be maximized by making a schedule to visit night shop 1 at time 0, night shop 2 at time 9, and night shop 4 at time 14.In this case, M = 8 + 2 + 6 = 16.

JOI-kun is playing with nails on the board.As shown in the figure below, JOI-kun inserted nails in the shape of an equilateral triangle with N lengths on each side.There are a nails lined up in the ath row from the top (1 ≤ a ≤ N).The bth nail (1 ≤ b ≤ a ) from the left is expressed as ( a , b ).If an equilateral triangle with a nail as its apex has ``each side parallel to one of the sides of the whole equilateral triangle, and the same direction as the whole equilateral triangle,'' this equilateral triangle is called a ``good equilateral triangle.''In other words, a ``good equilateral triangle'' is an equilateral triangle whose vertices are three nails ( a , b ), ( a + x , b ), ( a + x , b + x ) (where a , b , x satisfy 1 ≤ a < N , 1 ≤ b ≤ a , 1 ≤ x ≤ N - a ).JOI-kun decided to surround a ``good equilateral triangle'' using rubber bands.Given the number N of nails lined up on one side of an equilateral triangle, the number M of rubber bands held by JOI-kun, and the way a ``good equilateral triangle'' is surrounded by M rubber bands, write a program to find the number of nails surrounded by one or more rubber bands.2 ≤ N ≤ 5000 Number of nails lined up on one side 1 ≤ M ≤ 500000 (= 5 × 10 5 ) Number of rubber bands Read the following data from the standard input.Print the number of nails surrounded by one or more rubber bands on one line to the standard output.Of the scoring data, 30% of the points allocated satisfies M ≤ 10000.This example corresponds to how to enclose a ``good equilateral triangle'' as shown in Figure 2.In this example, 12 nails other than (1, 1), (4, 4), and (5, 5) are surrounded by one or more rubber bands.

There are N cities in the JOI country, and they are connected by M roads that can be passed in both directions.Citizens move between these cities through roads.There are many people in JOI countries who like festivals, and festivals are currently being held in K cities, making them extremely popular.On the other hand, some people dislike festivals because they are noisy and want to stay away from festivals as much as possible.Therefore, the king has asked you, an excellent programmer, to create a program that can quickly find out how far you can travel from one city to another without getting close to the city where the festival is being held, since the people hate such festivals.We are given road information, information on cities where festivals are held, and Q queries (pairs of the starting city S i and the desired city T i ).For each query i, write a program to find the distance to the festival of the route that has the maximum distance to the festival among all routes from town S i to town T i.However, the distance to a festival on a certain route is the minimum travel distance from the town on the route to the town where the festival is being held.2 ≤ N ≤ 100000 (= 10 5 ) Number of towns in JOI country 1 ≤ M ≤ 200000 (= 2 × 10 5 ) Number of roads in JOI country 1 ≤ K ≤ N Number of towns where festivals are held 1 ≤ Q ≤ 100000 (= 10 5 ) Number of queries 1 ≤ L i≤ 1000 Length of i-th road Read the following data from standard input.Print the answers to all queries in Q lines to standard output.That is, in the i-th line, output an integer that represents the distance to the festival of the route that has the maximum distance to the festival among all routes from town S i to town T i.Of the scoring data, 10% of the allocated points satisfy Q = 1.For 20% of the allocated points, N ≤ 5000, Q ≤ 5000 are satisfied.For 30% of the points allocated, at least one of these two conditions is met.Furthermore, there is no scoring data that satisfies both of these two conditions.Six towns are connected by six roads, and festivals are held in two towns, towns 1 and 6.There are three queries:

JOI, who has always struggled with homework during winter vacation, decided to carry out his homework in a planned manner this time.The homework consists of Japanese language and math drills, with the Japanese drills on page A and the math drills on page B.JOI can complete up to C pages of Japanese language drills and D pages of math drills in a day, but if he does homework, he will not be able to play on that day.There are L days of winter vacation, and JOI must complete his homework during the winter vacation.JOI Write a program to find the maximum number of days you can play during winter vacation.The input consists of 5 lines, each line containing one positive integer.The first line contains an integer L (2 ≦ L ≦ 40), which represents the number of days of winter vacation.The second line contains an integer A (1 ≦ A ≦ 1000), which represents the number of Japanese drill pages.The third line contains an integer B (1 ≦ B ≦ 1000), which represents the number of pages in the math drill.The fourth line contains an integer C (1 ≦ C ≦ 100), which represents the maximum number of pages of Japanese drills that JOI can complete in one day.The fifth line contains an integer D (1 ≦ D ≦ 100), which represents the maximum number of pages of math drills that JOI can complete in one day.However, given the input data, it is guaranteed that JOI will be able to complete his homework during winter vacation and be able to play for at least one day.Output the maximum number of days that JOI can play during winter vacation in one line.In input/output example 1, there are 20 days of winter vacation, 25 pages of Japanese language drills, and 30 pages of math drills.JOI can complete up to 6 pages of Japanese language drills and 8 pages of math drills in a day.For example, if JOI progresses through 6 pages of Japanese language drills and 8 pages of math drills from the first day of winter vacation, he can complete the Japanese language drills on the 5th day and the math drills on the 4th day, giving him 15 days of play.This is the maximum number of days that JOI can play during winter vacation, so output 15.In input/output example 2, for example, if JOI progresses through 4 pages of Japanese language drills and 6 pages of math drills from the first day, he will be able to finish both drills on the 8th day, allowing him to play for 7 days.This is the maximum number of days that JOI can play during winter vacation, so we output 7.

JOI You decided to play a game with your friend.N players participate in this game.The rules for one game are as follows: Each player writes any integer between 1 and 100 on a card and submits it.Each player gets the same number of points as the number he or she wrote if no one else wrote the same number as him.If someone else writes the same number as you, you will not receive points.JOI You guys played this game three times.Write a program that calculates the total points earned by each player in the three games, given the numbers written by each player in the three games.The input consists of 1 + N lines.The first line contains an integer N (2 ≦ N ≦ 200), which represents the number of players.In the i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ N) of the following N lines, three integers between 1 and 100 are written separated by spaces, each representing the number written by the i-th player in the first, second, and third game.The output consists of N lines.In the i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ N), output an integer representing the total score obtained by the i-th player in three games.In example input 1, the details of the points earned by each player over the three games are as follows:

JOI You decided to make a signboard for your store.There are N old signboards with letters written at equal intervals.JOI You make a signboard by erasing some letters from an old signboard.I want the remaining string of characters to be the name of the store, and I want to make sure that the remaining characters are evenly spaced.Signs must be made from a single old sign and must not be cut or joined together.Given the name of a store and N old signboards, write a program that calculates the number of signboards that JOI can make.However, even if there are multiple signs that can be made from one old sign, only one sign can be made.The input consists of 2 + N lines.The first line contains an integer N (1 ≦ N ≦ 100), which represents the number of old signboards.The second line contains a string of 3 to 25 lowercase letters that represents the store's name.In the i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ N) of the following N lines, a character string consisting of 1 to 100 lowercase letters of the alphabet is written, representing the character string written on the i-th old signboard.JOI Output in one line an integer that represents the number of signboards you can make.The name of the shop is bar.The first old signboard has the string abracadabra written on it.You can make a signboard by erasing all but the 2nd, 6th, and 10th characters from this old signboard.In the second picture, if you delete the second character, you can create the character string bar, but the remaining characters are not arranged at equal intervals.The third sheet can be used as a signboard without erasing any text.There are two ways to make a sign from the fourth old sign.One method is to delete all but the first, second, and third characters.Another method is to delete all characters except the 6th, 7th, and 8th characters.Therefore, JOI can make a signboard from the 1st, 3rd, and 4th old signboards, so he outputs 3.

While it is winter in Japan, the days in Australia, located in the southern hemisphere, continue to be hot.IOI, who lives in Australia, decided to plan the clothes he would wear based on the weather forecast for D days.The maximum temperature on day i (1 ≦ i ≦ D) is predicted to be T i degrees.IOI You have N types of clothes, numbered from 1 to N.Clothes j (1 ≦ j ≦ N) are suitable for wearing on days when the maximum temperature is above A j degrees and below B j degrees.Furthermore, each piece of clothing has a fixed integer called ``gaudiness,'' and the gaudiness of clothing j is C j.For each of the D days, IOI chooses one item of clothing suitable for wearing when the maximum temperature is according to the weather forecast.You can choose the same clothes as many times as you like, or you can choose some clothes even if you don't choose them even once in D days.IOI wanted to avoid wearing similar clothes consecutively as much as possible, so he tried to make the sum of the absolute values ​​of the differences in the flashiness of clothes worn on consecutive days as large as possible.In other words, if we choose clothes x i on day i, we want to maximize the value |C x 1 - C x 2 | + |C x 2 - C x 3 | + … + |C x D-1 - C x D |.Write a program to find this maximum value.The input consists of 1 + D + N lines.In the first line, two integers D and N (2 ≦ D ≦ 200, 1 ≦ N ≦ 200) are written separated by a space.D is the number of days to plan clothes, and N is the number of types of clothes that IOI has.In the i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ D) of the following D lines, one integer T i (0 ≦ T i ≦ 60) is written.This means that the maximum temperature on day i is predicted to be T i degrees.In the jth line (1 ≦ j ≦ N) of the following N lines, three integers A j , B j , C j (0 ≦ A j ≦ B j ≦ 60, 0 ≦ C j ≦ 100) are written.These represent that clothing j is suitable for wearing on days when the maximum temperature is above A j degrees and below B j degrees, and its flashiness is C j.It is guaranteed that for any day of D, there is at least one type of clothing suitable for wearing when the maximum temperature is according to the weather forecast.Output in one line the sum of the absolute values ​​of the differences in the flashiness of clothes worn on consecutive days, that is, the maximum value of the values ​​|C x 1 - C x 2 | + |C x 2 - C x 3 | + … + |C x D-1 - C x D |.In input/output example 1, the clothing candidates for the first day are clothing 3 and clothing 4, the clothing candidates for the second day are clothing 2 and clothing 3, and the clothing candidates for the third day are only clothing 3.Choose clothes 4 on the first day, clothes 2 on the second day, and clothes 3 on the third day.That is, x 1 = 4, x 2 = 2, x 3 = 3.At this time, the absolute value of the difference in the flashiness of the clothes on the first and second days is |40 - 90| = 50, and the absolute value of the difference in the flashiness of the clothes on the second and third days is |90 - 60| = 30.The total is 80, which is the maximum value.In input/output example 2, you must choose clothes 2 on the 1st day, clothes 2 on the 2nd day, clothes 1 on the 3rd day, clothes 2 on the 4th day, and clothes 1 on the 5th day.In this case, the desired value is |100 - 100| + |100 - 0| + |0 - 100| + |100 - 0| = 300.

To the west of the Australian continent lies the wide Indian Ocean.Mr. JOI, a marine researcher, is researching the properties of N types of fish that live in the Indian Ocean.Each type of fish has a rectangular parallelepiped habitat range in the sea.Fish can move anywhere within their range, including the boundaries, but they never leave their range.A point in the ocean is represented by three real numbers (x, y, d): (x, y, d) is the position x to the east and y to the north from a certain point when viewed from the sky, and represents the point at a depth of d from the sea surface.However, the sea surface is assumed to be flat.Mr. JOI would like to know how many places the habitat ranges of K or more types of fish overlap.Write a program to find the volume of such a place.The input consists of 1 + N lines.In the first line, two integers N and K (1 ≦ K ≦ N ≦ 50) are written separated by a space.This means that there are N types of fish, and we want to find the volume of the area where the habitat ranges of K or more types of fish overlap.The i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ N) of the following N lines contains six integers X i,1 , Y i,1 , D i,1 , X i,2 , Y i,2 , D i,2 (0 ≦ X i,1 ＜ X i,2 ≦ 1000000 (= 10 6 ), 0 ≦ Y i,1 ＜ Y i,2≦ 1000000 (= 10 6 ), 0 ≦ D i,1 ＜ D i,2 ≦ 1000000 (= 10 6 )) are written.This means that the habitat range of the i-th type of fish is 8 points:It represents a rectangular parallelepiped with vertices as Y i,1 , D i,2 ), (X i,2 , Y i,2 , D i,2 ), and (X i,1 , Y i,2 , D i,2 ).In one line, output the volume of the entire area where the habitat ranges of K or more types of fish overlap.In input/output example 1, for example, point (45, 65, 65) is the habitat range of the first and third types of fish, so it is a location that satisfies the condition.On the other hand, point (25, 35, 45) is the habitat range of only the second type of fish, so it is not a place that satisfies the conditions.The habitat range of fish is shown in the diagram below.Point O represents the reference point on the sea surface.

JOI went on a trip to Australia, enjoyed sightseeing in various places, and finally the day came to return home.JOI is now in the town where the international airport is from where his return flight departs.This town is organized into north, south, east, and west sections, and each section has roads, souvenir shops, residences, and an international airport.JOI You start from the northwestmost section and aim for the international airport in the southeasternmost section.JOI You can move from the section you are currently in to an adjacent section, but you cannot enter the section where the house is located.Also, in order to catch your flight, you should only move to the east or south of the area you are currently in.However, since you have some time to spare, you can move up to K times to the north or west of the area you are currently on.JOI You enter a section of souvenir shops and buy souvenirs for your Japanese friends.JOI You have carefully researched souvenir shops, so you know which souvenir shop you can go to and how many souvenirs you can buy.JOI Write a program to find the maximum number of souvenirs that you can purchase.However, the time taken to buy souvenirs can be ignored, and if you visit the same souvenir shop more than once, you will buy souvenirs only on the first visit.The input consists of 1 + H lines.In the first line, three integers H, W, K (2 ≦ H ≦ 50, 2 ≦ W ≦ 50, 1 ≦ K ≦ 3) are written separated by spaces.Each of the following H lines contains a string of W characters, representing information about the section.The i-th partition from the north and the j-th partition from the west are expressed as (i, j) (1 ≦ i ≦ H, 1 ≦ j ≦ W).The jth character of the i-th row is '.' if the block (i, j) is a road or an international airport, and '#' if it is a residential building.If it is a souvenir shop, it is one of '1', '2', ..., '9' and represents the number of souvenirs that can be purchased at that souvenir shop.Given the input data, it is guaranteed that the northwestmost section where JOI is at the beginning is a road.Also, it is guaranteed that JOI will be able to reach the international airport.JOI Output in one line the integer that represents the maximum number of souvenirs that you can purchase.In input/output example 1, JOI goes south three times and shops at the souvenir shop in section (4, 1), then goes south once, east three times, and then north twice to shop at the souvenir store in section (3, 4).Finally, if you go south twice and reach the international airport, you can buy a total of 11 souvenirs.

The shape of a triangle is determined by the length of its sides.When three positive integers are given in order, check whether there is a triangle whose side lengths match those values, and if so, determine whether it is an acute triangle, right triangle, or obtuse triangle, and proceed to the next input.If there are no triangles, output the number of triangles, right triangles, acute triangles, and obtuse triangles input so far, separated by spaces, ignore subsequent inputs, and exit.We can assume that there is always a triangle in the input. We do not know the number of lines in the input, but each line contains three positive integers separated by spaces.However, each integer must be 100 or less. In the output file, also include a line feed code on the last line of the output.

A positive integer n is written on the first line of the input file, and n positive integers are written on the second line, separated by one half-width space character.n is 2 or 3, and any integer written on the second line has a value less than or equal to 10 8.Find all the common divisors of these two or three numbers, and print them out one on each line, starting from the smallest.Also output the trivial common divisor (“1”).In the output file, also include a line feed code on the last line of the output.

A positive integer n (n≧3) is written in the first line of the input file, and one different positive integer a 1 , ..., a n is written in the following n lines.When the permutations made by choosing two different items from a 1 , ..., a n are arranged in ascending order (viewed as numbers), output the third one.However, even in cases such as a 1 = 1 and a 4 = 11, a 1 a 4 and a 4 a 1 are considered different permutations.Also, 1≦a i ≦10000 (i=1, ..., n) and 3≦n≦10 4.In the output file, also include a line feed code at the end of the output.

Given n points P 1 , ..., P n on a plane, we want to find the two points with the minimum distance.The first line of the input file contains an integer n.In each of the 2nd to n+1th lines, two positive integers x and y are written separated by one half-width space character.x and y in the i+1th row are the x coordinate of P i and the y coordinate of P i, respectively.When you select the two closest points from these n points, output the square of the distance between these two points.However, 2≤n≤500000 and -10000≤x≤10000, -10000≤y≤10000, and n≤100 in three of the five input files.Also, assume that all coordinates are different in any input file.In the output file, also include a line feed code on the last line of the output.

It was decided that the following class competition would be held at J Junior High School's sports day.Choose n pairs of male and female representatives from each class (male student b 1 , female student g 1 ), (male student b 2 , female student g 2 ), ... , (male student b n , female student g n ), have them freely choose flags with numbers from 0 to 9 written on them (there are enough flags for each flag), and line them up in a horizontal line.However, each male and female student in a pair must choose a flag with the same number, and the female students must line up in the reverse order g n … g 2 g 1, such as b 1 b 2 … b n c g n … g 2 g 1, which is the reverse order of the n male students (b 1 b 2 … b n).At c in the center, there are cases in which the homeroom teacher stands holding a flag with a number designated in advance by the chief judge, and cases in which the chief judge specifies that the class teacher not stand.If we consider the numbers on the flags held by the students (and the teacher) lined up in this way as a 2n-digit (or 2n+1-digit) integer, the winner is the one that is a prime number.However, if both classes are prime numbers, or if both classes are not prime numbers, the one with the larger number wins.In addition, numbers that begin with a 0, such as 0 b 2 … b n c g n … g 2 0 or 0 b 2 … b n g n … g 2 0, are prohibited because they are not a commonly used way of representing numbers.As a student at J Junior High School, you must think about how to line up in order for your class to win.On the first line of the input file, a positive integer n and a one-digit integer c (the number of the flag held by the teacher) are written separated by a single half-width space character.When c<0, it means that the teacher does not stand in the center.The given input is 1≦n≦10.Output the non-losing order b 1 b 2 … b n c g n … g 2 g 1 or b 1 b 2 … b n g n … g 2 g 1.In the output file, also include a line feed code at the end of the output.

There is data that counts the number of cars that pass per minute at the entrance and exit of a tunnel over a period of n minutes.The data consists of n+2 lines in total, and each line contains the following content.Let S j be the number of cars in the tunnel at the time j minutes have passed since the start of the investigation ( j = 0, 1, 2, ... , n ).Print the maximum value of S j.Also, it is impossible for the number of cars in the tunnel to become negative, so if S j becomes negative even once, output 0 to indicate an ``error.''However, n is less than 10000, and the number of cars passing through the entrance and exit of the tunnel in one minute is less than 100.In the output file, also include a line feed code on the last line of the output.

Write a calculator program that calculates addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.Each line of input data alternates between a number and one of the symbols +, -, *, /, =.The first line is a number.It does not consider the priority of operations +, -, *, / (multiplication/division *, / should be calculated before addition/subtraction +, -), but calculates in the order of input, and when it reaches the = line, outputs the calculation result.The numerical value of the input data should be a positive integer less than or equal to 10 8.During the calculation, the calculation result may be 0 or a negative number, but it will not exceed the range of -10 8 to 10 8 .Division is rounded down.Therefore, 100/3*3= becomes 99.In the output file, also include a line feed code on the last line of the output.

A factory receives orders for product production from various sales offices.I would like to summarize the orders from the previous day and find the total production for each product.The number n of order data is written in the first line of the input file, and the product name and order quantity are written in the next n lines separated by spaces.The product name is written in capital letters and has up to 5 characters.Order data may contain the same products, so the order may vary.Add up the number of orders for the same product that appears in this order data, and output the product name and total to an output file using spaces as delimiters.However, please attach the following order to the product name and output in that order.Order: alphabetically in ascending order of length, and if the lengths are the same, the first different character compared to the previous one is alphabetically ordered.The number of products, number of orders, and their total in the input data are all less than 10 6 .In the output file, also include a line feed code on the last line of the output.

IOI Real Estate rents out condominiums.The apartments handled by this company are 1LDK, with an area of ​​2xy+x+y as shown in the diagram below.However, x and y are positive integers.In the IOI real estate catalog, the area of ​​apartments is listed in ascending order (from the narrowest to the smallest), but it was discovered that there were some errors (inconceivable areas) mixed in.The catalog (input file) has N+1 lines, with the number of rooms written on the first line, and the area of ​​each room written in ascending order on the next N lines.However, the number of rooms is less than 100,000, and the area is less than (2 to the 31st power)-1 = 2,147,483,647.Up to three of the five input data have the number of rooms less than 1000 and the area less than 30000.Print the number of incorrect lines (the number of impossible rooms).In the output file, also include a line feed code on the last line of the output.

IOI Jewelry Store will be selling beaded necklaces.There are n colors of beads, and I would like to select at least m of each color to make a necklace consisting of r beads, and sell each one in a gift box.I would like to have one of each of the different color combinations, but none of the store staff knows how many gift boxes I should have.The store manager is worried that depending on n, m, and r, an unrealistic number of gift boxes may be needed.IOI Write a program that outputs the number of cosmetic boxes required on behalf of the manager of a jewelry store.n, m, r are integers, 0≦m＜n≦r≦10000.The input file consists of one line, with n, m, and r written in this order, separated by spaces.In three of the five input files, n≦10.In the output file, also include a line feed code on the last line of the output.

Five students participated in the class at JOI High School: Taro, Jiro, Saburo, Shiro, and Hanako.In this class, we conducted a final exam.All five students took the final exam.For students whose final exam score was 40 points or higher, their final exam score was used as their grade.All students who scored less than 40 points on the final exam took remedial classes and their grades improved to 40 points.Given the final exam scores of five students, write a program that calculates the average score of the five students.The input consists of 5 lines, with one integer written on each line.The first line contains Taro's final exam score.The second line contains Jiro's final exam score.On the third line, Saburo's final exam score is written.On the fourth line, Shiro's final exam score is written.In the fifth line, Hanako's final exam score is written.All students' final exam scores are integers between 0 and 100.All students' final exam scores are multiples of 5.The average score of the five students is always an integer.Output the integer representing the average score of the five students in one line.In input/output example 1, Taro and Shiro's final exam scores are less than 40 points, so Taro and Shiro's grades will be 40 points.Jiro, Saburo, and Hanako's final exam scores are over 40 points, so Jiro's score is 65 points, Saburo's score is 100 points, and Hanako's score is 95 points.The total score of the five students is 340, so the average score is 68 points.

A world-class sports tournament will be held in Tokyo in 20XX.Programming contests are enjoyed around the world as sports, and there is a possibility that they will be adopted as competitions.When we investigated the judging committee that decides which competitions will be accepted, we found the following.Given a list of competitions and committee member information, write a program that finds the number of the competition that received the most votes.The input consists of 1 + N + M lines.The first line contains integers N and M (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000, 1 ≤ M ≤ 1000), which represent the number of competitions and the number of committee members, respectively.The i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ N) of the following N lines contains an integer A i (1 ≤ A i ≤ 1000), which represents the cost A i required to hold competition i.In the jth line (1 ≤ j ≤ M) of the following M lines, an integer B j (1 ≤ B j ≤ 1000) is written, representing the examination criterion B j of committee member j.Given the input data, every committee member is guaranteed to cast one vote, and the competition that receives the most votes is guaranteed to be one.Print the number of the competition that received the most votes on one line.In input/output example 1, there are four competitions and three committee members.The four competitions in the list cost 5, 3, 1, and 4, respectively.Therefore, competition 2 receives 2 votes and competition 3 receives 1 vote.The competition that received the most votes is competition 2, so 2 is output.In input/output example 2, competition 1 receives 5 votes and competition 2 receives 1 vote.The competition that received the most votes is competition 1, so 1 is output.

JOI has decided to plan a sightseeing tour of a city called Choto in IOI country.The metropolis is divided into a grid pattern with W roads running straight north-south and H roads running straight east-west.The W roads in the north-south direction are numbered 1, 2, ..., W in order from the west.In addition, the H roads in the east-west direction are numbered 1, 2, ..., H in order from the south.The intersection of the i-th north-south road from the west and the j-th east-west road from the south is represented by (i, j).Furthermore, as shown in the figure below, there is a road from each intersection to one intersection northeast (excluding the intersection on the northernmost road and the intersection on the easternmost road).There is also a path to the intersection one place southwest (excluding the intersection on the southernmost road and the intersection on the westernmost road).In other words, from intersection (i, j), if there are intersections (i - 1, j), (i + 1, j), (i, j - 1), (i, j + 1), you can go to those intersections using one road.In addition, if there are intersections (i - 1, j - 1), (i + 1, j + 1), you can also use one road to reach those intersections.JOI has already decided in what order he will visit N tourist spots as part of his tour plan.The i-th (1 ≦ i ≦ N) tourist spot visited is at the intersection (X i , Y i ).JOI: You would like to reduce the number of roads you have to take in order to shorten the time it takes to tour as much as possible.Write a program that finds the minimum total number of roads that must be taken to visit tourist spots in a predetermined order.However, the starting point of the tour is the intersection (X 1 , Y 1 ).Also, participants are not allowed to move outside of Choto during the tour.JOI can also pass through intersections with tourist spots without visiting them.Supplementary note regarding "total number of roads".It is possible to pass the same route more than once during the tour.In that case, the ``total number of roads'' will include the number of times the road has been traveled.The input consists of 1 + N lines.In the first line, three integers W, H, N (2 ≤ W ≤ 10000, 2 ≤ H ≤ 10000, 1 ≤ N ≤ 1000) are written separated by spaces.In the i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ N) of the following N lines, two integers X i , Y i (1 ≤ X i ≤ W, 1 ≤ Y i ≤ H) are written separated by a space.This means that the intersection where the i-th tourist spot is located is (X i , Y i ).Output in one line the minimum total number of roads taken to visit tourist spots in order.In input/output example 1, for example, you can visit the intersections in the order of (1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3), (3, 2), (4, 2), (4, 1).As in input/output example 2, the same intersection may be visited multiple times.

There are three members in the programming club at IOI High School: Mr. J, Mr. O, and Mr. I.The programming club is trying to set up a schedule for club activities.I'm currently trying to decide on a schedule for N days of activities.There are two possible schedules for each activity day for each club member: whether or not to participate in the activity, and there are a total of eight schedules for the club.There is only one key to the clubroom, and Mr. J has it at first.On each activity day, one of the club members participating in that day's activities must have the key, and after the activity, one of the club members who participated takes the key home.In the programming department, a person in charge of each activity day was determined in advance to ensure that the activity was carried out on each activity day.The responsible person must be present at the day's activities.Given the number of days for which the schedule is to be determined and information on who is in charge of each activity day, write a program that calculates the remainder when dividing the number of possible schedules that allow club activities to be held on all activity days by 10007.However, the person taking the key home at the end of club activities may be any member participating in that day's activities, and on the last day, anyone may take the key home.The input consists of two lines.The first line contains an integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 1000) that represents the number of days for which the schedule is to be determined.The second line contains a string of N characters representing the person responsible for each activity day.The i-th character (1 ≤ i ≤ N) of this string represents the person in charge of the i-th activity day.In other words, if the i-th character is J, O, or I, it means that the person responsible for the i-th activity day is Mr. J, Mr. O, or Mr. I, respectively.Divide the number of possible schedule tables by 10007 and output the remainder in one line.In input/output example 1, consider the schedule for two days of activities.Mr. O is responsible for the first day, and Mr. I is responsible for the second day.There are seven possible schedules that satisfy the conditions in the problem statement.In this table, J, O, and I represent the participation of Mr. J, Mr. O, and Mr. I on that day, respectively.Please note that although Mr. O is in charge of the first day, it is Mr. J who initially has the key, so both Mr. J and Mr. O must participate in the activities on the first day.Also, note that the person who took the key home on the first day must also participate on the second day, so there must be at least one person who participates on both the first and second day.In input/output example 2, there are a total of 72493594992 possible schedule tables that satisfy the conditions.Divide it by 10007 and output the remainder 4976.

The IOI country consists of N towns from town 1 to town N, and the towns are connected by roads.There are K roads in the IOI country, and all roads connect two different towns.Cars can move freely on roads in both directions, but they cannot go from one town to another by anything other than roads.JOI, who lives in town 1 in IOI country, decides to take a taxi to his grandmother's house who lives in town N.There are N taxi companies in the IOI country, from taxi company 1 to taxi company N.Taxi companies in IOI countries have some special rules as follows.For example, if R 1 = 2, if you take a taxi from taxi company 1 from town 1, you can only travel on a maximum of two roads, so if you want to travel on three or more roads, you will need to change taxis at a town along the way.JOI You cannot get into a taxi or get out of a taxi at any point other than the town.Also, you cannot use any means of transportation other than taxis.JOI Write a program to find the minimum total fare required for you to reach town N.The input consists of 1 + N + K lines.In the first line, two integers N and K (2 ≤ N ≤ 5000, N - 1 ≤ K ≤ 10000) are written separated by a space.This means that the IOI country consists of N towns and that the number of roads in the IOI country is K.In the i-th line (1 ≤ i ≤ N) of the following N lines, two integers C i , R i (1 ≤ C i ≤ 10000, 1 ≤ R i ≤ N) are written separated by a space.This means that the fare of a taxi from taxi company i is C i, and the taxi can only travel on a maximum of R i roads consecutively after getting on the taxi.In the jth line (1 ≤ j ≤ K) of the following K lines, two different integers A j and B j (1 ≤ A j < B j ≤ N) are written separated by a space.This indicates that a road exists between town A j and town B j.The same pair (A j , B j ) is never written more than once.Given the input data, it is guaranteed that you can take a taxi from any town to any other town.JOI Output in one line the integer that represents the minimum total fare required for you to go from town 1 to town N.The input/output example above corresponds to the figure below.Circles represent towns and lines represent roads.In this input/output example, in order for JOI to reach town 6 with the minimum fare, do the following.If JOI takes this route, the total fare will be 400 + 300 = 700, so 700 will be output.

JOI You decided to eat xiao long bao at a Chinese restaurant for lunch.Xiaolongbao is a dish of ingredients and hot soup wrapped in a flour wrapper, and is known for the soup that splatters around when eaten.The set of xiao long bao that JOI ordered consists of N xiao long bao with different toppings and soups.N xiao long bao are lined up in a line at equal intervals and numbered from 1 to N in order.The distance between the i-th xiaolongbao and the j-th xiaolongbao is the absolute value |i - j|.JOI You eat the xiao long bao in a certain order.Initially, the deliciousness of all xiaolongbao is 0.When the i-th xiaolongbao is eaten, its juice splatters around and splashes on the uneaten xiaolongbaos whose distance from xiaolongbao i is less than or equal to D i.The taste of xiao long bao with soup increases by A i.In other words, when the i-th xiaolongbao is eaten, if the j-th xiaolongbao (1 ≤ j ≤ N and i - D i ≤ j ≤ i + D i ) remains uneaten, the tastiness of the jth xiaolongbao increases by A i.JOI wants to maximize the total deliciousness of the xiaolongbaos he eats by adjusting the order in which he eats them.Write a program that calculates the total deliciousness of the xiao long bao that JOI eats when eaten in the best order.The input file consists of three lines.One integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100) is written in the first line.On the second line, N integers D 1 , D 2 , ..., D N (0 ≤ D i ≤ 7) are written separated by spaces.On the third line, N integers A 1 , A 2 , ..., A N (0 ≤ A i ≤ 1000) are written separated by spaces.JOI Output the maximum value of the total deliciousness of the xiao long bao you eat in one line.In input/output example 1, if you eat in the order of 5th → 3rd → 1st → 2nd → 4th, the total deliciousness will be 20.This is the best option, as there is no way to eat more than 20 in total.

The Japan Information Olympics Committee decided to create a new JOI flag to support the athletes heading to the Taiwan Games.The JOI flag has the shape of squares arranged vertically in $M$ rows and horizontally in $N$ columns, and each square has one of the letters J, O, or I written on it.In addition to the JOI flag, the Japan Information Olympics Committee has decided on a JOI emblem.The JOI emblem has two rows of squares lined up vertically and two columns horizontally, and each square has one of the letters J, O, or I written on it.The number of JOI emblems included in the JOI flag is the number of JOI emblems in the two vertical rows and two horizontal columns included in the JOI flag, where the arrangement of J, O, and I in that area matches the JOI emblem (without rotation or flipping).Even if two vertical rows and two horizontal columns of areas that meet the conditions overlap, they are counted separately.The Japan Information Olympics Committee has an old JOI flag and a blank sheet of paper.The blank paper is the size of one square that makes up the JOI flag, and you can write any one letter of your choice among J, O, and I.The Information Olympics Japan Committee decided to create a new JOI flag by performing one of the following operations.The Information Olympics Japan Committee would like to include as many JOI emblems as possible on the new JOI flag.You now want to find the maximum number of JOI emblems that can be included in a new JOI flag.Given the information of the old JOI flag and JOI emblem, write a program that finds the maximum number of JOI emblems included in the new JOI flag.Read the following data from standard input.Output an integer representing the maximum number of JOI emblems included in the new JOI flag on one line to the standard output.All input data satisfies the following conditions.The old JOI flag and JOI emblem are as shown in the example in the question.If you change the square in the second row from the top and fourth column from the left to a J using a blank sheet of paper, you will get the following shape.After making these changes, the JOI flag has the following three areas with the same placement as the JOI emblem.There is no new JOI flag that has 4 or more areas with the same placement as the JOI emblem, so the maximum number of JOI emblems included in a new JOI flag is 3.Note that the maximum value may be obtained without using a blank sheet.In this input/output example, none of the possible new JOI flags would contain a single JOI emblem.

Incredible Okashi Inc. is a company that produces ``incredible okashi''.We will abbreviate it here as IOI company.IOI made a special IOI manju, so they decided to sell it.IOI made one manju of $M$ types.The $M$ manjus made are all the same size, but each one has a different taste, so the price varies, and the price of the $i$th $(1 \leq i \leq M)$ manju is $P_i$ yen.By the way, have you heard of Just Odd Inventions?This company's business is to make ``just odd inventions.''We will call it JOI for short here.IOI decided to place an order with JOI for high-quality boxes for packing steamed buns.There are $N$ types of manju boxes manufactured by JOI, and the $j$-th $(1 \leq j \leq N)$ box is large enough to hold up to $C_j$ manju, and its selling price is $E_j$ yen.It was decided that several types of these $N$ types of boxes (from 0 types to $N$ types or less) would be ordered one by one, and the manju would be packaged into those boxes and sold as a set.The price of each steamed bun set is the sum of the prices of the steamed buns included in it.If all the steamed bun sets are sold, what is the maximum profit that IOI can earn?Here, profit is the value obtained by subtracting the total price of the ordered boxes from the total price of the steamed bun sets sold.Note that the buns that are not packed in boxes will be enjoyed by IOI's staff, so profits will not be affected.Write a program to find the maximum profit that IOI can make, given the price of each manju and the size and price of each box.Read the following data from standard input.Output in one line to the standard output an integer that represents the maximum profit that IOI Company can obtain in yen.All input data satisfies the following conditions.In this example, the first box (100 yen) and the second box (120 yen) are ordered, and for example, the first box is filled with the first and second manju and sold as a set for $180 + 160 = 340$ yen, and the second box is filled with the third and fourth manju and sold for $170 + 190 = 360$.If sold as a set of yen, IOI's profit will be $700 - 220 = 480$ yen.In this example, to maximize your profit, you should not buy any boxes at all.

JOI-kun is about to eat a snack with his younger sisters, JOI Ko-chan and JOI Mi-chan.Today's snack is Baumkuchen, our three favorite food.Baumkuchen is a cylindrical sweet as shown in the picture below.In order to divide it into three people, JOI must cut it into three pieces by inserting the blade three times in the radial direction.However, this Baumkuchen is hard like real wood, so it is not easy to insert a knife into it.Therefore, this Baumkuchen has $N$ notches in advance, and JOI can only cut at the positions where there are notches.When the notches are numbered clockwise from 1 to $N$, the size of the part between the $i$th notch and the $i + 1$th notch is $A_i$ for $1 \leq i \leq N - 1$.Also, the size of the part between the $N$th notch and the first notch is $A_N$.The number of notches $N$ and the integer $A_1,...,A_N$ representing the size of each part are given.Write a program that outputs the maximum size of the smallest piece when Baumkuchen is cut into three pieces.Read the following data from standard input.Output in one line to the standard output an integer that represents the maximum size of the smallest piece when Baumkuchen is cut into three pieces.All input data satisfies the following conditions.

Sugar glider's JOI There are $N$ eucalyptus trees growing in the forest where you live, and these trees are numbered from 1 to $N$.The height of tree $i$ is $H_i$ meters.JOI There are $M$ pairs of trees from which you can jump directly between each other, and the time it takes to jump between each pair of trees is fixed.JOI While you are jumping between trees, your height from the ground decreases by 1 meter per second.In other words, if JOI's current height from the ground is $h$ meters and the time it takes to jump between trees is $t$ seconds, the height from the ground after jumping will be $h - t$ meters.However, if $h - t$ becomes smaller than 0 or larger than the height of the destination tree, you cannot jump.Furthermore, by moving the side of the tree up and down, JOI can increase or decrease its height from the ground within the range of 0 meters to the height of the tree it is currently in.JOI It takes 1 second for you to increase or decrease the height from the ground by 1 meter.JOI is trying to get from tree 1 at a height of $X$ meters to the top of tree $N$ (at a height of $H_N$ meters), and wants to know the minimum amount of time it will take to get there.The height of each tree, information on the set of trees that JOI can jump directly to, and the height of the initial location of JOI are given.Write a program to find the minimum time required to reach the top of tree $N$. Read the following data from standard input.On standard output, output in one line an integer representing the minimum time in seconds it takes to go from the height $X$ meters of tree 1 to the top of tree $N$.However, if there is no such method, output -1 instead.All input data satisfies the following conditions.For example, you can move as follows.1. Climb tree 1 for 50 meters.2. Jump from tree 1 to tree 2.3. Jump from tree 2 to tree 4.4. Jump from tree 4 to tree 5.5. Climb tree 5 for 10 meters.JOI You cannot jump from tree 1 to tree 2.

JOI Your hobby is paper craft.Today as well, JOI, you are trying to make a paper craft work.First, JOI printed $N$ cutting lines on a rectangular piece of paper according to the blueprint.Each cut line is a line segment parallel to the vertical or horizontal sides of the paper.Every piece of paper cut out is used as some kind of component in the work.Naturally, works with a large number of parts are difficult to manufacture.JOI You would like to know how many parts the paper will be divided into when it is cut out along all the cutting lines.Information about the paper size and $N$ cutting lines is given.Write a program that calculates how many parts the paper will be divided into when it is cut along these cutting lines.Read the following data from standard input.Print to standard output a single line containing an integer indicating how many parts the paper is divided into.All input data satisfies the following conditions.For this input, the cutting line will look like the image below.Therefore, the paper is divided into four parts by the cutting line.With this input, the cutting line will look like the image on the next page.Therefore, the paper is divided into five parts by the cutting line.

JOI took a test in six subjects: physics, chemistry, biology, earth science, history, and geography.Each test was scored out of 100 points.JOI-kun selects 3 subjects from 4 subjects: physics, chemistry, biology, and earth science, and 1 subject from 2 subjects: history and geography.When choosing a subject so that the total test score is the highest, JOI, find the total test score for the subject you selected.The input consists of 6 lines, with one integer written on each line.The first line shows JOI's physics test score, A.On the second line, JOI's chemistry test score B is written.On the third line, JOI's biology test score C is written.On the fourth line, JOI's earth science test score D is written.On the fifth line, JOI's history test score E is written.On the 6th line, JOI's geography test score, F, is written.The written integers A, B, C, D, E, and F are all between 0 and 100.JOI Output the total test score for the subject you selected in one line.In input/output example 1, when JOI chooses four subjects: physics, biology, earth science, and history, the total score on the test will be the highest.The scores for physics, biology, earth science, and history are 100, 76, 42, and 10, respectively, so the total test score for the selected subjects is 228.In input/output example 2, when JOI chooses four subjects: chemistry, biology, earth science, and geography, the total score on the test will be the highest.The scores for chemistry, biology, earth science, and geography are 21, 15, 42, and 62, respectively, so the total test score for the selected subjects is 140.In input/output example 2, even if JOI chooses four subjects: physics, chemistry, earth science, and geography, the total score of the selected tests is 140.

N students of JOI High School are lined up in a line from east to west.Student i is the i-th student from the west end of the column.Each student wears a bib with an integer written on it.Initially, an integer A i is written on student i's bib.There are M batons, and the batons are numbered from 1 to M.Perform the following operations for k = 1, 2, ..., M.The operations related to baton k (2 ≦ k ≦ M) are performed after the operations related to baton k - 1 are completed.Given the integer originally written on the student's bib and the number M of batons, write a program to find the integer on each student's bib after the teacher receives the baton M from student N.The input consists of 1 + N lines.In the first line, integers N and M (1 ≦ N ≦ 100, 1 ≦ M ≦ 100) are written separated by spaces, and represent the number of students and the number of batons, respectively.The i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ N) of the following N lines contains an integer A i (1 ≦ A i ≦ 1000), which represents the first integer A i written on student i's bib.The output consists of N lines.In the i-th line (1 ≦ i ≦ N), output the integer number of student i's bib after the teacher receives the baton M from student N.In input/output example 1, there are 6 students.Initially, the students' bib numbers are 3, 2, 8, 3, 1, and 5 in order.There are four batons.
