and pressing the button once will stop the currently spinning leftmost reel.Therefore, you need to press the button N times to get this rare item.Also, in order to stop at a special pattern, you need to press a button at a certain timing.However, since the timing of pressing this button is very critical, it was difficult to get it to work properly.Therefore, Nathan decided to use a memory viewer to analyze the game while checking the memory values ​​during the game.As a result of Nathan's analysis, he discovered that in order for the reels to stop at that special symbol, the "random number" written to address 007E0D1F in memory when the button was pressed had to be a specific value.It was also found that the value of the random number changes every frame using a linear congruential method, and that the determination of whether the button has been pressed is performed once every frame.Here, the linear congruential method is one of the methods for generating pseudorandom numbers, and the value is determined by the following formula.x' = ( A × x + B ) mod C Here, x is the current random number value, x' is the next random number value, and A , B , and C are some constants.Also, y mod z represents the remainder when y is divided by z.For example, suppose a slot machine has two reels, A = 5, B = 7, C = 11, and the first "random number" value is 10.Assume that the conditions for stopping the reels with a special symbol are 2 on the left reel and 4 on the right reel.At this time, the value of the random number in the first frame is (5 × 10 + 7) mod 11 = 2, so if you press the button in the first frame, the left reel will stop at a special symbol.The value of the random number in the second subsequent frame is (5 × 2 + 7) mod 11 = 6, so even if you press the button in the second frame, the reels on the right will not stop at the special symbol.In the following third frame, the random number value becomes (5 × 6 + 7) mod 11 = 4, so if you press the button in the third frame, the right reel will stop at a special symbol.Therefore, all reels can be stopped with a special symbol in as little as 3 frames, and rare items can be obtained.Your job is to help Nathan obtain rare items by writing a program that finds the shortest number of frames that will cause all the reels to stop at a special symbol.

Professor Matsuzaki is a scientist who studies the truth of the universe.It is said that the answer to life, the universe, and everything else is 42, but Professor Matsuzaki believes that this alone is not enough to elucidate the truth of the universe.Professor Matsuzaki believes that the truth of the universe is expressed as a function consisting of two parameters, and 42 is just one of them.The function M( N , P ) defined by Professor Matsuzaki represents the Pth number that appears when all the numbers obtained by selecting two prime numbers larger than N (or two of the same number) and arranging them in order from smallest to largest.Here, there are numbers that can be represented by two or more sums, but such numbers can be arranged in the same number as the number of combinations of sums.As an example, consider the case when N = 0.In this case, we select two of the prime numbers and add them together.If we consider the smallest number among such sums, we can see that 2 + 2 = 4, since it is allowed to choose the same number twice.That is, M(0, 1) = 4.The next smallest number is 2 + 3 = 5, so M(0, 2) = 5.Thinking in the same way, we can see that the sums arranged are 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 10, 12, 13, 14, 14, 16, ....That is, for example, M(0, 9) = 12.In the same way, if we consider the case of N = 10, in this case we will choose two prime numbers larger than 10 {11, 13, 17, 19, ...}, and if we arrange the resulting sums from smallest to largest, we will get 22, 24, 26, 28, 30, 30, 32, ....Your task is to write a program that computes M( N , P ) given N and P.

A tomboyish and brave princess from a poor country ends up marrying into another country for a political marriage.However, a thug who wants to kill the princess releases assassins on her way to her future bride.In order to safely send the princess to the other country, you have already decided on a safe route, but due to the princess's selfish wish to take a route she has never taken before, you decide to take a different route.So you look at the map and decide on the path the princess will take.All roads are roads that connect post stations.For convenience, the departure and destination points will also be designated as post stations.However, the new road had security issues.There is a high possibility that thieves or assassins who want to kill the princess will attack.It is advisable to hire an escort to travel through such a dangerous route.Guards can be hired at post stations, and can be used to protect the princess on a per-do basis.While the guard is protecting you, you will not be attacked by thieves or thugs, but you will be charged 1 gold per 1 distance.Therefore, in order to hire an escort, the distance to the next post must not be longer than the amount of money you have.Now, given the budget L, consider minimizing the number of thieves and assassins who attack the princess before she safely reaches her destination.Your task is to find that minimized number of people.It is assumed that you will not be attacked while staying at the post.

You are a programmer who belongs to a development team for a new type of recording medium.This recording medium allows random access for reading and erasing data.On the other hand, when writing data, data is always accessed sequentially from the beginning, and data can only be written to the first free space found.You have started building a file system for this storage medium.In this file system, due to limitations of the recording medium, data is written in order starting from the free space at the beginning.If an area containing other data is reached during writing, the remaining data is written from the empty area after it.Data is written in units called sectors.Sectors are assigned numbers starting from 0, and these numbers indicate their physical locations on the storage medium.Sector numbers are assigned sequentially from the beginning of the storage medium to the rear as 0, 1, 2, 3, etc.There are three commands for file systems: write, delete, and sector reference.Your job is to reproduce the behavior of this file system and then write a program that outputs which files are located in the target sector when the reference command is executed.Note that nothing is written to the recording medium in the initial state.For example, let's take a look at the first example of Sample Input.The first instruction writes a file with an identifier of 0 and a size of 2.In the initial state, nothing is written to the recording medium, that is, all sectors are empty areas, so writing is performed to the first two sectors, namely the 0th sector and the 1st sector.Therefore, the storage medium after writing is as follows.The second instruction writes the file with identifier 1 to the second and third sectors.The state of the storage medium after this is as follows.The third command deletes the file with an identifier of 0.The state of the storage medium is as follows.The fourth instruction writes the file with the identifier 2 to the 0th, 1st, 4th, and 5th sectors.The last instruction refers to the third sector.Now, the file with the identifier 1 is located in the third sector, so your program must output 1.

Twin adventurers Rin and Len are searching for treasure in the Mirror Cave.This cave has two paired rooms called the Hall of Mirrors, and it is said that a valuable treasure is hidden behind the door of each room.For convenience, each of the two rooms is assumed to be a grid of W × H cells.The outside of the room is surrounded by walls.Also, there are walls installed inside the room in some places, and you cannot enter the cells where the walls are located.In order to open the door to the treasure room, two people must move symmetrically and reach a specific cell at the same time.Moreover, if only one side reaches the door first, the door will be locked and cannot be opened.Symmetrical movement means moving north and north, west and east, east and west, and south and south at the same time.However, even in a situation where there is a wall in front of one side and there is no wall in front of the other side, it is recognized that the movement is symmetrical.In this case, the side with a wall will remain in place, and the side without a wall will move to the next cell.The initial position of the twins, the destination, and the placement of obstacles are given as input.Your task is to write a program to determine whether the twins can open the door.

You are a programmer working at your local city hall.The town you live in has a thriving tourism industry, and the beaches on the coasts of remote islands are especially popular.The area around this island is made up of sandy beaches, but the area of ​​the sandy beaches has been decreasing in recent years due to coastal erosion.The tourism department took the situation seriously and asked you to simulate the changes in the shape of the beach.In the simulation, the shape of the beach after a certain period of time is determined based on the current shape of the beach.For simplicity, the shape of the island's coastline is assumed to be a polygon (not necessarily convex).Assume that during this period, the beach will erode by a distance R from the current coastline to Manhattan and sink into the sea.Here, the Manhattan distance is the sum of the difference between the x-coordinates and the difference between the y-coordinates.That is, the Manhattan distance between ( x 1 , y 1 ) and ( x 2 , y 2 ) is given by | x 1 - x 2 | + | y ​​1 - y 2 |.Your task is to write a program that calculates the length of the coastline obtained as a result of the simulation from the shoreline of a sandy beach given as input.If an island is divided into two or more parts due to coastal erosion, calculate the length of the coastline for each divided island and output the sum of the lengths.Note that two line segments included in the coastline cannot be parallel and have a Manhattan distance of exactly 2 R.

Adam Ivan is working as a system administrator at Soy Group, Inc. He is now facing at a big trouble: a number of computers under his management have been infected by a computer virus. Unfortunately, anti-virus system in his company failed to detect this virus because it was very new. Adam has identified the first computer infected by the virus and collected the records of all data packets sent within his network. He is now trying to identify which computers have been infected. A computer is infected when receiving any data packet from any infected computer. The computer is not infected, on the other hand, just by sending data packets to infected computers. It seems almost impossible for him to list all infected computers by hand, because the size of the packet records is fairly large. So he asked you for help: write a program that can identify infected computers.

You are in a fantasy monster-ridden world. You are a slayer fighting against the monsters with magic spells. The monsters have hit points for each, which represent their vitality. You can decrease their hit points by your magic spells: each spell gives certain points of damage , by which monsters lose their hit points, to either one monster or all monsters in front of you (depending on the spell). Monsters are defeated when their hit points decrease to less than or equal to zero. On the other hand, each spell may consume a certain amount of your magic power . Since your magic power is limited, you want to defeat monsters using the power as little as possible. Write a program for this purpose.

A dial lock is a kind of lock which has some dials with printed numbers. It has a special sequence of numbers, namely an unlocking sequence, to be opened. You are working at a manufacturer of dial locks. Your job is to verify that every manufactured lock is unlocked by its unlocking sequence. In other words, you have to rotate many dials of many many locks. It’s a very hard and boring task. You want to reduce time to open the locks. It’s a good idea to rotate multiple dials at one time. It is, however, a difficult problem to find steps to open a given lock with the fewest rotations. So you decided to write a program to find such steps for given initial and unlocking sequences. Your company’s dial locks are composed of vertically stacked k (1 ≤ k ≤ 10) cylindrical dials. Every dial has 10 numbers, from 0 to 9, along the side of the cylindrical shape from the left to the right in sequence. The neighbor on the right of 9 is 0. A dial points one number at a certain position. If you rotate a dial to the left by i digits, the dial newly points the i -th right number. In contrast, if you rotate a dial to the right by i digits, it points the i -th left number. For example, if you rotate a dial pointing 8 to the left by 3 digits, the dial newly points 1. You can rotate more than one adjacent dial at one time. For example, consider a lock with 5 dials. You can rotate just the 2nd dial. You can rotate the 3rd, 4th and 5th dials at the same time. But you cannot rotate the 1st and 3rd dials at one time without rotating the 2nd dial. When you rotate multiple dials, you have to rotate them to the same direction by the same digits. Your program is to calculate the fewest number of rotations to unlock, for given initial and unlocking sequences. Rotating one or more adjacent dials to the same direction by the same digits is counted as one rotation.

Here we describe a typical problem. There are n balls and n boxes. Each ball is labeled by a unique number from 1 to n . Initially each box contains one of these balls. We can swap two balls in adjacent boxes. We are to sort these balls in increasing order by swaps, i.e. move the ball labeled by 1 to the first box, labeled by 2 to the second box, and so forth. The question is how many swaps are needed. Now let us consider the situation where the balls are doubled, that is, there are 2 n balls and n boxes, exactly two balls are labeled by k for each 1 ≤ k ≤ n , and the boxes contain two balls each. We can swap two balls in adjacent boxes, one ball from each box. We are to move the both balls labeled by 1 to the first box, labeled by 2 to the second box, and so forth. The question is again how many swaps are needed. Here is one interesting fact. We need 10 swaps to sort [5; 4; 3; 2; 1] (the state with 5 in the first box, 4 in the second box, and so forth): swapping 5 and 4, then 5 and 3, 5 and 2, 5 and 1, 4 and 3, 4 and 2, 4 and 1, 3 and 2, 3 and 1,and finally 2 and 1. Then how many swaps we need to sort [5, 5; 4, 4; 3, 3; 2, 2; 1, 1] (the state with two 5’s in the first box, two 4’s in the second box, and so forth)? Some of you might think 20 swaps - this is not true, but the actual number is 15. Write a program that calculates the number of swaps for the two-ball version and verify the above fact.

Open Binary and Object Group organizes a programming contest every year. Mr. Hex belongs to this group and joins the judge team of the contest. This year, he created a geometric problem with its solution for the contest. The problem required a set of points forming a line-symmetric polygon for the input. Preparing the input for this problem was also his task. The input was expected to cover all edge cases, so he spent much time and attention to make them satisfactory. However, since he worked with lots of care and for a long time, he got tired before he finished. So He might have made mistakes - there might be polygons not meeting the condition. It was not reasonable to prepare the input again from scratch. The judge team thus decided to find all line-asymmetric polygons in his input and fix them as soon as possible. They asked a programmer, just you, to write a program to find incorrect polygons. You can assume the following:

Some of you know an old story of Voronoi Island. There were N liege lords and they are always involved in territorial disputes. The residents of the island were despaired of the disputes. One day, a clever lord proposed to stop the disputes and divide the island fairly. His idea was to divide the island such that any piece of area belongs to the load of the nearest castle. The method is called Voronoi Division today. Actually, there are many aspects of whether the method is fair. According to one historian, the clever lord suggested the method because he could gain broader area than other lords. Your task is to write a program to calculate the size of the area each lord can gain. You may assume that Voronoi Island has a convex shape.

A country Gizevom is being under a sneak and fierce attack by their foe. They have to deploy one or more troops to every base immediately in order to defend their country. Otherwise their foe would take all the bases and declare "All your base are belong to us." You are asked to write a program that calculates the minimum time required for deployment, given the present positions and marching speeds of troops and the positions of the bases.

The volume of access to a web service varies from time to time in a day. Also, the hours with the highest volume of access varies from service to service. For example, a service popular in the United States may receive more access in the daytime in the United States, while another service popular in Japan may receive more access in the daytime in Japan. When you develop a web service, you have to design the system so it can handle all requests made during the busiest hours. You are a lead engineer in charge of a web service in the 30th century. It’s the era of Galaxy Wide Web (GWW), thanks to the invention of faster-than-light communication. The service can be accessed from all over the galaxy. Thus many intelligent creatures, not limited to human beings, can use the service. Since the volume of access to your service is increasing these days, you have decided to reinforce the server system. You want to design a new system that handles requests well even during the hours with the highest volume of access. However, this is not a trivial task. Residents in each planet have their specific length of a day , say, a cycle of life. The length of a day is not always 24 hours. Therefore, a cycle of the volume of access are different by planets of users. You have obtained hourly data of the volume of access for all planets where you provide the service. Assuming the volume of access follows a daily cycle for each planet, you want to know the highest volume of access in one hour. It should be a quite easy task for you, a famous talented engineer in the galaxy.

A tatami mat, a Japanese traditional floor cover, has a rectangular form with aspect ratio 1:2. When spreading tatami mats on a floor, it is prohibited to make a cross with the border of the tatami mats, because it is believed to bring bad luck. Your task is to write a program that reports how many possible ways to spread tatami mats of the same size on a floor of given height and width.

Two contries A and B have decided to make a meeting to get acquainted with each other. n ambassadors from A and B will attend the meeting in total. A round table is prepared for in the meeting. The ambassadors are getting seated at the round table, but they have agreed that more than k ambassadors from the same country does not sit down at the round table in a row for deeper exchange. Your task is to write a program that reports the number of possible arrangements when rotations are not counted. Your program should report the number modulo M = 1000003. Let us provide an example. Suppose n = 4 and k = 2. When rotations are counted as different arrangements, the following six arrangements are possible. However, when rotations are regarded as same, the following two arrangements are possible. Therefore the program should report 2.

A linear congruential generator produces a series R(⋅) of pseudo-random numbers by the following for- mulas: where S , A , C , and M are all parameters. In this problem, 0 ≤ S , A , C ≤ 15 and M = 256. Now suppose we have some input string I (⋅), where each character in the string is an integer between 0 and ( M - 1). Then, using the pseudo-random number series R (⋅), we obtain another string O (⋅) as the output by the following formula: Your task is to write a program that shows the parameters S , A , and C such that the information entropy of the output string O (⋅) is minimized. Here, the information entropy H is given by the following formula: where N is the length of the string and #( x ) is the number of occurences of the alphabet x .

Peter is a person with erratic sleep habits. He goes to sleep at twelve o'lock every midnight. He gets up just after one hour of sleep on some days; he may even sleep for twenty-three hours on other days. His sleeping duration changes in a cycle, where he always sleeps for only one hour on the first day of the cycle. Unfortunately, he has some job interviews this month. No doubt he wants to be in time for them. He can take anhydrous caffeine to reset his sleeping cycle to the beginning of the cycle anytime. Then he will wake up after one hour of sleep when he takes caffeine. But of course he wants to avoid taking caffeine as possible, since it may affect his health and accordingly his very important job interviews. Your task is to write a program that reports the minimum amount of caffeine required for Peter to attend all his interviews without being late, where the information about the cycle and the schedule of his interviews are given. The time for move to the place of each interview should be considered negligible.

We understand that reading English is a great pain to many of you. So we’ll keep this problem statememt simple. Write a program that reports the point equally distant from a set of lines given as the input. In case of no solutions or multiple solutions, your program should report as such.

Luigi's Tavern is a thriving tavern in the Kingdom of Nahaila. The owner of the tavern Luigi supports to organize a party, because the main customers of the tavern are adventurers. Each adventurer has a job: hero, warrior, cleric or mage. Any party should meet the following conditions: Now, the tavern has H heroes, W warriors, C clerics and M mages. Your job is to write a program to find the maximum number of parties they can form.

A young boy John is playing with eight triangular panels. These panels are all regular triangles of the same size, each painted in a single color; John is forming various octahedra with them. While he enjoys his playing, his father is wondering how many octahedra can be made of these panels since he is a pseudo-mathematician. Your task is to help his father: write a program that reports the number of possible octahedra for given panels. Here, a pair of octahedra should be considered identical when they have the same combination of the colors allowing rotation.

You are given a tree T that consists of N nodes. Each node is numbered from 1 to N , and node 1 is always the root node of T . Consider the following two operations on T : Your job is to write a program that performs a sequence of these operations on a given tree and calculates the value that each Q operation will print. To avoid too large output file, your program is requested to print the sum of the outputs of all query operations. Note that the judges confirmed that it is possible to calculate every output of query operations in a given sequence.

Alice and Bob are going to drive from their home to a theater for a date. They are very challenging - they have no maps with them even though they don’t know the route at all (since they have just moved to their new home). Yes, they will be going just by their feeling. The town they drive can be considered as an undirected graph with a number of intersections (vertices) and roads (edges). Each intersection may or may not have a sign. On intersections with signs, Alice and Bob will enter the road for the shortest route. When there is more than one such roads, they will go into one of them at random. On intersections without signs, they will just make a random choice. Each random selection is made with equal probabilities. They can even choose to go back to the road they have just come along, on a random selection. Calculate the expected distance Alice and Bob will drive before reaching the theater.

A small country called Maltius was governed by a queen. The queen was known as an oppressive ruler. People in the country suffered from heavy taxes and forced labor. So some young people decided to form a revolutionary army and fight against the queen. Now, they besieged the palace and have just rushed into the entrance. Your task is to write a program to determine whether the queen can escape or will be caught by the army. Here is detailed description.

Wind Corridor is a covered passageway where strong wind is always blowing. It is a long corridor of width W, and there are several pillars in it. Each pillar is a right prism and its face is a polygon (not necessarily convex). In this problem, we consider two-dimensional space where the positive x -axis points the east and the positive y -axis points the north. The passageway spans from the south to the north, and its length is infinity. Specifically, it covers the area 0 ≤ x ≤ W . The outside of the passageway is filled with walls. Each pillar is expressed as a polygon, and all the pillars are located within the corridor without conflicting or touching each other. Wind blows from the south side of the corridor to the north. For each second, w unit volume of air can be flowed at most if the minimum width of the path of the wind is w . Note that the path may fork and merge, but never overlaps with pillars and walls. Your task in this problem is to write a program that calculates the maximum amount of air that can be flowed through the corridor per second.

Mary Ice is a member of a spy group. She is about to carry out a secret operation with her colleague. She has got into a target place just now, but unfortunately the colleague has not reached there yet. She needs to hide from her enemy George Water until the colleague comes. Mary may want to make herself appear in George’s sight as short as possible, so she will give less chance for George to find her. You are requested to write a program that calculates the time Mary is in George’s sight before her colleague arrives, given the information about moves of Mary and George as well as obstacles blocking their sight. Read the Input section for the details of the situation.

Whist is a game played by four players with a standard deck of playing cards. The players seat around a table, namely, in north, east, south, and west. This game is played in a team-play basis: the players seating opposite to each other become a team. In other words, they make two teams we could call the north-south team and the east-west team. Remember that the standard deck consists of 52 cards each of which has a rank and a suit. The rank indicates the strength of the card and is one of the following: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, jack, queen, king, and ace (from the lowest to the highest). The suit refers to the type of symbols printed on the card, namely, spades, hearts, diamonds, and clubs. The deck contains exactly one card for every possible pair of a rank and a suit, thus 52 cards. One of the four players (called a dealer) shuffles the deck and deals out all the cards face down, one by one, clockwise from the player left to him or her. Each player should have thirteen cards. Then the last dealt card, which belongs to the dealer, is turned face up. The suit of this card is called trumps and has a special meaning as mentioned below. A deal of this game consists of thirteen tricks. The objective for each team is winning more tricks than another team. The player left to the dealer leads the first trick by playing one of the cards in his or her hand. Then the other players make their plays in the clockwise order. They have to play a card of the suit led if they have one; they can play any card otherwise. The trick is won by the player with the highest card of the suit led if no one plays a trump, or with the highest trump otherwise. The winner of this trick leads the next trick, and the remaining part of the deal is played similarly. After the thirteen tricks have been played, the team winning more tricks gains a score, one point per trick in excess of six. Your task is to write a program that determines the winning team and their score for given plays of a deal.

This is a story of a world somewhere far from the earth. In this world, the land is parted into a number of countries ruled by empires. This world is not very peaceful: they have been involved in army race. They are competing in production of missiles in particular. Nevertheless, no countries have started wars for years. Actually they have a reason they can’t get into wars - they have missiles much more than enough to destroy the entire world. Once a war would begin among countries, none of them could remain. These missiles have given nothing but scare to people. The competition has caused big financial and psychological pressure to countries. People have been tired. Military have been tired. Even empires have been tired. No one wishes to keep on missile production. So empires and diplomats of all countries held meetings quite a few times toward renouncement of missiles and abandon of further production. The meetings were quite difficult as they have different matters. However, they overcame such difficulties and finally came to the agreement of a treaty. The points include: Let us describe more details on the second point. Each missile has its capability, which represents how much it can destroy the target. The war potential of each country is measured simply by the sum of capability over missiles possessed by that country. The treaty requires the difference to be not greater than d between the maximum and minimum potential of all the countries. Unfortunately, it is not clear whether this treaty is feasible. Every country is going to dispose their missiles only in the order of time they were produced, from the oldest to the newest. Some missiles have huge capability, and disposal of them may cause unbalance in potential. Your task is to write a program to see this feasibility.

Champernown constant is an irrational number represented in decimal by " 0. " followed by concatenation of all positive integers in the increasing order. The first few digits of this constant are: 0.123456789101112... Your task is to write a program that outputs the K digits of Chapnernown constant starting at the N -th place for given two natural numbers K and N .

The sales department of Japanese Ancient Giant Corp. is visiting a hot spring resort for their recreational trip. For deepening their friendships, they are staying in one large room of a Japanese-style hotel called a ryokan. In the ryokan, people sleep in Japanese-style beds called futons. They all have put their futons on the floor just as they like. Now they are ready for sleeping but they have one concern: they don’t like to go into their futons with their legs toward heads — this is regarded as a bad custom in Japanese tradition. However, it is not obvious whether they can follow a good custom. You are requested to write a program answering their question, as a talented programmer. Here let's model the situation. The room is considered to be a grid on an xy -plane. As usual, x -axis points toward right and y -axis points toward up. Each futon occupies two adjacent cells. People put their pillows on either of the two cells. Their heads come to the pillows; their foots come to the other cells. If the cell of some person's foot becomes adjacent to the cell of another person's head, regardless their directions, then it is considered as a bad case. Otherwise people are all right.

Nathan O. Davis is challenging a kind of shooter game. In this game, enemies emit laser beams from outside of the screen. A laser beam is a straight line with a certain thickness. Nathan moves a circular-shaped machine within the screen, in such a way it does not overlap a laser beam. As in many shooters, the machine is destroyed when the overlap happens. Nathan is facing an uphill stage. Many enemies attack simultaneously in this stage, so eventually laser beams fill out almost all of the screen. Surprisingly, it is even possible he has no "safe area" on the screen. In other words, the machine cannot survive wherever it is located in some cases. The world is as kind as it is cruel! There is a special item that helps the machine to survive any dangerous situation, even if it is exposed in a shower of laser beams, for some seconds. In addition, another straight line (called "a warning line") is drawn on the screen for a few seconds before a laser beam is emit along that line. The only problem is that Nathan has a little slow reflexes. He often messes up the timing to use the special item. But he knows a good person who can write a program to make up his slow reflexes - it's you! So he asked you for help. Your task is to write a program to make judgement whether he should use the item or not, for given warning lines and the radius of the machine.

You built an apartment. The apartment has a water tank with a capacity of L in order to store water for the residents. The tank works as a buffer between the water company and the residents. It is required to keep the tank "not empty" at least during use of water. A pump is used to provide water into the tank. From the viewpoint of avoiding water shortage, a more powerful pump is better, of course. But such powerful pumps are expensive. That’s the life. You have a daily schedule table of water usage. It does not differ over days. The table is composed of some schedules. Each schedule is indicated by the starting time of usage, the ending time and the used volume per unit of time during the given time span. All right, you can find the minimum required speed of providing water for days from the schedule table. You are to write a program to compute it. You can assume the following conditions.

Maki is a house cat. One day she fortunately came at a wonderful-looking dried fish. Since she felt not hungry on that day, she put it up in her bed. However there was a problem; a rat was living in her house, and he was watching for a chance to steal her food. To secure the fish during the time she is asleep, she decided to build some walls to prevent the rat from reaching her bed. Maki's house is represented as a two-dimensional plane. She has hidden the dried fish at ( x t , y t ). She knows that the lair of the rat is located at ( x s , y s ). She has some candidate locations to build walls. The i -th candidate is described by a circle of radius r i centered at ( x i , y i ). She can build walls at as many candidate locations as she wants, unless they touch or cross each other. You can assume that the size of the fish, the rat’s lair, and the thickness of walls are all very small and can be ignored. Your task is to write a program which determines the minimum number of walls the rat needs to climb over until he can get to Maki's bed from his lair, assuming that Maki made an optimal choice of walls.

Edward Leven loves multiples of eleven very much. When he sees a number, he always tries to find consecutive subsequences (or substrings) forming multiples of eleven. He calls such subsequences as 11-sequences. For example, he can find an 11-sequence 781 in a number 17819. He thinks a number which has many 11-sequences is a good number. He would like to find out a very good number. As the first step, he wants an easy way to count how many 11-sequences are there in a given number. Even for him, counting them from a big number is not easy. Fortunately, one of his friends, you, is a brilliant programmer. He asks you to write a program to count the number of 11-sequences. Note that an 11-sequence must be a positive number without leading zeros.

Artistic Crystal Manufacture developed products named Crystal Jails. They are cool ornaments forming a rectangular solid. They consist of colorful crystal cubes. There are bright cores on the center of cubes, which are the origin of the name. The combination of various colored reflections shows fantastic dance of lights. The company wanted to make big sales with Crystal Jails. They thought nice-looking color patterns were the most important factor for attractive products. If they could provide several nice patterns, some customers would buy more than one products. However, they didn't have staff who could design nice patterns. So they hired a temporary designer to decide the patterns. The color pattern for mass production had a technical limitation: all cubes of the same color must be connected. In addition, they needed to send the pattern to the factory as a set of blocks , i.e. shapes formed by cubes of the same color. They requested him to represent the design in this form. After a week of work, he sent various ideas of the color patterns to them. At first, his designs looked nice, but they noticed some patterns couldn’t form a rectangular solid. He was a good designer, but they had not noticed he lacked space geometrical sense. They didn't have time to ask him to revise his design. Although it was acceptable to ignore bad patterns, it also took a lot of time to figure out all bad patterns manually. So the leader of this project decided to ask you, a freelance programmer, for help. Your task is to write a program to judge whether a pattern can form a rectangular solid. Note that blocks can be rotated.

Mike Smith is a man exploring caves all over the world. One day, he faced a scaring creature blocking his way. He got scared, but in short time he took his knife and then slashed it to attempt to kill it. Then they were split into parts, which soon died out but the largest one. He slashed the creature a couple of times more to make it small enough, and finally became able to go forward. Now let us think of his situation in a mathematical way. The creature is considered to be a polygon, convex or concave. Mike slashes this creature straight with his knife in some direction. We suppose here the direction is given for simpler settings, while the position is arbitrary. Then all split parts but the largest one disappears. Your task is to write a program that calculates the area of the remaining part when the creature is slashed in such a way that the area is minimized.

Many cats live on the campus of a certain school.Natsume's daily routine is to pet these cats.However, the cats may be on a whim and go for a walk outside of campus.The campus site is rectangular with each side parallel to the x-axis or y-axis, and is surrounded by a fence except for the gate area, but cats can freely enter and exit through the gate, and sometimes climb over the fence to get around.One day, Natsume wanted to know how many cats there were on campus in order to care for them as equally as possible.Your task is to find the number of cats on the campus, given the location of the campus on the coordinate plane and the coordinates of the cats.However, cats walking on the fence or just passing through the gate are considered to be on campus.

Heiankyo is known as a town with streets arranged in a grid pattern.Hokusai, a cat living in Heiankyo, has to go from his home to a secret location on the outskirts of town every day to go on patrol.However, taking the same route every day can get boring, and there is also the risk of being followed, so Hokusai wants to take a different route every day as much as possible.On the other hand, Hokusai is troublesome, so he doesn't want to take a path that takes him away from his destination.There are Actinidia fallen on the roads all over Heiankyo, and Hokusai cannot pass through the roads where Actinidia has fallen.This is because if you take such a path, you will become sluggish.Fortunately, there were no Actinidia fallen at the intersection.Hokusai wants to know the number of possible routes from his home to his secret location.Here, Hokusai's home is at (0, 0) and his secret location is at (g x, g y).The roads are laid out in a grid pattern at x = i (i is an integer), y = j (j is an integer).

Cats Gates and Jackie came up with rules for a card game played by two people.The rules are as follows.First, shuffle the cards with numbers 1 to 18 written on them and deal 9 cards to each player.Both players play one card at the same time and get a score based on its value.The player who played the higher value card adds the sum of the higher value and the lower value to his score.At that time, the player who plays a card with a low value will not receive a score.Also, once a card is put into play, it cannot be used again.The player with the highest score after all cards have been used wins.Gates and Jackie decide to play each other a random card.Given the initial cards dealt, find the probability that Gates and Jackie each win.

In the International System of Units (SI), various physical quantities are expressed in the form of ``number + prefix + unit'' using prefixes such as kilo, mega, giga.For example, "3.5 kilometers" or "5.1 milligrams".On the other hand, these physical quantities can also be expressed using exponential notation, such as "3.5 * 10^3 meters" or "5.1 * 10^-3 grams."Measurements of physical quantities always include errors.Therefore, the concept of significant figures is used to express the precision of a measured physical quantity.When notation takes significant figures into account, the last digit may contain an error, but other digits are considered reliable.For example, if you write "1.23", the true value is 1.225 or more and less than 1.235, and the first decimal place and above are reliable, but the second decimal place contains an error.The number of digits when a physical quantity is expressed in the form of ``reliable digit + 1 digit including error'' is called the number of significant digits.For example, "1.23" has 3 significant digits.If there is a 0 before the most significant non-zero digit, that 0 is not included in the number of significant digits.For example, "0.45" has two significant digits.If there is a 0 after the least significant non-zero digit, whether or not that 0 is included in the number of significant digits depends on the position of the decimal point.If there are 0s to the right of the decimal point, those 0s are included in the number of significant digits.For example, "12.300" has 5 significant digits.On the other hand, when there are no 0s to the right of the decimal point, such as "12300", it is not clear whether the 0s on the right should be included in the number of significant digits, but in this problem they will be included.In other words, "12300" has 5 significant digits.Natsume was given a physics problem for her school assignment.They have to do calculations that involve significant figures and units, but the problem is that Natsume doesn't quite understand how to use significant figures or units yet.Therefore, I would like you to help Natsume by creating a program that automatically calculates these things.You are writing a program that, given a prefix notation, converts it to exponential notation with the same number of significant digits.The following 20 prefixes are used.

Favre the cat comes up with a simple game using addition and decides to try it out with his friend Audrey, who is also a cat.The rules of the game are as follows.First, choose a positive integer and start from there.Each player selects two adjacent digits of the number to calculate the sum and replace the original two digits.For example, if you select the tens and hundreds digits of "1234", the next number will be "154".If you select the tens and hundreds places of "5555", it will be "5105".These operations are repeated alternately until the number reaches one digit, and the player who can no longer perform the operations loses.An integer value is given at the start of the game.Write a program to determine who will win when Favre, who bats first, and Audrey, who bats second, both take the optimal strategy.

An angel lives in the clouds floating above the city where Natsume lives.The angel, like Natsume, loves cats, and often comes down to earth to play with them.In order to descend to the earth, the angel built a long staircase leading from the clouds to the earth.However, the angel thought that it would be boring to just go down the stairs every time, so he modified the stairs so that they made a sound when he stepped on them.Using this, they descend while playing music.Music is played using 12 types of sounds.This time, we will ignore the octave of the sound and consider only 12 types: C, C#, D, D#, E, F, F#, G, G#, A, A#, and B.The difference between adjacent notes is called a semitone.For example, raising C by a semitone becomes C#, and raising C# by a semitone becomes D.Conversely, lowering G by a semitone becomes F#, and lowering F# by a semitone becomes F.Please note that raising E by a semitone will result in F, and raising B by a semitone will result in C.The mechanism by which sound is emitted from the stairs is as follows.First, the staircase consists of n white boards floating in the air.One of the 12 tones is assigned to each of the 1st to nth boards counting from the cloud.Let us write this as T i ( i = 1 ... n ).Also, for simplicity, consider the clouds to be the 0th step and the ground to be the n+1th step (however, no scale is assigned to these).When an angel is in step k ( k = 0 ... n ), it can go to any of the k -1, k +1, k +2 steps that exist next.However, once you land on step n+1 (ground), you cannot move, and once you leave step 0 (cloud), you cannot return there.When each movement is performed, a sound is produced according to the following rules.Information about the stairs T 1 ... T n and the song S 1 ... S m that the angel would like to play are given.At this time, do not play any other notes before, during, or after the song you want to play.Determine whether an angel can descend from the clouds to the earth while playing this song.

Natsume is a big cat lover.One day, Natsume is invited by the stray cats she is always close to to go to a mysterious bookstore opened by the cats.Natsume hears that the bookstore sells books with pictures of many cats, so she happily decides to go along.What Natsume didn't know was that the bookstore the cats took her to was a famous store with a chain of stores all over the country.The store has a well-developed staff manual, and staff behavior is consistent at all stores.Below is an excerpt from that manual.Given X, Y, the location of customers, and the location of staff, calculate how many seconds it takes for the store to become quiet after a customer enters.If the repeat doesn't stop, print out "You're always welcome!"

Natsume is a big cat lover.Today, Natsume decided to head to a corner of the schoolyard where stray cats usually gather to brush the stray cats as part of her daily routine.Today, n cats gathered at that spot.Natsume wanted to brush everyone, but just before she got there, she suddenly had something to do, so she was only able to brush one cat in time.All cats look forward to brushing, so if only one cat does it, the other cats will get jealous.In order to convince the cats, we decided to use a lottery to decide which cat would be brushed.Amidakuji consists of n vertical lines and m horizontal lines drawn in advance.A horizontal line must connect two vertical lines perpendicular to each other.Also, horizontal lines must not share end points.A hit mark is placed at the bottom of one vertical line.Examples of Amidakuji that meet these conditions are shown in the figure (these correspond to examples of sample input).Each cat chooses one from the top of the vertical line.The rule is that the cat that wins the lottery will win the right to have the cat brushed today.Natsume immediately created an Amidakuji.I wrote down the vertical lines, horizontal lines, and hits, and hid the horizontal lines so they couldn't be seen.When it was finally time for the cats to choose, Natsume noticed that among the n cats, there was a stray cat named Akutagawa, who doesn't usually show his face.Akutagawa hasn't been brushed for about a month, and his fur is stiff.Although Natsume feels bad for the other cats, she decides to cheat and arrange to brush Akutagawa.First, have each cat choose a vertical line as usual.Once everyone has made their selections, Natsume secretly adds some horizontal lines and changes the Amidakuji so that the Akutagawa will be a hit.However, depending on the vertical lines chosen by the akutagawa, there may be too many new horizontal lines drawn in order for the akutagawa to win, and the cats may discover the plan while crafting.So Natsume first decided to find out for each vertical line how many horizontal lines would have to be drawn to win if Akutagawa chose that line.Note that the new horizontal line drawn must also connect two vertical lines perpendicular to each other, and must be drawn so that the horizontal lines do not share an end point.

Natsume is a big cat lover.Natsume's house didn't have a cat for a long time, and Natsume, who loves cats, was always playing with stray cats.However, this time Natsume made up her mind and decided to keep a cat at her house.Natsume takes the cat home, names him Lennon, and begins to pet him.Natsume's house consists of many rooms and many doors that connect them, and there are two types of doors:Lennon loves summer.So when winter came around and the outside of his house was covered in white snow, he became in a very bad mood.However, he seemed to believe that one of the many doors in his house led to "summer."Natsume calls the door "the door to summer."And when Lennon gets cold and moody, he always wants to go behind that door.One winter day, Lennon decided to go inside the ``Door to Summer'' again.However, there is no guarantee that Lennon will be able to open the door alone and go inside the door to summer.At that time, of course, Natsume has to help Lennon.In other words, he opens some doors that only Natsume can open, allowing Lennon to go to the other side of the "Door to Summer."At first, all the doors in the house are closed.The connections of the rooms in the house and the initial positions of Natsume and Lennon are given.When Natsume and Lennon adopt the optimal strategy, calculate the minimum number of doors that Natsume must open in order for Lennon to go beyond the ``Door to Summer''.The diagram below illustrates an example of sample input.

Natsume is a big cat lover.On Natsume's school route, there is a house commonly known as the Nekoyashiki.The house was famous for having many cats, and Natsume often encountered them in front of the house on her way to school and played with them.One day, Natsume learned a shocking truth.It turns out that the owner of the cat house often mistreats his cats when he is in a bad mood.Natsume, who can no longer forgive the owner of the cat house, decides to steal the cats while the owner is away in order to save them.Natsume observes the behavioral patterns of the owner of the cat house and decides to steal the cats when he is out every week.The cat mansion is represented as a two-dimensional plane, and the position of each cat is known at the time Natsume sneaks into the mansion.Each cat is constantly circling a fixed route at a speed of 50 meters per minute.On the other hand, Natsume can move at speeds of up to 80 meters per minute.Natsume enters the mansion from a certain place, moves around the mansion, and exits through the exit before the master returns.When Natsume reaches the same point as the cat, she can hold the cat.The time it takes to do this is negligible.Natsume can hold as many cats as she wants, and her movement speed will not slow down no matter how many cats she carries, but she must escape from the mansion before her master returns.Unfortunately, Natsume may not be able to steal all the cats from the cat house.However, in order to make as many cats happy as possible, he decided to steal as many cats as possible.Also, if you can steal the same number of cats, escape from the mansion as soon as possible to reduce the risk of being caught by the owner of the mansion.The time and location when Natsume enters the mansion, the time when the owner of the mansion returns, the location of the escape exit, and the cat's initial location and patrol route are given.I want you to write a program that will tell you how many cats Natsume steals and at what time she can escape from the mansion.

Aristotle the cat has a hobby of thinking about mathematical problems.The questions he is currently thinking about are as follows.For natural numbers A and B (A < B) greater than or equal to 1, if C is the sum of the natural numbers from A to B, and D is the combination of A and B in decimal notation, then the two may be equal.For example, the sum of 1 to 5 is 15, the sum of 2 to 7 is 27, the sum of 7 to 119 is 7119, and so on.We will call such pairs of A and B cat numbers.Aristotle wanted to enumerate all the cat numbers given the number of digits in A and B.Aristotle came up with a way to check whether a solution existed when A or B were fixed, so he was able to obtain answers for small numbers of digits.However, as the number of digits increased, the calculations became more difficult, and I got fed up and gave up halfway through.So, I have a request for you.I would like you to write a program to enumerate all cat numbers with a specified number of digits on Aristotle's behalf.

You won the entrance exam battle that lasted several months and were able to successfully enter ICPC University.On the day of the admission procedure, an enthusiastic club recruitment campaign is underway on the university campus, and you return home having received a large amount of pamphlets.When you returned to your room, you found a piece of pamphlet that caught your attention.The pamphlet had been given to me by the university's public relations department.The pamphlet addressed the following issues:Curious about the answer to this problem, you decide to write a program to find out the answer.Therefore, your task is to write a program that outputs the answer to a problem given a positive integer N as input.

You are passionate about the popular web game ``Moonlight Ranch.''The goal of this game is to grow crops in the field, sell them to earn income, and use that income to grow your ranch.You wanted to grow your field quickly.Therefore, I decided to start by arranging the crops that can be grown in the game based on their income efficiency per hour.To grow crops, you must buy seeds.Here, the name of the species of crop i is given by L i and the price is given by P i .When a seed is planted in a field, it will sprout after time A i.Young leaves appear after time B i after buds appear.Leaves grow thick after time C i after young leaves appear.Flowers bloom a time D i after the leaves grow thick.A fruit will appear after a time E i after the flower blooms.One seed produces F i fruits, and each of these fruits can be sold for a price S i .Some crops are multi-season crops and produce a total of M i crops.In the case of a single-season crop, it is expressed as M i = 1.A multi-season crop returns to leaves after fruiting until the M ith fruit is produced.The income for a seed is the amount for which all fruit from that seed is sold minus the price of the seed.In addition, the income efficiency of a seed is the value obtained by dividing the income by the time from when the seed is planted until all the fruits have formed.Your job is to write a program that takes input crop information and sorts it in descending order of income efficiency.

Differential pulse code modulation is one of the compression methods mainly used to compress audio signals.Audio signals are treated as an integer sequence (impulse sequence) on a computer.The integer sequence is the input signal sampled at regular time intervals and the amplitude recorded.In general, this integer sequence tends to have close values ​​before and after it.Differential pulse code modulation uses this to encode the difference between the previous and subsequent values ​​and improve the compression rate.In this problem, we will consider selecting the difference value from a predetermined set of values.We call this set of values ​​a codebook.The audio signal y n after decoding is determined by the following formula.Here, k n is the output sequence output by the program, and C [ j ] is the j-th value of the codebook.However, if y n becomes a value less than 0 due to addition, it will be rounded to 0, and if it becomes a value greater than 255, it will be rounded to 255.Also, the value of y 0 is 128.Your job is to write a program that, given an input signal and a codebook, selects an output sequence such that the sum of squared differences between the original input signal and the decoded output signal is minimized, and outputs the sum of squared differences.For example, when compressing the sequence 131, 137 using the set of values {4, 2, 1, 0, -1, -2, -4} as a codebook, the sum of squares becomes (131 -132)^2 + (137 - 136)^2 = 2, which is the minimum.Similarly, when compressing the sequence 131, 123 using the set of values {4, 2, 1, 0, -1, -2, -4} as the codebook, y 0 = 128 , y 1 = 128 + 1 = 129 , y 2 = 129 - 4 = 125, which is closer to 131 than in the previous example.By not using +2, we can obtain a smaller sum of squares, which is (131 - 129) ^ 2 + (123 - 125) ^ 2 = 8.The two examples above are the first two examples of sample input.

English text is not available in this practice contest. One day, a nobleman fell in love with a tomboyish and brave princess from a poor country and asked her to marry her.The princess made certain conditions for the nobleman.The condition was that he bring a large amount of gems called ``immortal gems''.The Gem of Immortality is an extremely rare gem that can only be found in a specific location on a mountain.Moreover, it is extremely fragile, so a special method was required to collect it.The jewels of immortality are circular and exist in multiple numbers in two-dimensional space.To get these gems, you need to attract them with a special metal rod.A metal rod is a straight line with infinite length, and its thickness can be ignored.Each gemstone has a magnetic force of different strength, and if the metal is close enough to react to the magnetic force, the gemstone will be attracted.Specifically, when the distance between the metal and the surface of the gem is d, and the strength of the gem's magnetic force is m, the gem will be attracted to the metal if .Conversely, if the distance between the metal bar and the jewel is greater than their magnetic force, they cannot be attracted to each other.Also, if the rod penetrates even a small amount of jewelry, the jewelry will be broken and cannot be absorbed.Let's look at an example.The figure below is an example of a jewel placed in two-dimensional space.Assume that there are jewels 1 to 6, and their magnetic forces are 1, 0, 1, 1, 1, and 2, respectively.The figure below is an example of placing metal rods in addition to the figure above.The results of adsorbing gemstones are also shown in the table.In this example, jewel 3 is farther away than the magnetic force can reach, and jewel 4 is penetrated by the rod, so it cannot be attracted, but the remaining four can all be attracted.The aristocrat poured all his wealth into desperately searching for a special metal rod.However, this metal was also extremely valuable, so in the end only one piece could be obtained.Therefore, there is only one chance for adsorption.You are a programmer who works for a noble family.Your task is to write a program that calculates the maximum number of gems that can be absorbed by properly arranging metal rods for a given two-dimensional gem arrangement.

ACM country has a river that flows through the center from east to west.This river flows from the neighboring country in the west through the ACM country to the neighboring country in the east, and its total length within ACM country is K km.It is planned to install several locks on this river and use it as a canal.A lock is a mechanism that allows ships to move between two different water levels.A lock has two water gates on the upstream and downstream sides, and a small body of water called a lock chamber between them.After a ship is placed in this lock chamber, water is injected or drained, raising and lowering the water level in the lock chamber, thereby raising and lowering the ship.A schematic diagram is shown below.Since this river is not very wide, it has been decided that it will be a one-way canal from west to east.In order to operate the canal efficiently, those in charge of the design want to optimize the locations of the locks to match the expected ship navigation schedules.You are a programmer employed by a designer.Your task is to write a program that, given lock information and navigation schedules for multiple ships, uses simulation to find the time it takes for all ships to pass through the river.Each lock is represented by the following information.For convenience, in the simulation, it is assumed that the river continues indefinitely outside the ACM as well.At the start of the simulation, the water level in all locks is at the lower of the east and west water levels.In addition, the ships included in the sailing schedule are assumed to be lined up every 1 km from east to west in the order given by input, with the western edge of ACM country as the starting point.For convenience, let the initial position of the first ship be the 0km point.At the start of the simulation, the ship begins sailing eastward.At this time, no other vessel may be within 1 km in front of or behind the vessel.Each ship has a maximum speed V (km/h) set.A ship can reach any speed in an instant and even come to a standstill in an instant.Basically, ships sail at maximum speed, but if the maximum speed of the following ship is faster than the preceding ship, and the following ship catches up to 1 km before the preceding ship, the following ship will sail at the same speed as the preceding ship.The size of the ship and the lock can be ignored.A ship can enter a lock only when the water level on the west side of the lock is equal to the water level in the lock chamber.Similarly, exiting a lock is only possible when the water level on the east side of the lock is equal to the water level in the lock chamber.The water level in each lock, if there is no ship inside, rises or falls until it matches the water level on the west side of that lock.Also, if there is a ship, it will be displaced until it matches the water level on the east side of the lock.Even if a ship is anchored exactly 1 km ahead of the lock, the ship can leave the lock.However, in this case, the ship must anchor at the point where it exits the lock until the preceding ship departs.After passing the eastern edge of the ACM country, the ship sails as fast as possible to infinity.The simulation ends when all ships have passed the eastern edge of the ACM country.

This was a time when magic was still commonplace.A family of wizards lived on a square-shaped island created by magic.One day, a crisis came to this island.The Empire developed an intercontinental ballistic missile and set its sights on this island.Magic in this world can be classified into earth attributes, water attributes, fire attributes, wind attributes, etc., and magic missiles also belong to one of these four attributes.There are earth crystals, water crystals, fire crystals, and wind crystals placed in each of the four corners of the island, and by sending magical power into the crystals, you can block magical attacks of the corresponding attributes.The wizards were barely able to repel an attack by an intercontinental ballistic missile developed by the Empire.However, the Empire has developed a new intercontinental ballistic missile that belongs to the dark attribute, and has launched an attack on this island again.Magic that belongs to the dark attribute cannot be prevented by the crystals installed on this island.Therefore, the magicians decided to use a light-attribute defense formation that had been passed down to this island as a secret technique to block the missiles.This light-attribute defensive formation is activated by drawing a magic circle with a specific shape on the ground.A magic circle is a figure that divides the circumference of a circle with radius R into M equal parts and connects every K parts with a straight line.The magic circle can be drawn in any size, but the direction is important for the activation of magic, so it must be drawn with one corner facing due north.The area affected by the defensive circle corresponds to the inside (including the boundary) of the drawn magic circle.Also, the energy required to chant this magic is equal to the radius R of the drawn magic circle.Figure G-1 shows the case where M = 4 and K = 1.The gray area is the area affected by the magic square.Figure G-2 shows the case when M = 6 and K = 2.When multiple shapes occur as shown in the figure, if it is included in any of those shapes, it will be effective as a defensive formation.Your task is to write a program that finds the minimum amount of energy required to cover all the settlements, given the locations of the settlements and the values ​​of M and K.

Yui Hirasawa, a student at Sakuragaoka Girls' Private High School, has to submit her career choice by the day after tomorrow, but unfortunately, she hasn't decided on her career path yet.After consulting with his friend Kazu, he learned that Kazu's first choice was K University, so he went to his career advisor to see if he could get into K University as well.You, the career guidance teacher consulted, decide to use Yui's final exam scores as a reference in order to predict whether she will get into K University.However, since Yui's grades are greatly affected by whether or not she hits the top of the exam, she decided to look up her best and worst scores from past final exams.Data from past final exams only remained for the scores for each of the five subjects, and not the total scores for the exams.Therefore, your job is to write a program that takes the score data of each exam as input and outputs the best and worst scores from past final exams.

You have successfully completed your admission to higher education and are in the middle of preparing to move from the Komaba area to the Hongo area.When I was cleaning out my house, I found a large amount of lottery tickets that I bought last year.I checked the redemption deadline and found out that I have to cash it in by tomorrow, but since I haven't finished any preparations for moving yet, it's a hassle to go to the lottery ticket office to collect my small winnings.So you decide to find out how much money you can win.A lottery ticket consists of 8 digits.The winning number is also 8 digits and consists of numbers and '*', and if the number part matches the lottery ticket you have, you will be paid the winning amount according to the winning number.For example, if the lottery number you own is "12345678", you will win if the winning number is "******8" or "****5678", but you will not win if the winning number is "****4678".There can be multiple winning numbers, and winnings are paid out independently for each number.However, the winning numbers are chosen in such a way that no one lottery ticket can hit more than one winning number.For example, if the winning numbers are "******8" and "****5678", "12345678" will win both, so the winning numbers will not be selected in this way.Your job is to write a program that outputs how much money you will win given the input lottery numbers you have and the winning lottery numbers.

Masa Kita, who entered the University of Tokyo, joined a club called TSG (Tokyo University Super Gamers).This circle exhibits games at the Komaba Festival every year, and Masa Kita decided to create and exhibit a game as well.The game created by Masa Kita is a common falling object puzzle game like the one below.Masa Kita had finished writing most of the program for this game, but he couldn't write the process for when blocks stick together and disappear.So I decided to ask you, a friend from the circle, to write that part of the program.

Do you know what these are?These are all numbers attached to powers of 10.Have you ever seen these in practical use?Isn't there one?It is a shame that these numbers never see the light of day, even though our predecessors in the past chose these names after careful consideration.This problem was created to help contest participants learn about these number words.An overview of the problem is shown below.The relationship between units as described above is given as input.In this problem, only relationships between units are given where the units on the left side are powers of 10 times the units on the right side.A contradictory relationship between units means that the following relationships hold simultaneously from a given relationship:However, x≠y, and A and B are arbitrary units.Determine whether the relationships between the units given as input are inconsistent.Note that the input is given in exponential notation for simplicity.

The story of yesterday evening.After my lectures at the university ended and I completed my daily routine of feeding the cats on campus, I arrived home to find people in work clothes working on replacing the door to my house.If it were just that, it wouldn't have caused any more headaches for me, but the brown door with the familiar lock and lever that was removed and left was very ordinary, while the black door that was about to be installed was very strange, and I could easily imagine that my father had come up with something else.The bigoted father says that he can't trust the key because there's a chance he'll lose it.Even if you have the key with you, a copy of it may have been made while you were away.Gone are the days when we relied on physical objects for security.From now on, each member of my family will have to remember the password, and in doing so will protect my inventions from evil scientists bent on world domination.On the newly installed door, N+1 lines vertically and N+1 lines horizontally were drawn in light blue, forming a square with N squares vertically and N squares horizontally.The row numbers are carefully written: A to the left of the square in the first row, B to the left of the square in the second row, and so on.The column numbers appear to be numbers, with 1 above the square in the first row and 2 above the square in the second row.There is a switch in each of the N2 squares, and when it's off, it's completely black, but when it's on, a red circle appears, which is a gimmick that could be done a little more if you're thinking about security.And the door opens only when all the switches are turned on and off correctly, which seems to be a specification that my dad, who loves flashy things, will be very happy with.Now, my problem here is that I'm not confident that I can remember this password properly.I don't think I'm that stupid, so I think I'll be able to remember most of the passwords, but machines are inflexible, so if I make a slight mistake, I could end up spending the night out in the cold.However, if you copy the password and carry it with you, you can expect that if your father finds out, he will impose unreasonable sanctions on you, such as having you lose your pocket money.So I came up with a strategy.Make a note of the number of red circles in each square in each row and column and carry it with you.If you write down 2N numbers, such as the number of red circles in the N squares in the first row, the N numbers in the second row, the number of red circles in the N squares in the first row, the number of red circles in the second row, and so on, even if someone else sees it, it's just a list of numbers.You probably won't know what it is.Hey there, you who took the trouble to read this far.Ah yes, this strategy may have a fatal flaw.Multiple passwords may end up with the same memo.If that happens, we might end up with cold skies (omitted).This is a major problem that threatens our health.Therefore, I would like you to write a program that, when inputting the numbers in this memo, determines whether or not there is only one password that can be recovered.It's a program that just outputs "Yes" if it's fixed, and "No" if it's not.Isn't it easy?Maybe I miscounted the red circle when I took notes.In that case, there may not be a single password that can be recovered, but in that case, don't hesitate to answer No.Oh yeah, please don't try to enter my house without permission.There are dangerous machines and traps that are part of my father's hobby, so I can't guarantee your life.

UTF-8 is a method for encoding multibyte characters.Characters are treated as byte sequences on computers.In English, the Latin alphabet, numbers, and symbols can be expressed in 1 byte, but unfortunately the characters used all over the world cannot be expressed in 1 byte, so it is necessary to use multiple bytes to represent characters.For example, if you assign the 2-byte string 12354 (0x3042) to the character "a", if you line up the byte strings as they are, you won't be able to tell whether 0x30,0x42 is two characters or 0x3042 is one character.Therefore, UTF-8, a multi-byte character encoding system, overcomes this problem by allowing the length of the following byte string to be determined from the first byte.The specific bit pattern is as follows.Here x is any bit between 0 and 1.Also, y can be any bit between 0 and 1, but one of them must be 1.It is also assumed that all characters are 1, 2, 3, or 4 byte characters.The reason for the restriction that at least one bit of y is 1 is as follows.For example, when encoding '/' (0x2f) into a byte string, there are two ways to encode it: 0x2f and 1 byte, or 0xc0 0xaf and 2 bytes, but allowing such ambiguity may pose a security threat.After hearing the above, you may be wondering how much freedom UTF-8 has.Specifically, when some bits of a given byte string are unknown, how many combinations of byte strings are possible as UTF-8?Find the number of possible combinations of byte strings in this case, divide by 1,000,000, and print the remainder.

One day in 2012.You live in a rural town in the north and decide to take your beloved daughter to an observatory to see the stars.Unlike the big city, this town has few street lights and the air is clear, so on clear days you can see a sky full of stars.You're reasonably knowledgeable about astronomy, and you know about the famous stars and constellations of each season, as well as the myths surrounding them, but this is a town where stars fall, and you can see many constellations whose names you don't know.You couldn't answer your daughter's questions about the stars during stargazing the other day, so you wanted to find out which constellations could be seen at the time of stargazing.A planisphere is a useful item to find out which stars are visible at a particular date and time.Recently, planisphere apps are also available that are linked to the GPS on mobile devices.But unfortunately, you don't have a planisphere with you, and your phone is seven years old, so there's no way the latest apps will work.Thinking you were at a loss, you scoured your hard disk for your last hope and discovered a file that recorded the positions of the stars that made up each constellation as of midnight on January 1, 2012.With this, you should be able to find the position of the stars on any date and time!You decide to write a program to print out which constellations are visible.Information about stars is given as a group of coordinates on the celestial sphere centered on the observer.Coordinates are expressed as azimuth and altitude pairs.Azimuth is an angle between 0 degrees and less than 360 degrees measured clockwise, such as 0 degrees due north and 90 degrees due east, and altitude is an angle between -90 degrees and 90 degrees, with 0 degrees above the horizon, 90 degrees above the zenith, and -90 degrees above the nadir.Each celestial body rotates counterclockwise on the celestial sphere at a constant speed, with the axis of rotation being a straight line connecting the North Star and the observer.Its rotation speed is (1+1/365.24)×360 degrees per 24 hours.Polaris is located at an altitude of 43.2 degrees in the direction of true north.When a constellation is visible, it means that all the stars that make up that constellation are above the horizon.In the test data, it may be assumed that there is no star within an altitude of ±0.01 degrees from the horizon at that time.Note that there is no need to consider the deviation of the earth's axis or the distance to celestial bodies.Also, note that 2012 is a leap year.

Western confectionery store Stray Cats has many cats.There are so many cats that Nozomi, a staff member at this store, is having trouble feeding them.Even if you put food in several places, cats are greedy and will go for the food closest to them, resulting in too many cats concentrating in one food bowl.You decide to solve a problem for Nozomi, asking her to arrange the food so that the cat won't concentrate as much as possible.To simplify the problem, assume that the cat is on the plane where y>0 and does not move until the food is placed.Cats are divided into N groups, and there may be many cats in one place.The food containers are fixed at point M on y=0, and you will choose some of them to fill with food.When you put food in, the cat will go to the closest food bowl (in Euclidean distance) that contains food, but if there are two at the same distance, it will go to the one with the smaller x coordinate.Under these conditions, minimize the number of cats in the feeding bowl where the most cats are gathered.

A jewel, a national treasure of the Pal Kingdom, has been stolen by thieves.You, an adventurer, heard the rumor and headed to the thief's hideout, where you managed to retrieve the jewel.However, when he went to return the jewel to the castle in the Kingdom of Palu, the castle's guard told him, ``My king still doesn't trust you completely. He doubts whether the jewel is real, and the fact that he got the jewel back was a lie in the first place, and he suspects that you may be part of a group of thieves who are aiming for the king's life.''The guards have given you a test to see if you are truly trustworthy.The content of the trial was, ``Go to the underground warehouse of the castle and place the jewel in the designated place. There is a magic circle written there that will react if the jewel is real.
