However, to prevent you from doing anything suspicious, you must not follow the movement pattern given by the guard until you head from the entrance of the underground warehouse to the designated place.''For example, for the underground warehouse shape and prohibited pattern shown in the figure below, the movement pattern shown in the figure must not be taken.This is because a part of the movement pattern (highlighted in red) is included in the prohibited pattern.(Also, the second and third movements "↓↓" in this movement pattern are also included in the prohibited pattern.) On the other hand, the movement pattern shown below is allowed because no part of the movement pattern is included in the prohibited pattern.The shape of the underground warehouse and the prohibited pattern are given as input.Find the minimum number of moves required to move from the entrance to the magic circle without taking any prohibited patterns.

Masa Kita is researching various transformations at university.The conversions that Kitamasa has been interested in recently are as follows.Fix N+1 integers a 0 , ..., a N and take an integer z as input.Also, let P be a prime number.Masa Kita noticed that there are many z's that become t = 0 due to this transformation.So, Masa Kita asked you, a friend and super programmer, to calculate how many z's between 0 and P-1 that make t = 0 after conversion.

In 20XX, an efficient method for transferring matter to distant locations was established, and humanity's advancement into space was further accelerated.This transfer method was innovative in that it could in principle transfer larger amounts of material if it were to be transferred farther.Details of the transfer method are described below.First, the material to be transferred is divided into particles of each unit mass at the start coordinates (1, 1).Each particle must be given a different wave energy.Wave energy is expressed as a string of arbitrary length consisting of V and H, and defines how particles drift in space.V means movement on coordinates (+1, 0), and H means movement on coordinates (0, +1).For example, a particle given wave energy VHHV drifts through space along the trajectory (2, 1), (2, 2), (2, 3), and then arrives at the coordinates (3, 3).Additionally, there are multiple warp holes in space, which affect particle trajectories.A warp hole has an entrance and an exit, and if a particle moves to the entrance of a warp hole, it will always warp to the coordinates of the exit.If the coordinates of the entrance and exit of the i-th warp hole are (a i , b i ), (c i , d i ), then the condition a i ≤ c i , b i ≤ d i , (a i , b i ) ≠ (c i , d i ) is satisfied, and it is guaranteed that the entrance of the warp hole does not exist at the same coordinates as the entrance of other warp holes or at (1, 1).However, there are cases where there are multiple exits at the same location, or where the exit of one warp hole is the entrance of another warp hole (in this case, the warp is continuous).For example, if there is a warp hole with (1, 2) as the entrance and (3, 2) as the exit, a particle given HH and wave energy will move to (1, 2), then warp to (3, 2), and then arrive at (3, 3).When particles are given wave energy, they instantly move accordingly.When all particles reach their destination at the same time, they are automatically reassembled into the original substance.You are a programmer for the Japan Space Agency.Given the target coordinates of transfer (N, M) and the coordinate pairs of K warp holes, I would like you to write a program to find the maximum mass of matter that can be transferred at once.The answer can be a very large number, so print the remainder after dividing by 1,000,000,007.

JAVA (Japan Aerospace Voyage Agency) launched a probe in 2003 as part of its space development project.One of the purposes of this spacecraft launch is to approach the asteroid Ishikawa and collect samples of its surface materials.Despite encountering various accidents, the spacecraft reached Ishikawa, and finally returned to Earth in 2010, successfully recovering the capsule containing the Ishikawa sample.Masa Kita, who graduated from the University of Tokyo and started working at JAVA, is in charge of researching this sample.We would like to analyze the sample as soon as possible, but Kitamasa, who is extremely cautious, thought that if there was a ferocious alien inside the capsule, we would be in trouble.Therefore, before opening the capsule, they decided to use X-rays to examine the shape of the material inside to confirm that there were no aliens inside.When the capsule is exposed to X-rays, a photograph is obtained in which areas where the substance is present appear white, and areas where the substance is absent appear black.By taking X-rays from various angles and taking pictures, it is possible to determine the shape of the material inside.However, another problem arose here.The X-ray machine malfunctioned just after taking three photos: from the front, from the right side, and from the top.Furthermore, there is a possibility that the development of the three photographs failed, and some parts that should have been black ended up being white.As it is, it is not possible to accurately identify the shape of the substance, so it is impossible to confirm that there are no aliens inside, and it is impossible to open the capsule.In order to continue the research, JAVA has no choice but to buy a new X-ray machine, but due to budget cuts due to project separation, JAVA does not have the money to immediately replace the X-ray machine.Masa asked for an increase in next year's budget and thought about using that money to buy a new X-ray machine.Of course, the budget will not increase easily, so it is necessary to maximize the results of the spacecraft.The larger the substance contained in the capsule, the stronger the results can be promoted, so we decided to calculate the maximum volume that the substance can take.Among the shapes of the internal substances that are consistent with the three X-ray photographs, find the one with the largest volume, and output that volume.

KMC sells CDs every year at a doujin sales event called Comic Market.F decided to sell his CDs at the comic market, but due to F's popularity, the KMC sales floor was flooded with people, and he was unable to keep up with the change.Therefore, F decided to write a program that would print out the change immediately after inputting the amount.KMC only provides change in 100 yen coins, 500 yen coins, and 1000 yen bills.You can think of these coins and notes as having an infinite amount.Choose your change so that the number of coins and bills is minimized.Also, the price of CDs sold by KMC is a multiple of 100, and the amount paid by the purchaser is also a multiple of 100.

F, who loves dancing, decided to practice a hardcore dance called JUMPSTYLE at a certain dance hall.The floor is laid out with tiles arranged in an N x N grid.To support dance practice, each tile has the coordinates of the tile from which to jump to the next step written.Through daily practice, F has strong athletic nerves and can jump to any tile on the floor.F realized that if he continued this dance long enough, he would eventually reach a steady state and simply loop along the same route.F wonders how many such loops exist on this floor, and decides to consult you, the programmer.

The year is 200X, and the mysterious circle K that operates at K University announces a new game they have created called K Poker on the 4th floor of K East during the university's cultural festival.This game looks like normal poker at first glance, but it adds an element of basic points to the cards, making poker a much more profound game.The basic points of a card are determined by the picture written on each card, and the points in your hand are the sum of the basic points of the five cards in your hand multiplied by the multiplier of the hand.By introducing this basic point, even if you create a strong hand such as a full house, if the basic point is 0, you will be treated like a pig, and you may even lose to one pair.Other rules are the same as regular poker.You decided to port K Poker to your PC, so you decided to write a program to calculate the points of each hand.Please note that this game cannot be played by anyone under the age of 18.

THE BYDOLM@STER is a training simulation game from 1rem scheduled to be released on EXIDNA on April 1, 2010.Just to be clear, this probably has nothing to do with a certain arcade game whose network connection service stopped earlier this month.In this game, you select the members of the unit (formation) you want to produce from among the Baidols, and through lessons and communication with the members, you raise them to become the pinnacle of Baidols, the top Baidols.Each Baidol has three parameters as ability values: vocal, dance, and looks, and the ability value of a unit is the sum of the parameters of all Baidol belonging to the unit.The maximum of a unit's three ability values ​​becomes its rank.There is no limit to the number of units, and a unit can operate with 1, 3, or 100 members.Of course, you can hire more than one of the same Baidle, but hiring a Baidol costs money, so you must take this into account.You, the producer, decided to write a program and calculate in order to create the best unit.
Given the cubic equation: , I would like you to find out the number of positive real roots and the number of negative real roots.The number of roots shall be counted including multiple roots.

KULASIS is the name of a system developed and operated by the Organization for the Promotion of Higher Education Research, with the aim of providing web-based information on university-wide common subjects and improving support and services for students and faculty.KULASIS began with the development of an online syllabus in 2003, and has gradually expanded its system to include a Web bulletin board, course registration, and grade-related systems (grade registration and confirmation of grades from students).Students can use functions such as checking academic information (class cancellations, class changes, and reports), registering for courses, and checking grades, whether on or off campus, from a computer or mobile phone.The number of logins exceeds 10,000 on most days, and it has become widely used as Kyoto University's academic affairs information system, making it indispensable for taking university-wide common subjects.We are currently developing KULASIS so that it can be applied not only to university-wide common subjects, but also to undergraduate specialized courses and graduate schools.http://www.z.k.kyoto-u.ac.jp/introduction_kulasis.html Q, a Kyoto University student, was logging into KULASIS to create a syllabus for the second semester.As I was thinking about which subject to include, KULASIS suddenly emitted a bright light and transitioned to another page.The page I was redirected to looked like the one shown below.Subject names and grades (poor, fair, good, excellent) are written on the 5x5 squares, and 16 ● buttons are placed on the grid.Q didn't know what was going on, but apparently when he pressed the ● button on the grid, the grades for the subjects in the squares on the top right, bottom right, top left, and bottom left would change from poor to fair, fair to good, good to excellent, and excellent to poor.● You can press the button as many times as you like.Q is not sure if KULASIS has been rewritten by someone or if he is dreaming, but if he can confirm his grade with this, he wants to get the best grade possible.Q, who was confident that he had collected a large number of units, decided to maximize his combat power in preparation for being assigned to a laboratory in six months, rather than focusing on the number of units.Combat power is the sum of the evaluations for each subject, converted into Fail → 0 points, Fair → 60 points, Good → 70 points, and Excellent → 80 points, and this is thought to represent how superior you are when assigned to a laboratory (in Q departments).Now, given the subjects displayed on the KULASIS screen and their evaluations, I would like you to write a program that outputs the maximum value of combat power that can be obtained.

Natsuki and her friends were taken to the space by an alien and made friends with a lot of aliens. During the space travel, she discovered that aliens’ hands were often very different from humans’. Generally speaking, in a kind of aliens, there are N fingers and M bend rules on a hand. Each bend rule describes that a finger A always bends when a finger B bends. However, this rule does not always imply that the finger B bends when the finger A bends. When she were counting numbers with the fingers, she was anxious how many numbers her alien friends can count with the fingers. However, because some friends had too complicated rule sets, she could not calculate those. Would you write a program for her?

There is a frog living in a big pond. He loves jumping between lotus leaves floating on the pond. Interestingly, these leaves have strange habits. First, a leaf will sink into the water after the frog jumps from it. Second, they are aligned regularly as if they are placed on the grid points as in the example below. Recently, He came up with a puzzle game using these habits. At the beginning of the game, he is on some leaf and faces to the upper, lower, left or right side. He can jump forward or to the left or right relative to his facing direction, but not backward or diagonally. For example, suppose he is facing to the left side, then he can jump to the left, upper and lower sides but not to the right side. In each jump, he will land on the nearest leaf on his jumping direction and face to that direction regardless of his previous state. The leaf he was on will vanish into the water after the jump. The goal of this puzzle is to jump from leaf to leaf until there is only one leaf remaining. See the example shown in the figure below. In this situation, he has three choices, namely, the leaves A, B and C. Note that he cannot jump to the leaf D since he cannot jump backward. Suppose that he choose the leaf B. After jumping there, the situation will change as shown in the following figure. He can jump to either leaf E or F next. After some struggles, he found this puzzle difficult, since there are a lot of leaves on the pond. Can you help him to find out a solution?

Nicholas Y. Alford was a cat lover. He had a garden in a village and kept many cats in his garden. The cats were so cute that people in the village also loved them. One day, an evil witch visited the village. She envied the cats for being loved by everyone. She drove magical piles in his garden and enclosed the cats with magical fences running between the piles. She said “Your cats are shut away in the fences until they become ugly old cats.” like a curse and went away. Nicholas tried to break the fences with a hummer, but the fences are impregnable against his effort. He went to a church and asked a priest help. The priest looked for how to destroy the magical fences in books and found they could be destroyed by holy water. The Required amount of the holy water to destroy a fence was proportional to the length of the fence. The holy water was, however, fairly expensive. So he decided to buy exactly the minimum amount of the holy water required to save all his cats. How much holy water would be required?

In 2337, people are bored at daily life and have crazy desire of having extraordinary experience. These days,“Dungeon Adventure” is one of the hottest attractions, where brave adventurers risk their lives to kill the evil monsters and save the world. You are a manager of one of such dungeons. Recently you have been receiving a lot of complaints from soldiers who frequently enter your dungeon; they say your dungeon is too easy and they do not want to enter again. You are considering making your dungeon more difficult by increasing the distance between the entrance and the exit of your dungeon so that more monsters can approach the adventurers. The shape of your dungeon is a rectangle whose width is W and whose height is H . The dungeon is a grid which consists of W × H square rooms of identical size 1 × 1. The southwest corner of the dungeon has the coordinate (0; 0), and the northeast corner has ( W , H ). The outside of the dungeon is surrounded by walls, and there may be some more walls inside the dungeon in order to prevent adventurers from going directly to the exit. Each wall in the dungeon is parallel to either x -axis or y -axis, and both ends of each wall are at integer coordinates. An adventurer can move from one room to another room if they are adjacent vertically or horizontally and have no wall between. You would like to add some walls to make the shortest path between the entrance and the exit longer. However, severe financial situation only allows you to build at most only one wall of a unit length. Note that a new wall must also follow the constraints stated above. Furthermore, you need to guarantee that there is at least one path from the entrance to the exit. You are wondering where you should put a new wall to maximize the minimum number of steps between the entrance and the exit. Can you figure it out?

In the west of Tokyo, there is a city named “Academy City.” There are many schools and laboratories to develop psychics in Academy City. You are a psychic student of a school in Academy City. Your psychic ability is to give acceleration to a certain object. You can use your psychic ability anytime and anywhere, but there are constraints. If the object remains stationary, you can give acceleration to the object in any direction. If the object is moving, you can give acceleration to the object only in 1) the direction the object is moving to, 2) the direction opposite to it, or 3) the direction perpendicular to it. Today’s training menu is to move the object along a given course. For simplicity you can regard the course as consisting of line segments and circular arcs in a 2-dimensional space. The course has no branching. All segments and arcs are connected smoothly, i.e. there are no sharp corners. In the beginning, the object is placed at the starting point of the first line segment. You have to move the object to the ending point of the last line segment along the course and stop the object at that point by controlling its acceleration properly. Before the training, a coach ordered you to simulate the minimum time to move the object from the starting point to the ending point. Your task is to write a program which reads the shape of the course and the maximum acceleration a max you can give to the object and calculates the minimum time to move the object from the starting point to the ending point. The object follows basic physical laws. When the object is moving straight in some direction, with acceleration either forward or backward, the following equations hold: and where v , s , v 0 , a , and t are the velocity, the distance from the starting point, the initial velocity (i.e. the velocity at the starting point), the acceleration, and the time the object has been moving in that direction, respectively. Note that they can be simplified as follows: When the object is moving along an arc, with acceleration to the centroid, the following equations hold: wher v , a , and r are the velocity, the acceleration, and the radius of the arc, respectively. Note that the object cannot change the velocity due to the criteria on your psychic ability.

Bouldering is a style of rock climbing. Boulderers are to climb up the rock with bare hands without supporting ropes. Your friend supposed that it should be interesting and exciting, so he decided to come to bouldering gymnasium to practice bouldering. Since your friend has not tried bouldering yet, he chose beginner’s course. However, in the beginner’s course, he found that some of two stones have too distant space between them, which might result his accidentally failure of grabbing certain stone and falling off to the ground and die! He gradually becomes anxious and wonders whether the course is actually for the beginners. So, he asked you to write the program which simulates the way he climbs up and checks whether he can climb up to the goal successfully. For the sake of convenience, we assume that a boulderer consists of 5 line segments, representing his body, his right arm, his left arm, his right leg, and his left leg. One of his end of the body is connected to his arms on their end points. And the other end of the body is connected to his legs on their end points, too. The maximum length of his body, his arms, and his legs are A , B and C respectively. He climbs up the wall by changing the length of his body parts from 0 to their maximum length, and by twisting them in any angle (in other word, 360 degrees). Refer the following figure representing the possible body arrangements. 5 line segments representing his body, arms and legs. The length of his body, arms and legs are 8, 3 and 4 respectively. The picture describes his head as a filled circle for better understanding, which has no meaning in this problem. A boulderer climbs up the wall by grabbing at least three different rocks on the wall with his hands and feet. In the initial state, he holds 4 rocks with his hands and feet. Then he changes one of the holding rocks by moving his arms and/or legs. This is counted as one movement. His goal is to grab a rock named “destination rock” with one of his body parts. The rocks are considered as points with negligible size. You are to write a program which calculates the minimum number of movements required to grab the destination rock.

Arthur is an innocent man who used to live on the Earth. He had lived a really commonplace life, until the day when the Earth was destroyed by aliens, who were not evil invaders but just contractors ordered to build a hyperspace bypass. In the moment when the demolition beams were shot at the Earth by them, Arthur was in front of his house and almost about to be decomposed into hydrogen, oxygen, carbon and some other atoms. However, fortunately, he survived; one of his friend Ford, who was actually an alien and had come to the Earth in the course of his trip around the universe, took him up to a spaceship just before the beam reached him. Arthur and Ford successfully escaped, but it was only the beginning of their hardships. Soon it turned out that actually the spaceship was the contractor’s one itself, and as it can be easily imagined, they disliked humans. Therefore as soon as they found Arthur and Ford, they stopped at the nearest unexplored planet and dropped the two pity men out of the spaceship from 10 miles up above the land surface. Again they’re in a pinch! Fortunately our hero Ford has a special item that let them safely land to the planet, known as a parachute, so they don’t worry about that. The problem is that they are just falling freely and can’t change the landing point, which may be on ground or sea. They want to know if they can land peacefully or need some swimming to the nearest coast. Ford’s universal GPS gadget displays their expected position of landing by latitude/longitude. Also he has a guidebook that describes almost all the planets in the universe. According to it, the planet has the only one continent and hence only one sea. It has a description of the shape of the continent but, unfortunately, not in a intuitive way of graphical maps. So your task is to make a program to decide whether the point is on the land or not.

On a small island, there are two towns Darkside and Sunnyside, with steep mountains between them. There’s a company Intertown Company of Package Conveyance; they own a ropeway car between Darkside and Sunnyside and are running a transportation business. They want maximize the revenue by loading as much package as possible on the ropeway car. The ropeway car looks like the following. It’s L length long, and the center of it is hung from the rope. Let’s suppose the car to be a 1-dimensional line segment, and a package to be a point lying on the line. You can put packages at anywhere including the edge of the car, as long as the distance between the package and the center of the car is greater than or equal to R and the car doesn’t fall down. The car will fall down when the following condition holds: Here N is the number of packages; m i the weight of the i -th package; x i is the position of the i -th package relative to the center of the car. You, a Darkside programmer, are hired as an engineer of the company. Your task is to write a program which reads a list of package and checks if all the packages can be loaded in the listed order without the car falling down at any point.

A video game company called ICPC (International Company for Playing and Competing) is now developing a new arcade game. The new game features a lot of branches. This makes the game enjoyable for everyone, since players can choose their routes in the game depending on their skills. Rookie players can choose an easy route to enjoy the game, and talented players can choose their favorite routes to get high scores. In the game, there are many checkpoints connected by paths. Each path consists of several stages, and completing stages on a path leads players to the next checkpoint. The game ends when players reach a particular checkpoint. At some checkpoints, players can choose which way to go, so routes diverge at that time. Sometimes different routes join together at a checkpoint. The paths between checkpoints are directed, and there is no loop (otherwise, players can play the game forever). In other words, the structure of the game can be viewed as a DAG (directed acyclic graph), when considering paths between checkpoints as directed edges. Recently, the development team completed the beta version of the game and received feedbacks from other teams. They were quite positive overall, but there are some comments that should be taken into consideration. Some testers pointed out that some routes were very short compared to the longest ones. Indeed, in the beta version, the number of stages in one play can vary drastically depending on the routes. Game designers complained many brilliant ideas of theirs were unused in the beta version because of the tight development schedule. They wanted to have more stages included in the final product. However, it’s not easy to add more stages. this is an arcade game – if the playing time was too long, it would bring down the income of the game and owners of arcades would complain. So, the longest route of the final product can’t be longer than that of the beta version. Moreover, the producer of the game didn’t want to change the structure of paths (i.e., how the checkpoints connect to each other), since it would require rewriting the scenario, recording voices, creating new cutscenes, etc. Considering all together, the producer decided to add as many new stages as possible, while keeping the maximum possible number of stages in one play and the structure of paths unchanged. How many new stages can be added to the game?

Bingo is a party game played by many players and one game master. Each player is given a bingo card containing N 2 different numbers in a N × N grid (typically N = 5). The master draws numbers from a lottery one by one during the game. Each time a number is drawn, a player marks a square with that number if it exists. The player’s goal is to have N marked squares in a single vertical, horizontal, or diagonal line and then call “Bingo!” The first player calling “Bingo!” wins the game. In ultimately unfortunate cases, a card can have exactly N unmarked squares, or N ( N -1) marked squares, but not a bingo pattern. Your task in this problem is to write a program counting how many such patterns are possible from a given initial pattern, which contains zero or more marked squares.

A rabbit comes to a fair and finds that the prize of a game at a stall is a carrot cake. The rules of this game are as follows. There is a grid-like field of H squares x W squares, with at most one block placed in each square. Each block has a color represented by one of the capital letters ('A' - 'Z'). Blocks of the same color are arranged vertically or horizontally in a straight line n.When more than n blocks of the same color are lined up in a row, those blocks disappear. Participants can select two horizontally adjacent squares and swap their states with each other. When a block disappears in the square below the square where the block is due to swapping, disappearing, or falling, the block falls. At this time, if n or more blocks of the same color line up again, the blocks disappear. However, blocks disappear while there are falling blocks.This happens at the same time when all the blocks have finished falling. If all the blocks on the field are destroyed in one operation, the game is a success and you can get the cake as a prize. The rabbit wants to be sure to get the cake for the one-time entry fee, and if he can't do that, he doesn't want to participate. Based on the state of the field at the start of the game, answer whether the rabbit should participate in this game.

A rabbit is traveling in a country. There are n cities numbered from 1 to n in this country, and the rabbit is currently in city 1. City i is regarded as a point ( x i , y i ) on the coordinate plane. The rabbit travels so that the following conditions are met. When the rabbit moves from one city to another, he receives a carrot in the destination city.It is possible to visit the same city multiple times, and receive carrots each time. Find the maximum number of carrots that the rabbit can obtain on this trip.

There is a board with m × n squares. The i-row and j-column squares are represented by ( i , j ) (0 ≤ i < m , 0 ≤ j < n ). When the rabbit is at ( x , y ), it can jump to (( x + a ) mod m , ( y + b ) mod n ) or (( x + c ) mod m , ( y + d ) mod n ). Now,A rabbit is at (0, 0). If we assume that it cannot go back to the square it has jumped to, how many times can the rabbit jump?

There are n rabbits, each living in a hut numbered from 0 to n − 1. One day, the rabbits receive information that a secret organization is constructing an underground passage. The rabbits are happy because they will be able to use the underground passage to visit other rabbits' huts. The passages can go in both directions, and the passages do not intersect with each other.Due to various circumstances, a passageway that has been constructed may be destroyed. The construction and destruction of passageways is carried out one by one, and the rabbits are assumed to remain in their own huts during each construction process. The rabbits want to know in advance whether one rabbit and another rabbit will be able to play together during some stages of construction. The rabbits are good friends, soWhen they go out to play, they can freely pass through other rabbits' hutches.The rabbits, who like programming, thought it would be easy because they had solved a similar problem in the past, so they tried it, but they were unable to write an efficient program.The rabbits asked the rabbits to solve this problem in their place.

A rabbit is playing a role-playing game. Just before entering the castle, he is ambushed by an enemy!The battle was between one protagonist, controlled by a rabbit, and n enemies. Each character has four ability values: physical strength h i , attack power a i , defense power d i , and agility s i. i = 0 represents information about the protagonist, and 1 ≤ i ≤ n represents information about each enemy. The battle is turn-based. Each turn, the surviving charactersEnemies attack in descending order of their agility value. Enemies always attack the protagonist. The protagonist attacks one enemy, but the protagonist can choose which enemy to attack each turn. When a character with attack power a attacks a character with defense power d, max{ a − d , 0} damage is dealt. A character whose total damage received exceeds his or her physical strength value is immediately unable to fight.The battle ends when the protagonist becomes incapacitated or when all enemies become incapacitated.

The rabbit successfully defeated the enemy who had been waiting for him, and succeeded in advancing the protagonist into the enemy's castle. When the protagonist freed the cats that had been captured in the castle's dungeons, some of them came to the protagonist's aid. In the story of the cats, in order to reach the demon king in the depths of the castle, they would have to pass through n rooms numbered 1 to n in this order, and each room had oneEnemies are waiting for each of them, and they must be defeated one by one. For each of the m cats that have joined the team, the probability of winning against each enemy in each room is known, and the protagonist dispatches these cats one by one towards the depths of the castle. Each room cannot be passed until the enemy in that room has been defeated, so if one cat is defeated by the enemy in one room, the next cat will start fighting from the enemy in that room.The dispatched cats advance until they are defeated by the enemy, but each time you can see which room the dispatched cat was defeated by the enemy in, you can decide which cat to dispatch next. How can you maximize the probability that the cats can defeat all the enemies that await them?

n circles are drawn on a piece of paper. The rabbit has k colors of paint and colors the paper according to the following rules. The rabbit wants to paint as many areas as possible. Find the maximum number of areas that can be painted.

A rabbit has n tickets for a certain train. Each ticket has a number from 0 to n − 1, and using the ticket number k allows him to proceed p ⋅ a k + q ⋅ b k stations. The rabbit wants to go to the all-you-can-eat carrot restaurant located m stations from the station he is currently at, but he wants to keep the distance he has to walk as short as possible. The stations are evenly spaced.If the ticket is used only for traveling on the up train line, what is the minimum number of stations that the rabbit can walk to reach the store?

This is a town where the ground is made up of regular hexagonal squares. Each square is represented by two integers as shown in the diagram below. The cat is trying to go to square (0, 0). The mischievous black rabbit knows this and wants to get in the cat's way. The black rabbit jumps to the square where the cat is, and jumps to one of the six squares around it or two adjacent squares.The cat can choose one unblocked square out of the surrounding six squares and move forward. Whenever the cat moves, the black rabbit chases the cat and can block again. When the black rabbit moves, the original block is released. The figure below shows how blocking works. In this town, there are several squares that are the cat's territory. A territory is a set of squares that form the circumference of a regular hexagon.The black rabbit can enter the cat's territory, but cannot block it. In other words, the cat can move freely within the territory. There are k candidates for the cat's starting point, and they are squares ( x i , y i ) (1 ≤ i ≤ k ). For each, whether the cat can definitely reach the destination (0, 0) or not.Or determine if the cat can't reach its destination by successfully interfering with the black rabbit.

A rabbit and a cat are playing a game. The rules are as follows. First, each of the two animals writes n 2 integers on a piece of paper in an n-row, n-column square, and draws a card each. Next, the two animals shuffle 1 000 000 cards, each with a number from 1 to 1 000 000 written on it, and alternately draw one card at a time.Each time a card is drawn, the two animals mark it if the same number as the card is written on their paper. The winning condition is that the number of ``marked sets of n numbers that are lined up in a straight line'' is greater than or equal to the number of playing cards drawn at the beginning. Answer whether the rabbit or the cat will win up to the mth card given. However, the winning condition is as follows.The game is decided when only one of the two animals meets the victory conditions after a certain card has been drawn and marked; otherwise, the game is a draw. Cards may be drawn even after one of the two animals has fulfilled the victory conditions, but this does not affect the outcome.

As many of you know, we have two major calendar systems used in Japan today. One of them is Gregorian calendar which is widely used across the world. It is also known as “Western calendar” in Japan. The other calendar system is era-based calendar, or so-called “Japanese calendar.” This system comes from ancient Chinese systems. Recently in Japan it has been a common way to associate dates with the Emperors. In the era-based system, we represent a year with an era name given at the time a new Emperor assumes the throne. If the era name is “A”, the first regnal year will be “A 1”, the second year will be “A 2”, and so forth. Since we have two different calendar systems, it is often needed to convert the date in one calendar system to the other. In this problem, you are asked to write a program that converts western year to era-based year, given a database that contains association between several western years and era-based years. For the simplicity, you can assume the following: Please note that, however, the database you will see may be incomplete. In other words, some era that existed in the history may be missing from your data. So you will also have to detect the cases where you cannot determine exactly which era the given year belongs to.

Japanese video game company has developed the music video game called Step Step Evolution. The gameplay of Step Step Evolution is very simple. Players stand on the dance platform, and step on panels on it according to a sequence of arrows shown in the front screen. There are eight types of direction arrows in the Step Step Evolution: UP, UPPER RIGHT, RIGHT, LOWER RIGHT, DOWN, LOWER LEFT, LEFT, and UPPER LEFT. These direction arrows will scroll upward from the bottom of the screen. When the direction arrow overlaps the stationary arrow nearby the top, then the player must step on the corresponding arrow panel on the dance platform. The figure below shows how the dance platform looks like. In the game, you have to obey the following rule: As for the third condition, the following figure shows the examples of the valid and invalid footsteps. The first one (left foot for LEFT, right foot for DOWN) and the second one (left foot for LOWER LEFT, right foot for UPPER LEFT) are valid footstep. The last one (left foot for RIGHT, right foot for DOWN) is invalid footstep. Note that, at the beginning of the play, the left foot and right foot can be placed anywhere at the arrow panel. Also, you can start first step with left foot or right foot, whichever you want. To play this game in a beautiful way, the play style called “ Natural footstep style” is commonly known among talented players. “Natural footstep style” is the style in which players make steps by the left foot and the right foot in turn. However, when a sequence of arrows is difficult, players are sometimes forced to violate this style. Now, your friend has created an edit data (the original sequence of direction arrows to be pushed) for you. You are interested in how many times you have to violate “Natural footstep style” when you optimally played with this edit data. In other words, what is the minimum number of times you have to step on two consecutive arrows using the same foot?

The cave, called "Mass of Darkness", had been a agitating point of the evil, but the devil king and all of his soldiers were destroyed by the hero and the peace is there now. One day, however, the hero was worrying about the rebirth of the devil king, so he decided to ask security agency to patrol inside the cave. The information of the cave is as follows: The following is how the security agent patrols: Your task is to write a program to check whether the agent can finish his patrol without dying.

You are playing a popular video game which is famous for its depthful story and interesting puzzles. In the game you were locked in a mysterious house alone and there is no way to call for help, so you have to escape on yours own. However, almost every room in the house has some kind of puzzles and you cannot move to neighboring room without solving them. One of the puzzles you encountered in the house is following. In a room, there was a device which looked just like a dice and laid on a table in the center of the room. Direction was written on the wall. It read: "This cube is a remote controller and you can manipulate a remote room, Dice Room, by it. The room has also a cubic shape whose surfaces are made up of 3x3 unit squares and some squares have a hole on them large enough for you to go though it. You can rotate this cube so that in the middle of rotation at least one edge always touch the table, that is, to 4 directions. Rotating this cube affects the remote room in the same way and positions of holes on the room change. To get through the room, you should have holes on at least one of lower three squares on the front and back side of the room." You can see current positions of holes by a monitor. Before going to Dice Room, you should rotate the cube so that you can go though the room. But you know rotating a room takes some time and you don’t have much time, so you should minimize the number of rotation. How many rotations do you need to make it possible to get though Dice Room?

Alice and Bob were in love with each other, but they hate each other now. One day, Alice found a bag. It looks like a bag that Bob had used when they go on a date. Suddenly Alice heard tick-tack sound. Alice intuitively thought that Bob is to kill her with a bomb. Fortunately, the bomb has not exploded yet, and there may be a little time remained to hide behind the buildings. The appearance of the ACM city can be viewed as an infinite plane and each building forms a polygon. Alice is considered as a point and the bomb blast may reach Alice if the line segment which connects Alice and the bomb does not intersect an interior position of any building. Assume that the speed of bomb blast is infinite; when the bomb explodes, its blast wave will reach anywhere immediately, unless the bomb blast is interrupted by some buildings. The figure below shows the example of the bomb explosion. Left figure shows the bomb and the buildings(the polygons filled with black). Right figure shows the area(colored with gray) which is under the effect of blast wave after the bomb explosion. Note that even if the line segment connecting Alice and the bomb touches a border of a building, bomb blast still can blow off Alice. Since Alice wants to escape early, she wants to minimize the length she runs in order to make herself hidden by the building. Your task is to write a program which reads the positions of Alice, the bomb and the buildings and calculate the minimum distance required for Alice to run and hide behind the building.

International Car Production Company (ICPC), one of the largest automobile manufacturers in the world, is now developing a new vehicle called "Two-Wheel Buggy". As its name suggests, the vehicle has only two wheels. Quite simply, "Two-Wheel Buggy" is made up of two wheels (the left wheel and the right wheel) and a axle (a bar connecting two wheels). The figure below shows its basic structure. Before making a prototype of this new vehicle, the company decided to run a computer simula- tion. The details of the simulation is as follows. In the simulation, the buggy will move on the x-y plane. Let D be the distance from the center of the axle to the wheels. At the beginning of the simulation, the center of the axle is at (0, 0), the left wheel is at (- D , 0), and the right wheel is at ( D , 0). The radii of two wheels are 1. The movement of the buggy in the simulation is controlled by a sequence of instructions. Each instruction consists of three numbers, Lspeed , Rspeed and time . Lspeed and Rspeed indicate the rotation speed of the left and right wheels, respectively, expressed in degree par second. time indicates how many seconds these two wheels keep their rotation speed. If a speed of a wheel is positive, it will rotate in the direction that causes the buggy to move forward. Conversely, if a speed is negative, it will rotate in the opposite direction. For example, if we set Lspeed as -360, the left wheel will rotate 360-degree in one second in the direction that makes the buggy move backward. We can set Lspeed and Rspeed differently, and this makes the buggy turn left or right. Note that we can also set one of them positive and the other negative (in this case, the buggy will spin around). Your job is to write a program that calculates the final position of the buggy given a instruction sequence. For simplicity, you can can assume that wheels have no width, and that they would never slip.

"ACM48" is one of the most popular dance vocal units in Japan. In this winter, ACM48 is planning a world concert tour. You joined the tour as a camera engineer. Your role is to develop software which controls the camera on a stage. For simplicity you can regard the stage as 2-dimensional space. You can rotate the camera to an arbitrary direction by the software but cannot change its coordinate. During a stage performance, each member of ACM48 moves along her route and sings the part(s) assigned to her. Here, a route is given as a polygonal line. You have to keep focusing the camera on a member during a stage performance. You can change the member focused by the camera if and only if the current and next members are in the same direction from the camera. Your task is to write a program which reads the stage performance plan and calculates the maximum time that you can focus the camera on members that are singing. You may assume the following are satisfied:

King Mercer is the king of ACM kingdom. There are one capital and some cities in his kingdom. Amazingly, there are no roads in the kingdom now. Recently, he planned to construct roads between the capital and the cities, but it turned out that the construction cost of his plan is much higher than expected. In order to reduce the cost, he has decided to create a new construction plan by removing some roads from the original plan. However, he believes that a new plan should satisfy the following conditions: Many plans may meet the conditions above, but King Mercer wants to know the plan with minimum cost. Your task is to write a program which reads his original plan and calculates the cost of a new plan with the minimum cost.

The customer telephone support center of the computer sales company called JAG is now in- credibly confused. There are too many customers who request the support, and they call the support center all the time. So, the company wants to figure out how many operators needed to handle this situation. For simplicity, let us focus on the following simple simulation. Let N be a number of customers. The i -th customer has id i , and is described by three numbers, M i , L i and K i . M i is the time required for phone support, L i is the maximum stand by time until an operator answers the call, and K i is the interval time from hanging up to calling back. Let us put these in other words: It takes M i unit times for an operator to support i -th customer. If the i -th customer is not answered by operators for L i unit times, he hangs up the call. K i unit times after hanging up, he calls back. One operator can support only one customer simultaneously. When an operator finish a call, he can immediately answer another call. If there are more than one customer waiting, an operator will choose the customer with the smallest id. At the beginning of the simulation, all customers call the support center at the same time. The simulation succeeds if operators can finish answering all customers within T unit times. Your mission is to calculate the minimum number of operators needed to end this simulation successfully.

International Christmas Present Company (ICPC) is a company to employ Santa and deliver presents on Christmas. Many parents request ICPC to deliver presents to their children at specified time of December 24. Although same Santa can deliver two or more presents, because it takes time to move between houses, two or more Santa might be needed to finish all the requests on time. Employing Santa needs much money, so the president of ICPC employed you, a great program- mer, to optimize delivery schedule. Your task is to write a program to calculate the minimum number of Santa necessary to finish the given requests on time. Because each Santa has been well trained and can conceal himself in the town, you can put the initial position of each Santa anywhere.

The year is 20XX, and the world is a dystopia ruled by G○○gle.Currently, people's entertainment is limited to programming contests.For example, it is a very common sight to see families visiting T○pC○der Arena on weekends.However, this is just a programming contest in the world of light.On the other hand, in a dark world where G○○gle and rebel forces collide, a programming contest is being held where lives are at stake.I would like to participate in a programming contest for the first time in a while.To get used to it, let's first try writing a program that processes the results of a programming contest.

In the year 20XX, communication between people is limited due to the global control of G○○gle, and for example, it is basically not allowed to freely exchange information with other people.In particular, it is said that the powerhouses who made a name for themselves in programming contests in the past are judged to be dangerous, are separated from each other, and are secretly and carefully monitored.I feel like I was one of those people.However, in reality, I have lost my memory.I can't even remember what my name was.I think he used 'i', 'w', and parentheses, but even when I tried to write something like that, it didn't feel right.Come to think of it, what I just remembered is that it seems to be symmetrical left and right.

Yes, my handle name is (iwi).I certainly participated in a programming contest a long time ago.I also had a great time with many friends.I think all my friends were beautiful girls.I feel like the world of programming contests was my harem.G○○gle stole my harem, that's for sure.In order to get clues from my old friends, I still have to participate in programming contests.Let's register to participate in G○○gle Code Jam.Next time, let's consider using parentheses other than parentheses for the ID.

G○○gle Code Jam is a contest held once a year by G○○gle.This is the world's highest contest, where the winner is allowed to join G○○gle.However, of course, all other participants will not return.G○○gle Code Jam allows you to use your favorite programming language and processing system.I decided to participate using a programming language I developed called Defunge.I feel that by using this language, I can solve not only problems that are difficult to calculate, but even problems that cannot be determined.

In programming contests, for each problem, the name and time of the first person to correctly answer the problem is often mentioned in the commentary as the first acceptance (first correct answer).Even though I am participating in a programming contest for the first time in a while, I have no idea that I will fail in the preliminary rounds.So, in the beginning, rather than aiming to get a somewhat high score, wouldn't it be better to gain a lot of first acceptance and make your presence known?If you use your own ability, the moment you look at each problem, you will know how many minutes it will take you to solve it, and how many minutes after the start of the problem, it will be solved first by another participant.Let's create a program that uses this information to calculate how much first acceptance can be obtained.

The G○○gle Code Jam regional tournament is about to begin.Apparently the ID of the man sitting on the left seat is wata.When I was at the University of Tokyo, I remember having friends with similar IDs.However, every single one of my friends must have been a beautiful girl.In my memory, Wata liked Matroid.In particular, he was a bit of an unusual guy who loved Matroid crossovers and even got a kind of excitement from crossing various Matroids.I think he was saying that using the power of matroid theory, it is a very simple problem to find multiple spanning trees that do not share edges on a given graph.However, for special graphs, isn't there an algorithm that is faster than applying the Matroid algorithm directly?

The G○○gle Code Jam regional tournament is about to begin.Apparently the ID of the man sitting in the front seat is omeometo.Apparently the ID of the man sitting in the back seat is jellies.I remember when I was at the University of Tokyo, I had friends with similar IDs, and every single one of them must have been a beautiful girl.They have a book with an illustration of an ant on their desk.Isn't that the ``Programming Contest Challenge Book,'' the legendary book that was the first to cover algorithms for programming contests and is now banned?When omeometo went to the bathroom, I decided to borrow a little bit and read it....However, I finished reading the book in an instant.It's a surprise.It's too easy.Even though the book is a little old, I was motivated to sell it because of the content.For example, the first triangle problem.This is too simple to discuss.If it were me, I would do this.

The G○○gle Code Jam regional tournament is about to begin.The ID of the man sitting diagonally in the front right seat is lyrically.I remember when I was at the University of Tokyo, I had friends with similar IDs, and every single one of them must have been a beautiful girl.In my memory, lyrically had a reputation not only for the power of its algorithms and implementation, but also for its ability to answer problems with determination.For example, he was good at making even a program with a somewhat low computational complexity, by implementing it skillfully, make it faster and give the correct answer.In order to make a program faster, compatibility with cache memory is extremely important.

The G○○gle Code Jam world tournament is finally about to begin.In front, G○○gle's strongest coders, including P○tr, t○mek, and SnapDrag○n, are staring at us.If it shows strange movements, it will probably die.Close your eyes and wait patiently for the contest to start....Strange, the contest won't start.When I opened my eyes...oh my god.All the people in the room are collapsed.No, not everything.Except for one man, Wata.Ha...!! I remembered everything.I was the first person in the world to kill someone in a programming contest.And the person I killed was none other than one of my closest friends, kita_masa.kita_masa was a bit of an oddity, calling himself a master of bitwise operations and saying that any function can be written using bitwise operations.By the way, I have a function that I would like to create using bit operations, but I cannot ask kita_masa to do it.Therefore, I decided to create a program to replace kita_masa.

By the way, the ID of the man who was sitting on the right seat at the G○○gle Code Jam regional tournament seems to be rng_58.I remember when I was at the University of Tokyo, I had friends with similar IDs, and every single one of them must have been a beautiful girl.I feel like she was what you would call an expressionless, mysterious type of character.So he's a stranger.What does rng stand for?It's probably Random Number Generator.He probably specializes in random selection algorithms.By the way, Treap, a balanced binary search tree using random numbers, was often used in programming contests around 2011, but as of 20XX, it is not used much anymore.At that time, Treap was commonly used along with Splay and Scapegoat trees because it was easy to implement.Now, in the year 20XX, in a world where life-threatening programming contests are held on a daily basis, it is completely unthinkable to make a selection based on the basis that the implementation will be a little easier.Everyone is trying to make their programs as fast as possible, and it is natural for anyone participating in a world competition to be able to write a left-leaning red-black tree in about ten seconds.For example, it is said that the reason Treap is slower than red-black tree is that the height is multiplied by a constant.Let's regain mental stability by calmly considering it numerically.

At that time in 2011, programming contests were still very peaceful, and we were casually participating in them without carefully reading the rules.However, G○○gle's conspiracy was approaching.One day, the rules of the T○pC○der contest, which had been secretly purchased by G○○gle, were changed without anyone knowing, and it became a deadly programming contest.And there, without knowing anything, I was the first to exploit the overflow in kita_masa's program......Now that I think about it, this guy was defeating everyone in the room in an instant.What's going on?...I see! Is that so?During his university days, he was interested in making exponential time algorithms faster.Even in exponential time, if the base of the exponent is extremely small, it may be fast enough for realistic instances.It's amazing, more than I imagined...

Many G○○gle coders caught wind of the rebellion and came to arrest us.That's quite a number.Among them, there are many seniors who kindly guided us during our time at the University of Tokyo.Unfortunately, we are now enemies.When I looked outside the window where I heard the voice, I saw a group of people facing the G○○gle building.A T-shirt made of black fabric with I written in red, ○ in green, and M written in yellow...Are they I○M??And strangely, they were able to defeat the incoming G○○gle coders without even touching the keyboard.What's going on?In 2011, I○M leveraged their advanced technological capabilities to complete Wats○n, a system that responds quickly and accurately to questions asked in natural language, and defeated the humans who had been called quiz kings on quiz shows.Now, in 20XX, Wats○n has been reborn as a system for creating programs to answer programming contest questions.With the advent of I○M, the number of people surrounding us will decrease.By breaking away from my delusions, I remembered everything.Naturally, the knowledge and experience I had at that time was of a very low level and useless from today's perspective.However, I was able to remember what was most important.That is my love for programming contests.The excitement at the moment you open a question, the excitement at the moment your sample passes, the tension at the moment your status is updated, the joy at the moment you win.Then, I will come up with one question that I am confident about.This is a problem that I loved when I was at the University of Tokyo, ``Kth number,'' and made it general and large-scale using a unique technique.

A certain student thought of holding a Kyoto University Programming Contest (KUPC).So he decided to make a signboard and advertise it to everyone.Right now, I have several boards with letters of the alphabet written on them.You can create one signboard by selecting four boards with the letters 'K', 'U', 'P', and 'C' written on them from these boards.By repeating this process, you should be able to create many signboards.Now, using the given board, how many signs with KUPC written on them can be obtained in the end?A string is given on one line.Each character in the string represents a character written on each board.Calculate how many signboards named KUPC can be created and print that number on one line.

N You go to school every day, but the journey is very long.Furthermore, it is a hot summer.On the way, there were cicadas.I would like to avoid this if possible.By the way, in today's information-rich society, you can find out where and how many cicadas there are from the comfort of your home.Using that information, I decided to take the path to school where I would encounter the least number of cicadas on the way.The map with houses and schools drawn is rectangular and is divided into H × W blocks.N's house is in the upper left block, and the school is in the lower right block.He wants to go straight to school, so he doesn't turn back toward the house (he only moves to the right or bottom block on the map).Each block becomes as unpleasant as the number of cicadas there.What path should I choose to minimize the degree of discomfort, or in other words, minimize the number of cicadas I encounter?In the first line, two numbers H and W are given in this order.H indicates the number of blocks in the vertical direction of the map, and W indicates the number of blocks in the horizontal direction.A map with cicada information is given in the next H line.Each line consists of W characters, each character indicating the number of cicadas in the corresponding block.The number of cicadas in a block cannot exceed 9.Give the minimum number of cicadas encountered in one line.N Note that you can only move to the right or bottom block.

Since I had some free time, I decided to create an AI that plays Shiritori and play Shiritori with the AI.Shiritori is a game played by two players, in which both players take turns saying words that have never been said before (except on their first turn) and whose first letter is the last letter of the previous word.Since it was troublesome to implement word judgment in AI this time, the definition of word was defined as all strings consisting of lowercase letters of the alphabet.However, once the AI ​​was complete, it became a pain to play with the AI, so I decided to write a program and have it play against the AI ​​instead of me.Writing a program is good, but it's boring without a goal, so I decided to write a program that can beat AI as soon as possible.In other words, write a program that causes the AI ​​to respond incorrectly with as few interactions as possible.Note that the program is the first mover and the AI ​​is the second mover.When the program speaks the word Shiritori, it can listen to the AI's response.For example, to say the word abc in C/C++, use .Next, if you enter , the AI's response will be stored in str.Finally, to point out the error in the AI, output !OUT.The error must be pointed out immediately after the incorrect statement is made.The program should be terminated as soon as an error is pointed out, and no unnecessary output should be produced.If the error is correctly pointed out, the program is judged to be correct (Accepted).If the word response is inappropriate, if the error is incorrectly pointed out to the AI, or if the AI ​​does not point out the error, it will be determined as a wrong answer.The following example is an example of program input/output.The left column represents the output of the program, and the right column represents the input to the program (AI's utterances).First the program says the word abcdefghij.In response, the AI ​​returns jk.During the third response, the AI ​​utters the word jk, which has already been used, so the program says !OUT and points out the AI's error.In the example below, the AI ​​responds to the word aaa with bb, which is an unreasonable response, so it returns !OUT.

You will be given several cards with N / 2 different integers from 1 to N written on them, so I would like you to create and output one sequence seq of length N that satisfies the following conditions.For example, suppose you are given two cards with N = 8, and the numbers written on the cards are [1, 2, 7, 8] and [4, 5, 7, 8], respectively.In this case, setting seq=[0, 1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 0, 1] or seq=[0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1] satisfies the condition.In the first line, N is the length of the sequence to be constructed, and K is the number of cards.The following K lines give information about each card.card i [1], ..., card i [N/2] are the numbers written on the i-th card.Print the sequence seq on one line so that the i-th element of seq corresponds to the i-th character.Note that there is always at least one solution for any input.

Shieru the fox likes thinking about numbers.One day, Shieru learned that there was a thing called cat numbers in this world, and he wanted to have a number like that too.Therefore, I decided to call numbers that meet the following properties Fox Numbers.[Property]: An integer N is called a Fox number when it can be written as N = p 1 e 1 × ... × p k e k by k (≥ 1) prime numbers p 1 , ..., p k and positive integers e 1 , ..., e k satisfying p 1 < ... < p k , e 1 ≥ ... ≥ e k.By the way, Shieru decided on this property on a whim, so he has no idea what kind of property it has.Therefore, in order to investigate the properties of this number, I would like you to output the number of Fox Numbers included in the interval [A-B, A+B].The following two integers are given on the first line.On the first line, output the number of Fox Numbers greater than or equal to A-B and less than or equal to A+B.16 = 2 4 , 17 = 17 1 , 19 = 19 1 , 20 = 2 2 × 5 1 are Fox Numbers.On the other hand, 18 = 2 × 3 2 is not a Fox Number.There are a total of 4 Fox Numbers.

A ball is located at the origin on a two-dimensional plane, and the ball is about to be fired with uniform probability in a direction whose angle with the positive direction of the x-axis is between 0 degrees and 180 degrees when viewed counterclockwise (the direction in which it is fired is not necessarily an integer angle).The size of the ball is sufficiently small, and we will assume that it is a point on a plane.The objective in this problem is to capture this ball with the highest possible probability.N locations (x i , y i ) are given on the plane.To capture the ball, you can choose K locations out of N locations and place a person at each location.A person can move to a distance within radius r i to retrieve the ball from the i-th given location.If you maximize the probability of catching the ball by choosing the placement of people well, output the probability.The input is given in the following format.Print the probability in decimal notation on one line.You can output any number of digits after the decimal point, but the relative or absolute error must be less than 10 -6.There are two locations, and people can be placed in either of them.In this case, the probability is 1/2 no matter where you place it.

Computer Science Experiments and Exercises 3 is a class that uses CAD to design a CPU.A CPU cannot function without combining many circuits, one of which is an XOR circuit with n-bit input.Here, the XOR circuit is a circuit that outputs x 1 + x 2 + ... + x n \ (mod 2) for the input bit string x 1 x 2 ... x n.However, it takes time to design an XOR circuit that works perfectly, so I decided to create an XOR circuit A that uses only k bits out of n bits.In other words, there are some i 1 , ... , i k , and circuit A outputs x i 1 + x i 2 + ... + x i k \ (mod 2).After a while, I wanted an XOR circuit with k-bit input.Isn't it easy?You can use circuit A from earlier.Unfortunately, not only did I forget which k bits was used by circuit A, but I also accidentally deleted the blueprint for circuit A.However, the compiled circuit A remains.Therefore, by executing circuit A with the inputs x 1 x 2 ...x n, we can see the output x i 1 + x i 2 + ... + x i k \ (mod 2).Since we want to shorten the work time as much as possible, let's set an upper limit on the number of times circuit A is executed.How can we find the bits i 1 , ... , i k that circuit A depends on?First, n and k are given separated by spaces.From now on, the program can give input to circuit A and read its output.For example, to give a bit string x 1 x 2 ...x n to circuit A in C/C++, the following example is an example of program input/output.The left column shows the output of the program, and the right column shows the input to the program in chronological order.First, n k is given as input.Here n = 2, k = 1.Next, when we put the input 00 into circuit A, circuit A returned 0.Next, when we put the input 01 into circuit A, circuit A returned 0 again.This shows that circuit A uses only the 1st bit, and the program outputs 1 as the answer.In the example below, n = 2, k = 2, and we can immediately see that circuit A uses both the 1st and 2nd bits.Therefore, the program outputs 1 and 2 as the answers.

Shieru the fox likes to eat eel.2T+1 iron plates are lined up consecutively, numbered -T, -T+1, ..., T in order.Shieru is applying heat to these iron plates and trying to grill a live eel.The procedure for grilling eel is as follows.By the way, living eels are very lively and intelligent, so Shieru became worried that they might move in such a way as to minimize the total amount of heat exerted on them.If this happens, there is a risk that the eel may not be sufficiently heated just by applying heat to the iron plate.Therefore, I would like you to find the maximum value of the sum of heat that can be given to the eel, assuming that the eel always moves optimally.The input is given in the following format.Print the maximum value of the sum of heat given to the eel in the first line.You can output any number of digits after the decimal point, but the relative or absolute error must be less than 10 -6.In this case, it is optimal to add all the energy to iron plate number 0.

Mr. L is the mayor of a certain city.Mr. L's skills as a mayor were incredible: he was able to stop time and build a railway network throughout the city in an instant, turn a budget deficit into a huge surplus overnight, and even summon a gigantic monster from nowhere to use in a performance.Mr. L recently invented a game where he turns uneven land into a mountain shape by swapping adjacent rows or columns of land many times.The land is divided into H×W blocks, and the height of block (y,x) above sea level is measured to be h y,x.Let us write the height of block (y,x) after swapping as h' y,x.This land is mountain-shaped, as shown in the figure below, where there is a certain block (y^*,x^*) with that as the peak of the mountain, and the height decreases monotonically as you move away from the peak.H and W are given on the first line separated by a space.The height of the land is given in the next H line.Each row consists of W numbers, each representing the height of the corresponding location.If the land can be made into a mountain shape, output "YES", otherwise output "NO".In the example below, the land can be made into a mountain shape by swapping the second and third columns.

One evening, after the two had finished solving the contest problems, they were discussing today's problems.A: "Damn, if I had used mod 3 or mod 7 instead of mod 2, I would have known how to solve that Lights Out problem!" B: "Then, why don't you create a problem that can be solved in a different way using mod 3? Got it!" Thus, the following problem was born.There is an H × W chess board.Each square on the chessboard has an integer between 0 and 2 written on it.Place a knight on this chessboard.However, at most only one knight can be placed in each square.For each square, the arrangement of knights such that (number of squares + number of knights in the square attacking that square) = 0 mod 3 holds is called a good arrangement.The attacking square is a square that is either ±2 squares vertically and ±1 square horizontally, or ±1 square vertically and ±2 squares horizontally.Find the number of good knight placements.The answer may be large, so divide it by 1,000,000,007 and give the answer.H and W are given on the first line separated by a space.In the next H line, W integers of 0, 1, or 2 are given, separated by spaces, as the numbers written in the squares of the chessboard.Divide the number of good knight placements by 1,000,000,007 and print the remainder.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training involves playing whack-a-mole many times to improve reflexes and memory.I want to hit the moles that appear one after another and earn as many points as possible.The locations where moles may appear are lined up in a straight line, and the coordinates are determined by the distance from the reference point.As Usagi continues to practice for a while, she notices that moles always appear at the same place and time.Usagi decided to record all the information and analyze it using a computer.To hit a mole, you must move your hand to the location where the mole appears, and then hit the mole at exactly the same time it appears.If you can hit a mole well, you can get points depending on the mole.The motion of whacking a mole can be performed in an instant, but there is a limit to the speed at which the hand can be moved.Rabbits can use both their left and right hands to hit a mole.The left and right hands can be moved independently, but the left hand must always be at a position with smaller coordinates than the right hand.I would like to find out how many points I can get under these conditions.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training is to observe the many stars shining in the night sky and train your eyesight and concentration.Rabbits can record the characteristics of stars using their unique sense.Usagi decides to record the characteristics of the stars in her notebook as a ``star observation diary''.Here, we want to avoid recording two ``similar'' stars together.Whether two stars are "similar" is entirely determined by the rabbit.The relationship between ``similarity'' and not is not very complicated and satisfies the following conditions.The rabbit wants to record as many stars as possible without recording two similar stars.I would also like to know how many ways there are to do this.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training is to improve your ability to read hidden messages from text by looking for palindromes in character strings.There may be many palindromes, so you may want to count them while searching for them.Given two strings S and T, we would like to find the number of integer pairs ( i , j , k , l ) that satisfy the following.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training is to acquire the dexterity of one's hands so that one will never make a typo by carefully stacking the building blocks.Since we have a lot of building blocks, let's build a tall tower.There are N building blocks, and the i-th (1 ≤ i ≤ N ) building block has the shape of a 1 × A i × B i rectangular parallelepiped.We decided to use an edge with a length of 1 in the depth direction, and assign one edge with lengths A i and B i in the horizontal direction and one in the height direction.When stacking building blocks, the horizontal length of the building blocks on the upper level must be strictly shorter than the building blocks on the lower level.The building blocks can be used in any order, and some building blocks may not be used.I would like to create the tallest tower possible under these constraints.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training is to improve your luck by using the Amidakuji lottery to improve your ability to read the future.Of course, in addition to relying on intuition, careful probability calculations are also essential.The Amidakuji we will consider this time consists of N vertical bars with length H + 1 cm.The rabbit looks at the top of the sticks and chooses one out of N sticks.At the bottom of the stick, "Win" is written only at the Pth stick from the left.Amidakuji contains several horizontal bars.Consider the following conditions regarding the placement of horizontal bars.The rabbit drew M horizontal bars to satisfy these conditions.Unfortunately, the rabbit has a good memory, so he remembers all the winning positions and the positions of the horizontal bars, so he can't enjoy playing the Amidakuji game.So I decided to ask my friend's cat to add more horizontal bars.First, the rabbit chooses one of N sticks in hopes of winning.After that, the cat performs the following operation exactly K times.Then, determine whether the stick chosen by the rabbit was a hit.The way you trace the bars is the same as normal Amidakuji (every time you encounter a horizontal bar, you move to the next vertical bar).The rabbit wants to increase the probability of winning as much as possible.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training involves calculating very large numbers in order to improve your calculation skills and raise your consciousness.An example of an operation that easily yields large numbers is exponentiation.There are N non-negative integers A 1 , A 2 , ..., A N .Among these permutations B 1 , B 2 , ..., B N , we want to find the one that maximizes B 1 B 2 ... B N - 1 B N .Of course, this is common sense for rabbits, but this calculation is performed in order starting from the top right.We also promise that 0 0 = 1.There may be multiple B 1 , B 2 , ..., B N that satisfy the maximum condition.In such a case, choose the lexicographically smallest column among them.
