Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training is to acquire the power of business by running around the city and conducting trade.I want to earn as much money as possible for my future training.In this world, roads in the north, south, east, and west directions are lined up at equal intervals, forming a checkerboard shape.The location of the only existing market is (0, 0), and the x and y coordinates of the town are fixed (points with integer coordinates correspond to intersections).The rabbit can only move along roads, and it takes 1 minute to move between adjacent intersections.There are towns at some intersections.In trade, you buy goods in the city and sell them at the market, making a profit based on the difference in price.Usagi has sufficient initial funds, and there is no need to purchase products due to lack of money.However, each item has a weight, and the rabbit can only carry items with a total weight of up to W at the same time.Therefore, they have to go to the town to purchase products and return to the market repeatedly.In some cases, you may have to purchase items in multiple cities before returning to the market.It is assumed that purchasing products in the city and selling products in the market can be done in an instant.In addition, it is impossible for a product in the city to run out of stock, and it is possible to purchase an unlimited number of products.Usagi compiled the name, weight, and selling price of each product that she is trying to buy and sell, as well as the x- and y-coordinates of each town, and the name and price of the product being sold.The rabbit is now at position (0, 0), where the market is.I would like to use a program to find out how much money I can earn within a time limit of T minutes.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training is to improve your creativity by drawing pictures.Let's draw a pattern using a square stamp.I would like to use stamps of various sizes to complete a picture of red, green, and blue streets specified on a 4 × 4 grid of paper.Stamps are rectangular and are used to fit exactly into the grid.It is not possible to swap the height and width of the stamp.The paper is initially uncolored.When you stamp a piece of paper, the stamped area changes to the color of the stamp, and the underlying color becomes completely invisible.The color of the stamp is determined by the ink applied, so you can choose any color you like for any stamp.It is also possible to press a stamp with part of it protruding from the paper, and the protruding part will be ignored.It is possible to use one stamp multiple times.The same stamp may be used for different colors.Stamping is a bit nerve-wracking work, so you want to minimize the number of times you stamp.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training is to run on a straight path to develop physical strength and judgment.The rabbit is now standing at the starting line, looking out over the long road.There are some carrots placed along the way, and the rabbit can accelerate by eating the carrots.The rabbit's running speed when it is not accelerating is U meters per second, but by eating a carrot, its speed becomes V meters per second until T seconds after eating the last carrot.Also, a rabbit can hold up to K carrots without eating them.Even if you hold a carrot, your running speed will not change.Assuming that it does not take long to pick up and eat a carrot, we would like to find the shortest time required to reach the goal.

Training is essential to getting good grades at ICPC.Usagi wants to win at ICPC, so she decides to train again today.Today's training is to quickly solve popular puzzles and train your explosive power.Today's challenge is a puzzle where colorful tiles are lined up and you have to erase them successfully.In the initial state, tiles are placed in several squares on the grid.Each tile has a color.After starting the game, the player can perform the operations shown in the following steps any number of times.For example, consider the following situation.Squares with no tiles are indicated by periods, and the color of the tiles is indicated by uppercase letters.Now consider the operation of hitting the square in the second row from the top and third column from the left.Since there are three tiles to focus on, A, B, and B, the two B disappear and the board becomes as shown below, giving you 2 points.If you take your time with this puzzle, you will run out of time, and a part of the board will become invisible, making it difficult to see how much training you have lacked.Two tiles of each color are placed, but since it is not always possible to erase them all, we would like to have the program calculate the maximum score in advance.

Taro is a member of a programming contest circle. In this circle, the members manage their schedules in the system called Great Web Calender . Taro has just added some of his friends to his calendar so that he can browse their schedule on his calendar. Then he noticed that the system currently displays all the schedules in only one color, mixing the schedules for all his friends. This is difficult to manage because it's hard to tell whose schedule each entry is. But actually this calender system has the feature to change the color of schedule entries, based on the person who has the entries. So Taro wants to use that feature to distinguish their plans by color. Given the colors Taro can use and the number of members, your task is to calculate the subset of colors to color all schedule entries. The colors are given by "Lab color space". In Lab color space, the distance between two colors is defined by the square sum of the difference of each element. Taro has to pick up the subset of colors that maximizes the sum of distances of all color pairs in the set.

Miki is a high school student. She has a part time job, so she cannot take enough sleep on weekdays. She wants to take good sleep on holidays, but she doesn't know the best length of sleeping time for her. She is now trying to figure that out with the following algorithm: If her feeling is always correct, the steps described above should give her a very accurate optimal sleeping time. But unfortunately, she makes mistake in step 3 with the probability P . Assume you know the optimal sleeping time T for Miki. You have to calculate the probability PP that the absolute difference of T' and T is smaller or equal to E . It is guaranteed that the answer remains unaffected by the change of E in 10^{-10} .

Saving electricity is very important! You are in the office represented as R \times C grid that consists of walls and rooms. It is guaranteed that, for any pair of rooms in the office, there exists exactly one route between the two rooms. It takes 1 unit of time for you to move to the next room (that is, the grid adjacent to the current room). Rooms are so dark that you need to switch on a light when you enter a room. When you leave the room, you can either leave the light on, or of course you can switch off the light. Each room keeps consuming electric power while the light is on. Today you have a lot of tasks across the office. Tasks are given as a list of coordinates, and they need to be done in the specified order. To save electricity, you want to finish all the tasks with the minimal amount of electric power. The problem is not so easy though, because you will consume electricity not only when light is on, but also when you switch on/off the light. Luckily, you know the cost of power consumption per unit time and also the cost to switch on/off the light for all the rooms in the office. Besides, you are so smart that you don't need any time to do the tasks themselves. So please figure out the optimal strategy to minimize the amount of electric power consumed. After you finished all the tasks, please DO NOT leave the light on at any room. It's obviously wasting!

N people run a marathon. There are M resting places on the way. For each resting place, the i -th runner takes a break with probability P_i percent. When the i -th runner takes a break, he gets rest for T_i time. The i -th runner runs at constant speed V_i , and the distance of the marathon is L . You are requested to compute the probability for each runner to win the first place. If a runner arrives at the goal with another person at the same time, they are not considered to win the first place.

ICP city has an express company whose trucks run from the crossing S to the crossing T . The president of the company is feeling upset because all the roads in the city are one-way, and are severely congested. So, he planned to improve the maximum flow (edge disjoint paths) from the crossing S to the crossing T by reversing the traffic direction on some of the roads. Your task is writing a program to calculate the maximized flow from S to T by reversing some roads, and the list of the reversed roads.

KM country has N kinds of coins and each coin has its value a_i . The king of the country, Kita_masa, thought that the current currency system is poor, and he decided to make it beautiful by changing the values of some (possibly no) coins. A currency system is called beautiful if each coin has an integer value and the ( i +1)-th smallest value is divisible by the i -th smallest value for all i ( 1 \leq i \leq N-1 ). For example, the set {1, 5, 10, 50, 100, 500} is considered as a beautiful system, while the set {1, 5, 10, 25, 50, 100} is NOT, because 25 is not divisible by 10 . Since changing the currency system may confuse citizens, the king, Kita_masa, wants to minimize the maximum value of the confusion ratios. Here, the confusion ratio for the change in the i -th coin is defined as |a_i - b_i| / a_i , where a_i and b_i is the value of i -th coin before and after the structure changes, respectively. Note that Kita_masa can change the value of each existing coin, but he cannot introduce new coins nor eliminate existing coins. After the modification, the values of two or more coins may coincide.

A rabbit Taro decided to hold a party and invite some friends as guests. He has n rabbit friends, and m pairs of rabbits are also friends with each other. Friendliness of each pair is expressed with a positive integer. If two rabbits are not friends, their friendliness is assumed to be 0. When a rabbit is invited to the party, his satisfaction score is defined as the minimal friendliness with any other guests. The satisfaction of the party itself is defined as the sum of satisfaction score for all the guests. To maximize satisfaction scores for the party, who should Taro invite? Write a program to calculate the maximal possible satisfaction score for the party.

A palidrome craftsperson starts to work in the early morning, with the clear air allowing him to polish up his palindromes. On this morning, he is making his pieces to submit to the International Contest for Palindrome Craftspeople. By the way, in order to make palindromes, he uses a special dictionary which contains a set of words and a set of ordered pairs of the words. Any words and any ordered pairs of consecutive words in his palindromes must appear in the dictionary. We already have his dictionary, so let's estimate how long a palindrome he can make.

Angry Birds is a mobile game of a big craze all over the world. You were convinced that it was a waste of time to play the game, so you decided to create an automatic solver. You are describing a routine that optimizes the white bird's strategy to defeat a pig (enemy) by hitting an egg bomb. The white bird follows a parabolic trajectory from the initial position, and it can vertically drop egg bombs on the way. In order to make it easy to solve, the following conditions hold for the stages. The acceleration of gravity is 9.8 {\rm m/s^2} . Gravity exerts a force on the objects in the decreasing direction of y -coordinate.

You are recording a result of a secret experiment, which consists of a large set of N -dimensional vectors. Since the result may become very large, you are thinking of compressing it. Fortunately you already have a good compression method for vectors with small absolute values, all you have to do is to preprocess the vectors and make them small. You can record the set of vectors in any order you like. Let's assume you process them in the order v_1 , v_2 ,..., v_M . Each vector v_i is recorded either as is , or as a difference vector. When it is recorded as a difference, you can arbitrarily pick up an already chosen vector v_j (j<i) and a real value r . Then the actual vector value recorded is (v_i - r v_j) . The values of r and j do not affect the compression ratio so much, so you don't have to care about them. Given a set of vectors, your task is to write a program that calculates the minimum sum of the squared length of the recorded vectors.

What is surprising, however, is that the roses grown in this garden transcend blocks and intertwine to form a single living entity.These life forms can intertwine across blocks that share edges, and if they share one point, they are the same life form.Mami Tomoe shoots a huge gun in a straight line from the lower left corner of the garden using her special move ``Tiro Finale'', and when the bullet splits the life form in the straight line it passes through, she wants to cause damage by making sure that the life form is divided into as many divisions as possible.Find the maximum number of parts this creature can be divided into by Tyro Finale.The input is given in the following format.H represents the height of the garden, and W represents the width of the garden.f_{ij} is a character representing each block in the garden, and represents the state of the block in the i-th row from the top and the j-th column from the left.If roses are grown in the block, f_{ij} will be '#', otherwise it will be '.'.

Dr. Fukuoka has placed a simple robot in a two-dimensional maze. It moves within the maze and never goes out of the maze as there is no exit. The maze is made up of H × W grid cells as depicted below. The upper side of the maze faces north. Consequently, the right, lower and left sides face east, south and west respectively. Each cell is either empty or wall and has the coordinates of ( i , j ) where the north-west corner has (1, 1). The row i goes up toward the south and the column j toward the east. The robot moves on empty cells and faces north, east, south or west. It goes forward when there is an empty cell in front, and rotates 90 degrees to the right when it comes in front of a wall cell or on the edge of the maze. It cannot enter the wall cells. It stops right after moving forward by L cells. Your mission is, given the initial position and direction of the robot and the number of steps, to write a program to calculate the final position and direction of the robot.

Claire is a man-eater. She's a real man-eater. She's going around with dozens of guys. She's dating all the time. And one day she found some conflicts in her date schedule. D'oh! So she needs to pick some dates and give the others up. The dates are set by hours like 13:00 to 15:00. She may have more than one date with a guy. For example, she can have dates with Adam from 10:00 to 12:00 and from 14:00 to 16:00 and with Bob from 12:00 to 13:00 and from 18:00 to 20:00. She can have these dates as long as there is no overlap of time. Time of traveling, time of make-up, trouble from love triangles, and the likes are not of her concern. Thus she can keep all the dates with Adam and Bob in the previous example. All dates are set between 6:00 and 22:00 on the same day. She wants to get the maximum amount of satisfaction in total. Each guy gives her some satisfaction if he has all scheduled dates. Let's say, for example, Adam's satisfaction is 100 and Bob's satisfaction is 200. Then, since she can make it with both guys, she can get 300 in total. Your task is to write a program to satisfy her demand. Then she could spend a few hours with you... if you really want.

Taro, a junior high school student, is working on his homework. Today's homework is to read Chinese classic texts. As you know, Japanese language shares the (mostly) same Chinese characters but the order of words is a bit different. Therefore the notation called "returning marks" was invented in order to read Chinese classic texts in the order similar to Japanese language. There are two major types of returning marks: 'Re' mark and jump marks. Also there are a couple of jump marks such as one-two-three marks, top-middle-bottom marks. The marks are attached to letters to describe the reading order of each letter in the Chinese classic text. Figure 1 is an example of a Chinese classic text annotated with returning marks, which are the small letters at the bottom-left of the big Chinese letters. Taro generalized the concept of jump marks, and summarized the rules to read Chinese classic texts with returning marks as below. Your task is to help Taro by writing a program that interprets Chinese classic texts with returning marks following his rules, and outputs the order of reading of each letter. When two (or more) rules are applicable in each step, the latter in the list below is applied first, then the former. Let's see the first case of the sample input. We begin reading with the first letter because of the rule 1. However, since the first letter has a jump mark ' onetwo2 ', we must follow the rule 2 and skip the letter. Therefore the second letter, which has no returning mark, will be read first. Then the third letter will be read. The third letter has a jump mark ' onetwo1 ', so we must follow rule 3 and read a letter with a jump mark `onetwo2' next, if exists. The first letter has the exact jump mark, so it will be read third. Similarly, the fifth letter is read fourth, and then the sixth letter is read. Although we have two letters which have the same jump mark ' onetwo2 ', we must not take into account the first letter, which has already been read, and must read the fourth letter. Now we have read all six letters and no letter can be read next, so we finish reading. We have read the second, third, first, fifth, sixth, and fourth letter in this order, so the output is 2 3 1 5 6 4.

Champernowne constant is an irrational number. Its decimal representation starts with "0.", followed by concatenation of all positive integers in the increasing order. You will be given a sequence S which consists of decimal digits. Your task is to write a program which computes the position of the first occurrence of S in Champernowne constant after the decimal point.

Mr. Don is an administrator of a famous quiz website named QMACloneClone. The users there can submit their own questions to the system as well as search for question texts with arbitrary queries. This search system employs bi-gram search method. The bi-gram search method introduces two phases, namely preprocessing and search: Preprocessing Precompute the set of all the substrings of one or two characters long for each question text. Search Compute the set for the query string in the same way. Then nd the question texts whose precomputed sets completely contain the set constructed from the query. Everything looked fine for a while after the feature was released. However, one of the users found an issue: the search results occasionally contained questions that did not include the query string as-is. Those questions are not likely what the users want. So Mr. Don has started to dig into the issue and asked you for help. For each given search query, your task is to find the length of the shortest question text picked up by the bi-gram method but not containing the query text as its substring.

A robot in a two-dimensional maze again. The maze has an entrance and an exit this time, though. Just as in the previous problem, the maze is made up of H × W grid cells, its upper side faces north, and each cell is either empty or wall. Unlike the previous, on the other hand, one of the empty cells is connected to the entrance and another to the exit. The robot is rather complex - there is some control, but not full. It is associated with a controller that has two buttons, namely forward and turn . The forward button moves the robot forward to the next cell, if possible. The robot can not move into a wall nor outside the maze. The turn button turns the robot as programmed . Here the program is a finite sequence of N commands, each of which is either 'L' (indicating a left turn) or 'R' (a right turn). The first turn follows the first command; the second turn follows the second command; similar for the following turns. The turn button stops working once the commands are exhausted; the forward button still works in such a case though. The robot always turns by 90 degrees at once. The robot is initially put on the entrance cell, directed to the north. Your mission is to determine whether it can reach the exit cell if controlled properly.

Consider sets of natural numbers. Some sets can be sorted in the same order numerically and lexicographically. {2, 27, 3125, 9000} is one example of such sets; {2, 27, 243} is not since lexicographic sorting would yield {2, 243, 27}. Your task is to write a program that, for the set of integers in a given range [ A , B ] (i.e. between A and B inclusive), counts the number of non-empty subsets satisfying the above property. Since the resulting number is expected to be very huge, your program should output the number in modulo P given as the input.

Dr. Kay Em, a genius scientist, developed a new missile named "Ikan-no-i." This missile has N jet engines. When the i -th engine is ignited, the missile's velocity changes to ( vx i , vy i ) immediately. Your task is to determine whether the missile can reach the given target point ( X , Y ). The missile can be considered as a mass point in a two-dimensional plane with the y -axis pointing up, affected by the gravity of 9.8 downward (i.e. the negative y -direction) and initially set at the origin (0, 0). The engines can be ignited at any time in any order. Still, note that at least one of them needs to be ignited to get the missile launched.

The trafic on the Internet is increasing these days due to smartphones. The wireless carriers have to enhance their network infrastructure. The network of a wireless carrier consists of a number of base stations and lines. Each line connects two base stations bi-directionally. The bandwidth of a line increases every year and is given by a polynomial f ( x ) of the year x . Your task is, given the network structure, to write a program to calculate the maximal bandwidth between the 1-st and N -th base stations as a polynomial of x .

John is playing a famous console game named 'Tales of Algorithmers.' Now he is facing the last dungeon called 'Blue Forest.' To find out the fastest path to run through the very complicated dungeon, he tried to draw up the dungeon map. The dungeon consists of several floors. Each floor can be described as a connected simple plane graph. Vertices of the graph are identified by X-Y coordinate, and the length of an edge is calculated by Euclidean distance. A vertex may be equipped with a one-way warp gate. If John chooses to use the gate, it brings John to another vertex in a possibly different floor. The distance between a warp gate and its destination is considered as 0. One vertex has at most one warp gate, though some vertices might be the destination of multiple warp gates. He believed he made one map for each floor, however after drawing maps of all the floors, he noticed that he might have made a few mistakes. He might have drawn the same floor several times, and might have forgotten to mark some warp gates in the maps. However, he was sure he marked all warp gates at least once. So if the maps of same floor are unified to one map, all the warp gates must be described there. Luckily there are no floors which have the same shape as the other floors, so if two (or more) maps can be unified, they must be the maps of the same floor. Also, there is no floor which is circular symmetric (e.g. a regular triangle and a square). Map A and map B can be unified if map B can be transformed to map A using only rotation and parallel translation. Since some of the warp gates on maps might be missing, you should not consider the existence of warp gates when checking unification. If it is possible to unify map A and map B, a vertex on map A and the corresponding vertex on map B are considered as 'identical' vertices. In other words, you should treat warp gates on map B as those on map A where the warp gates are located on the corresponding vertices on map A. Destinations of warp gates should be treated similarly. After that you can forget map B. It is guaranteed that if both map A and map B have warp gates which are on the identical vertices, the destinations of them are also identical. Remember, your task is to find the shortest path from the entrance to the exit of the dungeon, using the unified maps.

The Quakugo Imperial Expeditionary Force is an evil organization that plans to invade Earth.In keeping with the tradition of invaders, they continued to send one monster every week, targeting areas around Tokyo, Japan.However, each time five warriors calling themselves Humanity Sentai Earth-Five appeared, and the monsters rampaging through the city were easily defeated.Walzad Thru (Earth name: Genga) is a female executive of the Quakugo Imperial Expeditionary Force who is deeply concerned about this current situation.She spends her days in pain under the command of a commander who doesn't seem to learn anything from his weekly defeats, and a genius scientist who repeatedly makes inventions that are somehow off.Meanwhile, the next plan is to send the vampire monster Draculas to Japan.Draculas is a terrifying monster who turns people who drink his blood into Draculas.Humans whose blood is sucked by Draculas also increase the number of their Draculas by sucking the blood of other humans.In this way, the plan is to fill the entire earth with Draculas.When Walzard heard this, he immediately understood.This operation is different from the usual secrecy operations such as hijacking a kindergarten bus.It was unusual for something that was planned by that no-good commander, and it actually had the potential to conquer Earth.The momentum of the game is terrifying.Draculas descended to the ground and quickly increased the number of his companions.If things continued like this, it seemed like an invasion of Earth was just a stone's throw away.However, at that moment, an intensely unpleasant premonition ran through Walzard's mind.Surely, if this monster, the original, is defeated, his friends will also be wiped out, right?When he hurriedly questioned the scientist who designed and developed Dracula, he found out that it was just as Walzard had feared.It is said that if the original Draculas is killed, all the humans whose blood was sucked will return to normal.Don't be silly, developers.Why did you add such an unnecessary function?Walzard delivered a flying knee kick to the person in charge of development and immediately began work on recovering the original.No matter how much the original and fake look exactly the same, if things continue as they are, it's obvious that Earth-Five, who rushes in, will see through the original for some reason and be defeated.According to the developer, all Draculas have the same weight, but the original Dracula is slightly heavier.If that's the case, you should be able to find the original by using a scale.We must find and retrieve the original Draculas as soon as possible before Earth-5 appears.

The long-awaited summer vacation begins tomorrow.So I decided to invite my friends and go to the beach.However, many of my friends are shy.If they knew that too many people were coming with them, they would probably hate it.In addition, many of my friends like to stand out.If they knew that not many people would come with them, they would probably hate it.Also, some of my friends are shy people who usually like to stand out.They will probably dislike having too many or too few people come with them.This kind of thing should be more fun if you go with a group.That's why I want to invite as many friends as possible.However, it's not a good idea to force your friends to take you with them if they don't like it.How many friends can I invite at most?I'm not very good at problems like this that make me use my brain.So, I have a request for you.If you don't mind, could you solve this problem for me?No, I would never say that it is impossible.However, if you can help me understand, I would be very happy.

All space-time unified dimension Sugoroku tournament.You are participating as a representative of Earth in the 21st century in the tournament to determine the one and only absolute Sugoroku champion out of over 500 trillion participants.The challenge you are currently taking on is a one-dimensional Sugoroku with yourself as a piece.You may be familiar with Sugoroku, where you start from a starting square at the end, roll a huge six-sided die with numbers 1 to 6 written on it, and move forward by the number of numbers rolled.When you stop at the goal square at the opposite end of the start square, you reach the goal.Of course, the fewer times you roll the dice before reaching the goal, the better your score will be.Among the squares, there are squares with special effects such as ``Go forward x square'' and ``Go back x square'', and if you stop there, you must move forward or return the specified number of squares.If you move due to the effect of a square and end up on the effective square again, continue moving as instructed.However, this is a time-space sugoroku that cannot be easily conquered.To be frightening, for example, it is possible that ``go back 3 squares'' is placed 3 squares ahead of ``go forward 3 squares''.If you stop on a square like this and end up in an infinite loop due to the square's effect, you will have to keep going back and forth between squares forever.However, fortunately, your body contains the supernatural ability ``Probability Curving'' that allows you to change any desired event into any event.Using this ability, you can also freely manipulate the outcome of the dice.If we take advantage of this advantage and proceed without falling into an infinite loop, what is the minimum number of times we will need to roll the dice to reach the goal?

I have many friends.All my friends are very small.I often go out with friends.Put some friends in your backpack and go out together.Every morning, I decide which friends to go out with that day.Place your friends one by one into the empty backpack.I'm not very strong.Therefore, there is a limit to the weight of friends that can be carried at the same time.I bring in friends so that I don't exceed the weight limit.The order you put them in depends on your mood.As long as I still have friends I can invite, I won't stop inviting them.Never stop....By the way, how many combinations of friends are there in your backpack?

Two types of people live in the Kingdom of Neva: the Totata tribe and the Tsutete tribe.The most distinctive feature of the Totata tribe is that they eat sweet and sour pork with pineapple.However, the Tsutete people eat pineapple with sweet and sour pork.There is no way these two peoples could get along with each other, and the Totata and Tsutete have been at war with each other for hundreds of years.One day, King Neva received petitions from two ethnic groups.According to the report, the Totata tribe wants a road to be built between Town A and Town B, where they live.On the other hand, the Tsutete tribe also wants a road to be built between Town C and Town D, where they live.To prevent conflicts between the two ethnic groups, the roads taken by the Totata people and the roads taken by the Tsutete people cannot intersect.Also, due to technical constraints, it is only possible to build roads that connect two cities in a straight line.In other words, if necessary, instead of directly connecting City A and City B with a road, it would be possible to connect City A and City B indirectly via several Totata towns (of course, not via the Tsutete town).At that time, the roads connecting Totata towns may intersect.The same applies to city C and city D.The cost of building a road is proportional to its length.Therefore, we would like to shorten the total length of the roads to be constructed as much as possible while satisfying the conditions.Now, what is the minimum length?

One evening.While you were watching TV in the living room as usual, your sister, who is in fifth grade, approached you for advice.When I talked to him, he told me that the math problem he was given at school today was difficult and he couldn't understand it, so he wanted you to teach him how to solve it.The problem that bothered my sister was, ``In a city where the streets are arranged in a grid pattern, how many ways are there to go from home (0, 0) to school (N, M) in the shortest distance?''Of course, it's an easier problem for you than twisting a baby's hand.I immediately drew a diagram like the one above and told him, ``If you add in order starting from the house (0, 0), you can solve the problem.''However, even after hearing this, my sister was still looking down and thinking.You wondered if your explanation was bad, but apparently that wasn't the case.I must have been thinking about this for about 3 minutes.My sister slowly looked up and said to you,``But if it were me, I'd probably take K detours on my way to school...Hey, big brother, how many different answers would there be?'' Now that's tough.In order to maintain my brother's dignity, I must answer this question no matter what.This problem can be formulated as follows.

When you wake up one morning, you find yourself in a labyrinth full of springs.I don't know why I'm in such a strange place.You had the option of staying here and waiting for help, but you knew from experience that if you stayed in a labyrinth like this for too long, a gust of wind would eventually come and blow you away.However, there is no way to judge how long it will take for the gusts to arrive.So you decide that the best strategy is to act in a way that minimizes the expected number of moves required to escape from the labyrinth.When I picked up an unidentified scroll that had fallen at my feet and tried reading it, I happened to be able to perceive the entire map of the labyrinth.Furthermore, by drinking grass that he happened to have, he was able to learn the locations of all the traps.Apparently, there are no dangerous monsters or traps other than springs in this labyrinth.This labyrinth has the shape of several types of tiles arranged in a rectangular shape, for example, as shown below.".":floor.You can walk around freely on top of this."#":wall.You can't go inside the walls."s": your starting position.Below this tile is the floor."g": stairs.If you step on this tile, you will have escaped the labyrinth."*": Spring.When you step on this tile, you will be randomly sent to any floor (excluding stairs and spring tiles).The probability of being sent to each floor is equal.The type of tile is one of these five types.You can move from the tile you are currently on to an adjacent tile one space at a time in either the up, down, left or right direction.However, it cannot be moved diagonally.Given a labyrinth map, find the expected number of moves required to escape from the labyrinth if the best strategy is adopted.The number of moves does not include being blown away by a spring.

You have many cats at home.Your daily routine is to take pictures of your cat taking a nap inside a clay pot (commonly known as a cat pot).The sight of several cats sleeping curled up together in a pot is truly adorable.Each cat has a defined ``weight'' m i and ``cute'' c i .Cute is a numerical value that comprehensively evaluates the cuteness, innocence, suppleness, softness, sweetness, etc. of a single cat, and the higher the value, the more attractive the cat is.Furthermore, a numerical value "UnCute" is defined for cat hotpot.This is a value that expresses the unbalance of the cat hotpot as a whole, and contrary to Cute, the smaller the value, the better the cat hot pot.The UnCute of a cat pot is expressed as C max - C min using the maximum value C max and minimum value C min of the cat's Cute inside.In other words, a good cat pot is one where all the cats inside have the same amount of cuteness.Considering when carrying a cat pot, the weight of the cat pot (=the total weight of the cats inside) must be less than the limit value W.I would like to choose a cat that satisfies this condition and make the UnCute in the cat pot smaller, but I wonder how small I can make it.The days when I felt completely satisfied after solving a problem like this are in the past.The other day, when you were cleaning out the warehouse at home, you found an old earthenware pot inside the warehouse.Now you have two clay pots in your house.Yes, from now on, it's the era of dual cat pots!Just a cat pot is already old!In a dual cat pot, clay pot A is for male cats only, and pot B is for female cats only.Furthermore, when defining UnCute of a dual cat pot, it is necessary to take into account not only the unbalance of Cute but also the unbalance of the weights of the two cat pots.Specifically, if the weight of the heavier cat pot is M max and the weight of the lighter cat pot is M min (if the weights of the two cat pots are the same, then M max = M min ), then UnCute of the dual cat pot can be expressed as follows.Note that C max and C min here are the maximum and minimum values ​​of the cuteness of the cat in cat pot A or cat pot B.Considering when carrying the cat pot, M max must be less than the limit value W.I would like to select a cat to be placed in the dual cat pot that satisfies these conditions and make the UnCute of the dual cat pot smaller, but how small can I make it?

Month 15 of the year 2119 in the Kikei calendar.A magic book was written by the court magician Sengemon Lugene.This magic book, ``In Magiray,'' was an innovative book in that it systemized ``double magic,'' which is deeply related to the foundations of the magical laws of this world, as a technique that anyone can learn.First, let's take a look at a summary of the contents of this book.[Elements] All objects in the world are made up of extremely small elements, such as springs, wind, feathers, flowers, sand, lights, etc.There are many types of elements, and by making a contract with an element, humans can activate double magic using the type of element they have made a contract with.[Double Magic] As the name suggests, double magic is a spell that combines two elements and releases them.The main element is called the ``main attribute,'' and the other element is called the ``subattribute.''For example, if the main attribute is ``light'' and the secondary attribute is ``flower,'' the double magic is written as (light, flower).(light, flower) and (flower, light) are different double magic.Further, the main attribute and the sub-attribute may be the same.[Keywords] Each element can have a one-to-one correspondence with a short keyword, such as "spring → huda," "wind → loar," "feather → sheza," and "flower → leide."Keywords are an epoch-making concept that symbolize the properties of an element in short words, making it possible to easily activate double magic just by chanting several consecutive keyword strings.[Spell] A "spell" is a sequence of one or more keywords such as "huda, leide, loar, sheza".By chanting the spell intended by the caster, the corresponding double magic can be activated.Specifically, a double magic is activated in which the element corresponding to the first keyword of the spell is the primary attribute, and the element corresponding to the last keyword is the secondary attribute.In the spell example from earlier, the double magic activated is (Izumi, Feather).Note that if a spell consists of only one keyword, both the primary attribute and the secondary attribute will be the element corresponding to that keyword.[Compatibility between elements] I have already mentioned that the double magic of (Izumi, Feather) is activated by the spell "huda, leide, loar, sheza".However, at first glance, it seems that if you want to use (Izumi, Feather), you don't have to go through the trouble of reciting a long spell and just ``huda・sheza''.But in reality, that is not possible.This is because ``huda'' and ``sheza'' do not go well together, so if you cast a spell with these two next to each other, a repulsion will occur and the elements will cancel each other out.On the other hand, all pairs of ``fountain (huda)'' and ``flower (leide),'' ``flower (leide)'' and ``wind (loar),'' ``wind (loar)'' and ``feather (sheza)'' are compatible, so spells such as ``huda・leide・loar・sheza'' or ``loar・leide・huda'' do not cause element cancellation.[Spell Logic] Those who wish to use double magic must first define which keyword is the ``higher word'' and which keyword is the ``lower word'' for each pair of compatible elements.When we define the keywords "A" and "B" of two elements that are compatible, A is defined as the upper word and B is the lower word, it is written as A > B.When A > B, A cannot immediately follow B in a spell.In other words, if "A 1 ・ A 2 ・ A 3 ・...A k" is a correct spell, then A 1 > A 2 , A 2 > A 3 , ..., A k -1 > A k must be true.By the way, even if A > B and B > C, it is not necessary that A > C.In this way, each spellcaster defines the upper and lower words to their own preference, which is called ``construction of spell logic.''This is a device that purposely imposes constraints on the syntax of the spell to clarify the flow of the elements and increase the probability of success for the desired dual magic.However, the trade-off is that constructing spell logic can result in double magic that cannot be activated no matter how you cast the spell (although it has been proven that there is no double magic that cannot be activated no matter how you construct spell logic).Because of these circumstances, deciding what kind of spell logic to construct is a troublesome problem that has troubled many magicians both now and in the past.Yuto, an apprentice magician who lives in a small town, has completed his contracts with all the elements, and tomorrow he is about to perform a ritual to construct his own spell logic.Yuto simply thought that a good spell logic was one that could activate as many types of dual magic as possible.You, a first-class electrical engineer (or what modern Japan would call a super programmer), are approached by your friend Yuto, who wants to know how many types of dual magic you can use if you can successfully construct spell logic.This is such a difficult problem that people all over the world have tried to solve, but no one has ever been able to solve it.If you can solve it, your name will be remembered throughout the world for eternity.

There is a directed graph of N points.There are no cycles in this graph.A number of stones are placed at each point.Two players play a game using this graph.Each person's turn comes alternately.Each player, on their turn, chooses a vertex v and removes one or more stones placed there.After that, for all vertices w whose edges extend from v to w, you can freely change the number of stones placed at w (no matter how many you increase, how many you decrease, or don't change).This is repeated until the first player to run out of stones loses.When both parties do their best, who wins, the first mover or the second mover?Or will the game never end?"...No, it's impossible, this." "What?" "Not what! How are you going to come up with a natural problem setting for this?" "Hahaha. Isn't it your job to think about that? You're in charge of the writing, right?" "There's a limit to round tossing... First of all, the rules of the game are too unnatural." "Is that so? I think it's a common stone-picking game.""Don't you think there are many stone-picking games where the player can increase the number of stones inexhaustibly according to his will?" "Oh, you just said something good." "Yeah, it doesn't matter if you create stones with your will... please think about that a little." "That's right. How about taking stones with the will of the deceased son who inherits the will of a doctor who died by hanging?" "That's not the point to think about! We're talking about the stone-picking game!""Are you sure? But 'tori' is difficult because the only homonym is bird." "Stop playing with words for now! I'm telling you to come up with a natural problem setting!" "So that's your job, right? I'm just an intermediary. I act as an intermediary between the drafter and the writer." "Damn... Drafter, why are you creating such a troublesome problem..." "A message from the drafter to you. "Heehee lol"I'm going to torture you as much as I can, lol!" "Damn that bastard! I'll hit you with one punch someday!" "Well, I guess it's thanks in part to my advice, ``Don't you think it's really difficult to set up a stone-picking game where the number of stones increases?'' "Damn you too! I'll hit you now, no matter when, I won't tell you!"``I'm going to write it down, okay?'' ``The title is ``The person in charge of problem writing doesn't work!'' The conversation we had is posted in its entirety.'' ``Please, stop!What will everyone say from now on if such a silly problem is published under my name?'' ``Hahaha. People who can't even meet deadlines deserve to be eliminated from society.'' ``Dammit!If this happens, I'll have to come up with a natural problem setting!'' (*In the end, it was published as is.)

International Carpenters Professionals Company (ICPC) is a top construction company with a lot of expert carpenters. What makes ICPC a top company is their original language. The syntax of the language is simply given in CFG as follows: In other words, a right parenthesis can be closed by a left parenthesis and a left parenthesis can be closed by a right parenthesis in this language. Alex, a grad student mastering linguistics, decided to study ICPC's language. As a first step of the study, he needs to judge whether a text is well-formed in the language or not. Then, he asked you, a great programmer, to write a program for the judgement. Alex's request is as follows: You have an empty string S in the beginning, and construct longer string by inserting a sequence of '(' or ')' into the string. You will receive q queries, each of which consists of three elements (p, c, n) , where p is the position to insert, n is the number of characters to insert and c is either '(' or ')', the character to insert. For each query, your program should insert c repeated by n times into the p -th position of S from the beginning. Also it should output, after performing each insert operation, "Yes" if S is in the language and "No" if S is not in the language. Please help Alex to support his study, otherwise he will fail to graduate the college.

A student, Kita_masa, is taking an English examination. In this examination, he has to write a sentence of length m . Since he completely forgot the English grammar, he decided to consider all sentences of length m constructed by concatenating the words he knows and write the K -th sentence among the candidates sorted in lexicographic order. He believes that it must be the correct sentence because K is today's lucky number for him. Each word may be used multiple times (or it's also fine not to use it at all) and the sentence does not contain any extra character between words. Two sentences are considered different if the order of the words are different even if the concatenation resulted in the same string.

There is an evil creature in a square on N-by-M grid ( 2 \leq N, M \leq 100 ), and you want to kill it using a laser generator located in a different square. Since the location and direction of the laser generator are fixed, you may need to use several mirrors to reflect laser beams. There are some obstacles on the grid and you have a limited number of mirrors. Please find out whether it is possible to kill the creature, and if possible, find the minimum number of mirrors. There are two types of single-sided mirrors; type P mirrors can be placed at the angle of 45 or 225 degrees from east-west direction, and type Q mirrors can be placed with at the angle of 135 or 315 degrees. For example, four mirrors are located properly, laser go through like the following. Note that mirrors are single-sided, and thus back side (the side with cross in the above picture) is not reflective. You have A type P mirrors, and also A type Q mirrors ( 0 \leq A \leq 10 ). Although you cannot put mirrors onto the squares with the creature or the laser generator, laser beam can pass through the square. Evil creature is killed if the laser reaches the square it is in.

You are a student looking for a job. Today you had an employment examination for an IT company. They asked you to write an efficient program to perform several operations. First, they showed you an N \times N square matrix and a list of operations. All operations but one modify the matrix, and the last operation outputs the character in a specified cell. Please remember that you need to output the final matrix after you finish all the operations. Followings are the detail of the operations:

You are a programmer who loves bishojo games (a sub-genre of dating simulation games). A game, which is titled "I * C * P * C!" and was released yesterday, has arrived to you just now. This game has multiple endings. When you complete all of those endings, you can get a special figure of the main heroine, Sakuya. So, you want to hurry and play the game! But, let's calm down a bit and think how to complete all of the endings in the shortest time first. In fact, you have a special skill that allows you to know the structure of branching points of games. By using the skill, you have found out that all of the branching points in this game are to select two choices "Yes" or "No", and once a different choice is taken the branched stories flow to different endings; they won't converge any more, like a binary tree. You also noticed that it takes exactly one minute to proceed the game from a branching point to another branching point or to an ending. In addition, you can assume it only takes negligible time to return to the beginning of the game (``reset'') and to play from the beginning to the first branching point. The game has an additional feature called "Quick Save", which can significantly reduce the playing time for completion. The feature allows you to record the point where you are currently playing and return there at any time later. You can record any number of times, but you can hold only the last recorded point. That is, when you use Quick Save, you overwrite the previous record. If you want to return to the overwritten point, you must play the game from the beginning once again. Well, let's estimate how long it will take for completing all of the endings in the shortest time.
An undirected graph is given. Each edge of the graph disappears with a constant probability. Calculate the probability with which the remained graph is connected.

``Domino effect'' is a famous play using dominoes. A player sets up a chain of dominoes stood. After a chain is formed, the player topples one end of the dominoes. The first domino topples the second domino, the second topples the third and so on. You are playing domino effect. Before you finish to set up a chain of domino, a domino block started to topple, unfortunately. You have to stop the toppling as soon as possible. The domino chain forms a polygonal line on a two-dimensional coordinate system without self intersections. The toppling starts from a certain point on the domino chain and continues toward the both end of the chain. If the toppling starts on an end of the chain, the toppling continue toward the other end. The toppling of a direction stops when you touch the toppling point or the toppling reaches an end of the domino chain. You can assume that: You will given the form of the domino chain, the starting point of the toppling, your coordinates when the toppling started, the toppling velocity and the your velocity. You are task is to write a program that calculates your optimal move to stop the toppling at the earliest timing and calculates the minimum time to stop the toppling.

The Sun is a great heavenly body. The Sun is worshiped by various religions. Bob loves the Sun and loves any object that is similar to the Sun. He noticed that he can find the shape of the Sun in certain graphs. He calls such graphs "Sunny". We define the property "Sunny" mathematically. A graph G=(V,E) with a vertex v \in V is called "Sunny" when there exists a subgraph G'=(V,E'), E' \subseteq E that has the following two properties. (Be careful, the set of vertices must be the same.) 1. The connected component containing v is a cycle that consists of three or more vertices. 2. Every other component has exactly two vertices. The following picture is an example of a subgraph G'=(V,E') that has the above property. Given a simple graph (In each edge, two end points are different. Every pair of vertices has one or no edge.) G=(V,E) , write a program that decides whether the given graph with the vertex 1 is "Sunny" or not.
A Boolean expression is given. In the expression, each variable appears exactly once. Calculate the number of variable assignments that make the expression evaluate to true.

Kita_masa is planning a trip around the world. This world has N countries and the country i has M_i cities. Kita_masa wants to visit every city exactly once, and return back to the starting city. In this world, people can travel only by airplane. There are two kinds of airlines: domestic and international lines. Since international airports require special facilities such as customs and passport control, only a few cities in each country have international airports. You are given a list of all flight routes in this world and prices for each route. Now it's time to calculate the cheapest route for Kita_masa's world trip!

Time Limit: 8 sec / Memory Limit: 64 MB 2012/2/8 No one has ever figured out which came first, the chicken or the egg?However, the answer is said to be written in the documents obtained during the excavation at the Lake Biwa ruins.2012/2/9 The document consisted only of the words chicken and egg.At this rate, I don't know the answer.2012/2/10 As I read the document further, I found that the order of the words in the document represents the order in which they were born.If it says "eggchicken", it means that the chicken was laid first, and then the chicken laid the egg.2012/2/11 I found the sentence "eggchickenchickenegg".In this sentence, an egg is laid first, and then a chicken is laid from that egg.Furthermore, if we follow yesterday's rules, this chicken will give birth to chickens.This is strange since chickens have to lay eggs.2012/2/12 While doing research, I found out that sentences are divided when the same word appears consecutively."eggchickenchickenegg" is split into the sentences "eggchicken" and "chickenegg".When there are multiple sentences, it seems that the first word indicates that they were born in the same era.In the previous example, the first word "egg" in "eggchicken" and the first word "chicken" in "chickenegg" seem to indicate that they were born in the same era.Furthermore, it means that the chicken and egg that produced these were also born in the same era.yeah?In this case, you won't know which one was laid first, the chicken or the egg.2012/2/13 It seems that among the ones born in the same era, the one written earlier in the sentence was born earlier.In yesterday's example, since the word "chicken" comes earlier in the sentence, it seems that the chicken was born first.I was able to understand the rules of decoding.I feel safe now.No, we still have to decipher a huge amount of documents.We must hurry to decipher it.2012/3/15 It seems that the lifespan is almost over.I can't move my arms or legs properly.In the end, I was unable to decipher this document due to lack of time.At this time, before you leave, could anyone please tell me which came first...You are a person who came at the request of a relative.Please decipher it to make the grandfather's last wish come true.

Time Limit: 8 sec / Memory Limit: 64 MB After spending too much money on gambling such as pachinko and horse racing, you are covered in debt and are taken to the underground prison of the T-ai group.Here you have to work like a slave until you pay off your debt.Also, since there are many people in the same situation as you in this prison, they are divided into several groups.One group has a custom of rolling three dice and putting them in a bowl for fun.I recently heard that the group leader there was in some trouble, but it has nothing to do with you directly.The group you belong to has a custom of rolling one die and having fun.The rules are simple: if you roll the dice and get the highest value, you win.However, the special thing is that the probability of a certain side of the dice being used is similarly improbable.There are also many types of dice, ranging from 10-sided ones to 100-sided ones.There are even sides with no values ​​written on them.If you come across a side with no value written on it, roll again.The team leader in charge of this game presented me with some dice that I could roll beforehand, but sometimes I don't feel like I can win no matter which one I use.Therefore, I would like to find out whether there are any given dice that have higher values ​​than the expected value of the dice used by the group leader.The expected value is high when it is greater than 0.0000001 than the group leader's dice.

Time Limit: 8 sec / Memory Limit: 64 MB Lucky bags are one of the business customs to celebrate the beginning of the year.The popular lucky bags have many people lining up even before the store opens and rushing to the target lucky bag.Fascinated by this special event, you have been making plans to get the lucky bag you want this year as well.However, the manager of a certain department store, who was the one who brought in the customers, was worried.It looks like the floor won't be ready in time for the store's opening.Then the store manager came up with an idea.The idea is that after the store opens, it may be possible to continue operating if the store is gradually opened up.You want to find out from what time of day the lucky bag you are looking for is available based on the ever-changing information in the store.

Time Limit: 8 sec / Memory Limit: 64 MB A synchronizer in your laboratory was suffering from a bug in the software he was implementing. His software runs at a very low layer, so he cannot use high-performance debuggers or testing tools. Since the environment is not supported by the OS, he cannot use things like exception handling that are supposed to be available in the language, and even most of the standard libraries cannot be used.A garbage collector is a dream come true. Therefore, he has been verifying and fixing it by himself through trial and error. However, this bug seems to be quite deep-rooted, and he has been repeating the verification for almost a month. Because he was unable to fix this bug by the deadline, he has decided not to submit his paper. His strange voice can be heard intermittently in the laboratory at night.I decided to help him verify his software. Thanks to his hard work so far, it seems that there is a high possibility that a so-called memory leak has occurred in the program. It seems that somewhere in the program, he forgot to free the allocated memory, and the available memory was used up. His program was too complicated as it was, so he prepared a pattern that simply expressed the process of allocating and freeing memory.He would like us to investigate the extent to which memory leaks occur in each of these patterns. First, it seems that in his program, a module called a memory manager provides functions such as allocating and releasing memory areas. To be exact, allocating a memory area means borrowing the necessary amount from a memory area called a heap managed by the memory manager and obtaining a "reference" to that area. Note that there is an upper limit to the heap.No more memory can be used by the program. The size of a memory area is measured in ``bytes.'' A ``reference'' is a value that indicates where the area is located. A reference can be assigned to a variable or used directly as an argument to pass to a function that allocates or releases another memory area. Releasing a memory area means returning a memory area that is no longer in use to the memory manager. The pattern he prepared is the following BNF.Variables function as a container for references, and can be named using any uppercase letter of the alphabet. Variables are allocated in an area separate from the heap, which is the problem here, so there is no need to worry about the amount of memory used by the variables themselves. Furthermore, variables are not updated unless a value is explicitly assigned to them. Also, their initial values are not particularly determined.You must be careful that you do not know what value it has. Note that a variable returns the reference it has as the evaluation result. Assign copies the value of the reference. The reference obtained by evaluating the expression on the right side is copied to the variable on the left side. Uninitialized variables or free variables can also be assigned. This is because a reference is only a "value" that indicates the location of an area.Even if you assign to another variable, you will not actually manipulate an unintended area. Note that the assignment itself returns the reference assigned to the variable. malloc, free, and clone indicate that you are calling the functions of the memory manager. Note that the memory allocation algorithms of malloc and clone have been refined, so the memory area for allocation will not become fragmented. Furthermore, the freed area is immediately released and can be reused.However, if you clone or free an uninitialized area or an area that has already been freed, you will end up copying or freeing an unintended memory area, and you don't know what will happen. This kind of behavior may be the cause of another bug, so you need to let him know. If you try to malloc beyond the given memory limit, a special reference called NULL will be returned. Similarly, if there is no memory left for the area to be copied with clone,A NULL is returned. This NULL indicates that a specific memory area is not referenced. Just like a normal reference, a NULL can be assigned to a variable and passed to clone or free which take the reference as an argument. However, if you pass NULL as an argument to free, nothing is guaranteed to happen. Also, if you pass NULL as an argument to clone, nothing will happen and NULL will be returned.Expressions (expr) that return a reference when evaluated can be nested using "(" ")". In this case, the result of the evaluation inside "(" ")" will be returned outside of it. Note that free does not return a reference, so it cannot be nested. Your job is to calculate the total number of bytes of unreferenced allocated memory space after executing all the lines given as a pattern.After executing all the given lines, you don't need to worry about the areas where one or more variables have references. Also, if you discover the cause of another bug, you must let him know. You have written a program to analyze patterns. To do this, you have considered some of the patterns he showed. Figure 1 shows the state transition when the following pattern is executed with a heap of 100 bytes. First,In the first line, we allocate a 10-byte area using malloc and assign a reference to that area to variable A.The remaining usable heap is 90 bytes.Next, in the second line, we pass variable A that has a reference to the area we just allocated to clone.As a result, a new 10-byte area is allocated, and the remaining usable heap is 80 bytes.The reference to the 10-byte area is assigned to variable B. Then, in the third line, variable A, which has a reference to the area allocated in the first line, is passed to free.This frees the 10-byte area, and the remaining usable heap is 90 bytes.This completes the execution of this pattern.In this case, there is no unreferenced allocated area, so the verification result isThe 10-byte area secured in the second line is not treated as a memory leak because variable B has a reference to it. Also, after variable A is passed to free in the third line, it has not been updated by assignment. Therefore, at this point, it still has a reference to the area that was released earlier. If there is a subsequent instruction to free or clone this area, an error will be returned as a verification result.Figure 2 shows the state transition when executing the following pattern with 100 heaps. In this case, malloc(10) is executed first, and a 10-byte area is allocated. A reference to this area is passed as is to clone, and an additional 10-byte area is allocated. The area allocated by this clone is also passed to the outer clone, and an additional 10-byte area is allocated.A reference to the last allocated area is assigned to variable A. None of the variables have references to the area allocated by the first malloc or the inner clone, and they cannot be released. Therefore, the verification result for this pattern is 20 bytes.

Time Limit: 8 sec / Memory Limit: 64 MB Your friend works part-time at a moving company with the Kirin mark.His job is to carry things out of buildings.This time, he had to carry some items out of an n-story building.Fortunately, this building is equipped with one elevator, so I decided to take my luggage out using the elevator.There are multiple pieces of luggage to be carried out on each floor, and each piece has a different weight.Elevators have a weight limit set, and no baggage exceeding weight W can be placed on the elevator.Elevators are spacious enough to carry as much luggage as you want, as long as it meets the weight limit.Elevators can be used to load and unload luggage at the floor where it has stopped.As a veteran part-time worker, he travels by stairs instead of taking elevators.After loading luggage into the elevator, the elevator goes up the stairs to the next floor where the elevator stops, and then loads and unloads the luggage.As a veteran part-time worker, he can load and unload luggage very quickly, so he can ignore the elevator stoppage time at each floor.Therefore, the time required to move all luggage to the first floor can be easily calculated from the distance traveled by the elevator.He wants to move his luggage to the first floor as soon as possible.Although he is a veteran part-time worker, he doesn't know what procedures to take in and out of luggage on each floor.So he asked you, his friend, for help.Your task is to find the minimum elevator travel required for him to move all his luggage to the first floor.

Time Limit: 8 sec / Memory Limit: 64 MB Rentaro Okabe (nicknamed Okaren) is a leading figure in Western music.He was a genius who was able to compose music from the age of five, and his song ``Siege Hammer'', which he wrote and composed, is very famous.Okaren was a talented man, but unfortunately he died after being hit by a fish he ate at a restaurant....At least, that's what happens in this world line.What is a world line?One history corresponds to one world line.The history we have walked through is just one of the infinite number of world lines, and a different history is written on another world line.In order to face the harsh future that awaits him, Okaren decides to set out on a journey to another world line.The world line Okalen is aiming for is the famous world line ``Hyadain's Gate.''At "Hyadain's Gate", all fish are frozen and preserved fresh using ice magic, so there is no one who can handle the fish.The world line is represented by a string of lowercase letters of the alphabet.The character string representing "Hyadain's Gate" has already been elucidated, but it is represented by a very long character string.To express this concisely, N repetitions of the string seq are compressed and expressed as N ( seq ).Here, N is a natural number greater than or equal to 1, and seq is a string of lowercase letters, a compressed string, or a string of several concatenated strings.For example, the following notation represents the string bbbababababxbbbababababx.Okaren is able to read the world line he is currently in and the next world line he can move to using his ``Magic Eye Reading Hyadainer.''The world line where he is located is far away from "Hyadain's Gate", so he cannot directly travel to "Hyadain's Gate".In order to get as close to "Hyadain's Gate" as possible, Okalen decided to move to a world line with a high degree of similarity to "Hyadain's Gate".If the string representing "Hyadain's Gate" is x and the string representing a certain world line is y, then the similarity of y to x is the number of substrings of y that also appear as substrings in x.However, even if the same string appears multiple times as a substring of y, they are counted separately.If there are multiple world lines with the same degree of similarity, the world line given as input first is considered to have a higher degree of similarity.Given a string representing the world line ``Hyadain's Gate'' and multiple character strings corresponding to the next world line to which Okaren can move, I would like you to find the world line with the highest degree of similarity to ``Hyadain's Gate.''I wish you good luck.El Psi Congaree.

Time Limit: 8 sec / Memory Limit: 64 MB The demon king who destroyed the legendary sword finally cornered the hero into a certain ruin.The hero is already a bag of mice, but his opponent is a hero who has received the blessings of the gods.Even without the legendary sword, there's no telling what kind of miracles will be on his side.The Demon King has also consumed a large amount of magical power due to the destruction of the legendary sword and the battles thus far, and is exhausted.Therefore, to be on the safe side, I decided to summon a large number of powerful familiars and launch a simultaneous attack.However, using a powerful familiar consumes a large amount of magical power.In the unlikely event that the hero overcomes the all-out attack by his familiar, he wants to conserve his magical power as much as possible.Therefore, it was decided to let the familiars rush in from the entrance at once and see how many familiars could attack the hero at once.As the Demon King's right-hand man, you summoned a low-grade familiar and had him investigate the structure of the ruins.However, perhaps because a low-grade familiar was used, the content reported was extremely difficult to understand.Since it would be useless as it is, I decided to analyze the familiar's report.First, regarding the entrance to the ruins, there are multiple entrances.It was found that these entrances were always located at the end of a passageway or at the intersection of several passageways.Also, it seems that the hero is not at the entrance, but at the end of the passage or at the intersection.The problem is the passage inside the ruins.There are no magical barriers or guardians in this ruin, but there are intricate passages.Fortunately, there don't seem to be any one-way traps or traps that make it impossible to pass through the passage again.Of course, there are no traps that change the structure of the entire interior of the ruins every time you enter.The familiars reported information about each passage in the form of the coordinates of the two end points of the passage within the ruins and the number of familiars who could pass through the passage at the same time.Passages within the ruins reported by familiars may intersect with each other.When two paths intersect at one point, this is counted as one intersection.At this time, the number of familiars that can pass through is distributed proportionally to the length of the passage.For example, as shown in Figure 1, if a path connects (0,0) and (10,0) through which 20 familiars can pass, but an intersection is created at (4.5,0), it will be divided into two paths: one connecting (0,0) and (4.5,0) through which 9 familiars can pass, and the path connecting (4.5,0) and (10,0) through which 11 familiars can pass.Furthermore, the reported passages may overlap.In this case, the sum of the overlapping passages will be the number that the familiar can pass through.For example, as shown in Figure 2, given a path connecting (0,0) and (10,0) through which 10 familiars can pass, and a path connecting (2,0) and (12,0) through which 20 familiars can pass, there is a path connecting (0,0) and (2,0) through which 2 familiars can pass,It is divided into a passage that connects (2,0) and (10,0), where 24 familiars (8+16) can pass, and a passage that connects (10,0) and (12,0), where four familiars can pass.When dividing or merging based on the reported path information, the number of familiars that can be passed through the calculations may be a decimal.Since a familiar cannot be made smaller or split in half, it would be best to round down the decimal part.By analyzing the information from the familiar to this point, the structure within the ruins has become clear.The only thing left to do is figure out how many familiars can attack the hero at once.

Mary Thomas has a number of sheets of squared paper. Some of squares are painted either in black or some colorful color (such as red and blue) on the front side. Cutting off the unpainted part, she will have eight opened-up unit cubes. A unit cube here refers to a cube of which each face consists of one square. She is going to build those eight unit cubes with the front side exposed and then a bicube with them. A bicube is a cube of the size 2 × 2 × 2, consisting of eight unit cubes, that satisfies the following conditions: Your task is to write a program that reads the specification of a sheet of squared paper and decides whether a bicube can be built with the eight unit cubes resulting from it.

A browser-based puzzle game called "Bubble Puzzle" is now popular on the Internet. The puzzle is played on a 4 × 4 grid, and initially there are several bubbles in the grid squares. Each bubble has a state expressed by a positive integer, and the state changes when the bubble gets stimulated. You can stimulate a bubble by clicking on it, and this will increase the bubble's state by 1. You can also click on an empty square. In this case, a bubble with state 1 is newly created in the square. When the state of a bubble becomes 5 or higher, the bubble blows up and disappears, and small waterdrops start flying in four directions (up, down, left and right). These waterdrops move one square per second. At the moment when a bubble blows up, 4 waterdrops are in the square the bubble was in, and one second later they are in the adjacent squares, and so on. A waterdrop disappears either when it hits a bubble or goes outside the grid. When a waterdrop hits a bubble, the state of the bubble gets increased by 1. Similarly, if a bubble is hit by more than one water drop at the same time, its state is increased by the number of the hitting waterdrops. Please note that waterdrops do not collide with each other. As shown in the figure above, waterdrops created by a blow-up may cause other blow-ups. In other words, one blow-up can trigger a chain reaction. You are not allowed to click on any square while waterdrops are flying (i.e., you have to wait until a chain reaction ends). The goal of this puzzle is to blow up all the bubbles and make all the squares empty as quick as possible. Your mission is to calculate the minimum number of clicks required to solve the puzzle.

There is a grid of size W × H surrounded by walls. Some cells in the grid are occupied by slimes. The slimes want to unite with each other and become a "King Slime". In each move, a slime can move to east, west, south and north direction until it enters a cell occupied by another slime or hit the surrounding wall. If two slimes come together, they unite and become a new slime. Your task is write a program which calculates the minimum number of moves that all the slimes unite and become a King Slime. Suppose slimes move one by one and they never move simultaneously.

Honestly, a rabbit does not matter. There is a rabbit playing a stage system action game. In this game, every stage has a difficulty level. The rabbit, which always needs challenges, basically wants to play more difficult stages than he has ever played. However, he sometimes needs rest too. So, to compromise, he admitted to play T or less levels easier stages than the preceding one. How many ways are there to play all the stages at once, while honoring the convention above? Maybe the answer will be a large number. So, let me know the answer modulo 1,000,000,007.

There is a tree that has n nodes and n-1 edges. There are military bases on t out of the n nodes. We want to disconnect the bases as much as possible by destroying k edges. The tree will be split into k+1 regions when we destroy k edges. Given the purpose to disconnect the bases, we only consider to split in a way that each of these k+1 regions has at least one base. When we destroy an edge, we must pay destroying cost. Find the minimum destroying cost to split the tree.

Rotation is one of several popular pocket billiards games. It uses 15 balls numbered from 1 to 15, and set them up as illustrated in the following figure at the beginning of a game. (Note: the ball order is modified from real-world Rotation rules for simplicity of the problem.) You are an engineer developing an automatic billiards machine. For the first step you had to build a machine that sets up the initial condition. This project went well, and finally made up a machine that could arrange the balls in the triangular shape. However, unfortunately, it could not place the balls in the correct order. So now you are trying to build another machine that fixes the order of balls by swapping them. To cut off the cost, it is only allowed to swap the ball #1 with its neighboring balls that are not in the same row. For example, in the case below, only the following pairs can be swapped: (1,2), (1,3), (1,8), and (1,9). Write a program that calculates the minimum number of swaps required.

For a positive integer a , let S(a) be the sum of the digits in base l . Also let L(a) be the minimum k such that S^k(a) is less than or equal to l-1 . Find the minimum a such that L(a) = N for a given N , and print a modulo m .

The king demon is waiting in his dungeon to defeat a brave man. His dungeon consists of H \times W grids. Each cell is connected to four (i.e. north, south, east and west) neighboring cells and some cells are occupied by obstacles. To attack the brave man, the king demon created and sent a servant that walks around in the dungeon. However, soon the king demon found that the servant does not work as he wants. The servant is too dumb. If the dungeon had cyclic path, it might keep walking along the cycle forever.
